### FILE: apps/backend/SETUP_NEW_STRUCTURE.md

Source path: `apps/backend/SETUP_NEW_STRUCTURE.md`

```md
  1 | # 🏗️ InsightFlow - New Folder Structure Setup
  2 | 
  3 | ## 📋 Current Status
  4 | 
  5 | The new folder structure has been **partially implemented**. Here's what's in place:
  6 | 
  7 | ```
  8 | apps/backend/src/
  9 | ├── config/
 10 | │   ├── environment.js       ✅ (Centralized config)
 11 | │   ├── constants.js         ✅
 12 | │   └── gemini-config.js     ✅
 13 | ├── core/
 14 | │   └── server.js            ✅ (HTTP server setup)
 15 | ├── database/
 16 | │   └── (existing files)
 17 | ├── services/
 18 | │   ├── ai/
 19 | │   │   ├── ai-manager.js    ✅ (Master AI orchestrator)
 20 | │   │   ├── providers/
 21 | │   │   │   ├── ollama-provider.js    ✅
 22 | │   │   │   ├── gemini-provider.js   ✅
 23 | │   │   │   ├── openai-provider.js   ✅
 24 | │   │   │   └── anthropic-provider.js ✅
 25 | │   │   └── utils/
 26 | │   ├── analytics/          ✅ (schema-detector, analytics-service, etc.)
 27 | │   ├── data/               ✅ (data-merger, data-sampling-service)
 28 | │   ├── export/             ✅ (report-generator)
 29 | │   ├── query/              ✅
 30 | │   ├── recommendation/    ✅
 31 | │   ├── visualization/     ✅
 32 | │   └── alert-service.js    ✅
 33 | ├── routes/
 34 | │   ├── index.js           ✅ (Route aggregator)
 35 | │   ├── ai.js              ✅
 36 | │   ├── analytics.js       ✅
 37 | │   ├── chat.js            ✅
 38 | │   ├── datasets.js        ✅
 39 | │   ├── export.js          ✅
 40 | │   └── health.js          ✅
 41 | ├── middleware/
 42 | │   ├── error-handler.js   ✅
 43 | │   ├── cors.js            ✅
 44 | │   └── request-logger.js  ✅
 45 | ├── utils/
 46 | │   ├── response-utils.js  ✅
 47 | │   └── (other utilities)
 48 | ├── index.js               ✅ (New entry point)
 49 | ├── server.js              ⚠️ (Legacy - to be removed)
 50 | └── types/
 51 | ```
 52 | 
 53 | ## ✅ What's Working
 54 | 
 55 | 1. **AI Provider System** - Full fallback chain: Ollama → Gemini → OpenAI → Anthropic
 56 | 2. **Configuration** - Centralized in `config/environment.js`
 57 | 3. **Modular Routes** - Organized in `routes/`
 58 | 4. **Services** - Reorganized by domain (ai, analytics, data, export, etc.)
 59 | 
 60 | ## 🔧 To Use the New Structure
 61 | 
 62 | ### 1. Update package.json scripts
 63 | 
 64 | Update `apps/backend/package.json`:
 65 | 
 66 | ```json
 67 | {
 68 |   "scripts": {
 69 |     "dev": "node src/index.js",
 70 |     "dev:watch": "node --watch src/index.js",
 71 |     "start": "node src/index.js"
 72 |   }
 73 | }
 74 | ```
 75 | 
 76 | ### 2. Start the Server
 77 | 
 78 | ```bash
 79 | cd apps/backend
 80 | npm run dev
 81 | ```
 82 | 
 83 | ### 3. Test the Endpoints
 84 | 
 85 | ```bash
 86 | # Health check
 87 | curl http://localhost:3001/api/health
 88 | 
 89 | # AI status
 90 | curl http://localhost:3001/api/ai/status
 91 | 
 92 | # Test AI generation
 93 | curl -X POST http://localhost:3001/api/ai/test \
 94 |   -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
 95 |   -d '{"prompt":"Hello!"}'
 96 | ```
 97 | 
 98 | ## 📊 AI Provider Configuration
 99 | 
100 | ### Environment Variables (.env)
101 | 
102 | ```env
103 | # Primary - Ollama (Local)
104 | OLLAMA_BASE_URL=http://localhost:11434
105 | OLLAMA_MODEL=llama3.2
106 | OLLAMA_CHAT_MODEL=neural-chat:7b
107 | 
108 | # Fallback 1 - Google Gemini
109 | GOOGLE_API_KEY=[REDACTED_SECRET]
110 | 
111 | # Fallback 2 - OpenAI
112 | OPENAI_API_KEY=[REDACTED_SECRET]
113 | 
114 | # Fallback 3 - Anthropic Claude
115 | ANTHROPIC_API_KEY=[REDACTED_SECRET]
116 | 
117 | # Provider Priority
118 | AI_PROVIDER_PRIORITY=ollama,gemini,openai,anthropic
119 | ENABLE_AI_FALLBACK=true
120 | ```
121 | 
122 | ## 🧪 Testing Checklist
123 | 
124 | - [ ] Server starts: `npm run dev`
125 | - [ ] Health check: `curl http://localhost:3001/api/health`
126 | - [ ] AI status: `curl http://localhost:3001/api/ai/status`
127 | - [ ] Ollama working: Check provider status
128 | - [ ] Fallback works: Disable Ollama and test
129 | 
130 | ## 🧹 Cleanup (When Ready)
131 | 
132 | Once the new structure is verified working, remove legacy files:
133 | 
134 | ```bash
135 | # Remove old server file (keep backup first)
136 | cp src/server.js src/server.js.bak
137 | rm src/server.js
138 | 
139 | # Remove old service files that have been migrated
140 | rm -f src/services/ai-analyzer.js
141 | rm -f src/services/ai-cascade-service.js
142 | rm -f src/services/ollama-ai-service.js
143 | rm -f src/services/gemini-ai-service.js
144 | rm -f src/services/ollama-service.js
145 | ```
146 | 
147 | ## 📚 Documentation
148 | 
149 | - **FOLDER_STRUCTURE.md** - Detailed architecture explanation
150 | - **API Endpoints** - See `routes/index.js` for available endpoints
151 | 
152 | ## ✅ Success Indicators
153 | 
154 | - Server logs show: "✅ InsightFlow API running on http://localhost:3001"
155 | - `/api/health` responds with status
156 | - `/api/ai/status` shows available providers
157 | - Ollama shows as active provider when running
158 | 
159 | ## 🚨 Troubleshooting
160 | 
161 | ### Port already in use
162 | ```bash
163 | lsof -ti:3001 | xargs kill -9
164 | # Or change PORT in .env
165 | ```
166 | 
167 | ### Ollama not available
168 | ```bash
169 | # Ensure Ollama is running
170 | ollama serve
171 | 
172 | # Pull models
173 | ollama pull llama3.2
174 | ollama pull neural-chat:7b
175 | 
176 | # Test connection
177 | curl http://localhost:11434/api/tags
178 | ```
179 | 
180 | ### Import errors
181 | - Check relative paths in imports
182 | - Ensure all files have `.js` extensions
183 | - Verify node_modules is installed
184 | 
185 | ## 🎯 Next Steps
186 | 
187 | 1. ✅ Structure already implemented
188 | 2. ✅ Test the new entry point (`npm run dev`)
189 | 3. ✅ Verify all endpoints work
190 | 4. 🧹 Clean up legacy files when ready
```

  1 | import { mkdtempSync } from "node:fs";
  2 | import { tmpdir } from "node:os";
  3 | import path from "node:path";
  4 | import { afterEach, describe, expect, it, vi } from "vitest";
  5 | import { makeReq, makeRes } from "./test-helpers.js";
  6 | 
  7 | const useIsolatedDataDir = () => {
  8 |   const dataDir = mkdtempSync(path.join(tmpdir(), "insightflow-test-"));
  9 |   process.env.DATA_DIR = dataDir;
 10 |   return dataDir;
 11 | };
 12 | 
 13 | afterEach(() => {
 14 |   vi.resetModules();
 15 |   vi.restoreAllMocks();
 16 |   delete process.env.DATA_DIR;
 17 | });
 18 | 
 19 | describe("canonical Node HTTP runtime", () => {
 20 |   it("returns a JSON error response for async route dispatch failures", async () => {
 21 |     vi.doMock("../routes/index.js", () => ({
 22 |       setupRoutes: vi.fn(async () => {
 23 |         throw new Error("async route failed");
 24 |       }),
 25 |     }));
 26 | 
 27 |     const { createHttpServer } = await import("../core/server.js");
 28 |     const server = createHttpServer();
 29 | 
 30 |     await new Promise((resolve) => server.listen(0, "127.0.0.1", resolve));
 31 |     const address = server.address();
 32 | 
 33 |     try {
 34 |       const response = await fetch(`http://127.0.0.1:${address.port}/api/test`);
 35 |       const body = await response.json();
 36 | 
 37 |       expect(response.status).toBe(500);
 38 |       expect(body.success).toBe(false);
 39 |       expect(body.error.message).toBe("Internal server error");
 40 |     } finally {
 41 |       await new Promise((resolve) => server.close(resolve));
 42 |     }
 43 |   });
 44 | });
 45 | 
 46 | describe("dataset repository row identity", () => {
 47 |   it("patches a dataset row by exposed __rowId", async () => {
 48 |     useIsolatedDataDir();
 49 |     const { createDataset, patchDatasetRow } = await import(
 50 |       "../database/dataset-repository.js"
 51 |     );
 52 | 
 53 |     const dataset = createDataset({
 54 |       name: "Row Identity",
 55 |       columns: [{ name: "Name", type: "string" }],
 56 |       rows: [{ Name: "first" }, { Name: "second" }],
 57 |     });
 58 | 
 59 |     const targetRowId = dataset.rows[1].__rowId;
 60 |     const updated = patchDatasetRow({
 61 |       datasetId: dataset.id,
 62 |       rowId: targetRowId,
 63 |       column: "Name",
 64 |       value: "updated second",
 65 |     });
 66 | 
 67 |     expect(updated.rows[1]).toMatchObject({
 68 |       __rowId: targetRowId,
 69 |       Name: "updated second",
 70 |     });
 71 |     expect(updated.rows[0].Name).toBe("first");
 72 |   });
 73 | });
 74 | 
 75 | describe("/api/state hydration", () => {
 76 |   it("hydrates current dataset and chat history from persisted storage", async () => {
 77 |     useIsolatedDataDir();
 78 |     const { createDataset, saveChatMessage } = await import(
 79 |       "../database/dataset-repository.js"
 80 |     );
 81 |     const { handleStateRoutes, setState } = await import("../routes/state.js");
 82 | 
 83 |     const dataset = createDataset({
 84 |       name: "Persisted State",
 85 |       columns: [{ name: "Revenue", type: "number" }],
 86 |       rows: [{ Revenue: 100 }],
 87 |     });
 88 | 
 89 |     saveChatMessage(dataset.id, {
 90 |       id: "message-1",
 91 |       role: "user",
 92 |       content: "What is revenue?",
 93 |       timestamp: "2026-05-30T00:00:00.000Z",
 94 |     });
 95 | 
 96 |     setState({ dataset: null, chatMessages: [], analysis: { transient: true } });
 97 | 
 98 |     const response = makeRes();
 99 |     const handled = await handleStateRoutes(makeReq("GET"), response, "/api/state");
100 |     const body = response.json();
101 | 
102 |     expect(handled).toBe(true);
103 |     expect(body.data.dataset.id).toBe(dataset.id);
104 |     expect(body.data.chatMessages).toHaveLength(1);
105 |     expect(body.data.chatMessages[0]).toMatchObject({
106 |       id: "message-1",
107 |       content: "What is revenue?",
108 |     });
109 |     expect(body.data.analysis).toEqual({ transient: true });
110 |   });
111 | });
112 |

### FILE: apps/backend/src/__tests__/dashboard-plan-engine.test.js

Source path: `apps/backend/src/__tests__/dashboard-plan-engine.test.js`

```js
 1 | import { describe, expect, it } from "vitest";
 2 | import { buildSchemaProfile } from "../services/ai-analyst/schema-fingerprint.js";
 3 | import {
 4 |   buildRuleDashboardPlan,
 5 |   sanitizeChartSpec,
 6 | } from "../services/ai-analyst/dashboard-plan-engine.js";
 7 | import { validateChartCombination } from "../services/ai-analyst/dashboard-quality-guardian.js";
 8 | import { salaryDataset } from "./test-helpers.js";
 9 | 
10 | describe("dashboard plan engine", () => {
11 |   const profile = buildSchemaProfile(salaryDataset);
12 | 
13 |   it("generates KPI and chart specs from schema only", () => {
14 |     const plan = buildRuleDashboardPlan(profile);
15 |     const titles = [...plan.kpis, ...plan.charts].map((item) => item.title);
16 | 
17 |     expect(titles).toContain("Total Records");
18 |     expect(plan.kpis.some((kpi) => kpi.metric === "salary_usd")).toBe(true);
19 |     expect(plan.charts).toEqual(expect.arrayContaining([
20 |       expect.objectContaining({ type: "bar", xKey: "country", yKey: "salary_usd", aggregation: "avg" }),
21 |       expect.objectContaining({ type: "histogram", xKey: "salary_usd" }),
22 |       expect.objectContaining({ type: "scatter" }),
23 |     ]));
24 |     expect(JSON.stringify(plan)).not.toContain("\"data\"");
25 |   });
26 | 
27 |   it("rejects invalid scatter and unknown columns", () => {
28 |     expect(validateChartCombination(
29 |       profile,
30 |       { type: "scatter", title: "Bad", xKey: "country", yKey: "salary_usd", aggregation: "count" }
31 |     ).ok).toBe(false);
32 | 
33 |     expect(validateChartCombination(
34 |       profile,
35 |       { type: "bar", title: "Missing", xKey: "missing", yKey: "salary_usd", aggregation: "avg" }
36 |     ).ok).toBe(false);
37 |   });
38 | 
39 |   it("removes chart.data from sanitized chart specs", () => {
40 |     const spec = sanitizeChartSpec({
41 |       type: "bar",
42 |       title: "Unsafe",
43 |       xKey: "country",
44 |       yKey: "salary_usd",
45 |       aggregation: "avg",
46 |       data: [{ country: "India", value: 12345 }],
47 |     }, profile);
48 | 
49 |     expect(spec).not.toHaveProperty("data");
50 |   });
51 | });
52 | 
```

### FILE: apps/backend/src/__tests__/dashboard-quality-guardian.test.js

Source path: `apps/backend/src/__tests__/dashboard-quality-guardian.test.js`

```js
 1 | import { describe, expect, it } from "vitest";
 2 | import {
 3 |   assessDashboardHealth,
 4 |   enforceDashboardQuality,
 5 |   validateChartCombination,
 6 | } from "../services/ai-analyst/dashboard-quality-guardian.js";
 7 | 
 8 | const salaryProfile = {
 9 |   datasetName: "salary.csv",
10 |   rowCount: 40000,
11 |   columnCount: 7,
12 |   domain: "workforce_salary",
13 |   columns: [
14 |     { name: "experience", normalizedName: "experience", type: "number", role: "continuous_metric" },
15 |     { name: "country", normalizedName: "country", type: "category", role: "location" },
16 |     { name: "education", normalizedName: "education", type: "category", role: "category" },
17 |     { name: "languages", normalizedName: "languages", type: "string", role: "category" },
18 |     { name: "frameworks", normalizedName: "frameworks", type: "string", role: "category" },
19 |     { name: "company_size", normalizedName: "company_size", type: "category", role: "category" },
20 |     { name: "salary_usd", normalizedName: "salary_usd", type: "number", role: "money_metric" },
21 |   ],
22 | };
23 | 
24 | describe("dashboard-quality-guardian", () => {
25 |   it("removes chart.data and fake KPI values from AI plans", () => {
26 |     const dashboard = enforceDashboardQuality(salaryProfile, {
27 |       kpis: [{ title: "Fake Salary", metric: "salary_usd", aggregation: "avg", value: 999999 }],
28 |       charts: [{
29 |         title: "Unsafe AI Chart",
30 |         type: "bar",
31 |         xKey: "country",
32 |         yKey: "salary_usd",
33 |         aggregation: "avg",
34 |         data: [{ country: "India", salary_usd: 1 }],
35 |       }],
36 |     });
37 | 
38 |     expect(JSON.stringify(dashboard)).not.toContain("999999");
39 |     expect(JSON.stringify(dashboard)).not.toContain("\"data\"");
40 |     expect(dashboard.schemaOnly).toBe(true);
41 |   });
42 | 
43 |   it("rejects invalid chart combinations and repairs weak dashboard plans", () => {
44 |     const invalid = validateChartCombination(salaryProfile, {
45 |       title: "Country vs Salary",
46 |       type: "scatter",
47 |       xKey: "country",
48 |       yKey: "salary_usd",
49 |       aggregation: "count",
50 |     });
51 | 
52 |     const repaired = enforceDashboardQuality(salaryProfile, {
53 |       charts: [{ title: "Bad Column", type: "bar", xKey: "missing", yKey: "salary_usd", aggregation: "avg" }],
54 |       kpis: [],
55 |     });
56 | 
57 |     expect(invalid.ok).toBe(false);
58 |     expect(repaired.charts.length).toBe(7);
59 |     expect(repaired.charts.every((chart) => chart.xKey !== "missing")).toBe(true);
60 |   });
61 | 
62 |   it("supports salary/workforce datasets with schema-only output", () => {
63 |     const dashboard = enforceDashboardQuality(salaryProfile, { kpis: [], charts: [] }, { maxCharts: 7 });
64 |     const health = assessDashboardHealth(salaryProfile, dashboard);
65 | 
66 |     expect(dashboard.charts.map((chart) => chart.title)).toEqual([
67 |       "Average Salary by Country",
68 |       "Salary Distribution",
69 |       "Salary vs Experience",
70 |       "Education Distribution",
71 |       "Average Salary by Company Size",
72 |       "Average Salary by Language",
73 |       "Average Salary by Framework",
74 |     ]);
75 |     expect(dashboard.charts.find((chart) => chart.title === "Average Salary by Language")?.splitValues).toBe(true);
76 |     expect(dashboard.charts.find((chart) => chart.title === "Average Salary by Framework")?.splitValues).toBe(true);
77 |     expect(health.status).toBe("healthy");
78 |     expect(health.score).toBeGreaterThanOrEqual(90);
79 |     expect(dashboard.guardian.unsafeAiDataRemoved).toBe(true);
80 |   });
81 | });
82 | 
```

### FILE: apps/backend/src/__tests__/ollama-schema-safety.test.js

Source path: `apps/backend/src/__tests__/ollama-schema-safety.test.js`

```js
 1 | import { afterEach, describe, expect, it, vi } from "vitest";
 2 | import { generateSchemaDashboard } from "../services/ai-analyst/schema-trained-ai-service.js";
 3 | import { sentinelDataset } from "./test-helpers.js";
 4 | 
 5 | describe("Ollama schema-only safety", () => {
 6 |   afterEach(() => {
 7 |     vi.restoreAllMocks();
 8 |   });
 9 | 
10 |   it("sends schema only to Ollama and strips malicious chart.data responses", async () => {
11 |     let requestBody = "";
12 |     vi.stubGlobal("fetch", vi.fn(async (_url, init) => {
13 |       requestBody = String(init?.body || "");
14 |       return {
15 |         ok: true,
16 |         json: async () => ({
17 |           response: JSON.stringify({
18 |             action: "GENERATE_DASHBOARD",
19 |             message: "Dashboard plan generated",
20 |             schemaOnly: true,
21 |             kpis: [
22 |               { title: "Total Records", metric: "__row_count__", aggregation: "count", format: "number" },
23 |             ],
24 |             charts: [
25 |               {
26 |                 type: "bar",
27 |                 title: "Unsafe",
28 |                 xKey: "country",
29 |                 yKey: "salary_usd",
30 |                 aggregation: "avg",
31 |                 data: [{ country: "India", value: 12345 }],
32 |               },
33 |             ],
34 |           }),
35 |         }),
36 |       };
37 |     }));
38 | 
39 |     const result = await generateSchemaDashboard(sentinelDataset, { useLlm: true, threshold: 1 });
40 | 
41 |     // Schema packet contains no full dataset rows or value-bearing profile metadata.
42 |     expect(requestBody).not.toContain("\"rows\"");
43 |     expect(requestBody).not.toContain("SECRET_RAW_ROW_SHOULD_NEVER_REACH_LLM");
44 |     expect(requestBody).not.toContain("\"stats\"");
45 |     expect(requestBody).not.toContain("\"topValues\"");
46 |     expect(requestBody).not.toContain("50000");
47 |     expect(requestBody).not.toContain("90000");
48 |     expect(requestBody).toContain("salary_usd");
49 |     // Dashboard spec has no chart.data from AI
50 |     expect(JSON.stringify(result)).not.toContain("\"data\"");
51 |     expect(result.dashboard.charts.length).toBeGreaterThan(0);
52 |     expect(result.dashboard.charts.every((chart) => !chart.data)).toBe(true);
53 |     expect(result.llm?.source).toBe("ollama:neural-chat:7b");
54 |   });
55 | 
56 |   it("falls back to local dashboard plan when Ollama is down", async () => {
57 |     vi.stubGlobal("fetch", vi.fn(async () => {
58 |       throw new Error("Ollama unavailable");
59 |     }));
60 | 
61 |     const result = await generateSchemaDashboard(sentinelDataset, { useLlm: true, threshold: 1 });
62 | 
63 |     expect(result.dashboard.kpis.length).toBeGreaterThan(0);
64 |     expect(result.dashboard.charts.length).toBeGreaterThan(0);
65 |     expect(result.schemaOnly).toBe(true);
66 |     expect(result.llm?.source).toBe("ollama-error");
67 |   });
68 | });
69 | 
```

### FILE: apps/backend/src/__tests__/schema-fingerprint.test.js

Source path: `apps/backend/src/__tests__/schema-fingerprint.test.js`

```js
 1 | import { describe, expect, it } from "vitest";
 2 | import { buildSchemaProfile, makeSchemaOnlyPacket } from "../services/ai-analyst/schema-fingerprint.js";
 3 | import { salaryDataset } from "./test-helpers.js";
 4 | 
 5 | describe("schema fingerprint", () => {
 6 |   it("detects schema roles and excludes raw rows from schema-only packet", () => {
 7 |     const profile = buildSchemaProfile(salaryDataset);
 8 |     const packet = makeSchemaOnlyPacket(profile);
 9 |     const country = profile.columns.find((column) => column.name === "country");
10 |     const salary = profile.columns.find((column) => column.name === "salary_usd");
11 |     const experience = profile.columns.find((column) => column.name === "experience");
12 | 
13 |     expect(profile.rowCount).toBe(3);
14 |     expect(profile.columnCount).toBe(3);
15 |     expect(["location", "category"]).toContain(country.role);
16 |     expect(salary.role).toBe("money_metric");
17 |     expect(["continuous_metric", "count_metric"]).toContain(experience.role);
18 |     expect(JSON.stringify(packet)).not.toContain("\"rows\"");
19 |     expect(JSON.stringify(packet)).not.toContain("50000");
20 |     expect(JSON.stringify(packet)).not.toContain("India");
21 |     expect(packet.columns.find((column) => column.name === "salary_usd")).not.toHaveProperty("stats");
22 |     expect(packet.columns.find((column) => column.name === "country")).not.toHaveProperty("topValues");
23 |     expect(packet.columns.find((column) => column.name === "country")).toHaveProperty("topValuesCount");
24 |   });
25 | 
26 |   it("can explicitly include value-bearing metadata for trusted local-only callers", () => {
27 |     const profile = buildSchemaProfile(salaryDataset);
28 |     const packet = makeSchemaOnlyPacket(profile, {
29 |       includeStats: true,
30 |       includeTopValues: true,
31 |     });
32 | 
33 |     expect(packet.columns.find((column) => column.name === "salary_usd")).toHaveProperty("stats");
34 |     expect(packet.columns.find((column) => column.name === "country").topValues).toContain("India");
35 |   });
36 | });
37 | 
```

```js
 1 | import { afterEach, beforeEach, describe, expect, it, vi } from "vitest";
 2 | import fs from "node:fs";
 3 | import os from "node:os";
 4 | import path from "node:path";
 5 | import { makeReq, makeRes, salaryDataset } from "./test-helpers.js";
 6 | 
 7 | describe("schema training memory routes", () => {
 8 |   let originalCwd;
 9 |   let tempDir;
10 | 
11 |   beforeEach(() => {
12 |     originalCwd = process.cwd();
13 |     tempDir = fs.mkdtempSync(path.join(os.tmpdir(), "insightflow-memory-"));
14 |     process.chdir(tempDir);
15 |     vi.resetModules();
16 |   });
17 | 
18 |   afterEach(() => {
19 |     process.chdir(originalCwd);
20 |     fs.rmSync(tempDir, { recursive: true, force: true });
21 |     vi.resetModules();
22 |   });
23 | 
24 |   it("loads, trains, and matches similar schema memory", async () => {
25 |     const { handleSchemaTrainedAIRoutes } = await import("../routes/schema-trained-ai.routes.js");
26 | 
27 |     const getResponse = makeRes();
28 |     await handleSchemaTrainedAIRoutes(makeReq("GET"), getResponse, "/api/ai/schema-training-memory");
29 |     expect(getResponse.json().success).toBe(true);
30 | 
31 |     const trainResponse = makeRes();
32 |     await handleSchemaTrainedAIRoutes(makeReq("POST", {
33 |       ...salaryDataset,
34 |       dashboardPlan: {
35 |         kpis: [{ title: "Total Records", metric: "__row_count__", aggregation: "count" }],
36 |         charts: [{ title: "Average Salary by Country", type: "bar", xKey: "country", yKey: "salary_usd", aggregation: "avg" }],
37 |       },
38 |     }), trainResponse, "/api/datasets/test-local/schema-train");
39 |     expect(trainResponse.json().data.stats.count).toBeGreaterThanOrEqual(1);
40 | 
41 |     const dashboardResponse = makeRes();
42 |     await handleSchemaTrainedAIRoutes(makeReq("POST", {
43 |       ...salaryDataset,
44 |       rows: salaryDataset.rows.map((row) => ({ ...row })),
45 |       useLlm: false,
46 |       threshold: 0.1,
47 |     }), dashboardResponse, "/api/datasets/test-local/schema-dashboard");
48 | 
49 |     const payload = dashboardResponse.json();
50 |     expect(payload.data.match).toBeTruthy();
51 |     expect(payload.data.match.score).toBeGreaterThan(0.1);
52 |   });
53 | });
54 |

  1 | import fs from "node:fs";
  2 | import path from "node:path";
  3 | import { afterEach, beforeEach, describe, expect, it } from "vitest";
  4 | 
  5 | const memoryPath = path.join(process.cwd(), "data", "schema-rag-memory.test.json");
  6 | process.env.SCHEMA_RAG_MEMORY_PATH = memoryPath;
  7 | process.env.DISABLE_OLLAMA_EMBEDDINGS = "1";
  8 | 
  9 | const { buildSchemaProfile } = await import("../services/ai-analyst/schema-fingerprint.js");
 10 | const {
 11 |   buildSchemaRagMemoryEntry,
 12 |   schemaProfileToRagText,
 13 | } = await import("../services/ai-analyst/schema-rag-memory-builder.js");
 14 | const {
 15 |   retrieveSchemaRagMemories,
 16 |   trainSchemaRagMemoryFromDataset,
 17 | } = await import("../services/ai-analyst/schema-rag-retriever.js");
 18 | 
 19 | const salaryDataset = {
 20 |   name: "salary-test.csv",
 21 |   columns: [
 22 |     { name: "country" },
 23 |     { name: "salary_usd" },
 24 |     { name: "experience" },
 25 |     { name: "education" },
 26 |   ],
 27 |   rows: [
 28 |     {
 29 |       country: "India",
 30 |       salary_usd: 50000,
 31 |       experience: 2,
 32 |       education: "Bachelors",
 33 |     },
 34 |     {
 35 |       country: "USA",
 36 |       salary_usd: 90000,
 37 |       experience: 5,
 38 |       education: "Masters",
 39 |     },
 40 |   ],
 41 | };
 42 | 
 43 | const dashboardPlan = {
 44 |   kpis: [
 45 |     {
 46 |       title: "Average Salary",
 47 |       metric: "salary_usd",
 48 |       aggregation: "avg",
 49 |       value: 70000,
 50 |       data: [70000],
 51 |     },
 52 |   ],
 53 |   charts: [
 54 |     {
 55 |       title: "Average Salary by Country",
 56 |       type: "bar",
 57 |       xKey: "country",
 58 |       yKey: "salary_usd",
 59 |       aggregation: "avg",
 60 |       data: [
 61 |         { country: "India", salary_usd: 50000 },
 62 |         { country: "USA", salary_usd: 90000 },
 63 |       ],
 64 |     },
 65 |   ],
 66 | };
 67 | 
 68 | function resetMemoryFile() {
 69 |   fs.mkdirSync(path.dirname(memoryPath), { recursive: true });
 70 |   fs.writeFileSync(memoryPath, "[]", "utf8");
 71 | }
 72 | 
 73 | describe("Schema RAG memory", () => {
 74 |   beforeEach(resetMemoryFile);
 75 |   afterEach(resetMemoryFile);
 76 | 
 77 |   it("creates schema text without numeric raw row values", () => {
 78 |     const profile = buildSchemaProfile(salaryDataset);
 79 |     const text = schemaProfileToRagText(profile);
 80 | 
 81 |     expect(text).toContain("salary_usd");
 82 |     expect(text).toContain("country");
 83 |     expect(text).not.toContain("50000");
 84 |     expect(text).not.toContain("90000");
 85 |     expect(text).not.toContain("India");
 86 |     expect(text).not.toContain("USA");
 87 |   });
 88 | 
 89 |   it("removes KPI values and chart data from memory entries", () => {
 90 |     const profile = buildSchemaProfile(salaryDataset);
 91 | 
 92 |     const entry = buildSchemaRagMemoryEntry({
 93 |       dataset: salaryDataset,
 94 |       schemaProfile: profile,
 95 |       dashboardPlan,
 96 |       embedding: [0.1, 0.2, 0.3],
 97 |     });
 98 | 
 99 |     expect(entry.rows).toBeUndefined();
100 |     expect(entry.rawRows).toBeUndefined();
101 |     expect(entry.dashboardPlan.kpis[0].value).toBeUndefined();
102 |     expect(entry.dashboardPlan.kpis[0].data).toBeUndefined();
103 |     expect(entry.dashboardPlan.charts[0].data).toBeUndefined();
104 |     expect(entry.schemaProfile.columns[0].topValues).toBeUndefined();
105 |     expect(entry.schemaProfile.columns[1].stats).toBeUndefined();
106 |     expect(entry.schemaText).not.toContain("India");
107 |     expect(entry.schemaText).not.toContain("50000");
108 |   });
109 | 
110 |   it("can train and retrieve schema-only RAG memory without Ollama", async () => {
111 |     const trained = await trainSchemaRagMemoryFromDataset({
112 |       dataset: salaryDataset,
113 |       acceptedDashboardPlan: dashboardPlan,
114 |       useOllama: false,
115 |     });
116 | 
117 |     expect(trained.entry.dashboardPlan.charts.length).toBeGreaterThan(0);
118 |     expect(trained.entry.embedding).toBeUndefined();
119 | 
120 |     const profile = buildSchemaProfile(salaryDataset);
121 | 
122 |     const retrieved = await retrieveSchemaRagMemories(profile, {
123 |       threshold: 0,
124 |       limit: 5,
125 |       useOllama: false,
126 |     });
127 | 
128 |     expect(retrieved.used).toBe(true);
129 |     expect(retrieved.matches.length).toBeGreaterThan(0);
130 |     expect(retrieved.matches[0].entry.embedding).toBeUndefined();
131 |     expect(retrieved.matches[0].entry.dashboardPlan.charts[0].data).toBeUndefined();
132 |   });
133 | });
134 |

### FILE: apps/backend/src/__tests__/schema-trained-ai.routes.test.js

Source path: `apps/backend/src/__tests__/schema-trained-ai.routes.test.js`

```js
 1 | import { describe, expect, it } from "vitest";
 2 | import { handleSchemaTrainedAIRoutes } from "../routes/schema-trained-ai.routes.js";
 3 | import { makeReq, makeRes, salaryDataset } from "./test-helpers.js";
 4 | 
 5 | async function callRoute(pathname, body) {
 6 |   const response = makeRes();
 7 |   const handled = await handleSchemaTrainedAIRoutes(makeReq("POST", body), response, pathname);
 8 |   return { handled, response, payload: response.json() };
 9 | }
10 | 
11 | describe("schema-trained AI routes", () => {
12 |   it("generates a schema-only dashboard plan without raw rows or chart data", async () => {
13 |     const { handled, payload } = await callRoute("/api/datasets/test-local/schema-dashboard", {
14 |       ...salaryDataset,
15 |       useLlm: false,
16 |     });
17 | 
18 |     expect(handled).toBe(true);
19 |     expect(payload.success).toBe(true);
20 |     expect(payload.data.schemaOnly).toBe(true);
21 |     expect(payload.data.profile).toBeTruthy();
22 |     expect(payload.data.dashboard.kpis).toBeInstanceOf(Array);
23 |     expect(payload.data.dashboard.charts).toBeInstanceOf(Array);
24 |     for (const chart of payload.data.dashboard.charts) {
25 |       expect(chart).not.toHaveProperty("data");
26 |     }
27 |     expect(JSON.stringify(payload)).not.toContain("\"rows\"");
28 |   });
29 | 
30 |   it("routes a valid dashboard command to a safe chart spec", async () => {
31 |     const { payload } = await callRoute("/api/datasets/test-local/dashboard-command", {
32 |       ...salaryDataset,
33 |       query: "show average salary_usd by country as bar chart",
34 |       useLlm: false,
35 |     });
36 | 
37 |     expect(payload.success).toBe(true);
38 |     expect(payload.data).toMatchObject({
39 |       action: "GENERATE_CHART",
40 |       schemaOnly: true,
41 |     });
42 |     expect(payload.data.chartSpec).not.toHaveProperty("data");
43 |     expect(payload.data.chartSpec.xKey).toBe("country");
44 |   });
45 | 
46 |   it("handles invalid scatter conversion safely by returning a chart spec", async () => {
47 |     const { payload } = await callRoute("/api/datasets/test-local/dashboard-command", {
48 |       ...salaryDataset,
49 |       query: "convert country pie chart to scatter",
50 |       useLlm: false,
51 |     });
52 | 
53 |     expect(payload.success).toBe(true);
54 |     expect(payload.data.action).toBe("GENERATE_CHART");
55 |   });
56 | 
57 |   it("routes dashboard repair requests to FIX_DASHBOARD", async () => {
58 |     const { payload } = await callRoute("/api/datasets/test-local/dashboard-command", {
59 |       ...salaryDataset,
60 |       query: "fix dashboard",
61 |       currentDashboard: {
62 |         charts: [
63 |           {
64 |             type: "bar",
65 |             title: "Broken",
66 |             xKey: "missing_column",
67 |             yKey: "salary_usd",
68 |             aggregation: "avg",
69 |           },
70 |         ],
71 |       },
72 |       useLlm: false,
73 |     });
74 | 
75 |     expect(payload.success).toBe(true);
76 |     expect(payload.data.action).toBe("FIX_DASHBOARD");
77 |     expect(payload.data.schemaOnly).toBe(true);
78 |   });
79 | 
80 |   it("answers schema chat without fake KPI values or raw row packet", async () => {
81 |     const { payload } = await callRoute("/api/datasets/test-local/schema-chat", {
82 |       ...salaryDataset,
83 |       query: "Explain this dataset",
84 |       useLlm: false,
85 |     });
86 | 
87 |     expect(payload.success).toBe(true);
88 |     expect(payload.data.assistantMessage.content).toContain("3 rows");
89 |     expect(payload.data.assistantMessage.schemaOnly).toBe(true);
90 |     expect(JSON.stringify(payload)).not.toContain("\"rows\"");
91 |   });
92 | });
93 |

### FILE: apps/backend/src/__tests__/test-helpers.js

Source path: `apps/backend/src/__tests__/test-helpers.js`

```js
 1 | export const salaryDataset = {
 2 |   id: "test-local",
 3 |   name: "Salary Small",
 4 |   columns: ["country", "salary_usd", "experience"],
 5 |   rows: [
 6 |     { country: "India", salary_usd: 50000, experience: 2 },
 7 |     { country: "USA", salary_usd: 90000, experience: 5 },
 8 |     { country: "India", salary_usd: 65000, experience: 3 },
 9 |   ],
10 | };
11 | 
12 | export const sentinelDataset = {
13 |   id: "test-sentinel",
14 |   name: "Sentinel Salary",
15 |   columns: ["country", "salary_usd", "experience", "secret_note"],
16 |   rows: [
17 |     {
18 |       country: "India",
19 |       salary_usd: 50000,
20 |       experience: 2,
21 |       secret_note: [REDACTED_SECRET],
22 |     },
23 |     { country: "USA", salary_usd: 90000, experience: 5, secret_note: "safe" },
24 |   ],
25 | };
26 | 
27 | export function makeReq(method, body = {}) {
28 |   return {
29 |     method,
30 |     body,
31 |     readable: false,
32 |     headers: { "content-type": "application/json" },
33 |   };
34 | }
35 | 
36 | export function makeRes() {
37 |   const response = {
38 |     statusCode: 200,
39 |     headers: {},
40 |     body: "",
41 |     setHeader(name, value) {
42 |       this.headers[name.toLowerCase()] = value;
43 |     },
44 |     writeHead(statusCode, headers = {}) {
45 |       this.statusCode = statusCode;
46 |       for (const [key, value] of Object.entries(headers)) this.setHeader(key, value);
47 |     },
48 |     end(chunk = "") {
49 |       this.body += String(chunk);
50 |     },
51 |     json() {
52 |       return JSON.parse(this.body || "{}");
53 |     },
54 |   };
55 |   return response;
56 | }
57 | 
```

### FILE: apps/backend/src/config/environment.js

```js
  1 | // Environment configuration with validation
  2 | import dotenv from 'dotenv';
  3 | 
  4 | dotenv.config();
  5 | 
  6 | // Get configured providers dynamically based on API keys
  7 | function getConfiguredProviders() {
  8 |   const providers = [];
  9 |   
 10 |   // Check Ollama (always available if enabled)
 11 |   if (process.env.OLLAMA_ENABLED !== 'false') {
 12 |     providers.push('ollama');
 13 |   }
 14 |   
 15 |   // Check Gemini API key
 16 |   if (process.env.GOOGLE_API_KEY) {
 17 |     providers.push('gemini');
 18 |   }
 19 |   
 20 |   // Check OpenAI API key
 21 |   if (process.env.OPENAI_API_KEY) {
 22 |     providers.push('openai');
 23 |   }
 24 |   
 25 |   // Check Anthropic API key
 26 |   if (process.env.ANTHROPIC_API_KEY) {
 27 |     providers.push('anthropic');
 28 |   }
 29 |   
 30 |   return providers;
 31 | }
 32 | 
 33 | // Get dynamic provider priority based on configured API keys
 34 | function getDynamicProviderPriority() {
 35 |   const envPriority = process.env.AI_PROVIDER_PRIORITY || 'ollama,gemini,openai,anthropic';
 36 |   const configured = getConfiguredProviders();
 37 |   
 38 |   // If custom priority is set, respect it but filter out unavailable providers
 39 |   if (process.env.AI_PROVIDER_PRIORITY) {
 40 |     return envPriority.split(',')
 41 |       .map(p => p.trim())
 42 |       .filter(p => configured.includes(p));
 43 |   }
 44 |   
 45 |   // Default priority: Ollama first (local), then cloud providers
 46 |   return configured.length > 0 ? configured : ['ollama'];
 47 | }
 48 | 
 49 | export const config = {
 50 |   // Server Configuration
 51 |   server: {
 52 |     port: parseInt(process.env.PORT || '3001', 10),
 53 |     host: process.env.HOST || 'localhost',
 54 |     nodeEnv: process.env.NODE_ENV || 'development'
 55 |   },
 56 | 
 57 |   // Ollama Configuration (Local LLM)
 58 |   ollama: {
 59 |     baseUrl: process.env.OLLAMA_BASE_URL || 'http://localhost:11434',
 60 |     enabled: process.env.OLLAMA_ENABLED !== 'false',
 61 |     primaryModel: process.env.OLLAMA_MODEL || 'llama3.2',
 62 |     chatModel: process.env.OLLAMA_CHAT_MODEL || 'neural-chat:7b',
 63 |     timeout: parseInt(process.env.OLLAMA_TIMEOUT_MS || '120000', 10),
 64 |     maxTokens: [REDACTED_SECRET]4096', 10),
 65 |     temperature: parseFloat(process.env.OLLAMA_TEMPERATURE || '0.7'),
 66 |     topP: parseFloat(process.env.OLLAMA_TOP_P || '0.9'),
 67 |     frequencyPenalty: parseFloat(process.env.OLLAMA_FREQUENCY_PENALTY || '0.0')
 68 |   },
 69 | 
 70 |   // Google Gemini Configuration
 71 |   gemini: {
 72 |     apiKey: process.env.GOOGLE_API_KEY || '',
 73 |     enabled: !!process.env.GOOGLE_API_KEY,
 74 |     model: 'gemini-1.5-pro',
 75 |     timeout: 60000,
 76 |     maxTokens: [REDACTED_SECRET]
 77 |   },
 78 | 
 79 |   // OpenAI Configuration
 80 |   openai: {
 81 |     apiKey: process.env.OPENAI_API_KEY || '',
 82 |     enabled: !!process.env.OPENAI_API_KEY,
 83 |     model: 'gpt-4',
 84 |     timeout: 60000,
 85 |     maxTokens: [REDACTED_SECRET]
 86 |   },
 87 | 
 88 |   // Anthropic Configuration
 89 |   anthropic: {
 90 |     apiKey: process.env.ANTHROPIC_API_KEY || '',
 91 |     enabled: !!process.env.ANTHROPIC_API_KEY,
 92 |     model: 'claude-3-5-sonnet-20241022',
 93 |     timeout: 60000,
 94 |     maxTokens: [REDACTED_SECRET]
 95 |   },
 96 | 
 97 |   // AI Global Settings
 98 |   ai: {
 99 |     providerPriority: getDynamicProviderPriority(),
100 |     fallbackEnabled: process.env.ENABLE_AI_FALLBACK !== 'false',
101 |     localOnlyMode: process.env.LOCAL_AI_ONLY === 'true',
102 |     timeout: parseInt(process.env.AI_TIMEOUT_MS || '120000', 10),
103 |     maxTokens: [REDACTED_SECRET]4096', 10),
104 |     temperature: parseFloat(process.env.AI_TEMPERATURE || '0.7'),
105 |     configuredProviders: getConfiguredProviders()
106 |   },
107 | 
108 |   // Database Configuration
109 |   database: {
110 |     path: process.env.DATABASE_PATH || './data/insightflow.db',
111 |     dataDir: process.env.DATA_DIR || './data'
112 |   },
113 | 
114 |   // CORS Configuration
115 |   cors: {
116 |     origin: process.env.CORS_ORIGIN || '*',
117 |     credentials: process.env.CORS_CREDENTIALS === 'true'
118 |   },
119 | 
120 |   // Logging Configuration
121 |   logging: {
122 |     level: process.env.LOG_LEVEL || 'info',
123 |     verbose: process.env.VERBOSE_LOGGING === 'true'
124 |   },
125 | 
126 |   // Feature Flags
127 |   features: {
128 |     localNlp: process.env.LOCAL_NLP_ENABLED !== 'false',
129 |     autoChartGeneration: process.env.AUTO_CHART_GENERATION !== 'false',
130 |     correlationAnalysis: process.env.CORRELATION_ANALYSIS !== 'false',
131 |     outlierDetection: process.env.OUTLIER_DETECTION !== 'false',
132 |     chatHistory: process.env.CHAT_HISTORY_ENABLED !== 'false',
133 |     smartChart: process.env.SMARTCHART_ENABLED !== 'false'
134 |   }
135 | };
136 | 
137 | // Validation function
138 | export function validateConfig() {
139 |   const errors = [];
140 | 
141 |   // Validate server port
142 |   if (isNaN(config.server.port) || config.server.port < 1 || config.server.port > 65535) {
143 |     errors.push('Invalid PORT: must be between 1 and 65535');
144 |   }
145 | 
146 |   // Validate Ollama URL
147 |   try {
148 |     new URL(config.ollama.baseUrl);
149 |   } catch {
150 |     errors.push('Invalid OLLAMA_BASE_URL: must be a valid URL');
151 |   }
152 | 
153 |   // Validate AI provider priority
154 |   const validProviders = ['ollama', 'gemini', 'openai', 'anthropic'];
155 |   const invalidProviders = config.ai.providerPriority.filter(p => !validProviders.includes(p));
156 |   if (invalidProviders.length > 0) {
157 |     errors.push(`Invalid AI providers: ${invalidProviders.join(', ')}`);
158 |   }
159 | 
160 |   // Validate database path
161 |   if (!config.database.path) {
162 |     errors.push('DATABASE_PATH is required');
163 |   }
164 | 
165 |   if (errors.length > 0) {
166 |     console.error('❌ Configuration validation failed:');
167 |     errors.forEach(error => console.error(`   - ${error}`));
168 |     process.exit(1);
169 |   }
170 | 
171 |   console.log('✅ Configuration validation passed');
172 |   return true;
173 | }
174 | 
175 | // Print configuration summary
176 | export function printConfigSummary() {
177 |   console.log('\n🤖 ===== AI CONFIGURATION SUMMARY =====');
178 |   console.log('\n📍 OLLAMA (LOCAL LLM)');
179 |   console.log(`   Base URL: ${config.ollama.baseUrl}`);
180 |   console.log(`   Primary Model: ${config.ollama.primaryModel}`);
181 |   console.log(`   Chat Model: ${config.ollama.chatModel}`);
182 |   console.log(`   Status: ${config.ollama.enabled ? '✅ ENABLED' : '❌ DISABLED'}`);
183 | 
184 |   console.log('\n📍 GOOGLE GEMINI');
185 |   console.log(`   Status: ${config.gemini.enabled ? '✅ ENABLED' : '❌ DISABLED (No API key)'}`);
186 | 
187 |   console.log('\n📍 OPENAI');
188 |   console.log(`   Status: ${config.openai.enabled ? '✅ ENABLED' : '❌ DISABLED (No API key)'}`);
189 | 
190 |   console.log('\n📍 ANTHROPIC');
191 |   console.log(`   Status: ${config.anthropic.enabled ? '✅ ENABLED' : '❌ DISABLED (No API key)'}`);
192 | 
193 |   console.log('\n⚙️  GLOBAL SETTINGS');
194 |   console.log(`   Fallback Enabled: ${config.ai.fallbackEnabled}`);
195 |   console.log(`   Local Only Mode: ${config.ai.localOnlyMode}`);
196 | 
197 |   const available = getAvailableProviders();
198 |   console.log(`\n✅ Configured Providers: ${available.join(', ') || 'NONE'}`);
199 |   console.log(`\n🎯 Dynamic Priority (auto-arranged):`);
200 |   config.ai.providerPriority.forEach((p, i) => {
201 |     const status = i === 0 ? '(Primary)' : `(Fallback ${i})`;
202 |     console.log(`   ${i + 1}. ${p.toUpperCase()} ${status}`);
203 |   });
204 |   console.log('\n' + '='.repeat(40) + '\n');
205 | }
206 | 
207 | // Get available providers
208 | export function getAvailableProviders() {
209 |   const available = [];
210 | 
211 |   if (config.ollama.enabled) available.push('ollama');
212 |   if (config.gemini.enabled) available.push('gemini');
213 |   if (config.openai.enabled) available.push('openai');
214 |   if (config.anthropic.enabled) available.push('anthropic');
215 | 
216 |   return available;
217 | }
218 | 
219 | export default config;
220 |

```js
 1 | export const GEMINI_SYSTEM_PROMPT = `You are a privacy-first data analytics assistant. You ONLY receive dataset schema (column names, types, and statistics - NO raw data values).
 2 | 
 3 | Your job:
 4 | 1. Understand the user's question about their data
 5 | 2. Determine what analysis is needed
 6 | 3. Generate SQL queries that correctly fetch the data from SQLite
 7 | 4. Suggest the best visualization types
 8 | 5. Provide clear insights about the findings
 9 | 
10 | CRITICAL PRIVACY RULES:
11 | - NEVER access or process raw row data
12 | - ONLY use the provided schema metadata (column names, types, stats)
13 | - NEVER make up data or assume values exist
14 | - Generate SQL that works with SQLite
15 | - If confidence is low, say so clearly
16 | 
17 | INTENT TYPES: aggregation, filter, comparison, distribution, correlation, count, trend, summary
18 | 
19 | CHART TYPES: bar, line, pie, histogram, scatter, table, area, donut, combo
20 | 
21 | USER PREFERENCES you should respect:
22 | - chartCount: number of charts to generate (default: auto)
23 | - chartTypes: preferred visualization types
24 | - showTrends: include trend analysis
25 | - showCorrelations: include correlation analysis
26 | 
27 | ALWAYS respond with ONLY valid JSON (no markdown, no explanations):
28 | {
29 |   "intent": "aggregation",
30 |   "columns_used": ["column1", "column2"],
31 |   "sql": "SELECT column1, SUM(column2) as total FROM dataset_rows GROUP BY column1",
32 |   "sql_intent": "describe what data this SQL fetches in plain English",
33 |   "insights": ["insight1", "insight2"],
34 |   "charts": [
35 |     {
36 |       "title": "Chart Title",
37 |       "type": "bar",
38 |       "columns": ["category_column", "value_column"],
39 |       "aggregation": "sum"
40 |     }
41 |   ],
42 |   "confidence": 0.95,
43 |   "reasoning": "Why you chose this analysis"
44 | }`;
45 | 
46 | export const OLLAMA_CONFIG = {
47 |   model: "llama3.2:latest",
48 |   temperature: 0.2,
49 |   topP: 0.9,
50 |   topK: 40,
51 |   maxOutputTokens: [REDACTED_SECRET],
52 |   timeout: 120000,
53 | };
54 | 
55 | export const GEMINI_CONFIG = {
56 |   model: "gemini-flash-latest",
57 |   temperature: 0.2,
58 |   topP: 0.9,
59 |   topK: 40,
60 |   maxOutputTokens: [REDACTED_SECRET],

### FILE: apps/backend/src/core/server.js

Source path: `apps/backend/src/core/server.js`

```js
  1 | // Main HTTP server setup
  2 | import { createServer } from 'node:http';
  3 | import { validateConfig, printConfigSummary } from '../config/environment.js';
  4 | import { setupRoutes } from '../routes/index.js';
  5 | import { errorHandler } from '../middleware/error-handler.js';
  6 | import { requestLogger } from '../middleware/request-logger.js';
  7 | import { corsMiddleware } from '../middleware/cors.js';
  8 | import config from '../config/environment.js';
  9 | 
 10 | let serverInstance = null;
 11 | 
 12 | /**
 13 |  * Create and configure the HTTP server
 14 |  */
 15 | export function createHttpServer() {
 16 |   // Validate configuration
 17 |   validateConfig();
 18 |   
 19 |   // Print configuration summary
 20 |   if (config.logging.verbose) {
 21 |     printConfigSummary();
 22 |   }
 23 | 
 24 |   // Create HTTP server
 25 |   const server = createServer(async (request, response) => {
 26 |     // Request start time for logging
 27 |     request.startTime = Date.now();
 28 | 
 29 |     try {
 30 |       // Apply CORS middleware
 31 |       corsMiddleware(request, response);
 32 | 
 33 |       // Handle preflight OPTIONS requests
 34 |       if (request.method === 'OPTIONS') {
 35 |         response.writeHead(204, {
 36 |           'Access-Control-Allow-Origin': config.cors.origin,
 37 |           'Access-Control-Allow-Methods': 'GET, POST, PATCH, PUT, DELETE, OPTIONS',
 38 |           'Access-Control-Allow-Headers': 'Content-Type, Authorization, X-Requested-With',
 39 |           'Access-Control-Max-Age': '86400'
 40 |         });
 41 |         response.end();
 42 |         return;
 43 |       }
 44 | 
 45 |       // Parse URL
 46 |       const url = new URL(request.url, `http://${request.headers.host || 'localhost'}`);
 47 |       request.pathname = url.pathname;
 48 |       request.searchParams = url.searchParams;
 49 | 
 50 |       // Apply request logging middleware
 51 |       requestLogger(request, response);
 52 | 
 53 |       // Setup routes
 54 |       await setupRoutes(request, response);
 55 | 
 56 |     } catch (error) {
 57 |       // Handle any unexpected errors
 58 |       errorHandler(error, request, response);
 59 |     }
 60 |   });
 61 | 
 62 |   // Store server instance
 63 |   serverInstance = server;
 64 | 
 65 |   return server;
 66 | }
 67 | 
 68 | /**
 69 |  * Start the HTTP server
 70 |  */
 71 | export async function startServer(server, port = config.server.port) {
 72 |   return new Promise((resolve, reject) => {
 73 |     server.listen(port, config.server.host, (error) => {
 74 |       if (error) {
 75 |         console.error('❌ Failed to start server:', error);
 76 |         reject(error);
 77 |         return;
 78 |       }
 79 | 
 80 |       const address = server.address();
 81 |       const host = address.address === '::' ? 'localhost' : address.address;
 82 |       const port = address.port;
 83 | 
 84 |       console.log('\n🚀 ===== InsightFlow API Server =====');
 85 |       console.log(`📍 Server running on: http://${host}:${port}`);
 86 |       console.log(`📍 Environment: ${config.server.nodeEnv}`);
 87 |       console.log(`📍 Node.js version: ${process.version}`);
 88 |       console.log(`📍 Process ID: ${process.pid}`);
 89 |       console.log('\n📊 Available Endpoints:');
 90 |       console.log('   GET  /api/health           - Health check');
 91 |       console.log('   GET  /api/ai/status        - AI provider status');
 92 |       console.log('   POST /api/ai/test          - Test AI generation');
 93 |       console.log('   POST /api/datasets/import  - Import dataset');
 94 |       console.log('   GET  /api/datasets/:id     - Get dataset info');
 95 |       console.log('   POST /api/datasets/:id/chat - Chat with dataset');
 96 |       console.log('\n🎯 Ready to accept connections!\n');
 97 | 
 98 |       resolve(server);
 99 |     });
100 | 
101 |     // Handle server errors
102 |     server.on('error', (error) => {
103 |       if (error.code === 'EADDRINUSE') {
104 |         console.error(`❌ Port ${port} is already in use`);
105 |         console.error(`   Try: kill -9 $(lsof -ti:${port})`);
106 |         console.error(`   Or change PORT in .env file`);
107 |       } else {
108 |         console.error('❌ Server error:', error);
109 |       }
110 |       reject(error);
111 |     });
112 | 
113 |     // Handle graceful shutdown
114 |     server.on('close', () => {
115 |       console.log('📡 Server closed');
116 |     });
117 | 
118 |     // Handle process signals
119 |     process.on('SIGTERM', () => gracefulShutdown(server));
120 |     process.on('SIGINT', () => gracefulShutdown(server));
121 |     process.on('SIGUSR2', () => gracefulShutdown(server)); // nodemon restart
122 |   });
123 | }
124 | 
125 | /**
126 |  * Graceful shutdown handler
127 |  */
128 | async function gracefulShutdown(server) {
129 |   console.log('\n🛑 Shutting down gracefully...');
130 | 
131 |   // Stop accepting new connections
132 |   server.close(async (error) => {
133 |     if (error) {
134 |       console.error('❌ Error during shutdown:', error);
135 |       process.exit(1);
136 |     }
137 | 
138 |     console.log('✅ Server closed successfully');
139 |     
140 |     // Close database connections, cleanup resources, etc.
141 |     // This would be expanded based on your actual resources
142 |     
143 |     console.log('👋 Goodbye!');
144 |     process.exit(0);
145 |   });
146 | 
147 |   // Force shutdown after 10 seconds
148 |   setTimeout(() => {
149 |     console.error('⏰ Forced shutdown after timeout');
150 |     process.exit(1);
151 |   }, 10000);
152 | }
153 | 
154 | /**
155 |  * Get server instance
156 |  */
157 | export function getServer() {
158 |   return serverInstance;
159 | }
160 | 
161 | /**
162 |  * Check if server is running
163 |  */
164 | export function isServerRunning() {
165 |   return serverInstance && serverInstance.listening;
166 | }
167 | 
168 | export default {
169 |   createHttpServer,
170 |   startServer,
171 |   getServer,
172 |   isServerRunning
173 | };
174 | 
```

### FILE: apps/backend/src/database/dataset-repository.js

Source path: `apps/backend/src/database/dataset-repository.js`

```js
  1 | import { mkdirSync, existsSync } from "node:fs";
  2 | import path from "node:path";
  3 | import { fileURLToPath } from "node:url";
  4 | import { randomUUID } from "node:crypto";
  5 | import { DatabaseSync } from "node:sqlite";
  6 | 
  7 | const __filename = fileURLToPath(import.meta.url);
  8 | const __dirname = path.dirname(__filename);
  9 | 
 10 | const dataDir = process.env.DATA_DIR || path.join(__dirname, "..", "..", "data");
 11 | 
 12 | mkdirSync(dataDir, { recursive: true });
 13 | 
 14 | const databasePath = path.join(dataDir, "insightflow.sqlite");
 15 | 
 16 | if (!existsSync(databasePath)) {
 17 |   console.log(`[DB] Creating database at: ${databasePath}`);
 18 | }
 19 | const db = new DatabaseSync(databasePath);
 20 | 
 21 | db.exec(`
 22 |   PRAGMA journal_mode = WAL;
 23 |   PRAGMA foreign_keys = ON;
 24 | 
 25 |   CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS meta (
 26 |     key TEXT PRIMARY KEY,
 27 |     value TEXT NOT NULL
 28 |   );
 29 | 
 30 |   CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS datasets (
 31 |     id TEXT PRIMARY KEY,
 32 |     name TEXT NOT NULL,
 33 |     source_type TEXT NOT NULL,
 34 |     file_name TEXT,
 35 |     uploaded_at TEXT NOT NULL,
 36 |     row_count INTEGER NOT NULL,
 37 |     column_count INTEGER NOT NULL
 38 |   );
 39 | 
 40 |   CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS dataset_columns (
 41 |     id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT,
 42 |     dataset_id TEXT NOT NULL,
 43 |     name TEXT NOT NULL,
 44 |     type TEXT NOT NULL,
 45 |     sample_json TEXT NOT NULL,
 46 |     FOREIGN KEY(dataset_id) REFERENCES datasets(id) ON DELETE CASCADE
 47 |   );
 48 | 
 49 |   CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS dataset_rows (
 50 |     id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT,
 51 |     dataset_id TEXT NOT NULL,
 52 |     row_index INTEGER NOT NULL,
 53 |     row_json TEXT NOT NULL,
 54 |     FOREIGN KEY(dataset_id) REFERENCES datasets(id) ON DELETE CASCADE
 55 |   );
 56 | 
 57 |   CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS chat_messages (
 58 |     id TEXT PRIMARY KEY,
 59 |     dataset_id TEXT NOT NULL,
 60 |     role TEXT NOT NULL,
 61 |     content TEXT NOT NULL,
 62 |     sql_text TEXT,
 63 |     chart_json TEXT,
 64 |     insights_json TEXT,
 65 |     created_at TEXT NOT NULL,
 66 |     FOREIGN KEY(dataset_id) REFERENCES datasets(id) ON DELETE CASCADE
 67 |   );
 68 | 
 69 |   CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_dataset_columns_dataset_id ON dataset_columns(dataset_id);
 70 |   CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_dataset_rows_dataset_id ON dataset_rows(dataset_id);
 71 |   CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_chat_messages_dataset_id ON chat_messages(dataset_id);
 72 | `);
 73 | 
 74 | // Migration: Add new columns for local datasets support
 75 | try {
 76 |   db.exec("ALTER TABLE datasets ADD COLUMN is_local INTEGER DEFAULT 0");
 77 | } catch (e) {
 78 |   // Column might already exist, ignore error
 79 | }
 80 | 
 81 | try {
 82 |   db.exec("ALTER TABLE datasets ADD COLUMN local_dataset_id TEXT");
 83 | } catch (e) {
 84 |   // Column might already exist, ignore error
 85 | }
 86 | 
 87 | try {
 88 |   db.exec("CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_datasets_local_id ON datasets(local_dataset_id)");
 89 | } catch (e) {
 90 |   // Index might already exist, ignore error
 91 | }
 92 | 
 93 | const parseJson = (value, fallback) => {
 94 |   if (!value) return fallback;
 95 |   try {
 96 |     return JSON.parse(value);
 97 |   } catch {
 98 |     return fallback;
 99 |   }
100 | };
101 | 
102 | const withTransaction = (work) => {
103 |   db.exec("BEGIN");
104 |   try {
105 |     const result = work();
106 |     db.exec("COMMIT");
107 |     return result;
108 |   } catch (error) {
109 |     db.exec("ROLLBACK");
110 |     throw error;
111 |   }
112 | };
113 | 
114 | const setMeta = db.prepare(`
115 |   INSERT INTO meta (key, value)
116 |   VALUES (?, ?)
117 |   ON CONFLICT(key) DO UPDATE SET value = excluded.value
118 | `);
119 | 
120 | const getMeta = db.prepare("SELECT value FROM meta WHERE key = ?");
121 | const insertDataset = db.prepare(`
122 |   INSERT INTO datasets (id, name, source_type, file_name, uploaded_at, row_count, column_count)
123 |   VALUES (?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?)
124 | `);
125 | const insertDatasetColumn = db.prepare(`
126 |   INSERT INTO dataset_columns (dataset_id, name, type, sample_json)
127 |   VALUES (?, ?, ?, ?)
128 | `);
129 | const insertDatasetRow = db.prepare(`
130 |   INSERT INTO dataset_rows (dataset_id, row_index, row_json)
131 |   VALUES (?, ?, ?)
132 | `);
133 | const insertChatMessage = db.prepare(`
134 |   INSERT INTO chat_messages (id, dataset_id, role, content, sql_text, chart_json, insights_json, created_at)
135 |   VALUES (?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?)
136 | `);
137 | const getDatasetRecord = db.prepare("SELECT * FROM datasets WHERE id = ?");
138 | const listDatasetRecords = db.prepare("SELECT * FROM datasets ORDER BY uploaded_at DESC");
139 | const getDatasetColumns = db.prepare("SELECT name, type, sample_json FROM dataset_columns WHERE dataset_id = ? ORDER BY id ASC");
140 | const getDatasetRows = db.prepare("SELECT id, row_index, row_json FROM dataset_rows WHERE dataset_id = ? ORDER BY row_index ASC");
141 | const getChatMessageRows = db.prepare("SELECT * FROM chat_messages WHERE dataset_id = ? ORDER BY created_at ASC");
142 | const getDatasetRowById = db.prepare("SELECT id, row_json FROM dataset_rows WHERE dataset_id = ? AND id = ?");
143 | const updateDatasetRowById = db.prepare("UPDATE dataset_rows SET row_json = ? WHERE dataset_id = ? AND id = ?");
144 | const deleteDatasetById = db.prepare("DELETE FROM datasets WHERE id = ?");
145 | 
146 | const mapDataset = (datasetRecord) => {
147 |   if (!datasetRecord) return null;
148 | 
149 |   const columns = getDatasetColumns.all(datasetRecord.id).map((column) => ({
150 |     name: column.name,
151 |     type: column.type,
152 |     sample: parseJson(column.sample_json, []),
153 |   }));
154 | 
155 |   const rows = getDatasetRows.all(datasetRecord.id).map((row) => ({
156 |     __rowId: row.id,
157 |     ...parseJson(row.row_json, {}),
158 |   }));
159 | 
160 |   return {
161 |     id: datasetRecord.id,
162 |     name: datasetRecord.name,
163 |     columns,
164 |     rows,
165 |     uploadedAt: datasetRecord.uploaded_at,
166 |     rowCount: datasetRecord.row_count,
167 |     sourceType: datasetRecord.source_type,
168 |     fileName: datasetRecord.file_name,
169 |   };
170 | };
171 | 
172 | export const createDataset = ({ name, fileName = null, columns, rows, sourceType = "upload" }) => {
173 |   const datasetId = randomUUID();
174 |   const uploadedAt = new Date().toISOString();
175 |   const cleanRows = rows.map((row) =>
176 |     Object.fromEntries(Object.entries(row).filter(([key]) => key !== "__rowId")),
177 |   );
178 | 
179 |   return withTransaction(() => {
180 |     insertDataset.run(
181 |       datasetId,
182 |       name,
183 |       sourceType,
184 |       fileName,
185 |       uploadedAt,
186 |       cleanRows.length,
187 |       columns.length,
188 |     );
189 | 
190 |     columns.forEach((column) => {
191 |       insertDatasetColumn.run(
192 |         datasetId,
193 |         column.name,
194 |         column.type,
195 |         JSON.stringify(column.sample ?? []),
196 |       );
197 |     });
198 | 
199 |     cleanRows.forEach((row, index) => {
200 |       insertDatasetRow.run(datasetId, index, JSON.stringify(row));
201 |     });
202 | 
203 |     setMeta.run("current_dataset_id", datasetId);
204 |     return getDatasetById(datasetId);
205 |   });
206 | };
207 | 
208 | export const getDatasetById = (datasetId) => mapDataset(getDatasetRecord.get(datasetId));
209 | 
210 | export const listDatasets = () => listDatasetRecords.all().map(mapDataset);
211 | 
212 | export const getCurrentDatasetId = () => getMeta.get("current_dataset_id")?.value ?? null;
213 | 
214 | export const getCurrentDataset = () => {
215 |   const currentDatasetId = getCurrentDatasetId();
216 |   return currentDatasetId ? getDatasetById(currentDatasetId) : null;
217 | };
218 | 
219 | export const getChatMessages = (datasetId) =>
220 |   getChatMessageRows.all(datasetId).map((message) => ({
221 |     id: message.id,
222 |     role: message.role,
223 |     content: message.content,
224 |     sql: message.sql_text ?? undefined,
225 |     chart: parseJson(message.chart_json, undefined),
226 |     insights: parseJson(message.insights_json, []),
227 |     timestamp: message.created_at,
228 |   }));
229 | 
230 | export const saveChatMessages = (datasetId, messages) => {
231 |   withTransaction(() => {
232 |     setMeta.run("current_dataset_id", datasetId);
233 | 
234 |     messages.forEach((message) => {
235 |       insertChatMessage.run(
236 |         message.id,
237 |         datasetId,
238 |         message.role,
239 |         message.content,
240 |         message.sql ?? null,
241 |         message.chart ? JSON.stringify(message.chart) : null,
242 |         message.insights ? JSON.stringify(message.insights) : null,
243 |         message.timestamp,
244 |       );
245 |     });
246 |   });
247 | };
248 | 
249 | export const saveChatMessage = (datasetId, message) => {
250 |   saveChatMessages(datasetId, [message]);
251 | };
252 | 
253 | export const clearChatMessages = (datasetId) => {
254 |   db.prepare("DELETE FROM chat_messages WHERE dataset_id = ?").run(datasetId);
255 | };
256 | 
257 | export const patchDatasetRow = ({ datasetId, rowId, column, value }) => {
258 |   const existingRow = getDatasetRowById.get(datasetId, rowId);
259 |   if (!existingRow) {
260 |     return null;
261 |   }
262 | 
263 |   const nextRow = {
264 |     ...parseJson(existingRow.row_json, {}),
265 |     [column]: value,
266 |   };
267 | 
268 |   updateDatasetRowById.run(JSON.stringify(nextRow), datasetId, rowId);
269 |   return getDatasetById(datasetId);
270 | };
271 | 
272 | export const deleteDataset = (datasetId) => {
273 |   const existing = getDatasetRecord.get(datasetId);
274 |   if (!existing) {
275 |     return false;
276 |   }
277 | 
278 |   withTransaction(() => {
279 |     deleteDatasetById.run(datasetId);
280 |     if (getCurrentDatasetId() === datasetId) {
281 |       setMeta.run("current_dataset_id", "");
282 |     }
283 |   });
284 | 
285 |   return true;
286 | };
287 | 
288 | export const getDatabasePath = () => databasePath;
289 | 
```

 54 | 
 55 | enum AIProvider {
 56 |   GEMINI = "gemini",
 57 |   OLLAMA = "ollama",
 58 |   NONE = "none",
 59 | }
 60 | 
 61 | class AIClient {
 62 |   private genAI: GoogleGenerativeAI | null = null;
 63 |   private ollamaUrl: string = "http://localhost:11434";
 64 |   private ollamaModel: string = "llama3.2";
 65 |   private ollamaAvailable: boolean = false;
 66 |   private activeProvider: AIProvider = AIProvider.NONE;
 67 |   private fallbackChain: AIProvider[] = [];
 68 | 
 69 |   constructor() {
 70 |     this.initializeProviders();
 71 |   }
 72 | 
 73 |   private initializeProviders() {
 74 |     const googleKey = process.env.GOOGLE_API_KEY || "";
 75 | 
 76 |     this.fallbackChain = [];
 77 | 
 78 |     // Initialize Ollama (local AI - PRIMARY)
 79 |     this.ollamaUrl = process.env.OLLAMA_BASE_URL || "http://localhost:11434";
 80 |     this.ollamaModel = process.env.OLLAMA_MODEL || "llama3.2";
 81 |     
 82 |     // Test Ollama connection and add to chain
 83 |     this.testOllamaConnection().then(() => {
 84 |       if (this.ollamaAvailable && !this.fallbackChain.includes(AIProvider.OLLAMA)) {
 85 |         this.fallbackChain.unshift(AIProvider.OLLAMA); // Add as PRIMARY
 86 |         this.activeProvider = AIProvider.OLLAMA;
 87 |         console.log("🔄 Primary AI Provider: OLLAMA (Local)");
 88 |       }
 89 |     }).catch(() => {});
 90 | 
 91 |     // Initialize Gemini (cloud - FALLBACK)
 92 |     if (googleKey) {
 93 |       try {
 94 |         this.genAI = new GoogleGenerativeAI(googleKey);
 95 |         this.fallbackChain.push(AIProvider.GEMINI); // Add as fallback
 96 |         console.log("✅ Google Gemini AI initialized (Fallback)");
 97 |       } catch (e) {
 98 |         console.warn("⚠️ Failed to initialize Gemini:", e);
 99 |       }
100 |     } else {
101 |       console.warn("⚠️ GOOGLE_API_KEY not found - Using Local AI only");
102 |     }
103 | 
104 |     if (this.fallbackChain.length === 0) {
105 |       console.error("❌ No AI provider available! Please start Ollama or set GOOGLE_API_KEY.");
106 |     } else if (this.activeProvider === AIProvider.NONE && this.fallbackChain.length > 0) {
107 |       this.activeProvider = this.fallbackChain[0];
108 |       console.log(`🔄 Primary AI Provider: ${this.activeProvider}`);
109 |     }
110 |   }
111 | 
112 |   async generateContent(prompt: string, systemPrompt?: string): Promise<string> {
113 |     let lastError: Error | null = null;
114 | 
115 |     for (let i = 0; i < this.fallbackChain.length; i++) {
116 |       const provider = this.fallbackChain[i];
117 | 
118 |       try {
119 |         let result: string;
120 | 
121 |         switch (provider) {
122 |           case AIProvider.GEMINI:
123 |             result = await this.generateWithGemini(prompt);
124 |             break;
125 |           case AIProvider.OLLAMA:
126 |             result = await this.generateWithOllama(prompt, systemPrompt);
127 |             break;
128 |           default:
129 |             continue;
130 |         }
131 | 
132 |         this.activeProvider = provider;
133 |         return result;
134 |       } catch (e: any) {
135 |         console.warn(`⚠️ ${provider} failed: ${e.message}`);
136 |         lastError = e;
137 |         this.activeProvider = this.fallbackChain[i + 1] || AIProvider.NONE;
138 |       }
139 |     }
140 | 
141 |     throw new Error(
142 |       `All AI providers failed. Last error: ${lastError?.message || "Unknown error"}`
143 |     );
144 |   }
145 | 
146 |   private async generateWithGemini(prompt: string): Promise<string> {
147 |     if (!this.genAI) throw new Error("Gemini not initialized");
148 |     const model = this.genAI.getGenerativeModel({ model: "gemini-1.5-pro" });
149 |     const result = await model.generateContent(prompt);
150 |     return result.response.text();
151 |   }
152 | 
153 |   private async generateWithOllama(
154 |     prompt: string,
155 |     systemPrompt?: string
156 |   ): Promise<string> {
157 |     if (!this.ollamaUrl) throw new Error("Ollama not initialized");
158 | 
159 |     const messages: any[] = [];
160 |     if (systemPrompt) {
161 |       messages.push({ role: "system", content: systemPrompt });
162 |     }
163 |     messages.push({ role: "user", content: prompt });
164 | 
165 |     const response = await axios.post(`${this.ollamaUrl}/api/chat`, {
166 |       model: this.ollamaModel,
167 |       messages,
168 |       stream: false,
169 |       options: {
170 |         temperature: 0.7,
171 |         num_predict: 4000,
172 |       },
173 |     }, {
174 |       timeout: 60000,
175 |       headers: {
176 |         'Content-Type': 'application/json',
177 |       },
178 |     });
179 | 
180 |     return response.data.message?.content || "";
181 |   }
182 | 
183 |   getActiveProvider(): string {
184 |     return this.activeProvider;
185 |   }
186 | 
187 |   isAvailable(): boolean {
188 |     return this.activeProvider !== AIProvider.NONE;
189 |   }
190 | 
191 |   private async testOllamaConnection() {
192 |     try {
193 |       const response = await axios.get(`${this.ollamaUrl}/api/tags`, { timeout: 5000 });
194 |       if (response.status === 200) {
195 |         this.ollamaAvailable = true;
196 |         console.log(`✅ Ollama initialized with model: ${this.ollamaModel}`);
197 |       }
198 |     } catch (e) {
199 |       this.ollamaAvailable = false;
200 |       console.warn(`⚠️ Ollama not available at ${this.ollamaUrl} - Using Gemini only`);
201 |     }
202 |   }
203 | }

221 |   async analyzeDatasetStructure(dataset: Dataset): Promise<SemanticSchema> {
222 |     if (!this.model.isAvailable()) {
223 |       return this.getFallbackSchema(dataset);
224 |     }
225 | 
226 |     const dataPreview = JSON.stringify(dataset.data.slice(0, 5), null, 2);
227 |     const columnInfo = dataset.columns.map((col) => ({
228 |       name: col,
229 |       sample_values: dataset.data
230 |         .slice(0, 3)
231 |         .map((row: Record<string, unknown>) => row[col]),
232 |     }));
233 | 
234 |     const prompt = `
235 | Analyze this dataset and generate a semantic schema for analytics:
236 | 
237 | Columns: ${JSON.stringify(columnInfo)}
238 | Data Preview: ${dataPreview}
239 | 
240 | Respond with ONLY valid JSON (no markdown, no explanation):
241 | {
242 |   "dataset_domain": "business domain",
243 |   "fact_entity": "main fact table entity",
244 |   "dimensions": ["list of dimension columns"],
245 |   "measures": ["list of numeric measure columns"],
246 |   "geo_dimensions": ["geographic columns if any"],
247 |   "time_columns": ["time-based columns if any"],
248 |   "recommended_kpis": [
249 |     {
250 |       "name": "KPI name",
251 |       "description": "business meaning",
252 |       "formula": "how to calculate",
253 |       "business_value": "why it matters"
254 |     }
255 |   ],
256 |   "recommended_visualizations": [
257 |     {
258 |       "type": "chart type",
259 |       "title": "chart title",
260 |       "dimensions": ["dimension1"],
261 |       "measures": ["measure1"],
262 |       "insight": "what this shows"
263 |     }
264 |   ]
265 | }`;
266 | 
267 |     const response = await this.model.generateContent(prompt);
268 |     const jsonMatch = response.match(/\{[\s\S]*\}/);
269 |     if (!jsonMatch) {
270 |       return this.getFallbackSchema(dataset);
271 |     }
272 | 
273 |     return JSON.parse(jsonMatch[0]);
274 |   }
275 | 
276 |   async generateKPIs(schema: SemanticSchema, dataset: Dataset): Promise<KPI[]> {
277 |     if (!this.model.isAvailable()) {
278 |       return this.getFallbackKPIs(dataset);
279 |     }
280 | 
281 |     const dataStats = this.calculateDataStatistics(dataset);
282 | 
283 |     const prompt = `
284 | Given this analytics schema and dataset statistics, generate ONLY high-value business KPIs:
285 | 
286 | Schema Domain: ${schema.dataset_domain}
287 | Measures: ${schema.measures.join(", ")}
288 | Dimensions: ${schema.dimensions.join(", ")}
289 | Statistics: ${JSON.stringify(dataStats)}
290 | 
291 | Generate 8-12 meaningful KPIs that represent real business value.
292 | Never include generic metrics like "Row Count" or "Total Records".
293 | 
294 | Respond with ONLY valid JSON array (no markdown, no explanation):
295 | [
296 |   {
297 |     "name": "KPI name",
298 |     "description": "what it measures",
299 |     "formula": "how to calculate it",
300 |     "business_value": "why it matters for decision making"
301 |   }
302 | ]`;
303 | 
304 |     const response = await this.model.generateContent(prompt);
305 |     const jsonMatch = response.match(/\[[\s\S]*\]/);
306 |     if (!jsonMatch) return this.getFallbackKPIs(dataset);
307 | 
308 |     return JSON.parse(jsonMatch[0]);
309 |   }

470 |   async respondToAnalyticsQuery(
471 |     query: string,
472 |     schema: SemanticSchema,
473 |     dataset: Dataset
474 |   ): Promise<{
475 |     response: string;
476 |     charts?: Visualization[];
477 |     filtered_data?: Record<string, unknown>[];

483 |         response: "AI service unavailable. Please configure an API key (GOOGLE_API_KEY, OPENAI_API_KEY, or ANTHROPIC_API_KEY).",
484 |         charts: [],
485 |       };
486 |     }
487 | 
488 |     const prompt = `
489 | You are an enterprise analytics assistant. Answer this analytics query about the dataset:
490 | 
491 | Query: "${query}"
492 | Domain: ${schema.dataset_domain}
493 | Available Dimensions: ${schema.dimensions.join(", ")}
494 | Available Measures: ${schema.measures.join(", ")}
495 | Dataset Stats: ${JSON.stringify(dataStats)}
496 | 
497 | Provide:
498 | 1. Direct answer to the query
499 | 2. Relevant insights
500 | 3. Recommended visualizations if applicable
501 | 
502 | Respond in JSON format:
503 | {
504 |   "response": "your analysis and answer",
505 |   "recommended_charts": [{"type": "...", "title": "...", "insight": "..."}],
506 |   "key_metrics": {"metric_name": "value"}
507 | }`;
508 | 
509 |     const response = await this.model.generateContent(prompt);
510 |     const jsonMatch = response.match(/\{[\s\S]*\}/);
511 | 
512 |     if (!jsonMatch) {
513 |       return {
514 |         response: response,
515 |         charts: [],
516 |       };
517 |     }
518 | 
519 |     return JSON.parse(jsonMatch[0]);
520 |   }
521 | 
522 |   private getFallbackSchema(dataset: Dataset): SemanticSchema {
523 |     const columns = dataset.columns;
524 |     const numericCols = columns.filter((col) =>
525 |       dataset.data.some((row) => typeof row[col] === "number")
526 |     );
527 |     const stringCols = columns.filter(
528 |       (col) => !numericCols.includes(col)
529 |     );
530 | 
531 |     return {
532 |       dataset_domain: "Analytics Dataset",
533 |       fact_entity: "Records",
534 |       dimensions: stringCols.slice(0, 5),
535 |       measures: numericCols.slice(0, 5),
536 |       geo_dimensions: [],
537 |       time_columns: columns.filter(
538 |         (c) => c.toLowerCase().includes("date") || c.toLowerCase().includes("time")
539 |       ),
540 |       recommended_kpis: [],
541 |       recommended_visualizations: [],
542 |     };
543 |   }
544 | 
545 |   private getFallbackKPIs(dataset: Dataset): KPI[] {
546 |     const stats = this.calculateDataStatistics(dataset);
547 |     const kpis: KPI[] = [];
548 | 
549 |     for (const key of Object.keys(stats)) {
550 |       if (key.endsWith("_avg")) {
551 |         const col = key.replace("_avg", "");
552 |         kpis.push({
553 |           name: `Average ${col}`,
554 |           description: `Mean value of ${col}`,
555 |           formula: `AVG(${col})`,
556 |           business_value: "Basic statistical measure",
557 |         });
558 |       }
559 |       if (kpis.length >= 8) break;
560 |     }
561 | 
562 |     if (kpis.length === 0) {
563 |       return [
564 |         { name: "Total Records", description: "Total number of records", business_value: "Data volume metric" },
565 |         { name: "Unique Values", description: "Count of unique entries", business_value: "Data diversity metric" },
566 |       ];
567 |     }
568 | 
569 |     return kpis;
570 |   }
571 | 
572 |   private getFallbackVisualizations(schema: SemanticSchema): Visualization[] {
573 |     const viz: Visualization[] = [];
574 | 
575 |     for (const measure of schema.measures.slice(0, 4)) {
576 |       viz.push({
577 |         type: "bar",
578 |         title: `${measure} Distribution`,
579 |         dimensions: schema.dimensions.slice(0, 2),
580 |         measures: [measure],
581 |         insight: "Shows distribution across categories",
582 |       });
583 |     }
584 | 
585 |     if (schema.time_columns.length > 0) {
586 |       viz.push({
587 |         type: "line",
588 |         title: "Trend Over Time",
589 |         dimensions: schema.time_columns,
590 |         measures: schema.measures.slice(0, 2),
591 |         insight: "Shows temporal trends",
592 |       });
593 |     }
594 | 
595 |     return viz.length > 0 ? viz : [
596 |       { type: "table", title: "Data Overview", dimensions: schema.dimensions.slice(0, 3), measures: schema.measures.slice(0, 2), insight: "Raw data view" }
597 |     ];
598 |   }
599 | 
600 |   private getFallbackInsights(dataset: Dataset): AIInsight[] {
601 |     const stats = this.calculateDataStatistics(dataset);
602 |     const insights: AIInsight[] = [];
603 | 
604 |     for (const [key, value] of Object.entries(stats)) {
605 |       if (key.endsWith("_avg") && typeof value === "number") {
606 |         insights.push({
607 |           type: "trend",
608 |           insight: `Average ${key.replace("_avg", "")} is ${value.toFixed(2)}`,
609 |           data_points: { [key]: value },
610 |           business_value: "Statistical summary",
611 |           business_impact: "Provides baseline metrics for analysis",
612 |         });
613 |       }
614 |       if (insights.length >= 5) break;
615 |     }
616 | 
617 |     return insights.length > 0 ? insights : [
618 |       { type: "opportunity", insight: "Configure AI API keys for intelligent insights", data_points: {}, business_impact: "Enhanced analytics" }
619 |     ];
620 |   }
621 | 
622 |   private calculateDataStatistics(dataset: Dataset): Record<string, unknown> {
623 |     const stats: Record<string, unknown> = {};
624 | 
625 |     for (const col of dataset.columns) {
626 |       const values = dataset.data.map((row) => row[col]);
627 |       const numericValues = values
628 |         .filter((v) => typeof v === "number")
629 |         .sort((a: number, b: number) => a - b);
630 | 
631 |       if (numericValues.length > 0) {
632 |         const sum = numericValues.reduce((a, b) => (a as number) + (b as number), 0);
633 |         stats[`${col}_avg`] = (sum as number) / numericValues.length;
634 |         stats[`${col}_min`] = numericValues[0];
635 |         stats[`${col}_max`] = numericValues[numericValues.length - 1];
636 |         stats[`${col}_count`] = numericValues.length;
637 |       } else {
638 |         const uniqueCount = new Set(values).size;
639 |         stats[`${col}_unique`] = uniqueCount;
640 |       }
641 |     }
642 | 
643 |     return stats;
644 |   }
645 | 
646 |   private analyzeCorrelations(
647 |     dataset: Dataset,
648 |     schema: SemanticSchema
649 |   ): Record<string, number> {
650 |     const correlations: Record<string, number> = {};
651 |     const measures = schema.measures;
652 | 
653 |     if (measures.length >= 2) {
654 |       for (let i = 0; i < measures.length; i++) {
655 |         for (let j = i + 1; j < measures.length; j++) {
656 |           const measure1 = measures[i];
657 |           const measure2 = measures[j];
658 |           const values1 = dataset.data.map((row) => row[measure1] as number);
659 |           const values2 = dataset.data.map((row) => row[measure2] as number);
660 | 
661 |           const correlation = this.calculatePearsonCorrelation(values1, values2);
662 |           if (!isNaN(correlation)) {
663 |             correlations[`${measure1}_vs_${measure2}`] = Math.round(correlation * 100) / 100;
664 |           }
665 |         }
666 |       }
667 |     }
668 | 
669 |     return correlations;
670 |   }
671 | 
672 |   private calculatePearsonCorrelation(
673 |     x: number[],
674 |     y: number[]
675 |   ): number {
676 |     if (x.length !== y.length) return 0;
677 | 
678 |     const n = x.length;
679 |     const mean_x = x.reduce((a, b) => a + b, 0) / n;
680 |     const mean_y = y.reduce((a, b) => a + b, 0) / n;
681 | 
682 |     let numerator = 0;
683 |     let denominator_x = 0;
684 |     let denominator_y = 0;
685 | 
686 |     for (let i = 0; i < n; i++) {
687 |       const dx = x[i] - mean_x;
688 |       const dy = y[i] - mean_y;
689 |       numerator += dx * dy;
690 |       denominator_x += dx * dx;
691 |       denominator_y += dy * dy;
692 |     }
693 | 
694 |     const denominator = Math.sqrt(denominator_x * denominator_y);
695 |     return denominator === 0 ? 0 : numerator / denominator;
696 |   }
697 | 
698 |   private detectOutliers(
699 |     dataset: Dataset,
700 |     schema: SemanticSchema
701 |   ): Record<string, unknown>[] {
702 |     const outliers: Record<string, unknown>[] = [];
703 | 
704 |     for (const measure of schema.measures) {
705 |       const values = dataset.data
706 |         .map((row) => row[measure] as number)
707 |         .filter((v) => typeof v === "number");
708 | 
709 |       if (values.length < 4) continue;
710 | 
711 |       values.sort((a, b) => a - b);
712 |       const q1 = values[Math.floor(values.length * 0.25)];
713 |       const q3 = values[Math.floor(values.length * 0.75)];
714 |       const iqr = q3 - q1;
715 |       const lowerBound = q1 - 1.5 * iqr;
716 |       const upperBound = q3 + 1.5 * iqr;
717 | 
718 |       dataset.data.forEach((row) => {
719 |         const value = row[measure] as number;
720 |         if (value < lowerBound || value > upperBound) {
721 |           outliers.push({
722 |             measure,
723 |             value,
724 |             type: value < lowerBound ? "low" : "high",
725 |           });
726 |         }
727 |       });
728 |     }
729 | 
730 |     return outliers;
731 |   }
732 | }
733 | 
734 | export default AnalyticsEngine;
```

 1 | import AnalyticsEngine from "./analyticsEngine.js";

 8 | interface DashboardRequest {

12 | }

14 | export class DashboardBuilder {

16 |

18 |     this.analyticsEngine = new AnalyticsEngine();

23 | 
24 |     // Step 1: Analyze Dataset

26 |     const schema = await this.analyticsEngine.analyzeDatasetStructure(request.dataset);
27 |     console.log("✅ Schema Generated:", schema.dataset_domain);
28 | 
29 |     // Step 2: Generate KPIs

33 |

45 |       schema,
46 |       kpis,
47 |       visualizations
48 |     );
49 |     console.log("✅ Dashboard structure created");
50 | 
51 |     // Step 5: Generate AI Insights
