### FILE: apps/backend/src/routes/export.js

Source path: `apps/backend/src/routes/export.js`

```js
  1 | // Export-related routes
  2 | import { sendSuccess, sendError } from '../utils/response-utils.js';
  3 | import { HTTP_STATUS, ERROR_CODES } from '../config/constants.js';
  4 | import { getDatasetById } from '../database/dataset-repository.js';
  5 | 
  6 | export async function handleExportRoutes(request, response, pathname) {
  7 |   const { method } = request;
  8 | 
  9 |   // GET /api/datasets/:id/export/:format - Export dataset
 10 |   if (pathname.match(/^\/api\/datasets\/[^/]+\/export\/(json|csv|md)$/) && method === 'GET') {
 11 |     try {
 12 |       const parts = pathname.split('/');
 13 |       const datasetId = parts[3];
 14 |       const format = parts[5];
 15 |       
 16 |       const dataset = getDatasetById(datasetId);
 17 |       
 18 |       if (!dataset) {
 19 |         sendError(response, HTTP_STATUS.NOT_FOUND, 'Dataset not found', ERROR_CODES.NOT_FOUND);
 20 |         return true;
 21 |       }
 22 |       
 23 |       let content;
 24 |       let contentType;
 25 |       let filename;
 26 |       
 27 |       switch (format) {
 28 |         case 'json':
 29 |           content = JSON.stringify(dataset, null, 2);
 30 |           contentType = 'application/json';
 31 |           filename = `${dataset.name || 'dataset'}.json`;
 32 |           break;
 33 |           
 34 |         case 'csv':
 35 |           content = convertToCSV(dataset);
 36 |           contentType = 'text/csv';
 37 |           filename = `${dataset.name || 'dataset'}.csv`;
 38 |           break;
 39 |           
 40 |         case 'md':
 41 |           content = convertToMarkdown(dataset);
 42 |           contentType = 'text/markdown';
 43 |           filename = `${dataset.name || 'dataset'}.md`;
 44 |           break;
 45 |           
 46 |         default:
 47 |           sendError(response, HTTP_STATUS.BAD_REQUEST, 'Unsupported format', ERROR_CODES.VALIDATION_ERROR);
 48 |           return true;
 49 |       }
 50 |       
 51 |       // Set headers for file download
 52 |       response.setHeader('Content-Type', contentType);
 53 |       response.setHeader('Content-Disposition', `attachment; filename="${filename}"`);
 54 |       response.setHeader('Content-Length', Buffer.byteLength(content));
 55 |       response.writeHead(200);
 56 |       response.end(content);
 57 |       
 58 |       console.log(`[EXPORT] ✅ Exported dataset as ${format}`);
 59 |       return true;
 60 |     } catch (error) {
 61 |       console.error('Dataset export error:', error);
 62 |       sendError(response, HTTP_STATUS.INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR, 'Failed to export dataset', ERROR_CODES.DATABASE_ERROR);
 63 |       return true;
 64 |     }
 65 |   }
 66 | 
 67 |   // POST /api/datasets/:id/export/report - Generate report
 68 |   if (pathname.match(/^\/api\/datasets\/[^/]+\/export\/report$/) && method === 'POST') {
 69 |     try {
 70 |       const datasetId = pathname.split('/')[3];
 71 |       const dataset = getDatasetById(datasetId);
 72 |       
 73 |       if (!dataset) {
 74 |         sendError(response, HTTP_STATUS.NOT_FOUND, 'Dataset not found', ERROR_CODES.NOT_FOUND);
 75 |         return true;
 76 |       }
 77 |       
 78 |       const report = generateReport(dataset);
 79 |       
 80 |       sendSuccess(response, { report }, 'Report generated');
 81 |       return true;
 82 |     } catch (error) {
 83 |       console.error('Report generation error:', error);
 84 |       sendError(response, HTTP_STATUS.INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR, 'Failed to generate report', ERROR_CODES.DATABASE_ERROR);
 85 |       return true;
 86 |     }
 87 |   }
 88 | 
 89 |   return false;
 90 | }
 91 | 
 92 | function convertToCSV(dataset) {
 93 |   const { rows, columns } = dataset;
 94 |   const headers = columns.map(c => c.name);
 95 |   const lines = [headers.join(',')];
 96 |   
 97 |   rows.forEach(row => {
 98 |     const values = headers.map(h => {
 99 |       const val = row[h];
100 |       if (val === null || val === undefined) return '';
101 |       if (typeof val === 'string' && (val.includes(',') || val.includes('"'))) {
102 |         return `"${val.replace(/"/g, '""')}"`;
103 |       }
104 |       return val;
105 |     });
106 |     lines.push(values.join(','));
107 |   });
108 |   
109 |   return lines.join('\n');
110 | }
111 | 
112 | function convertToMarkdown(dataset) {
113 |   const { rows, columns, name } = dataset;
114 |   let md = `# ${name || 'Dataset Export'}\n\n`;
115 |   md += `**Rows:** ${rows.length}  \n`;
116 |   md += `**Columns:** ${columns.length}  \n\n`;
117 |   
118 |   md += `## Columns\n\n`;
119 |   columns.forEach(c => {
120 |     md += `- **${c.name}**: ${c.type || c.inferredType || 'unknown'}\n`;
121 |   });
122 |   
123 |   md += `\n## Sample Data (first 10 rows)\n\n`;
124 |   md += `| ${columns.map(c => c.name).join(' | ')} |\n`;
125 |   md += `| ${columns.map(() => '---').join(' | ')} |\n`;
126 |   
127 |   rows.slice(0, 10).forEach(row => {
128 |     md += `| ${columns.map(c => row[c.name] ?? '').join(' | ')} |\n`;
129 |   });
130 |   
131 |   return md;
132 | }
133 | 
134 | function generateReport(dataset) {
135 |   const { rows, columns, name } = dataset;
136 |   const numericCols = columns.filter(c => c.type === 'number' || c.inferredType === 'numeric');
137 |   
138 |   return {
139 |     title: `Analysis Report: ${name || 'Dataset'}`,
140 |     generatedAt: new Date().toISOString(),
141 |     summary: {
142 |       totalRows: rows.length,
143 |       totalColumns: columns.length,
144 |       numericColumns: numericCols.length,
145 |       categoricalColumns: columns.length - numericCols.length
146 |     },
147 |     statistics: numericCols.reduce((acc, col) => {
148 |       const values = rows.map(r => parseFloat(r[col.name])).filter(v => !isNaN(v));
149 |       if (values.length > 0) {
150 |         acc[col.name] = {
151 |           min: Math.min(...values),
152 |           max: Math.max(...values),
153 |           mean: (values.reduce((a, b) => a + b, 0) / values.length).toFixed(2),
154 |           median: values.sort((a, b) => a - b)[Math.floor(values.length / 2)]
155 |         };
156 |       }
157 |       return acc;
158 |     }, {}),
159 |     recommendations: [
160 |       'Consider visualizing numeric distributions',
161 |       'Check for correlations between numeric columns',
162 |       'Look for outliers in the data'
163 |     ]
164 |   };
165 | }
166 | 
167 | export default { handleExportRoutes };
168 | 
```

### FILE: apps/backend/src/routes/health.js

Source path: `apps/backend/src/routes/health.js`

```js
  1 | // Health check endpoints
  2 | import { sendHealth, sendSuccess } from '../utils/response-utils.js';
  3 | import { HTTP_STATUS } from '../config/constants.js';
  4 | 
  5 | export async function handleHealthRoutes(request, response, pathname) {
  6 |   const { method } = request;
  7 | 
  8 |   // GET /api/health - Main health check
  9 |   if (pathname === '/api/health' && method === 'GET') {
 10 |     const healthData = {
 11 |       status: 'healthy',
 12 |       timestamp: new Date().toISOString(),
 13 |       uptime: process.uptime(),
 14 |       version: process.env.npm_package_version || '2.0.0',
 15 |       environment: process.env.NODE_ENV || 'development',
 16 |       memory: process.memoryUsage(),
 17 |       cpu: process.cpuUsage(),
 18 |       nodeVersion: process.version,
 19 |       platform: process.platform,
 20 |       arch: process.arch
 21 |     };
 22 | 
 23 |     sendHealth(response, 'healthy', healthData);
 24 |     return true;
 25 |   }
 26 | 
 27 |   // GET /api/health/detailed - Detailed health check
 28 |   if (pathname === '/api/health/detailed' && method === 'GET') {
 29 |     const detailedHealth = {
 30 |       status: 'healthy',
 31 |       timestamp: new Date().toISOString(),
 32 |       uptime: process.uptime(),
 33 |       version: process.env.npm_package_version || '2.0.0',
 34 |       environment: process.env.NODE_ENV || 'development',
 35 |       
 36 |       // System information
 37 |       system: {
 38 |         nodeVersion: process.version,
 39 |         platform: process.platform,
 40 |         arch: process.arch,
 41 |         pid: process.pid
 42 |       },
 43 |       
 44 |       // Memory usage
 45 |       memory: {
 46 |         rss: process.memoryUsage().rss,
 47 |         heapTotal: process.memoryUsage().heapTotal,
 48 |         heapUsed: process.memoryUsage().heapUsed,
 49 |         external: process.memoryUsage().external,
 50 |         arrayBuffers: process.memoryUsage().arrayBuffers
 51 |       },
 52 |       
 53 |       // CPU usage
 54 |       cpu: {
 55 |         user: process.cpuUsage().user,
 56 |         system: process.cpuUsage().system
 57 |       },
 58 |       
 59 |       // Event loop lag
 60 |       eventLoop: {
 61 |         lag: getEventLoopLag()
 62 |       },
 63 |       
 64 |       // Process info
 65 |       process: {
 66 |         title: process.title,
 67 |         execPath: process.execPath,
 68 |         execArgv: process.execArgv,
 69 |         argv: process.argv
 70 |       }
 71 |     };
 72 | 
 73 |     sendHealth(response, 'healthy', detailedHealth);
 74 |     return true;
 75 |   }
 76 | 
 77 |   // GET /api/health/ping - Simple ping
 78 |   if (pathname === '/api/health/ping' && method === 'GET') {
 79 |     sendSuccess(response, { 
 80 |       pong: true, 
 81 |       timestamp: new Date().toISOString() 
 82 |     }, 'Pong');
 83 |     return true;
 84 |   }
 85 | 
 86 |   // GET /api/health/ready - Readiness probe
 87 |   if (pathname === '/api/health/ready' && method === 'GET') {
 88 |     // Check if all critical services are ready
 89 |     const isReady = await checkReadiness();
 90 |     
 91 |     if (isReady.ready) {
 92 |       sendHealth(response, 'healthy', isReady);
 93 |     } else {
 94 |       sendHealth(response, 'unhealthy', isReady);
 95 |     }
 96 |     return true;
 97 |   }
 98 | 
 99 |   // GET /api/health/live - Liveness probe
100 |   if (pathname === '/api/health/live' && method === 'GET') {
101 |     // Basic liveness check - process is responsive
102 |     sendHealth(response, 'healthy', {
103 |       live: true,
104 |       timestamp: new Date().toISOString()
105 |     });
106 |     return true;
107 |   }
108 | 
109 |   return false;
110 | }
111 | 
112 | /**
113 |  * Get event loop lag in milliseconds
114 |  */
115 | function getEventLoopLag() {
116 |   const start = process.hrtime.bigint();
117 |   return setImmediate(() => {
118 |     const lag = Number(process.hrtime.bigint() - start) / 1000000; // Convert to milliseconds
119 |     return lag;
120 |   });
121 | }
122 | 
123 | /**
124 |  * Check if the application is ready to serve traffic
125 |  */
126 | async function checkReadiness() {
127 |   const checks = {
128 |     database: false,
129 |     aiProviders: false,
130 |     fileSystem: false,
131 |     memory: false
132 |   };
133 | 
134 |   // Check file system access
135 |   try {
136 |     const fs = require('fs');
137 |     fs.accessSync('./data', fs.constants.R_OK | fs.constants.W_OK);
138 |     checks.fileSystem = true;
139 |   } catch (error) {
140 |     console.warn('File system check failed:', error.message);
141 |   }
142 | 
143 |   // Check memory usage
144 |   const memoryUsage = process.memoryUsage();
145 |   const memoryUsagePercent = (memoryUsage.heapUsed / memoryUsage.heapTotal) * 100;
146 |   checks.memory = memoryUsagePercent < 90; // Less than 90% memory usage
147 | 
148 |   // Check database (placeholder - would implement actual DB check)
149 |   checks.database = true; // Placeholder
150 | 
151 |   // Check AI providers (placeholder - would check actual providers)
152 |   checks.aiProviders = true; // Placeholder
153 | 
154 |   const allChecksPass = Object.values(checks).every(check => check === true);
155 | 
156 |   return {
157 |     ready: allChecksPass,
158 |     checks,
159 |     timestamp: new Date().toISOString()
160 |   };
161 | }
162 | 
163 | export default {
164 |   handleHealthRoutes
165 | };
166 | 
```

```js
  1 | // Main route aggregator
  2 | import { handleDatasetRoutes } from './datasets.js';
  3 | import { handleChatRoutes } from './chat.js';
  4 | import { handleAnalyticsRoutes } from './analytics.js';
  5 | import { handleAnalyticsBrainRoutes } from './analytics-brain.js';
  6 | import { handleAIRoutes } from './ai.js';
  7 | import { handleHealthRoutes } from './health.js';
  8 | import { handleExportRoutes } from './export.js';
  9 | import { handleMLRoutes } from './machine-learning.js';
 10 | import { handleStateRoutes } from './state.js';
 11 | import { handleQrUploadRoutes } from './qr-upload.js';
 12 | import { handlePlaybookAnalysisRoutes } from './playbook-analysis.js';
 13 | import { handlePdfRoutes } from './pdf.js';
 14 | import { handleAiAnalystRoutes } from './ai-analyst.routes.js';
 15 | import { handleSchemaTrainedAIRoutes } from './schema-trained-ai.routes.js';
 16 | import { handleDashboardQualityRoutes } from './dashboard-quality.js';
 17 | import { handleDashboardAiRoutes } from './dashboardAiRoutes.js';
 18 | import {
 19 |   handleE2ECompatRoutes,
 20 |   handleE2ENotFound,
 21 | } from './e2e-compat.routes.js';
 22 | import { sendError, sendSuccess } from '../utils/response-utils.js';
 23 | import { HTTP_STATUS } from '../config/constants.js';
 24 | 
 25 | export async function setupRoutes(request, response) {
 26 |   const { method, pathname } = request;
 27 | 
 28 |   try {
 29 |     if (await handleE2ECompatRoutes(request, response, pathname)) {
 30 |       return;
 31 |     }
 32 | 
 33 |     // State routes (for frontend state management)
 34 |     if (await handleStateRoutes(request, response, pathname)) {
 35 |       return;
 36 |     }
 37 | 
 38 |     // Health check routes (highest priority)
 39 |     if (await handleHealthRoutes(request, response, pathname)) {
 40 |       return;
 41 |     }
 42 | 
 43 |     // Schema-trained dashboard/chat/RAG routes before legacy dashboard/chat handlers
 44 |     if (await handleSchemaTrainedAIRoutes(request, response, pathname)) {
 45 |       return;
 46 |     }
 47 | 
 48 |     // Dashboard quality validation routes
 49 |     if (await handleDashboardQualityRoutes(request, response, pathname)) {
 50 |       return;
 51 |     }
 52 | 
 53 |     // Schema-only AI dashboard planner + local analytics engine
 54 |     if (await handleDashboardAiRoutes(request, response, pathname)) {
 55 |       return;
 56 |     }
 57 | 
 58 |     // QR upload routes
 59 |     if (await handleQrUploadRoutes(request, response, pathname)) {
 60 |       return;
 61 |     }
 62 | 
 63 |     // AI provider routes
 64 |     if (await handleAIRoutes(request, response, pathname)) {
 65 |       return;
 66 |     }
 67 | 
 68 |     // PDF import and PDF Q&A routes
 69 |     if (await handlePdfRoutes(request, response, pathname)) {
 70 |       return;
 71 |     }
 72 | 
 73 |     // Dataset routes
 74 |     if (await handleDatasetRoutes(request, response, pathname)) {
 75 |       return;
 76 |     }
 77 | 
 78 |     // Chat routes
 79 |     if (await handleChatRoutes(request, response, pathname)) {
 80 |       return;
 81 |     }
 82 | 
 83 |     // Analytics routes
 84 |     if (await handleAnalyticsRoutes(request, response, pathname)) {
 85 |       return;
 86 |     }
 87 | 
 88 |     // DataAnalyticsProjects playbook route
 89 |     if (await handlePlaybookAnalysisRoutes(request, response, pathname)) {
 90 |       return;
 91 |     }
 92 | 
 93 |     // AI Analyst routes
 94 |     if (await handleAiAnalystRoutes(request, response, pathname)) {
 95 |       return;
 96 |     }
 97 | 
 98 |     // Schema-safe analytics brain routes
 99 |     if (await handleAnalyticsBrainRoutes(request, response, pathname)) {
100 |       return;
101 |     }
102 | 
103 |     // Export routes
104 |     if (await handleExportRoutes(request, response, pathname)) {
105 |       return;
106 |     }
107 | 
108 |     // ML/AutoML routes
109 |     if (await handleMLRoutes(request, response, pathname)) {
110 |       return;
111 |     }
112 | 
113 |     // Route not found
114 |     if (method === 'GET' && pathname === '/') {
115 |       // Root endpoint
116 |       sendSuccess(response, {
117 |         name: 'InsightFlow API',
118 |         version: '2.0.0',
119 |         description: 'AI-powered data analytics platform',
120 |         endpoints: {
121 |           health: '/api/health',
122 |           ai: '/api/ai/*',
123 |           datasets: '/api/datasets/*',
124 |           chat: '/api/datasets/:id/chat',
125 |           analytics: '/api/datasets/:id/analyze',
126 |           export: '/api/datasets/:id/export'
127 |         },
128 |         documentation: '/api/docs',
129 |         timestamp: new Date().toISOString()
130 |       }, 'InsightFlow API is running');
131 |     } else {
132 |       // 404 Not Found
133 |       handleE2ENotFound(request, response);
134 |     }
135 | 
136 |   } catch (error) {
137 |     console.error('Route setup error:', error);
138 |     sendError(response, HTTP_STATUS.INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR, 'Internal server error', 'ROUTE_ERROR');
139 |   }
140 | }
141 | 
142 | export default {
143 |   setupRoutes
144 | };
145 | 
```

```js
  1 | /**
  2 |  * ML Routes - AutoML endpoint handlers
  3 |  */
  4 | import { automlService } from '../services/ml/automl-service.js';
  5 | import { sendJson } from '../utils/response-utils.js';
  6 | 
  7 | export async function handleMLRoutes(request, response, pathname) {
  8 |   const url = new URL(pathname, `http://${request.headers.host}`);
  9 |   const pathParts = url.pathname.split('/').filter(Boolean);
 10 | 
 11 |   // GET /api/ml/health
 12 |   if (pathname === '/api/ml/health' && request.method === 'GET') {
 13 |     return sendJson(response, 200, {
 14 |       success: true,
 15 |       status: 'ready',
 16 |       message: 'AutoML service is running locally (no external service needed)',
 17 |       availableModels: automlService.listModels().length
 18 |     });
 19 |   }
 20 | 
 21 |   // POST /api/ml/train - Train a model
 22 |   if (pathname === '/api/ml/train' && request.method === 'POST') {
 23 |     let body = '';
 24 |     
 25 |     for await (const chunk of request) {
 26 |       body += chunk;
 27 |     }
 28 |     
 29 |     try {
 30 |       const data = JSON.parse(body);
 31 |       const { dataset_id, rows, target_column, problem_type } = data;
 32 |       
 33 |       if (!dataset_id || !rows || !target_column) {
 34 |         return sendJson(response, 400, {
 35 |           success: false,
 36 |           error: 'Missing required fields: dataset_id, rows, target_column'
 37 |         });
 38 |       }
 39 | 
 40 |       const result = await automlService.trainModel(
 41 |         dataset_id,
 42 |         rows,
 43 |         target_column,
 44 |         problem_type || 'auto'
 45 |       );
 46 | 
 47 |       if (result.success) {
 48 |         return sendJson(response, 200, result);
 49 |       } else {
 50 |         return sendJson(response, 500, result);
 51 |       }
 52 |     } catch (error) {
 53 |       return sendJson(response, 500, {
 54 |         success: false,
 55 |         error: error.message
 56 |       });
 57 |     }
 58 |   }
 59 | 
 60 |   // POST /api/ml/predict - Make predictions
 61 |   if (pathname === '/api/ml/predict' && request.method === 'POST') {
 62 |     let body = '';
 63 |     
 64 |     for await (const chunk of request) {
 65 |       body += chunk;
 66 |     }
 67 |     
 68 |     try {
 69 |       const data = JSON.parse(body);
 70 |       const { dataset_id, input_data } = data;
 71 |       
 72 |       if (!dataset_id || !input_data) {
 73 |         return sendJson(response, 400, {
 74 |           success: false,
 75 |           error: 'Missing required fields: dataset_id, input_data'
 76 |         });
 77 |       }
 78 | 
 79 |       const result = await automlService.predict(dataset_id, input_data);
 80 | 
 81 |       if (result.success) {
 82 |         return sendJson(response, 200, result);
 83 |       } else {
 84 |         return sendJson(response, 404, result);
 85 |       }
 86 |     } catch (error) {
 87 |       return sendJson(response, 500, {
 88 |         success: false,
 89 |         error: error.message
 90 |       });
 91 |     }
 92 |   }
 93 | 
 94 |   // GET /api/ml/models - List all models
 95 |   if (pathname === '/api/ml/models' && request.method === 'GET') {
 96 |     return sendJson(response, 200, {
 97 |       success: true,
 98 |       models: automlService.listModels()
 99 |     });
100 |   }
101 | 
102 |   // GET /api/ml/models/:id - Get model info
103 |   if (pathParts[2] === 'models' && pathParts[3] && request.method === 'GET') {
104 |     const datasetId = pathParts[3];
105 |     const modelInfo = automlService.getModelInfo(datasetId);
106 |     
107 |     if (modelInfo) {
108 |       return sendJson(response, 200, {
109 |         success: true,
110 |         model: modelInfo
111 |       });
112 |     } else {
113 |       return sendJson(response, 404, {
114 |         success: false,
115 |         error: 'Model not found'
116 |       });
117 |     }
118 |   }
119 | 
120 |   // DELETE /api/ml/models/:id - Delete model
121 |   if (pathParts[2] === 'models' && pathParts[3] && request.method === 'DELETE') {
122 |     const datasetId = pathParts[3];
123 |     const result = automlService.deleteModel(datasetId);
124 |     
125 |     if (result.success) {
126 |       return sendJson(response, 200, result);
127 |     } else {
128 |       return sendJson(response, 404, result);
129 |     }
130 |   }
131 | 
132 |   // POST /api/ml/cluster - Cluster analysis
133 |   if (pathname === '/api/ml/cluster' && request.method === 'POST') {
134 |     let body = '';
135 |     
136 |     for await (const chunk of request) {
137 |       body += chunk;
138 |     }
139 |     
140 |     try {
141 |       const data = JSON.parse(body);
142 |       const { rows, feature_columns, num_clusters } = data;
143 |       
144 |       if (!rows || !feature_columns) {
145 |         return sendJson(response, 400, {
146 |           success: false,
147 |           error: 'Missing required fields: rows, feature_columns'
148 |         });
149 |       }
150 | 
151 |       const result = await automlService.clusterAnalysis(
152 |         rows,
153 |         feature_columns,
154 |         num_clusters || 3
155 |       );
156 | 
157 |       return sendJson(response, result.success ? 200 : 500, result);
158 |     } catch (error) {
159 |       return sendJson(response, 500, {
160 |         success: false,
161 |         error: error.message
162 |       });
163 |     }
164 |   }
165 | 
166 |   // POST /api/ml/pca - PCA analysis
167 |   if (pathname === '/api/ml/pca' && request.method === 'POST') {
168 |     let body = '';
169 |     
170 |     for await (const chunk of request) {
171 |       body += chunk;
172 |     }
173 |     
174 |     try {
175 |       const data = JSON.parse(body);
176 |       const { rows, feature_columns, n_components } = data;
177 |       
178 |       if (!rows || !feature_columns) {
179 |         return sendJson(response, 400, {
180 |           success: false,
181 |           error: 'Missing required fields: rows, feature_columns'
182 |         });
183 |       }
184 | 
185 |       const result = await automlService.pcaAnalysis(
186 |         rows,
187 |         feature_columns,
188 |         n_components || 2
189 |       );
190 | 
191 |       return sendJson(response, result.success ? 200 : 500, result);
192 |     } catch (error) {
193 |       return sendJson(response, 500, {
194 |         success: false,
195 |         error: error.message
196 |       });
197 |     }
198 |   }
199 | 
200 |   return false;
201 | }
```

```js
  1 | import fs from "node:fs";
  2 | import fsp from "node:fs/promises";
  3 | import path from "node:path";
  4 | import { randomUUID } from "node:crypto";
  5 | import Busboy from "busboy";
  6 | 
  7 | import { sendSuccess, sendError } from "../utils/response-utils.js";
  8 | import { HTTP_STATUS, ERROR_CODES } from "../config/constants.js";
  9 | import { createDataset } from "../database/dataset-repository.js";
 10 | import { updateDataset, updateAnalysis } from "./state.js";
 11 | import { parsePdfWithOpenDataLoader } from "../services/pdf/pdf-loader-service.js";
 12 | import { buildPdfDataset } from "../services/pdf/pdf-dataset-builder.js";
 13 | import { getPdfKnowledgeBase, savePdfKnowledgeBase } from "../services/pdf/pdf-store.js";
 14 | import { answerPdfQuestion } from "../services/pdf/pdf-qa-service.js";
 15 | import { buildDataAnalyticsProjectsDashboard } from "../services/playbooks/playbook-dashboard-engine.js";
 16 | 
 17 | const uploadDir = path.resolve("data", "uploads", "pdfs");
 18 | await fsp.mkdir(uploadDir, { recursive: true });
 19 | 
 20 | function readJsonBody(request) {
 21 |   return new Promise((resolve, reject) => {
 22 |     let body = "";
 23 | 
 24 |     request.on("data", (chunk) => {
 25 |       body += chunk.toString();
 26 | 
 27 |       if (body.length > 10 * 1024 * 1024) {
 28 |         reject(new Error("Request body too large"));
 29 |       }
 30 |     });
 31 | 
 32 |     request.on("end", () => {
 33 |       try {
 34 |         resolve(body ? JSON.parse(body) : {});
 35 |       } catch {
 36 |         reject(new Error("Invalid JSON body"));
 37 |       }
 38 |     });
 39 | 
 40 |     request.on("error", reject);
 41 |   });
 42 | }
 43 | 
 44 | function parsePdfMultipart(request) {
 45 |   return new Promise((resolve, reject) => {
 46 |     const contentType = request.headers["content-type"] || "";
 47 | 
 48 |     if (!contentType.includes("multipart/form-data")) {
 49 |       reject(new Error("Content-Type must be multipart/form-data"));
 50 |       return;
 51 |     }
 52 | 
 53 |     const busboy = Busboy({
 54 |       headers: request.headers,
 55 |       limits: {
 56 |         files: 1,
 57 |         fileSize: 30 * 1024 * 1024,
 58 |       },
 59 |     });
 60 | 
 61 |     let uploadedFile = null;
 62 |     let settled = false;
 63 |     const fields = {};
 64 |     const writeTasks = [];
 65 | 
 66 |     const fail = (error) => {
 67 |       if (settled) return;
 68 |       settled = true;
 69 |       reject(error);
 70 |     };
 71 | 
 72 |     busboy.on("field", (name, value) => {
 73 |       fields[name] = value;
 74 |     });
 75 | 
 76 |     busboy.on("file", (fieldName, file, info) => {
 77 |       const { filename, mimeType } = info;
 78 | 
 79 |       if (mimeType !== "application/pdf") {
 80 |         file.resume();
 81 |         fail(new Error("Only PDF files are allowed"));
 82 |         return;
 83 |       }
 84 | 
 85 |       const safeName = String(filename || "document.pdf").replace(/[^\w.-]+/g, "_");
 86 |       const storedName = `${randomUUID()}-${safeName}`;
 87 |       const filePath = path.join(uploadDir, storedName);
 88 |       const stream = fs.createWriteStream(filePath);
 89 | 
 90 |       uploadedFile = {
 91 |         fieldName,
 92 |         originalName: safeName,
 93 |         storedName,
 94 |         filePath,
 95 |         mimeType,
 96 |       };
 97 | 
 98 |       file.pipe(stream);
 99 | 
100 |       writeTasks.push(
101 |         new Promise((resolveStream, rejectStream) => {
102 |           stream.on("finish", resolveStream);
103 |           stream.on("error", rejectStream);
104 |           file.on("limit", () => rejectStream(new Error("PDF file is too large")));
105 |         }),
106 |       );
107 |     });
108 | 
109 |     busboy.on("error", fail);
110 | 
111 |     busboy.on("finish", async () => {
112 |       if (settled) return;
113 | 
114 |       try {
115 |         await Promise.all(writeTasks);
116 | 
117 |         if (!uploadedFile) {
118 |           fail(new Error("PDF file is required"));
119 |           return;
120 |         }
121 | 
122 |         settled = true;
123 |         resolve({ file: uploadedFile, fields });
124 |       } catch (error) {
125 |         fail(error);
126 |       }
127 |     });
128 | 
129 |     request.pipe(busboy);
130 |   });
131 | }
132 | 
133 | function safeNumber(value) {
134 |   const parsed = Number(String(value ?? "").replace(/[,$₹%]/g, ""));
135 |   return Number.isFinite(parsed) ? parsed : null;
136 | }
137 | 
138 | function buildFallbackAnalysis(dataset) {
139 |   const columns = dataset.columns || [];
140 |   const rows = dataset.rows || [];
141 |   const numeric = columns.find((column) => column.type === "number");
142 |   const dimension = columns.find((column) => column.type !== "number");
143 |   const kpis = [
144 |     {
145 |       id: randomUUID(),
146 |       title: "Extracted Rows",
147 |       value: rows.length.toLocaleString(),
148 |       metric: "*",
149 |       aggregation: "count",
150 |     },
151 |     {
152 |       id: randomUUID(),
153 |       title: "Extracted Columns",
154 |       value: columns.length.toLocaleString(),
155 |       metric: "*",
156 |       aggregation: "count",
157 |     },
158 |   ];
159 |   const chartRecommendations = [];
160 | 
161 |   if (dimension) {
162 |     const counts = new Map();
163 | 
164 |     for (const row of rows) {
165 |       const key = String(row[dimension.name] || "Unknown");
166 |       counts.set(key, (counts.get(key) || 0) + 1);
167 |     }
168 | 
169 |     chartRecommendations.push({
170 |       id: randomUUID(),
171 |       title: `Rows by ${dimension.name}`,
172 |       type: "bar",
173 |       xKey: dimension.name,
174 |       yKey: "count",
175 |       aggregation: "count",
176 |       data: [...counts.entries()]
177 |         .map(([label, count]) => ({ [dimension.name]: label, count }))
178 |         .sort((a, b) => b.count - a.count)
179 |         .slice(0, 10),
180 |     });
181 |   }
182 | 
183 |   if (numeric && dimension) {
184 |     const groups = new Map();
185 | 
186 |     for (const row of rows) {
187 |       const key = String(row[dimension.name] || "Unknown");
188 |       const value = safeNumber(row[numeric.name]);
189 |       if (value === null) continue;
190 |       if (!groups.has(key)) groups.set(key, []);
191 |       groups.get(key).push(value);
192 |     }
193 | 
194 |     chartRecommendations.push({
195 |       id: randomUUID(),
196 |       title: `Average ${numeric.name} by ${dimension.name}`,
197 |       type: "bar",
198 |       xKey: dimension.name,
199 |       yKey: numeric.name,
200 |       aggregation: "avg",
201 |       data: [...groups.entries()]
202 |         .map(([label, values]) => ({
203 |           [dimension.name]: label,
204 |           [numeric.name]: values.reduce((sum, value) => sum + value, 0) / values.length,
205 |         }))
206 |         .sort((a, b) => b[numeric.name] - a[numeric.name])
207 |         .slice(0, 10),
208 |     });
209 |   }
210 | 
211 |   return {
212 |     dataType: "pdf_document",
213 |     dataTypeLabel: "PDF Extracted Dataset",
214 |     kpis,
215 |     chartRecommendations,
216 |     insights: [
217 |       {
218 |         type: "summary",
219 |         title: "PDF Imported",
220 |         message: `Extracted ${rows.length.toLocaleString()} rows and ${columns.length.toLocaleString()} columns from PDF tables.`,
221 |       },
222 |     ],
223 |     privacy: {
224 |       rawPdfSentToLLM: false,
225 |       schemaOnly: true,
226 |     },
227 |   };
228 | }
229 | 
230 | async function analyzePdfDataset(dataset) {
231 |   try {
232 |     const analysis = await buildDataAnalyticsProjectsDashboard(dataset);
233 |     if (analysis) return analysis;
234 |   } catch {
235 |     // fallback below
236 |   }
237 | 
238 |   return buildFallbackAnalysis(dataset);
239 | }
240 | 
241 | export async function handlePdfRoutes(request, response, pathname) {
242 |   const { method } = request;
243 | 
244 |   if (pathname === "/api/pdf/import" && method === "POST") {
245 |     try {
246 |       const { file } = await parsePdfMultipart(request);
247 |       const parsed = await parsePdfWithOpenDataLoader(file.filePath);
248 |       const { dataset: extractedDataset, knowledgeBase, tables } = buildPdfDataset({
249 |         fileName: file.originalName,
250 |         pdfJson: parsed.json,
251 |         markdown: parsed.markdown,
252 |       });
253 |       const pdfId = randomUUID();
254 |       const dataset = createDataset({
255 |         name: extractedDataset.name,
256 |         fileName: extractedDataset.fileName,
257 |         sourceType: "pdf",
258 |         columns: extractedDataset.columns,
259 |         rows: extractedDataset.rows,
260 |       });
261 |       const finalDataset = dataset || { ...extractedDataset, id: pdfId };
262 | 
263 |       updateDataset(finalDataset);
264 | 
265 |       savePdfKnowledgeBase(pdfId, {
266 |         pdfId,
267 |         datasetId: finalDataset.id,
268 |         fileName: file.originalName,
269 |         jobId: parsed.jobId,
270 |         outputDir: parsed.outputDir,
271 |         knowledgeBase,
272 |         tables,
273 |       });
274 | 
275 |       const analysis = await analyzePdfDataset(finalDataset);
276 |       updateAnalysis(analysis);
277 | 
278 |       sendSuccess(
279 |         response,
280 |         {
281 |           pdf: {
282 |             id: pdfId,
283 |             datasetId: finalDataset.id,
284 |             fileName: file.originalName,
285 |             jobId: parsed.jobId,
286 |             tableCount: tables.length,
287 |             chunkCount: knowledgeBase.chunks.length,
288 |             textElementCount: knowledgeBase.textElements.length,
289 |           },
290 |           dataset: finalDataset,
291 |           analysis,
292 |           knowledgeBaseSummary: {
293 |             tableCount: tables.length,
294 |             chunkCount: knowledgeBase.chunks.length,
295 |             textElementCount: knowledgeBase.textElements.length,
296 |           },
297 |           privacy: {
298 |             rawPdfSentToLLM: false,
299 |             extractedTextCanBeUsedForRAG: true,
300 |             dashboardValuesCalculatedLocally: true,
301 |           },
302 |         },
303 |         "PDF imported and analyzed",
304 |       );
305 | 
306 |       return true;
307 |     } catch (error) {
308 |       sendError(
309 |         response,
310 |         HTTP_STATUS.BAD_REQUEST,
311 |         error.message || "PDF import failed",
312 |         ERROR_CODES.VALIDATION_ERROR,
313 |       );
314 | 
315 |       return true;
316 |     }
317 |   }
318 | 
319 |   const askMatch = pathname.match(/^\/api\/pdf\/([^/]+)\/ask$/);
320 | 
321 |   if (askMatch && method === "POST") {
322 |     try {
323 |       const [, pdfId] = askMatch;
324 |       const body = await readJsonBody(request);
325 | 
326 |       if (!body.query) {
327 |         sendError(response, HTTP_STATUS.BAD_REQUEST, "Query is required", ERROR_CODES.VALIDATION_ERROR);
328 |         return true;
329 |       }
330 | 
331 |       const stored = getPdfKnowledgeBase(pdfId);
332 | 
333 |       if (!stored) {
334 |         sendError(response, HTTP_STATUS.NOT_FOUND, "PDF knowledge base not found", ERROR_CODES.DATASET_NOT_FOUND);
335 |         return true;
336 |       }
337 | 
338 |       const result = await answerPdfQuestion({
339 |         query: body.query,
340 |         knowledgeBase: stored.knowledgeBase,
341 |       });
342 | 
343 |       sendSuccess(response, result, "PDF question answered");
344 |       return true;
345 |     } catch (error) {
346 |       sendError(
347 |         response,
348 |         HTTP_STATUS.INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR,
349 |         error.message || "PDF question failed",
350 |         ERROR_CODES.AI_GENERATION_FAILED,
351 |       );
352 | 
353 |       return true;
354 |     }
355 |   }
356 | 
357 |   return false;
358 | }
359 | 
```

### FILE: apps/backend/src/routes/playbook-analysis.js

Source path: `apps/backend/src/routes/playbook-analysis.js`

```js
 1 | import { getDatasetById } from "../database/dataset-repository.js";
 2 | import { buildDataAnalyticsProjectsDashboard } from "../services/playbooks/playbook-dashboard-engine.js";
 3 | import { HTTP_STATUS, ERROR_CODES } from "../config/constants.js";
 4 | import { sendError, sendSuccess } from "../utils/response-utils.js";
 5 | 
 6 | export async function handlePlaybookAnalysisRoutes(request, response, pathname) {
 7 |   const { method } = request;
 8 | 
 9 |   if (pathname.match(/^\/api\/datasets\/[^/]+\/playbook-analysis$/) && method === "POST") {
10 |     try {
11 |       const datasetId = pathname.split("/")[3];
12 |       const dataset = getDatasetById(datasetId);
13 | 
14 |       if (!dataset) {
15 |         sendError(response, HTTP_STATUS.NOT_FOUND, "Dataset not found", ERROR_CODES.NOT_FOUND);
16 |         return true;
17 |       }
18 | 
19 |       const analysis = await buildDataAnalyticsProjectsDashboard(dataset);
20 |       sendSuccess(response, analysis, "Playbook analysis generated");
21 |       return true;
22 |     } catch (error) {
23 |       console.error("[PLAYBOOK ANALYSIS] Error:", error);
24 |       sendError(
25 |         response,
26 |         HTTP_STATUS.INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR,
27 |         error.message || "Failed to generate playbook analysis",
28 |         ERROR_CODES.AI_ERROR,
29 |       );
30 |       return true;
31 |     }
32 |   }
33 | 
34 |   return false;
35 | }
36 | 
37 | export default {
38 |   handlePlaybookAnalysisRoutes,
39 | };
40 | 
```

```js
 19 | function readJsonBody(request) {
 20 |   return new Promise((resolve, reject) => {
 21 |     let body = "";
 22 | 
 23 |     request.on("data", (chunk) => {
 24 |       body += chunk.toString();
 25 | 
 26 |       if (body.length > 2 * 1024 * 1024) {
 27 |         reject(new Error("Request body too large."));
 28 |       }
 29 |     });
 30 | 
 31 |     request.on("end", () => {
 32 |       try {
 33 |         resolve(body ? JSON.parse(body) : {});
 34 |       } catch {
 35 |         reject(new Error("Invalid JSON body."));
 36 |       }
 37 |     });
 38 | 
 39 |     request.on("error", reject);
 40 |   });
 41 | }
 42 | 
 43 | function getRequestUrl(request) {
 44 |   return new URL(request.url, `http://${request.headers.host}`);
 45 | }
 46 | 
 47 | function getPortalBaseUrl(request) {
 48 |   const configuredBaseUrl =
 49 |     process.env.FRONTEND_PUBLIC_URL ||
 50 |     process.env.VITE_PUBLIC_APP_URL ||
 51 |     process.env.PUBLIC_APP_URL ||
 52 |     process.env.CLIENT_ORIGIN ||
 53 |     (process.env.CORS_ORIGIN && process.env.CORS_ORIGIN !== "*"
 54 |       ? process.env.CORS_ORIGIN
 55 |       : "");
 56 | 
 57 |   if (configuredBaseUrl) {
 58 |     return configuredBaseUrl.replace(/\/$/, "");
 59 |   }
 60 | 
 61 |   if (request.headers.origin) {
 62 |     return request.headers.origin.replace(/\/$/, "");
 63 |   }
 64 | 
 65 |   const protocol = request.headers["x-forwarded-proto"] || "http";
 66 |   const host = request.headers.host || "localhost:3001";
 67 | 
 68 |   if (host === "localhost:3001") {
 69 |     return `${protocol}://localhost:5173`;
 70 |   }
 71 | 
 72 |   if (host === "127.0.0.1:3001") {
 73 |     return `${protocol}://127.0.0.1:5173`;
 74 |   }
 75 | 
 76 |   return `${protocol}://${host}`;
 77 | }
 78 | 
 79 | function buildMobilePortalUrl(request, sessionId, token) {
 80 |   const redirectUrl = new URL(
 81 |     `/mobile-upload/${encodeURIComponent(sessionId)}`,
 82 |     getPortalBaseUrl(request)
 83 |   );
 84 | 
 85 |   if (token) {
 86 |     redirectUrl.searchParams.set("token", token);
 87 |   }
 88 | 
 89 |   return redirectUrl.toString();
 90 | }
 91 | 
 92 | function parseMultipartFiles(request) {
 93 |   return new Promise((resolve, reject) => {
 94 |     const busboy = Busboy({
 95 |       headers: request.headers,
 96 |       limits: {
 97 |         files: 10,
 98 |         fileSize: 80 * 1024 * 1024,
 99 |       },
100 |     });
101 | 
102 |     const fileTasks = [];
103 | 
104 |     busboy.on("file", (fieldName, file, info) => {
105 |       const chunks = [];
106 |       const filename = String(info.filename || "dataset").replace(/[^\w.-]+/g, "_");
107 | 
108 |       file.on("data", (chunk) => chunks.push(chunk));
109 |       file.on("limit", () => reject(new Error(`${filename} is too large.`)));
110 | 
111 |       fileTasks.push(
112 |         new Promise((resolveFile, rejectFile) => {
113 |           file.on("end", () => {
114 |             resolveFile({
115 |               fieldName,
116 |               filename,
117 |               mimeType: info.mimeType,
118 |               buffer: Buffer.concat(chunks),
119 |             });
120 |           });
121 | 
122 |           file.on("error", rejectFile);
123 |         })
124 |       );
125 |     });
126 | 
127 |     busboy.on("finish", async () => {
128 |       try {
129 |         const files = await Promise.all(fileTasks);
130 | 
131 |         if (!files.length) {
132 |           reject(new Error("No files uploaded."));
133 |           return;
134 |         }
135 | 
136 |         resolve({ files });
137 |       } catch (error) {
138 |         reject(error);
139 |       }
140 |     });
141 | 
142 |     busboy.on("error", reject);
143 |     request.pipe(busboy);
144 |   });
145 | }
146 | 
147 | function safeNumber(value) {
148 |   const parsed = Number(String(value ?? "").replace(/[,$₹%]/g, ""));
149 |   return Number.isFinite(parsed) ? parsed : null;
150 | }
151 | 
152 | function buildBasicAnalysis(dataset) {
153 |   const rows = dataset.rows || [];
154 |   const columns = dataset.columns || [];
155 | 
156 |   const metric =
157 |     columns.find((column) =>
158 |       ["salary", "salary_usd", "revenue", "sales", "amount", "score", "price"].some((key) =>
159 |         column.name.toLowerCase().includes(key)
160 |       )
161 |     ) || columns.find((column) => column.type === "number");
162 | 
163 |   const dimension =
164 |     columns.find((column) =>
165 |       ["country", "region", "education", "product", "category", "company"].some((key) =>
166 |         column.name.toLowerCase().includes(key)
167 |       )
168 |     ) || columns.find((column) => column.type !== "number");
169 | 
170 |   const kpis = [
171 |     {
172 |       id: randomUUID(),
173 |       title: "Total Records",
174 |       value: rows.length.toLocaleString(),
175 |       metric: "*",
176 |       aggregation: "count",
177 |     },
178 |     {
179 |       id: randomUUID(),
180 |       title: "Columns",
181 |       value: columns.length.toLocaleString(),
182 |       metric: "*",
183 |       aggregation: "count",
184 |     },
185 |   ];
186 | 
187 |   if (metric) {
188 |     const nums = rows
189 |       .map((row) => safeNumber(row[metric.name]))
190 |       .filter((value) => value !== null);
191 | 
192 |     if (nums.length) {
193 |       kpis.push(
194 |         {
195 |           id: randomUUID(),
196 |           title: `Average ${metric.name}`,
197 |           value: Number((nums.reduce((a, b) => a + b, 0) / nums.length).toFixed(2)).toLocaleString(),
198 |           metric: metric.name,
199 |           aggregation: "avg",
200 |         },
201 |         {
202 |           id: randomUUID(),
203 |           title: `Highest ${metric.name}`,
204 |           value: Math.max(...nums).toLocaleString(),
205 |           metric: metric.name,
206 |           aggregation: "max",
207 |         }
208 |       );
209 |     }
210 |   }
211 | 
212 |   const chartRecommendations = [];
213 | 
214 |   if (dimension) {
215 |     const counts = new Map();
216 | 
217 |     for (const row of rows) {
218 |       const label = String(row[dimension.name] ?? "Unknown").trim() || "Unknown";
219 |       counts.set(label, (counts.get(label) || 0) + 1);
220 |     }
221 | 
222 |     chartRecommendations.push({
223 |       id: randomUUID(),
224 |       type: "bar",
225 |       title: `Rows by ${dimension.name}`,
226 |       xKey: dimension.name,
227 |       yKey: "count",
228 |       aggregation: "count",
229 |       data: [...counts.entries()]
230 |         .map(([label, count]) => ({
231 |           [dimension.name]: label,
232 |           count,
233 |         }))
234 |         .sort((a, b) => b.count - a.count)
235 |         .slice(0, 10),
236 |     });
237 |   }
238 | 
239 |   return {
240 |     dataType: "qr_upload_dataset",
241 |     dataTypeLabel: "QR Uploaded Dataset",
242 |     kpis,
243 |     chartRecommendations,
244 |     insights: [
245 |       {
246 |         type: "summary",
247 |         title: "Dataset Uploaded",
248 |         message: `Uploaded ${rows.length.toLocaleString()} rows and ${columns.length.toLocaleString()} columns from QR/mobile upload.`,
249 |       },
250 |     ],
251 |     privacy: {
252 |       schemaOnly: true,
253 |       dashboardValuesCalculatedLocally: true,
254 |     },
255 |   };
256 | }
257 | 
258 | export async function handleQrUploadRoutes(request, response, pathname) {
259 |   const { method } = request;
260 |   const url = getRequestUrl(request);
261 | 
262 |   if (pathname === "/api/qr-upload/generate" && method === "POST") {
263 |     try {
264 |       const body = await readJsonBody(request);
265 | 
266 |       const portalBaseUrl =
267 |         body.portalBaseUrl ||
268 |         process.env.FRONTEND_PUBLIC_URL ||
269 |         request.headers.origin ||
270 |         `http://${request.headers.host}`;
271 | 
272 |       const session = createQrUploadSession({
273 |         portalBaseUrl,
274 |         workspaceName: body.workspaceName || "InsightFlow Workspace",
275 |       });
276 | 
277 |       const qrDataUrl = await QRCode.toDataURL(session.uploadUrl, {
278 |         margin: 1,
279 |         width: 320,
280 |         color: {
281 |           dark: "#111827",
282 |           light: "#ffffff",
283 |         },
284 |       });
285 | 
286 |       sendSuccess(
287 |         response,
288 |         {
289 |           sessionId: session.sessionId,
290 |           uploadToken: [REDACTED_SECRET],
291 |           uploadUrl: session.uploadUrl,
292 |           qrDataUrl,
293 |           workspaceName: session.workspaceName,
294 |           status: session.status,
295 |           expiresAt: session.expiresAt,
296 |         },
297 |         "QR upload session generated"
298 |       );
299 | 
300 |       return true;
301 |     } catch (error) {
302 |       sendError(
303 |         response,
304 |         HTTP_STATUS.INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR,
305 |         error.message || "Failed to generate QR session",
306 |         ERROR_CODES.INTERNAL_ERROR
307 |       );
308 | 
309 |       return true;
310 |     }
311 |   }
312 | 
313 |   const statusMatch = pathname.match(/^\/api\/qr-upload\/([^/]+)\/status$/);
314 | 
315 |   if (statusMatch && method === "GET") {
316 |     try {
317 |       const [, sessionId] = statusMatch;
318 |       const token = url.searchParams.get("token");
319 |       const session = verifyQrUploadSession(sessionId, token);
320 | 
321 |       sendSuccess(
322 |         response,
323 |         {
324 |           sessionId: session.sessionId,
325 |           status: session.status,
326 |           workspaceName: session.workspaceName,
327 |           files: session.files,
328 |           dataset: session.dataset,
329 |           analysis: session.analysis,
330 |           error: session.error,
331 |           expiresAt: session.expiresAt,
332 |         },
333 |         "QR session status"
334 |       );
335 | 
336 |       return true;
337 |     } catch (error) {
338 |       sendError(
339 |         response,
340 |         HTTP_STATUS.BAD_REQUEST,
341 |         error.message || "QR session status failed",
342 |         ERROR_CODES.VALIDATION_ERROR
343 |       );
344 | 
345 |       return true;
346 |     }
347 |   }
348 | 
349 |   const uploadMatch = pathname.match(/^\/api\/qr-upload\/([^/]+)\/upload$/);
350 | 
351 |   if (uploadMatch && method === "GET") {
352 |     const [, sessionId] = uploadMatch;
353 |     const token = url.searchParams.get("token");
354 |     const session = getQrUploadSession(sessionId);
355 | 
356 |     sendRedirect(
357 |       response,
358 |       session?.uploadUrl || buildMobilePortalUrl(request, sessionId, token),
359 |       302
360 |     );
361 | 
362 |     return true;
363 |   }
364 | 
365 |   if (uploadMatch && method === "POST") {
366 |     const [, sessionId] = uploadMatch;
367 |     const token = url.searchParams.get("token");
368 | 
369 |     try {
370 |       verifyQrUploadSession(sessionId, token);
371 | 
372 |       updateQrUploadSession(sessionId, {
373 |         status: "uploading",
374 |       });
375 | 
376 |       const { files } = await parseMultipartFiles(request);
377 | 
378 |       const parsedFiles = files.map((file) => {
379 |         const parsed = parseDatasetFile({
380 |           filename: file.filename,
381 |           buffer: file.buffer,
382 |         });
383 | 
384 |         return {
385 |           ...parsed,
386 |           sizeBytes: file.buffer.length,
387 |           status: "uploaded",
388 |         };
389 |       });
390 | 
391 |       const merged = mergeParsedDatasets(parsedFiles);
392 | 
393 |       const savedResult = createDataset({
394 |         name: merged.name,
395 |         fileName: merged.fileName,
396 |         sourceType: merged.sourceType,
397 |         columns: merged.columns,
398 |         rows: merged.rows,
399 |       });
400 | 
401 |       const dataset =
402 |         savedResult?.dataset ||
403 |         savedResult || {
404 |           id: randomUUID(),
405 |           ...merged,
406 |           uploadedAt: new Date().toISOString(),
407 |         };
408 | 
409 |       const analysis = buildBasicAnalysis(dataset);
410 | 
411 |       try {
412 |         updateDataset(dataset);
413 |         updateAnalysis(analysis);
414 |       } catch {
415 |         // keep response working
416 |       }
417 | 
418 |       const updated = updateQrUploadSession(sessionId, {
419 |         status: "completed",
420 |         files: parsedFiles.map((file) => ({
421 |           name: file.fileName,
422 |           rowCount: file.rowCount,
423 |           columnCount: file.columnCount,
424 |           sizeBytes: file.sizeBytes,
425 |           status: "uploaded",
426 |         })),
427 |         dataset,
428 |         analysis,
429 |       });
430 | 
431 |       sendSuccess(
432 |         response,
433 |         {
434 |           sessionId,
435 |           status: updated.status,
436 |           workspaceName: updated.workspaceName,
437 |           expiresAt: updated.expiresAt,
438 |           files: updated.files,
439 |           dataset,
440 |           analysis,
441 |         },
442 |         "Files uploaded and dataset synced"
443 |       );
444 | 
445 |       return true;
446 |     } catch (error) {
447 |       updateQrUploadSession(sessionId, {
448 |         status: "error",
449 |         error: error.message,
450 |       });
451 | 
452 |       sendError(
453 |         response,
454 |         HTTP_STATUS.BAD_REQUEST,
455 |         error.message || "QR upload failed",
456 |         ERROR_CODES.VALIDATION_ERROR
457 |       );
458 | 
459 |       return true;
460 |     }
461 |   }
462 | 
463 |   return false;
464 | }
465 | 
466 | export default {
467 |   handleQrUploadRoutes,
468 | };
469 | 
```

```js
 25 | async function readJsonBody(request) {
 26 |   try {
 27 |     if (request.body && typeof request.body === "object" && !request.readable) return request.body;
 28 |     let raw = "";
 29 |     for await (const chunk of request) raw += chunk;
 30 |     return raw ? JSON.parse(raw) : {};
 31 |   } catch {
 32 |     return {};
 33 |   }
 34 | }
 35 | 
 36 | function sendJson(response, statusCode, payload) {
 37 |   response.statusCode = statusCode;
 38 |   response.setHeader("Content-Type", "application/json; charset=utf-8");
 39 |   response.end(JSON.stringify(payload));
 40 | }
 41 | 
 42 | function ok(response, data, message = "OK") {
 43 |   sendJson(response, 200, { success: true, data, message });
 44 | }
 45 | 
 46 | function fail(response, statusCode, message, details) {
 47 |   sendJson(response, statusCode, { success: false, error: { message, details } });
 48 | }
 49 | 
 50 | async function tryCall(fn) {
 51 |   try { return await fn(); } catch { return null; }
 52 | }
 53 | 
 54 | async function loadDatasetById(datasetId, body = {}) {
 55 |   if (body.dataset?.rows) return body.dataset;
 56 |   if (Array.isArray(body.rows)) return { id: datasetId, name: body.name || datasetId, columns: body.columns || [], rows: body.rows, dictionaryRows: body.dictionaryRows || [] };
 57 | 
 58 |   const repo = await tryCall(() => import("../database/dataset-repository.js"));
 59 |   const localDb = await tryCall(() => import("../services/local-database-service.js"));
 60 | 
 61 |   const candidates = [
 62 |     repo?.getDatasetById,
 63 |     repo?.getDataset,
 64 |     repo?.datasetRepository?.getById,
 65 |     repo?.default?.getDatasetById,
 66 |     repo?.default?.getById,
 67 |     localDb?.getDatasetById,
 68 |     localDb?.getDataset,
 69 |     localDb?.default?.getDatasetById,
 70 |   ].filter(Boolean);
 71 | 
 72 |   for (const candidate of candidates) {
 73 |     const dataset = await tryCall(() => candidate(datasetId));
 74 |     if (dataset?.rows) return dataset;
 75 |     if (dataset?.data?.rows) return dataset.data;
 76 |   }
 77 | 
 78 |   return null;
 79 | }
 80 | 
 81 | export async function handleSchemaTrainedAIRoutes(request, response, pathname) {
 82 |   const method = request.method;
 83 | 
 84 |   if (method === "GET" && pathname === "/api/ai/schema-training-memory") {
 85 |     ok(response, { stats: getMemoryStats(), memory: readSchemaTrainingMemory() }, "Schema training memory loaded");
 86 |     return true;
 87 |   }
 88 | 
 89 |   if (method === "POST" && pathname === "/api/ai/schema-training/train-memory") {
 90 |     try {
 91 |       const body = await readJsonBody(request);
 92 |       const datasets = Array.isArray(body.datasets) ? body.datasets : [];
 93 |       if (!datasets.length) return fail(response, 400, "datasets[] is required");
 94 |       const trained = trainManySchemaExamples(datasets.map((dataset) => ({ dataset, dashboardPlan: dataset.dashboardPlan, rating: dataset.rating || "good", source: "bulk-api" })));
 95 |       ok(response, { trained, stats: getMemoryStats() }, "Schema memory trained");
 96 |       return true;
 97 |     } catch (error) {
 98 |       fail(response, 500, error.message || "Training failed");
 99 |       return true;
100 |     }
101 |   }
102 | 
103 |   if (method === "GET" && pathname === "/api/ai/schema-rag-memory") {
104 |     ok(
105 |       response,
106 |       {
107 |         stats: getSchemaRagStats(),
108 |         memory: readSchemaRagMemory().map((item) => {
109 |           const { embedding, ...safe } = item;
110 |           return safe;
111 |         }),
112 |       },
113 |       "Schema RAG memory loaded"
114 |     );
115 |     return true;
116 |   }
117 | 
118 |   if (method === "POST" && pathname === "/api/ai/schema-rag/train") {
119 |     try {
120 |       const body = await readJsonBody(request);
121 |       const dataset = body.dataset;
122 | 
123 |       if (!dataset?.rows && !body.schemaProfile?.columns) {
124 |         return fail(response, 400, "dataset.rows or schemaProfile.columns is required");
125 |       }
126 | 
127 |       const result = await trainSchemaRagMemoryFromDataset({
128 |         dataset,

130 |         acceptedDashboardPlan:
131 |           body.acceptedDashboardPlan || body.dashboardPlan || body.currentDashboard,

133 |         notes: body.notes || "",
134 |         source: body.source || "api",
135 |         useOllama: body.useOllama !== false,
136 |       });
137 | 
138 |       ok(response, result, "Schema RAG memory trained");
139 |       return true;
140 |     } catch (error) {
141 |       fail(response, 500, error.message || "Schema RAG training failed");
142 |       return true;
143 |     }
144 |   }
145 | 
146 |   if (method === "POST" && pathname === "/api/ai/schema-rag/retrieve") {
147 |     try {
148 |       const body = await readJsonBody(request);
149 |       const schemaProfile = body.schemaProfile?.columns
150 |         ? body.schemaProfile
151 |         : buildSchemaProfile(body.dataset || { rows: body.rows || [], columns: body.columns || [] });
152 |

157 |       });

265 |       const result = await generateSchemaDashboard(dataset, {

286 |       const entry = trainSchemaDashboard(dataset, {
287 |         dashboardPlan: body.dashboardPlan || body.acceptedDashboard,
288 |         rating: body.rating || "good",
289 |         notes: body.notes || "",
290 |         source: "user-feedback",
291 |       });
292 |       ok(response, { entry, stats: getMemoryStats() }, "Schema pattern trained");
293 |       return true;
294 |     } catch (error) {
295 |       fail(response, 500, error.message || "Schema training failed");
296 |       return true;
297 |     }
298 |   }
299 | 
300 |   const commandMatch = pathname.match(/^\/api\/datasets\/([^/]+)\/dashboard-command$/);
301 |   if (method === "POST" && commandMatch) {
302 |     try {
303 |       const body = await readJsonBody(request);
304 |       const dataset = await loadDatasetById(commandMatch[1], body);
305 |       if (!dataset) return fail(response, 404, "Dataset not found");
306 |       const query = String(body.query || "").trim();
307 |       if (!query) return fail(response, 400, "query is required");
308 | 
309 |       if (
310 |         /fix|validate|correct|wrong|repair|regenerate/i.test(query) &&
311 |         /dashboard|chart|charts|kpi|broken|invalid|wrong/i.test(query)
312 |       ) {
313 |         const result = validateAndFixDashboard(dataset, body.currentDashboard || {});
314 |         ok(response, {
315 |           action: "FIX_DASHBOARD",
316 |           message: result.message,
317 |           issues: result.issues,
318 |           observations: result.observations,
319 |           correctedDashboard: result.correctedDashboard,
320 |           schemaOnly: true,
321 |           provider: "local-integrity-engine",
322 |           model: "local-calculation",
323 |         }, "Dashboard fixed");
324 |         return true;
325 |       }
326 | 
327 |       const result = await runDashboardCommand({ dataset, query, currentDashboard: body.currentDashboard || {}, useLlm: body.useLlm !== false });
328 |       ok(response, result, "Dashboard command processed");
329 |       return true;
330 |     } catch (error) {
331 |       fail(response, 500, error.message || "Dashboard command failed");
332 |       return true;
333 |     }
334 |   }
335 | 
336 |   const chatMatch = pathname.match(/^\/api\/datasets\/([^/]+)\/schema-chat$/);
337 |   if (method === "POST" && chatMatch) {
338 |     try {
339 |       const body = await readJsonBody(request);
340 |       const dataset = await loadDatasetById(chatMatch[1], body);
341 |       if (!dataset) return fail(response, 404, "Dataset not found");
342 |       const query = String(body.query || body.message || "").trim();
343 |       if (!query) return fail(response, 400, "query is required");
344 |       const result = await runSchemaChat({ dataset, query, useLlm: body.useLlm !== false });
345 |       ok(response, {
346 |         userMessage: { role: "user", content: query, timestamp: new Date().toISOString() },
347 |         assistantMessage: { role: "assistant", content: result.answer, model: result.model, provider: result.provider, schemaOnly: true, timestamp: new Date().toISOString() },
348 |       }, "AI chat response generated");
349 |       return true;
350 |     } catch (error) {
351 |       fail(response, 500, error.message || "AI chat failed");
352 |       return true;
353 |     }
354 |   }
355 | 
356 |   return false;
357 | }
358 | 
359 | export default { handleSchemaTrainedAIRoutes };
360 | 
```

### FILE: apps/backend/src/routes/state.js

Source path: `apps/backend/src/routes/state.js`

```js
  1 | // Application state endpoint
  2 | import { sendSuccess, sendError } from '../utils/response-utils.js';
  3 | import { HTTP_STATUS } from '../config/constants.js';
  4 | import { getChatMessages, getCurrentDataset } from '../database/dataset-repository.js';
  5 | 
  6 | // In-memory UI state. Datasets and chat history hydrate from SQLite; analysis
  7 | // remains in memory until a persisted analysis store exists.
  8 | let appState = {
  9 |   dataset: null,
 10 |   chatMessages: [],
 11 |   analysis: null
 12 | };
 13 | 
 14 | export async function handleStateRoutes(request, response, pathname) {
 15 |   const { method } = request;
 16 | 
 17 |   // GET /api/state - Get current application state
 18 |   if (pathname === '/api/state' && method === 'GET') {
 19 |     try {
 20 |       if (!appState.dataset) {
 21 |         appState.dataset = getCurrentDataset();
 22 |       }
 23 | 
 24 |       const persistedDataset = getCurrentDataset();
 25 |       if (persistedDataset) {
 26 |         appState.dataset = persistedDataset;
 27 |         appState.chatMessages = getChatMessages(persistedDataset.id);
 28 |       }
 29 | 
 30 |       sendSuccess(response, {
 31 |         dataset: appState.dataset,
 32 |         chatMessages: appState.chatMessages,
 33 |         analysis: appState.analysis
 34 |       }, 'Application state retrieved');
 35 |       return true;
 36 |     } catch (error) {
 37 |       console.error('Error getting state:', error);
 38 |       sendError(response, HTTP_STATUS.INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR, 'Failed to get state', 'STATE_ERROR');
 39 |       return true;
 40 |     }
 41 |   }
 42 | 
 43 |   // POST /api/state/reset - Reset application state
 44 |   if (pathname === '/api/state/reset' && method === 'POST') {
 45 |     try {
 46 |       appState = {
 47 |         dataset: null,
 48 |         chatMessages: [],
 49 |         analysis: null
 50 |       };
 51 |       
 52 |       sendSuccess(response, appState, 'Application state reset');
 53 |       return true;
 54 |     } catch (error) {
 55 |       console.error('Error resetting state:', error);
 56 |       sendError(response, HTTP_STATUS.INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR, 'Failed to reset state', 'STATE_ERROR');
 57 |       return true;
 58 |     }
 59 |   }
 60 | 
 61 |   // PUT /api/state - Update application state
 62 |   if (pathname === '/api/state' && method === 'PUT') {
 63 |     try {
 64 |       let body = '';
 65 |       
 66 |       for await (const chunk of request) {
 67 |         body += chunk.toString();
 68 |       }
 69 |       
 70 |       const updates = JSON.parse(body);
 71 |       
 72 |       // Update state with provided values
 73 |       if (updates.dataset !== undefined) {
 74 |         appState.dataset = updates.dataset;
 75 |       }
 76 |       if (updates.chatMessages !== undefined) {
 77 |         appState.chatMessages = updates.chatMessages;
 78 |       }
 79 |       if (updates.analysis !== undefined) {
 80 |         appState.analysis = updates.analysis;
 81 |       }
 82 |       
 83 |       sendSuccess(response, appState, 'Application state updated');
 84 |       return true;
 85 |     } catch (error) {
 86 |       console.error('Error updating state:', error);
 87 |       sendError(response, HTTP_STATUS.INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR, 'Failed to update state', 'STATE_ERROR');
 88 |       return true;
 89 |     }
 90 |   }
 91 | 
 92 |   return false;
 93 | }
 94 | 
 95 | // Export state manager for use in other routes
 96 | export function getState() {
 97 |   return appState;
 98 | }
 99 | 
100 | export function setState(newState) {
101 |   appState = { ...appState, ...newState };
102 | }
103 | 
104 | export function updateDataset(dataset) {
105 |   appState.dataset = dataset;
106 | }
107 | 
108 | export function updateChatMessages(messages) {
109 |   appState.chatMessages = messages;
110 | }
111 | 
112 | export function updateAnalysis(analysis) {
113 |   appState.analysis = analysis;
114 | }
115 | 
116 | export function addChatMessage(message) {
117 |   appState.chatMessages.push(message);
118 | }
119 | 
120 | export function clearChat() {
121 |   appState.chatMessages = [];
122 | }
123 | 
124 | export default {
125 |   handleStateRoutes,
126 |   getState,
127 |   setState,
128 |   updateDataset,
129 |   updateChatMessages,
130 |   updateAnalysis,
131 |   addChatMessage,
132 |   clearChat
133 | };
134 | 
```

```js
 1 | import { describe, it, expect } from 'vitest';

 3 |   buildSchemaPacket,

 7 | } from '../schema-packet-builder.js';

 9 | describe('Schema Packet Builder', () => {
10 |   const mockDataset = {
11 |     name: 'Test Dataset',
12 |     rowCount: 100,
13 |     columns: [
14 |       { name: 'age', type: 'number' },
15 |       { name: 'category', type: 'string' },
16 |     ],
17 |     rows: Array.from({ length: 100 }, (_, i) => ({
18 |       age: Math.floor(Math.random() * 60) + 20,
19 |       category: ['A', 'B', 'C'][Math.floor(Math.random() * 3)],
20 |     })),
21 |   };
22 | 
23 |   it('builds valid schema packet', () => {
24 |     const packet = buildSchemaPacket(mockDataset);
25 |     expect(packet).toBeDefined();
26 |     expect(packet.name).toBe('Test Dataset');
27 |     expect(packet.rowCount).toBe(100);
28 |     expect(packet.columns.length).toBe(2);
29 |   });
30 | 
31 |   it('extracts numeric statistics', () => {
32 |     const packet = buildSchemaPacket(mockDataset);
33 |     const ageColumn = packet.columns.find(c => c.name === 'age');
34 |     expect(ageColumn.type).toBe('numeric');
35 |     expect(ageColumn.isValid).toBe(true);
36 |     expect(ageColumn.min).toBeLessThan(ageColumn.max);
37 |     expect(ageColumn.mean).toBeGreaterThan(0);
38 |   });
39 | 
40 |   it('extracts categorical statistics', () => {
41 |     const packet = buildSchemaPacket(mockDataset);
42 |     const catColumn = packet.columns.find(c => c.name === 'category');
43 |     expect(catColumn.type).toBe('categorical');
44 |     expect(catColumn.isValid).toBe(true);
45 |     expect(Object.keys(catColumn.topValues).length).toBeGreaterThan(0);
46 |   });
47 | 
48 |   it('handles empty datasets', () => {
49 |     expect(() => {
50 |       buildSchemaPacket({ ...mockDataset, rows: [] });
51 |     }).toThrow();
52 |   });
53 | 
54 |   it('validates columns exist', () => {
55 |     const packet = buildSchemaPacket(mockDataset);
56 |     expect(() => {
57 |       validateColumnsExist(['age', 'category'], packet);
58 |     }).not.toThrow();
59 |   });
60 | 
61 |   it('rejects invalid columns', () => {

63 |     expect(() => {
64 |       validateColumnsExist(['age', 'invalid_column'], packet);
65 |     }).toThrow();
66 |   });
67 | 
68 |   it('calculates data quality score', () => {
69 |     const packet = buildSchemaPacket(mockDataset);
70 |     const score = getDataQualityScore(packet);
71 |     expect(score).toBeGreaterThanOrEqual(0);
72 |     expect(score).toBeLessThanOrEqual(100);
73 |   });
74 |

81 |   });
```

### FILE: apps/backend/src/services/ai-analyst/ai-analyst-orchestrator.js

Source path: `apps/backend/src/services/ai-analyst/ai-analyst-orchestrator.js`

```js
  1 | import { randomUUID } from "node:crypto";
  2 | import { buildSchemaProfile } from "./schema-profiler.js";
  3 | import { buildAutoKpis } from "./kpi-engine.js";
  4 | import { buildAutoCharts, buildChartFromCommand } from "./chart-engine.js";
  5 | import { detectBestPlaybook } from "./analyst-playbooks.js";
  6 | import { findMemoryMatch, saveAnalystMemory } from "./analyst-memory.js";
  7 | import { routeAnalystCommand } from "./command-router.js";
  8 | 
  9 | function cleanRows(rows = []) {
 10 |   return rows.filter((row) => {
 11 |     const keys = Object.keys(row || {}).map((key) => key.toLowerCase());
 12 |     const source = String(row?._sourceFile || row?.source || "").toLowerCase();
 13 | 
 14 |     const looksLikeDictionary =
 15 |       keys.includes("column") &&
 16 |       keys.includes("type") &&
 17 |       keys.includes("description");
 18 | 
 19 |     return !source.includes("dictionary") && !looksLikeDictionary;
 20 |   });
 21 | }
 22 | 
 23 | export async function runFullAutoAnalysis(dataset = {}) {
 24 |   const rows = cleanRows(dataset.rows || []);
 25 | 
 26 |   const cleanedDataset = {
 27 |     ...dataset,
 28 |     rows,
 29 |     rowCount: rows.length,
 30 |   };
 31 | 
 32 |   const schema = buildSchemaProfile(cleanedDataset);
 33 |   const playbook = detectBestPlaybook(schema);
 34 |   const memoryMatch = findMemoryMatch({
 35 |     domain: playbook.domain,
 36 |     columnSignature: schema.columnSignature,
 37 |   });
 38 | 
 39 |   const kpis = buildAutoKpis({
 40 |     dataset: cleanedDataset,
 41 |     schema,
 42 |     playbook,
 43 |     memoryMatch,
 44 |   });
 45 | 
 46 |   const charts = buildAutoCharts({
 47 |     dataset: cleanedDataset,
 48 |     schema,
 49 |     playbook,
 50 |     memoryMatch,
 51 |   });
 52 | 
 53 |   const analysis = {
 54 |     id: randomUUID(),
 55 |     dataType: playbook.domain,
 56 |     dataTypeLabel: playbook.label,
 57 |     rowCount: cleanedDataset.rowCount,
 58 |     columnCount: schema.columns.length,
 59 |     kpis,
 60 |     chartRecommendations: charts,
 61 |     insights: [
 62 |       {
 63 |         type: "summary",
 64 |         title: "AI Analyst Summary",
 65 |         message: `Detected ${playbook.label}. Generated ${kpis.length} KPIs and ${charts.length} dashboard charts automatically.`,
 66 |       },
 67 |       {
 68 |         type: "privacy",
 69 |         title: "Schema-only AI Mode",
 70 |         message: "Raw rows are not sent to the LLM. KPI and chart values are calculated locally by the analytics engine.",
 71 |       },
 72 |     ],
 73 |     schemaPacket: schema.safePacket,
 74 |     memory: {
 75 |       usedPreviousPattern: Boolean(memoryMatch),
 76 |       similarity: memoryMatch?.similarity || 0,
 77 |       domain: playbook.domain,
 78 |       columnSignature: schema.columnSignature,
 79 |     },
 80 |     privacy: {
 81 |       schemaOnly: true,
 82 |       rawRowsSentToAI: false,
 83 |     },
 84 |   };
 85 | 
 86 |   saveAnalystMemory({
 87 |     domain: playbook.domain,
 88 |     columnSignature: schema.columnSignature,
 89 |     kpis,
 90 |     charts,
 91 |   });
 92 | 
 93 |   return analysis;
 94 | }
 95 | 
 96 | export async function runAnalystCommand({
 97 |   dataset,
 98 |   currentAnalysis,
 99 |   command,
100 |   filters = {},
101 | }) {
102 |   const rows = cleanRows(dataset.rows || []);
103 |   const cleanedDataset = {
104 |     ...dataset,
105 |     rows,
106 |     rowCount: rows.length,
107 |   };
108 | 
109 |   const schema = buildSchemaProfile(cleanedDataset);
110 | 
111 |   const routed = await routeAnalystCommand({
112 |     schema,
113 |     command,
114 |   });
115 | 
116 |   if (routed.action === "CLEAR_FILTERS") {
117 |     return {
118 |       action: "CLEAR_FILTERS",
119 |       message: "All filters cleared.",
120 |     };
121 |   }
122 | 
123 |   if (routed.action === "FILTER") {
124 |     return {
125 |       action: "FILTER",
126 |       message: routed.message || "Filter applied.",
127 |       filters: routed.filters || {},
128 |     };
129 |   }
130 | 
131 |   if (routed.action === "DELETE_CHART") {
132 |     return {
133 |       action: "DELETE_CHART",
134 |       message: "Removed the selected chart.",
135 |     };
136 |   }
137 | 
138 |   if (routed.action === "ADD_KPI") {
139 |     const kpis = buildAutoKpis({
140 |       dataset: cleanedDataset,
141 |       schema,
142 |       playbook: detectBestPlaybook(schema),
143 |     });
144 | 
145 |     return {
146 |       action: "ADD_KPI",
147 |       message: "Added KPI summary.",
148 |       kpis,
149 |     };
150 |   }
151 | 
152 |   if (routed.action === "GENERATE_CHART" || routed.action === "MODIFY_CHART") {
153 |     const chart = buildChartFromCommand({
154 |       dataset: cleanedDataset,
155 |       schema,
156 |       chartSpec: routed.chartSpec,
157 |       filters,
158 |     });
159 | 
160 |     if (!chart) {
161 |       return {
162 |         action: "ANSWER",
163 |         message: "I could not create a valid chart from this schema.",
164 |       };
165 |     }
166 | 
167 |     return {
168 |       action: routed.action,
169 |       message: routed.message || `Created chart: ${chart.title}`,
170 |       chart,
171 |     };
172 |   }
173 | 
174 |   return {
175 |     action: "ANSWER",
176 |     message:
177 |       routed.message ||
178 |       "I analyzed the schema, but this command does not require a dashboard change.",
179 |   };
180 | }
181 | 
```

### FILE: apps/backend/src/services/ai-analyst/analyst-memory.js

Source path: `apps/backend/src/services/ai-analyst/analyst-memory.js`

```js
 1 | import fs from "node:fs";
 2 | import path from "node:path";
 3 | import { randomUUID } from "node:crypto";
 4 | 
 5 | const MEMORY_FILE = path.resolve("data", "ai-analyst-memory.json");
 6 | 
 7 | function ensureFile() {
 8 |   fs.mkdirSync(path.dirname(MEMORY_FILE), { recursive: true });
 9 | 
10 |   if (!fs.existsSync(MEMORY_FILE)) {
11 |     fs.writeFileSync(MEMORY_FILE, JSON.stringify([], null, 2));
12 |   }
13 | }
14 | 
15 | function readMemory() {
16 |   ensureFile();
17 | 
18 |   try {
19 |     return JSON.parse(fs.readFileSync(MEMORY_FILE, "utf8"));
20 |   } catch {
21 |     return [];
22 |   }
23 | }
24 | 
25 | function writeMemory(memory) {
26 |   ensureFile();
27 |   fs.writeFileSync(MEMORY_FILE, JSON.stringify(memory, null, 2));
28 | }
29 | 
30 | function similarity(a = "", b = "") {
31 |   const setA = new Set(a.split("|").filter(Boolean));
32 |   const setB = new Set(b.split("|").filter(Boolean));
33 | 
34 |   if (!setA.size || !setB.size) return 0;
35 | 
36 |   const intersection = [...setA].filter((item) => setB.has(item)).length;
37 |   const union = new Set([...setA, ...setB]).size;
38 | 
39 |   return intersection / union;
40 | }
41 | 
42 | export function findMemoryMatch({ domain, columnSignature }) {
43 |   const memory = readMemory();
44 | 
45 |   const best = memory
46 |     .filter((item) => item.domain === domain)
47 |     .map((item) => ({
48 |       ...item,
49 |       similarity: similarity(item.columnSignature, columnSignature),
50 |     }))
51 |     .sort((a, b) => b.similarity - a.similarity)[0];
52 | 
53 |   if (!best || best.similarity < 0.65) return null;
54 | 
55 |   return best;
56 | }
57 | 
58 | export function saveAnalystMemory({ domain, columnSignature, kpis, charts }) {
59 |   const memory = readMemory();
60 | 
61 |   const existing = memory.find(
62 |     (item) => item.domain === domain && item.columnSignature === columnSignature
63 |   );
64 | 
65 |   const item = {
66 |     id: existing?.id || randomUUID(),
67 |     domain,
68 |     columnSignature,
69 |     kpiTemplates: kpis.map((kpi) => ({
70 |       title: kpi.title,
71 |       metric: kpi.metric,
72 |       aggregation: kpi.aggregation,
73 |     })),
74 |     chartTemplates: charts.map((chart) => ({
75 |       title: chart.title,
76 |       type: chart.type,
77 |       xKey: chart.xKey,
78 |       yKey: chart.yKey,
79 |       aggregation: chart.aggregation,
80 |     })),
81 |     usageCount: existing ? existing.usageCount + 1 : 1,
82 |     createdAt: existing?.createdAt || new Date().toISOString(),
83 |     lastUsedAt: new Date().toISOString(),
84 |   };
85 | 
86 |   const next = existing
87 |     ? memory.map((entry) => (entry.id === existing.id ? item : entry))
88 |     : [...memory, item];
89 | 
90 |   writeMemory(next);
91 | 
92 |   return item;
93 | }
94 | 
```

```js
210 | 
211 | function normalize(value = "") {
212 |   return String(value).toLowerCase().replace(/[^a-z0-9]+/g, "_");
213 | }
214 | 
215 | export function detectBestPlaybook(schema) {
216 |   const names = schema.columns.map((column) => column.normalizedName).join(" ");
217 | 
218 |   const scored = ANALYST_PLAYBOOKS.map((playbook) => {
219 |     let score = 0;
220 | 
221 |     for (const keyword of playbook.keywords) {
222 |       if (names.includes(normalize(keyword))) score += 2;
223 |     }
224 | 
225 |     const hasDate = schema.columns.some((column) => column.role === "date");
226 |     const hasMetric = schema.columns.some((column) => column.role === "metric");
227 |     const hasDimension = schema.columns.some((column) => column.role === "dimension");
228 | 
229 |     if (playbook.domain === "time_series" && hasDate && hasMetric) score += 5;
230 |     if (playbook.domain === "generic" && hasMetric && hasDimension) score += 1;
231 | 
232 |     return { playbook, score };
233 |   }).sort((a, b) => b.score - a.score);
234 | 
235 |   return scored[0]?.score > 0
236 |     ? scored[0].playbook
237 |     : ANALYST_PLAYBOOKS.find((playbook) => playbook.domain === "generic");
238 | }
239 | 
```
