Source path: `apps/backend/src/services/ai-data-service.js`

```js
  1 | /**
  2 |  * AI-Powered Data Analysis Service
  3 |  * Provides intelligent data profiling, quality assessment, and recommendations
  4 |  */
  5 | 
  6 | import { callGeminiAI, isGeminiConfigured } from "./gemini-ai-service.js";
  7 | 
  8 | /**
  9 |  * Analyze uploaded data and provide AI-powered profiling
 10 |  */
 11 | export async function analyzeDatasetProfile(rows, columns) {
 12 |   const profile = {
 13 |     rowCount: rows.length,
 14 |     columnCount: columns.length,
 15 |     columns: [],
 16 |     dataQuality: {},
 17 |     recommendations: [],
 18 |     insights: [],
 19 |     suggestedAnalyses: [],
 20 |   };
 21 | 
 22 |   for (const col of columns) {
 23 |     const values = rows.map(r => r[col.name]).filter(v => v !== null && v !== undefined && v !== "");
 24 |     const numericValues = values
 25 |       .map(v => parseFloat(v))
 26 |       .filter(v => !isNaN(v));
 27 |     
 28 |     const colProfile = {
 29 |       name: col.name,
 30 |       type: col.type,
 31 |       populated: values.length,
 32 |       missing: rows.length - values.length,
 33 |       missingPercent: ((rows.length - values.length) / rows.length * 100).toFixed(1),
 34 |       unique: new Set(values).size,
 35 |     };
 36 | 
 37 |     if (col.type === "number" && numericValues.length > 0) {
 38 |       colProfile.stats = {
 39 |         min: Math.min(...numericValues),
 40 |         max: Math.max(...numericValues),
 41 |         mean: numericValues.reduce((a, b) => a + b, 0) / numericValues.length,
 42 |         median: getMedian(numericValues),
 43 |       };
 44 |     }
 45 | 
 46 |     profile.columns.push(colProfile);
 47 |   }
 48 | 
 49 |   profile.dataQuality = calculateDataQuality(rows, columns);
 50 |   profile.recommendations = generateRecommendations(profile.dataQuality, columns);
 51 |   profile.suggestedAnalyses = await generateSuggestedAnalyses(rows, columns, profile.dataQuality);
 52 | 
 53 |   if (isGeminiConfigured()) {
 54 |     try {
 55 |       const aiInsights = await generateAIInsights(rows, columns, profile);
 56 |       profile.insights = aiInsights;
 57 |     } catch (e) {
 58 |       console.warn("[ai-data] AI insights generation failed:", e.message);
 59 |     }
 60 |   }
 61 | 
 62 |   return profile;
 63 | }
 64 | 
 65 | function getMedian(values) {
 66 |   const sorted = [...values].sort((a, b) => a - b);
 67 |   const mid = Math.floor(sorted.length / 2);
 68 |   return sorted.length % 2 ? sorted[mid] : (sorted[mid - 1] + sorted[mid]) / 2;
 69 | }
 70 | 
 71 | function calculateDataQuality(rows, columns) {
 72 |   const quality = {
 73 |     score: 100,
 74 |     issues: [],
 75 |     completeness: {},
 76 |     consistency: {},
 77 |   };
 78 | 
 79 |   for (const col of columns) {
 80 |     const values = rows.map(r => r[col.name]);
 81 |     const emptyCount = values.filter(v => v === null || v === undefined || v === "").length;
 82 |     const completeness = ((values.length - emptyCount) / values.length * 100);
 83 |     
 84 |     quality.completeness[col.name] = completeness.toFixed(1);
 85 |     
 86 |     if (completeness < 100) {
 87 |       quality.score -= (100 - completeness) * 0.1;
 88 |     }
 89 |     if (completeness < 80) {
 90 |       quality.issues.push({
 91 |         column: col.name,
 92 |         type: "missing_values",
 93 |         severity: completeness < 50 ? "high" : "medium",
 94 |         message: `${col.name} has ${(100 - completeness).toFixed(1)}% missing values`,
 95 |       });
 96 |     }
 97 | 
 98 |     if (col.type === "number") {
 99 |       const numericValues = values.map(v => parseFloat(v)).filter(v => !isNaN(v));
100 |       if (numericValues.length > 0) {
101 |         const mean = numericValues.reduce((a, b) => a + b, 0) / numericValues.length;
102 |         const stdDev = Math.sqrt(numericValues.reduce((sum, v) => sum + Math.pow(v - mean, 2), 0) / numericValues.length);
103 |         const outliers = numericValues.filter(v => Math.abs(v - mean) > 3 * stdDev);
104 |         
105 |         if (outliers.length > 0) {
106 |           quality.issues.push({
107 |             column: col.name,
108 |             type: "outliers",
109 |             severity: outliers.length > numericValues.length * 0.1 ? "high" : "low",
110 |             message: `${col.name} has ${outliers.length} potential outliers`,
111 |           });
112 |           quality.score -= 5;
113 |         }
114 |       }
115 |     }
116 |   }
117 | 
118 |   quality.score = Math.max(0, Math.min(100, quality.score));
119 |   return quality;
120 | }
121 | 
122 | function generateRecommendations(quality, columns) {
123 |   const recommendations = [];
124 | 
125 |   if (quality.score < 80) {
126 |     recommendations.push({
127 |       type: "data_quality",
128 |       priority: "high",
129 |       message: `Data quality score is ${quality.score.toFixed(0)}%. Consider cleaning before analysis.`,
130 |     });
131 |   }
132 | 
133 |   const missingColumns = quality.issues.filter(i => i.type === "missing_values");
134 |   if (missingColumns.length > 0) {
135 |     recommendations.push({
136 |       type: "imputation",
137 |       priority: "medium",
138 |       message: `Consider imputing missing values in: ${missingColumns.map(i => i.column).join(", ")}`,
139 |     });
140 |   }
141 | 
142 |   const outlierColumns = quality.issues.filter(i => i.type === "outliers");
143 |   if (outlierColumns.length > 0) {
144 |     recommendations.push({
145 |       type: "outlier_analysis",
146 |       priority: "medium",
147 |       message: `Review outliers in: ${outlierColumns.map(i => i.column).join(", ")}`,
148 |     });
149 |   }
150 | 
151 |   const numericCols = columns.filter(c => c.type === "number");
152 |   const categoricalCols = columns.filter(c => c.type === "string");
153 |   
154 |   if (numericCols.length >= 2) {
155 |     recommendations.push({
156 |       type: "correlation_analysis",
157 |       priority: "low",
158 |       message: "You have multiple numeric columns - try correlation analysis to find relationships",
159 |     });
160 |   }
161 | 
162 |   if (categoricalCols.length >= 1 && numericCols.length >= 1) {
163 |     recommendations.push({
164 |       type: "segmentation",
165 |       priority: "low",
166 |       message: `Compare metrics across ${categoricalCols[0].name} to discover patterns`,
167 |     });
168 |   }
169 | 
170 |   return recommendations;
171 | }
172 | 
173 | async function generateSuggestedAnalyses(rows, columns, quality) {
174 |   const suggestions = [];
175 |   const numericCols = columns.filter(c => c.type === "number");
176 |   const categoricalCols = columns.filter(c => c.type === "string");
177 | 
178 |   for (const numCol of numericCols) {
179 |     suggestions.push({
180 |       type: "aggregation",
181 |       query: `What is the total and average ${numCol.name}?`,
182 |       priority: "high",
183 |     });
184 |   }
185 | 
186 |   for (const catCol of categoricalCols) {
187 |     if (new Set(rows.map(r => r[catCol.name])).size <= 10) {
188 |       const metricCol = numericCols[0];
189 |       suggestions.push({
190 |         type: "breakdown",
191 |         query: `Show ${metricCol?.name ? metricCol.name : 'distribution'} by ${catCol.name}`,
192 |         priority: "high",
193 |       });
194 |     }
195 |   }
196 | 
197 |   // When the dataset has quality issues but no numeric columns, still offer a
198 |   // useful starting point instead of returning an empty list.
199 |   if (suggestions.length === 0 && quality?.issues?.length) {
200 |     suggestions.push({
201 |       type: "data_quality",
202 |       query: `Show me the data quality issues for this dataset`,
203 |       priority: "high",
204 |     });
205 |   }
206 | 
207 |   for (let i = 0; i < numericCols.length; i++) {
208 |     for (let j = i + 1; j < numericCols.length; j++) {
209 |       suggestions.push({
210 |         type: "correlation",
211 |         query: `What is the correlation between ${numericCols[i].name} and ${numericCols[j].name}?`,
212 |         priority: "medium",
213 |       });
214 |     }
215 |   }
216 | 
217 |   if (isGeminiConfigured()) {
218 |     try {
219 |       const aiSuggestions = await callGeminiAI(
220 |         { name: "Dataset", columns, rowCount: rows.length },
221 |         "Based on this dataset schema, suggest 3-5 interesting analysis questions a user might want to ask. Return only the questions, one per line."
222 |       );
223 |       if (aiSuggestions.success && aiSuggestions.suggestions) {
224 |         aiSuggestions.suggestions.split("\n").forEach((q, idx) => {
225 |           if (q.trim() && idx < 3) {
226 |             suggestions.push({
227 |               type: "ai_suggested",
228 |               query: q.trim(),
229 |               priority: "medium",
230 |             });
231 |           }
232 |         });
233 |       }
234 |     } catch (e) {
235 |       console.warn("[ai-data] AI suggestion generation failed:", e.message);
236 |     }
237 |   }
238 | 
239 |   return suggestions.filter(Boolean).slice(0, 8);
240 | }
241 | 
242 | async function generateAIInsights(rows, columns, profile) {
243 |   const datasetInfo = {
244 |     name: "Dataset",
245 |     columns: columns.map(c => ({ name: c.name, type: c.type })),
246 |     rowCount: rows.length,
247 |     columnCount: columns.length,
248 |   };
249 | 
250 |   const prompt = `Analyze this dataset and provide 3-5 key insights. Focus on:
251 | 1. Interesting patterns or trends
252 | 2. Data distribution observations
253 | 3. Notable relationships between columns
254 | 4. Any anomalies or surprising values
255 | 
256 | Dataset: ${JSON.stringify(datasetInfo)}
257 | 
258 | Provide insights as a numbered list, each on a new line.`;
259 | 
260 |   try {
261 |     const response = await callGeminiAI(datasetInfo, prompt);
262 |     if (response.success && response.insight) {
263 |       return response.insight.split("\n").filter(l => l.trim());
264 |     }
265 |   } catch (e) {
266 |     console.warn("[ai-data] AI insight generation failed:", e.message);
267 |   }
268 |   return [];
269 | }
270 | 
271 | /**
272 |  * Detect anomalies in the dataset
273 |  */
274 | export async function detectAnomalies(rows, columns) {
275 |   const anomalies = {
276 |     statistical: [],
277 |     pattern: [],
278 |     dataQuality: [],
279 |   };
280 | 
281 |   for (const col of columns) {
282 |     if (col.type === "number") {
283 |       const values = rows
284 |         .map(r => parseFloat(r[col.name]))
285 |         .filter(v => !isNaN(v));
286 |       
287 |       if (values.length > 10) {
288 |         const mean = values.reduce((a, b) => a + b, 0) / values.length;
289 |         const stdDev = Math.sqrt(values.reduce((sum, v) => sum + Math.pow(v - mean, 2), 0) / values.length);
290 |         
291 |         const outliers = values.filter(v => Math.abs(v - mean) > 3 * stdDev);
292 |         
293 |         if (outliers.length > 0) {
294 |           anomalies.statistical.push({
295 |             column: col.name,
296 |             type: "outlier",
297 |             count: outliers.length,
298 |             percentage: (outliers.length / values.length * 100).toFixed(1),
299 |             message: `${col.name} has ${outliers.length} outliers (values > 3 standard deviations from mean)`,
300 |           });
301 |         }
302 |       }
303 |     }
304 | 
305 |     if (col.type === "string") {
306 |       const values = rows.map(r => r[col.name]);
307 |       const emptyCount = values.filter(v => !v || v === "").length;
308 |       if (emptyCount > values.length * 0.2) {
309 |         anomalies.dataQuality.push({
310 |           column: col.name,
311 |           type: "high_missing",
312 |           count: emptyCount,
313 |           message: `${col.name} has ${emptyCount} missing/empty values (${(emptyCount / values.length * 100).toFixed(1)}%)`,
314 |         });
315 |       }
316 |     }
317 |   }
318 | 
319 |   if (isGeminiConfigured()) {
320 |     try {
321 |       const aiAnomalies = await callGeminiAI(
322 |         { columns, rowCount: rows.length },
323 |         "Analyze this dataset for any unusual patterns, data quality issues, or anomalies. Return 2-3 sentences describing any issues found."
324 |       );
325 |       if (aiAnomalies.success && aiAnomalies.insight) {
326 |         anomalies.pattern.push({
327 |           type: "ai_detected",
328 |           message: aiAnomalies.insight,
329 |         });
330 |       }
331 |     } catch (e) {
332 |       console.warn("[ai-data] AI anomaly detection failed:", e.message);
333 |     }
334 |   }
335 | 
336 |   return anomalies;
337 | }

342 | export async function generateNarrative(datasetName, analysisResults) {
343 |   const narrative = {
344 |     title: `Analysis of ${datasetName}`,
345 |     summary: "",
346 |     sections: [],
347 |   };
348 |

350 |     narrative.sections.push({

355 |

367 |     });

375 |       );

387 | /**
388 |  * Map column relationships using AI
389 |  */
390 | export async function mapColumnRelationships(rows, columns) {
391 |   const relationships = {
392 |     correlated: [],
393 |     categorical: [],
394 |     temporal: [],
395 |     inferred: [],
396 |   };
397 | 
398 |   const numericCols = columns.filter(c => c.type === "number");
399 |   
400 |   for (let i = 0; i < numericCols.length; i++) {
401 |     for (let j = i + 1; j < numericCols.length; j++) {
402 |       const values1 = rows.map(r => parseFloat(r[numericCols[i].name])).filter(v => !isNaN(v));
403 |       const values2 = rows.map(r => parseFloat(r[numericCols[j].name])).filter(v => !isNaN(v));
404 |       
405 |       const minLen = Math.min(values1.length, values2.length);
406 |       if (minLen > 10) {
407 |         const correlation = calculatePearsonCorrelation(
408 |           values1.slice(0, minLen),
409 |           values2.slice(0, minLen)
410 |         );
411 |         
412 |         if (Math.abs(correlation) > 0.5) {
413 |           relationships.correlated.push({
414 |             columns: [numericCols[i].name, numericCols[j].name],
415 |             strength: Math.abs(correlation),
416 |             direction: correlation > 0 ? "positive" : "negative",
417 |             type: Math.abs(correlation) > 0.8 ? "strong" : "moderate",
418 |           });
419 |         }
420 |       }
421 |     }
422 |   }
423 | 
424 |   const categoricalCols = columns.filter(c => c.type === "string");
425 |   for (const col of categoricalCols) {
426 |     const uniqueValues = new Set(rows.map(r => r[col.name])).size;
427 |     if (uniqueValues <= 15) {
428 |       relationships.categorical.push({
429 |         column: col.name,
430 |         uniqueValues: uniqueValues,
431 |         suggestedFor: "grouping",
432 |       });
433 |     }
434 |   }
435 | 
436 |   const datePatterns = /date|month|year|day|quarter|time/i;
437 |   for (const col of columns) {
438 |     if (datePatterns.test(col.name)) {
439 |       relationships.temporal.push({
440 |         column: col.name,
441 |         suggestedFor: "time_series",
442 |       });
443 |     }
444 |   }
445 | 
446 |   if (isGeminiConfigured()) {
447 |     try {
448 |       const aiRelations = await callGeminiAI(
449 |         { columns: columns.map(c => c.name), rowCount: rows.length },
450 |         "Based on column names, suggest how these columns might relate to each other. Look for potential cause-effect, hierarchical, or transactional relationships."
451 |       );
452 |       if (aiRelations.success && aiRelations.intent) {
453 |         relationships.inferred.push({
454 |           type: "ai_suggested",
455 |           message: aiRelations.intent,
456 |         });
457 |       }
458 |     } catch (e) {
459 |       console.warn("[ai-data] AI relationship mapping failed:", e.message);
460 |     }
461 |   }
462 | 
463 |   return relationships;
464 | }
465 | 
466 | function calculatePearsonCorrelation(x, y) {
467 |   const n = x.length;
468 |   if (n !== y.length || n === 0) return 0;
469 |   
470 |   const sumX = x.reduce((a, b) => a + b, 0);
471 |   const sumY = y.reduce((a, b) => a + b, 0);
472 |   const sumXY = x.reduce((sum, xi, i) => sum + xi * y[i], 0);
473 |   const sumX2 = x.reduce((sum, xi) => sum + xi * xi, 0);
474 |   const sumY2 = y.reduce((sum, yi) => sum + yi * yi, 0);
475 |   
476 |   const numerator = n * sumXY - sumX * sumY;
477 |   const denominator = Math.sqrt((n * sumX2 - sumX * sumX) * (n * sumY2 - sumY * sumY));
478 |   
479 |   if (denominator === 0) return 0;
480 |   return numerator / denominator;
481 | }
482 | 
483 | /**
484 |  * Generate drill-down suggestions
485 |  */
486 | export function generateDrillDownSuggestions(schema, currentAnalysis) {
487 |   const suggestions = [];
488 |   
489 |   if (schema.dimensions && schema.dimensions.length > 1) {
490 |     suggestions.push({
491 |       type: "add_dimension",
492 |       message: `Try adding "${schema.dimensions[1]}" as a secondary dimension for more detailed analysis`,
493 |       query: `Show ${schema.metrics?.[0] || 'values'} by ${schema.dimensions[0]} and ${schema.dimensions[1]}`,
494 |     });
495 |   }
496 | 
497 |   if (schema.timeDimension) {
498 |     suggestions.push({
499 |       type: "time_analysis",
500 |       message: "Analyze trends over time",
501 |       query: `Show trend of ${schema.metrics?.[0] || 'values'} over ${schema.timeDimension}`,
502 |     });
503 |   }
504 | 
505 |   return suggestions;
506 | }
507 | 
508 | /**
509 |  * Suggest data cleaning operations
510 |  */
511 | export async function suggestDataCleaning(rows, columns) {
512 |   const suggestions = [];
513 | 
514 |   for (const col of columns) {
515 |     const values = rows.map(r => r[col.name]);
516 |     const emptyCount = values.filter(v => !v || v === "").length;
517 |     
518 |     if (emptyCount > 0 && emptyCount < values.length * 0.3) {
519 |       const nonEmpty = values.filter(v => v);
520 |       const mode = getMode(nonEmpty);
521 |       suggestions.push({
522 |         column: col.name,
523 |         operation: "impute_missing",
524 |         method: "mode",
525 |         value: mode,
526 |         affectedRows: emptyCount,
527 |         description: `Impute ${emptyCount} missing values with mode "${mode}"`,
528 |       });
529 |     }
530 | 
531 |     if (col.type === "string") {
532 |       const trimmed = values.map(v => typeof v === "string" ? v.trim() : v);
533 |       const hasWhitespace = values.some((v, i) => v !== trimmed[i]);
534 |       if (hasWhitespace) {
535 |         suggestions.push({
536 |           column: col.name,
537 |           operation: "trim_whitespace",
538 |           affectedRows: values.filter((v, i) => v !== trimmed[i]).length,
539 |           description: `Trim whitespace from ${col.name}`,
540 |         });
541 |       }
542 |     }
543 | 
544 |     if (col.type === "number") {
545 |       const numericValues = values.map(v => parseFloat(v)).filter(v => !isNaN(v));
546 |       if (numericValues.length > 0 && numericValues.some(v => v < 0)) {
547 |         const negativeCount = numericValues.filter(v => v < 0).length;
548 |         if (negativeCount > 0) {
549 |           suggestions.push({
550 |             column: col.name,
551 |             operation: "review_negatives",
552 |             affectedRows: negativeCount,
553 |             description: `Review ${negativeCount} negative values in ${col.name}`,
554 |           });
555 |         }
556 |       }
557 |     }
558 |   }
559 | 
560 |   return suggestions;
561 | }
562 | 
563 | function getMode(values) {
564 |   const frequency = {};
565 |   let maxFreq = 0;
566 |   let mode = values[0];
567 |   
568 |   for (const value of values) {
569 |     frequency[value] = (frequency[value] || 0) + 1;
570 |     if (frequency[value] > maxFreq) {
571 |       maxFreq = frequency[value];
572 |       mode = value;
573 |     }
574 |   }
575 |   
576 |   return mode;
577 | }
578 | 
```

Source path: `apps/backend/src/services/ai-providers/ai-router.js`

  9 |     this.providerStats = {
 10 |       ollama: { calls: 0, successes: 0, errors: 0 },

 18 |     return priority.split(',').map(p => p.trim().toLowerCase());

 21 |   async getAvailableProviders() {
 22 |     const providers = [];

 24 |     // Check Ollama availability
 25 |     if (this.providerPriority.includes('ollama')) {
 26 |       const ollamaAvailable = await ollamaAIService.isAvailable();
 27 |       if (ollamaAvailable) {
 28 |         providers.push({ name: 'ollama', service: ollamaAIService });
 29 |       }
 30 |     }
 31 | 
 32 |     // Check Gemini availability
 33 |     if (this.providerPriority.includes('gemini')) {
 34 |       const geminiAvailable = await geminiService.isAvailable();
 35 |       if (geminiAvailable) {
 36 |         providers.push({ name: 'gemini', service: geminiService });
 37 |       }
 38 |     }
 39 | 
 40 |     // Always include local NLP as fallback
 41 |     if (this.providerPriority.includes('local')) {
 42 |       const localAvailable = await localNLPService.isAvailable();
 43 |       if (localAvailable) {
 44 |         providers.push({ name: 'local', service: localNLPService });
 45 |       }
 46 |     }
 47 | 
 48 |     return providers;
 49 |   }
 50 | 
 51 |   async generateResponse(prompt, context = {}) {
 52 |     const availableProviders = await this.getAvailableProviders();
 53 |     
 54 |     if (availableProviders.length === 0) {
 55 |       return {
 56 |         success: false,
 57 |         error: 'No AI providers available',
 58 |         provider: 'none',
 59 |         content: 'I apologize, but no AI services are currently available. Please check your configuration.',
 60 |       };
 61 |     }
 62 | 
 63 |     // Try providers in priority order
 64 |     for (const { name, service } of availableProviders) {
 65 |       try {
 66 |         this.providerStats[name].calls++;
 67 |         
 68 |         const result = await service.generateResponse(prompt, context);
 69 |         
 70 |         if (result.success) {
 71 |           this.providerStats[name].successes++;
 72 |           this.lastUsedProvider = name;
 73 |           
 74 |           return {
 75 |             ...result,
 76 |             fallbackUsed: availableProviders.length > 1 && name !== availableProviders[0].name,
 77 |             availableProviders: availableProviders.map(p => p.name),
 78 |           };
 79 |         } else {
 80 |           this.providerStats[name].errors++;
 81 |           console.warn(`${name} provider failed:`, result.error);
 82 |           // Continue to next provider
 83 |         }
 84 |       } catch (error) {
 85 |         this.providerStats[name].errors++;
 86 |         console.error(`${name} provider error:`, error.message);
 87 |         // Continue to next provider
 88 |       }
 89 |     }
 90 | 
 91 |     // All providers failed
 92 |     return {
 93 |       success: false,
 94 |       error: 'All AI providers failed',
 95 |       provider: 'none',
 96 |       content: 'I apologize, but all AI services are currently experiencing issues. Please try again later.',
 97 |       providerStats: this.providerStats,
 98 |     };
 99 |   }
100 | 
101 |   async testAllProviders() {
102 |     const results = {};
103 |     
104 |     for (const providerName of this.providerPriority) {
105 |       let service;
106 |       
107 |       switch (providerName) {
108 |         case 'ollama':
109 |           service = ollamaAIService;
110 |           break;
111 |         case 'gemini':
112 |           service = geminiService;
113 |           break;
114 |         case 'local':
115 |           service = localNLPService;
116 |           break;
117 |         default:
118 |           continue;
119 |       }
120 |       
121 |       try {
122 |         results[providerName] = await service.testConnection();
123 |       } catch (error) {
124 |         results[providerName] = {
125 |           success: false,
126 |           error: error.message,
127 |         };
128 |       }
129 |     }
130 |     
131 |     return {
132 |       results,
133 |       priority: this.providerPriority,
134 |       lastUsed: this.lastUsedProvider,
135 |       stats: this.providerStats,
136 |     };
137 |   }
138 | 
139 |   getProviderStats() {
140 |     return {
141 |       priority: this.providerPriority,
142 |       lastUsed: this.lastUsedProvider,
143 |       stats: this.providerStats,
144 |     };
145 |   }
146 | 
147 |   resetStats() {
148 |     this.providerStats = {
149 |       ollama: { calls: 0, successes: 0, errors: 0 },
150 |       gemini: { calls: 0, successes: 0, errors: 0 },
151 |       local: { calls: 0, successes: 0, errors: 0 },
152 |     };
153 |   }
154 | 
155 |   async healthCheck() {
156 |     const availableProviders = await this.getAvailableProviders();
157 |     return {
158 |       status: availableProviders.length > 0 ? 'healthy' : 'unhealthy',
159 |       availableProviders: availableProviders.map(p => p.name),
160 |       configuredProviders: this.providerPriority,
161 |       lastUsedProvider: this.lastUsedProvider,
162 |     };
163 |   }
164 | }
165 | 
166 | export const aiRouter = new AIRouter();
167 | 
```

  1 | import { GoogleGenerativeAI } from '@google/generative-ai';
  2 | import { extractSchemaForAI, buildSchemaOnlyPrompt, validateSchemaOnlyContext } from '../../utils/schema-extractor.js';
  3 | 
  4 | class GeminiService {
  5 |   constructor() {
  6 |     this.apiKey = process.env.GEMINI_API_KEY?.trim() || process.env.GOOGLE_API_KEY?.trim() || '';
  7 |     this.timeout = parseInt(process.env.AI_TIMEOUT_MS) || 30000;
  8 |     this.maxTokens = parseInt(process.env.AI_MAX_TOKENS) || 2048;
  9 |     this.temperature = parseFloat(process.env.AI_TEMPERATURE) || 0.7;
 10 |     
 11 |     if (this.apiKey) {
 12 |       this.genAI = new GoogleGenerativeAI(this.apiKey);
 13 |       this.model = this.genAI.getGenerativeModel({ 
 14 |         model: 'gemini-1.5-flash',
 15 |         generationConfig: {
 16 |           maxOutputTokens: [REDACTED_SECRET],
 17 |           temperature: this.temperature,
 18 |         }
 19 |       });
 20 |     }
 21 |   }
 22 | 
 23 |   async isAvailable() {
 24 |     return !!(this.apiKey && this.model);
 25 |   }
 26 | 
 27 |   async generateResponse(prompt, context = {}) {
 28 |     try {
 29 |       if (!this.isAvailable()) {
 30 |         return {
 31 |           success: false,
 32 |           error: 'Gemini API key not configured',
 33 |           provider: 'gemini',
 34 |         };
 35 |       }
 36 | 
 37 |       const systemPrompt = this.buildSystemPrompt(context);
 38 |       const fullPrompt = `${systemPrompt}\n\nUser: ${prompt}\nAssistant:`;
 39 | 
 40 |       const result = await Promise.race([
 41 |         this.model.generateContent(fullPrompt),
 42 |         new Promise((_, reject) => 
 43 |           setTimeout(() => reject(new Error('Request timeout')), this.timeout)
 44 |         )
 45 |       ]);
 46 | 
 47 |       const response = await result.response;
 48 |       const content = response.text();
 49 | 
 50 |       return {
 51 |         success: true,
 52 |         content: content?.trim() || '',
 53 |         provider: 'gemini',
 54 |         model: 'gemini-1.5-flash',
 55 |       };
 56 |     } catch (error) {
 57 |       console.error('Gemini API Error:', error.message);
 58 |       return {
 59 |         success: false,
 60 |         error: error.message,
 61 |         provider: 'gemini',
 62 |       };
 63 |     }
 64 |   }
 65 | 
 66 |   buildSystemPrompt(context) {
 67 |     // Validate that no actual data is being sent
 68 |     const validation = validateSchemaOnlyContext(context);
 69 |     if (!validation.isValid) {
 70 |       console.warn('Gemini service detected potential data leakage:', validation.violations);
 71 |     }
 72 |     
 73 |     const { dataset, schema } = context;
 74 |     
 75 |     // Use schema-only approach
 76 |     if (dataset && schema) {
 77 |       return buildSchemaOnlyPrompt('', extractSchemaForAI(dataset, schema));
 78 |     }
 79 |     
 80 |     return `You are an intelligent data analyst assistant for InsightFlow. You help users analyze datasets and provide insights based on schema information only.
 81 | 
 82 | Your capabilities:
 83 | - Answer questions about data patterns and trends based on schema structure
 84 | - Generate SQL queries for data analysis
 85 | - Create data visualizations recommendations
 86 | - Provide business insights and recommendations
 87 | 
 88 | Guidelines:
 89 | 1. Be concise but thorough in your responses
 90 | 2. When suggesting charts, specify the chart type (bar, line, area, pie)
 91 | 3. Provide SQL queries when relevant for data analysis
 92 | 4. Focus on actionable insights based on schema structure
 93 | 5. If you don't have enough information, ask clarifying questions
 94 | 6. NEVER reference specific data values - you only have access to schema information
 95 | 
 96 | Respond in a helpful, professional manner.`;
 97 |   }
 98 | 
 99 |   async testConnection() {
100 |     try {
101 |       if (!this.isAvailable()) {
102 |         return {
103 |           success: false,
104 |           error: 'Gemini API key not configured',
105 |         };
106 |       }
107 | 
108 |       const result = await Promise.race([
109 |         this.model.generateContent('Hello, can you respond with "Connection successful"?'),
110 |         new Promise((_, reject) => 
111 |           setTimeout(() => reject(new Error('Request timeout')), 5000)
112 |         )
113 |       ]);
114 | 
115 |       const response = await result.response;
116 |       const content = response.text();
117 | 
118 |       return {
119 |         success: true,
120 |         model: 'gemini-1.5-flash',
121 |         response: content?.trim() || '',
122 |       };
123 |     } catch (error) {
124 |       return {
125 |         success: false,
126 |         error: error.message,
127 |       };
128 |     }
129 |   }
130 | }
131 | 
132 | export const geminiService = new GeminiService();
133 | 
```

### FILE: apps/backend/src/services/ai-providers/local-nlp-service.js

Source path: `apps/backend/src/services/ai-providers/local-nlp-service.js`

```js
  1 | class LocalNLPService {
  2 |   constructor() {
  3 |     this.enabled = process.env.LOCAL_NLP_ENABLED === 'true';
  4 |   }
  5 | 
  6 |   async isAvailable() {
  7 |     return this.enabled;
  8 |   }
  9 | 
 10 |   async generateResponse(prompt, context = {}) {
 11 |     try {
 12 |       if (!this.enabled) {
 13 |         return {
 14 |           success: false,
 15 |           error: 'Local NLP service is disabled',
 16 |           provider: 'local',
 17 |         };
 18 |       }
 19 | 
 20 |       // Use schema-only context for consistency
 21 |       const sanitizedContext = { ...context };
 22 |       if (sanitizedContext.dataset && sanitizedContext.dataset.rows) {
 23 |         delete sanitizedContext.dataset.rows;
 24 |       }
 25 |       
 26 |       const analysis = this.analyzeQuery(prompt, sanitizedContext);
 27 |       const response = this.generateLocalResponse(analysis, sanitizedContext);
 28 | 
 29 |       return {
 30 |         success: true,
 31 |         content: response,
 32 |         provider: 'local',
 33 |         model: 'rule-based',
 34 |       };
 35 |     } catch (error) {
 36 |       console.error('Local NLP Error:', error.message);
 37 |       return {
 38 |         success: false,
 39 |         error: error.message,
 40 |         provider: 'local',
 41 |       };
 42 |     }
 43 |   }
 44 | 
 45 |   analyzeQuery(prompt, context) {
 46 |     const { dataset, schema } = context;
 47 |     const normalizedPrompt = prompt.toLowerCase().trim();
 48 |     
 49 |     const analysis = {
 50 |       intent: 'general',
 51 |       entities: [],
 52 |       metrics: [],
 53 |       dimensions: [],
 54 |       timeframes: [],
 55 |       aggregations: [],
 56 |       chartTypes: [],
 57 |     };
 58 | 
 59 |     // Detect intent
 60 |     if (normalizedPrompt.includes('trend') || normalizedPrompt.includes('over time') || normalizedPrompt.includes('monthly')) {
 61 |       analysis.intent = 'trend';
 62 |       analysis.chartTypes.push('line', 'area');
 63 |     }
 64 |     if (normalizedPrompt.includes('compare') || normalizedPrompt.includes('difference')) {
 65 |       analysis.intent = 'comparison';
 66 |       analysis.chartTypes.push('bar');
 67 |     }
 68 |     if (normalizedPrompt.includes('distribution') || normalizedPrompt.includes('breakdown')) {
 69 |       analysis.intent = 'distribution';
 70 |       analysis.chartTypes.push('pie', 'donut');
 71 |     }
 72 |     if (normalizedPrompt.includes('correlation') || normalizedPrompt.includes('relationship')) {
 73 |       analysis.intent = 'correlation';
 74 |       analysis.chartTypes.push('scatter');
 75 |     }
 76 | 
 77 |     // Extract metrics and dimensions from schema
 78 |     if (schema) {
 79 |       schema.columns.forEach(column => {
 80 |         const columnName = column.name.toLowerCase();
 81 |         if (normalizedPrompt.includes(columnName)) {
 82 |           if (column.role === 'metric') {
 83 |             analysis.metrics.push(column.name);
 84 |           } else if (column.role === 'dimension') {
 85 |             analysis.dimensions.push(column.name);
 86 |           }
 87 |         }
 88 |       });
 89 |     }
 90 | 
 91 |     // Detect aggregations
 92 |     if (normalizedPrompt.includes('sum') || normalizedPrompt.includes('total')) {
 93 |       analysis.aggregations.push('sum');
 94 |     }
 95 |     if (normalizedPrompt.includes('average') || normalizedPrompt.includes('mean')) {
 96 |       analysis.aggregations.push('avg');
 97 |     }
 98 |     if (normalizedPrompt.includes('count')) {
 99 |       analysis.aggregations.push('count');
100 |     }
101 |     if (normalizedPrompt.includes('maximum') || normalizedPrompt.includes('highest')) {
102 |       analysis.aggregations.push('max');
103 |     }
104 |     if (normalizedPrompt.includes('minimum') || normalizedPrompt.includes('lowest')) {
105 |       analysis.aggregations.push('min');
106 |     }
107 | 
108 |     // Detect timeframes
109 |     const timePatterns = [
110 |       { pattern: 'daily|day', timeframe: 'daily' },
111 |       { pattern: 'weekly|week', timeframe: 'weekly' },
112 |       { pattern: 'monthly|month', timeframe: 'monthly' },
113 |       { pattern: 'quarterly|quarter', timeframe: 'quarterly' },
114 |       { pattern: 'yearly|year', timeframe: 'yearly' },
115 |     ];
116 | 
117 |     timePatterns.forEach(({ pattern, timeframe }) => {
118 |       if (new RegExp(pattern).test(normalizedPrompt)) {
119 |         analysis.timeframes.push(timeframe);
120 |       }
121 |     });
122 | 
123 |     return analysis;
124 |   }
125 | 
126 |   generateLocalResponse(analysis, context) {
127 |     const { dataset, schema } = context;
128 |     let response = '';
129 | 
130 |     switch (analysis.intent) {
131 |       case 'trend':
132 |         response = this.generateTrendResponse(analysis, context);
133 |         break;
134 |       case 'comparison':
135 |         response = this.generateComparisonResponse(analysis, context);
136 |         break;
137 |       case 'distribution':
138 |         response = this.generateDistributionResponse(analysis, context);
139 |         break;
140 |       case 'correlation':
141 |         response = this.generateCorrelationResponse(analysis, context);
142 |         break;
143 |       default:
144 |         response = this.generateGeneralResponse(analysis, context);
145 |     }
146 | 
147 |     return response;
148 |   }
149 | 
150 |   generateTrendResponse(analysis, context) {
151 |     const { dataset } = context;
152 |     const metrics = analysis.metrics.length > 0 ? analysis.metrics : ['value'];
153 |     const dimensions = analysis.dimensions.length > 0 ? analysis.dimensions : ['date'];
154 |     
155 |     return `Based on your trend analysis request, I can see you want to analyze ${metrics.join(' and ')} over time. 
156 | 
157 | To visualize this trend, I recommend using a ${analysis.chartTypes[0] || 'line'} chart with ${dimensions[0]} on the x-axis and ${metrics[0]} on the y-axis.
158 | 
159 | SQL Query Example:
160 | ` + "```sql\n" +
161 | `SELECT 
162 |   ${dimensions[0]},
163 |   ${analysis.aggregations[0] || 'SUM'}(${metrics[0]}) as ${metrics[0]}
164 | FROM your_table
165 | GROUP BY ${dimensions[0]}
166 | ORDER BY ${dimensions[0]}
167 | ` + "```\n" +
168 | `This will show you how ${metrics[0]} changes over ${dimensions[0]}. Would you like me to help you analyze any specific patterns or periods?`;
169 |   }
170 | 
171 |   generateComparisonResponse(analysis, context) {
172 |     const metrics = analysis.metrics.length > 0 ? analysis.metrics : ['value'];
173 |     const dimensions = analysis.dimensions.length > 0 ? analysis.dimensions.slice(0, 2) : ['category'];
174 |     
175 |     return `For your comparison analysis, I'll help you compare ${metrics.join(' and ')} across different ${dimensions[0]} values.
176 | 
177 | I recommend using a bar chart to clearly show the differences between categories.
178 | 
179 | SQL Query Example:
180 | ` + "```sql\n" +
181 | `SELECT 
182 |   ${dimensions[0]},
183 |   ${analysis.aggregations[0] || 'AVG'}(${metrics[0]}) as average_${metrics[0]}
184 | FROM your_table
185 | GROUP BY ${dimensions[0]}
186 | ORDER BY average_${metrics[0]} DESC
187 | ` + "```\n" +
188 | `This query will show you the average ${metrics[0]} for each ${dimensions[0]}, making it easy to identify the highest and lowest values. Would you like to focus on any specific comparisons?`;
189 |   }
190 | 
191 |   generateDistributionResponse(analysis, context) {
192 |     const dimensions = analysis.dimensions.length > 0 ? analysis.dimensions : ['category'];
193 |     
194 |     return `To analyze the distribution across ${dimensions[0]}, I recommend using a ${analysis.chartTypes[0] || 'pie'} chart to show the proportion of each category.
195 | 
196 | SQL Query Example:
197 | ` + "```sql\n" +
198 | `SELECT 
199 |   ${dimensions[0]},
200 |   COUNT(*) as count
201 | FROM your_table
202 | GROUP BY ${dimensions[0]}
203 | ORDER BY count DESC
204 | ` + "```\n" +
205 | `This will show you how many records belong to each ${dimensions[0]} category, helping you understand the data distribution. Would you like me to highlight any specific patterns in the distribution?`;
206 |   }
207 | 
208 |   generateCorrelationResponse(analysis, context) {
209 |     const metrics = analysis.metrics.length >= 2 ? analysis.metrics : ['value1', 'value2'];
210 |     
211 |     return `For correlation analysis between ${metrics[0]} and ${metrics[1]}, I recommend using a scatter plot to visualize the relationship.
212 | 
213 | SQL Query Example:
214 | ` + "```sql\n" +
215 | `SELECT 
216 |   ${metrics[0]},
217 |   ${metrics[1]}
218 | FROM your_table
219 | WHERE ${metrics[0]} IS NOT NULL 
220 |   AND ${metrics[1]} IS NOT NULL
221 | ` + "```\n" +
222 | `This will help you identify patterns, trends, or correlations between these two variables. A positive correlation would show points trending upward from left to right, while a negative correlation would trend downward. Would you like me to help calculate the correlation coefficient?`;
223 |   }
224 | 
225 |   generateGeneralResponse(analysis, context) {
226 |     const { schema } = context;
227 |     
228 |     if (!schema) {
229 |       return `I can help you analyze your data! To get started, please upload a dataset or create a demo dataset. Once you have data loaded, I can assist with:
230 | 
231 | - Trend analysis over time
232 | - Comparisons between categories  
233 | - Data distribution analysis
234 | - Correlation analysis between variables
235 | - Generating SQL queries
236 | - Creating chart recommendations
237 | 
238 | What would you like to explore?`;
239 |     }
240 | 
241 |     const columnCount = schema.columns?.length || 0;
242 |     const rowCount = context.dataset?.rowCount || 'unknown';
243 |     const dimensions = schema.columns?.filter(col => col.role === 'dimension') || [];
244 |     const metrics = schema.columns?.filter(col => col.role === 'metric') || [];
245 |     
246 |     return `I can help you analyze your dataset which contains ${rowCount} rows and ${columnCount} columns based on its schema structure.
247 | 
248 | **Schema Analysis:**
249 | - Dimensions available: ${dimensions.map(col => col.name).join(', ') || 'none'}
250 | - Metrics available: ${metrics.map(col => col.name).join(', ') || 'none'}
251 | 
252 | **Available Analysis Types:**
253 | - **Trend Analysis**: Track metrics over time periods
254 | - **Comparison**: Compare values across categories
255 | - **Distribution**: Understand data spread and proportions
256 | - **Correlation**: Find relationships between variables
257 | 
258 | **Quick SQL Examples:**
259 | ` + "```sql\n" +
260 | `-- Get basic statistics
261 | SELECT COUNT(*) as total_rows FROM your_table;
262 | 
263 | -- Find top categories
264 | SELECT category, COUNT(*) as count 
265 | FROM your_table 
266 | GROUP BY category 
267 | ORDER BY count DESC 
268 | LIMIT 10;
269 | ` + "```\n" +
270 | `What specific aspect of your data would you like to explore? I can generate targeted queries and visualizations based on your schema structure.`;
271 |   }
272 | 
273 |   async testConnection() {
274 |     return {
275 |       success: this.enabled,
276 |       model: 'rule-based',
277 |       status: this.enabled ? 'enabled' : 'disabled',
278 |     };
279 |   }
280 | }
281 |

### FILE: apps/backend/src/services/ai-providers/ollama-service.js

```js
  1 | import axios from 'axios';
  2 | import { extractSchemaForAI, buildSchemaOnlyPrompt, validateSchemaOnlyContext } from '../../utils/schema-extractor.js';
  3 | 
  4 | class OllamaService {
  5 |   constructor() {
  6 |     this.baseUrl = process.env.OLLAMA_BASE_URL || 'http://localhost:11434';
  7 |     this.model = process.env.OLLAMA_MODEL || 'llama3.2:latest';
  8 |     this.timeout = parseInt(process.env.AI_TIMEOUT_MS) || 30000;
  9 |   }
 10 | 
 11 |   async isAvailable() {
 12 |     try {
 13 |       const response = await axios.get(`${this.baseUrl}/api/tags`, { timeout: 5000 });
 14 |       return response.status === 200;
 15 |     } catch (error) {
 16 |       return false;
 17 |     }
 18 |   }
 19 | 
 20 |   async generateResponse(prompt, context = {}) {
 21 |     try {
 22 |       const systemPrompt = this.buildSystemPrompt(context);
 23 |       const fullPrompt = `${systemPrompt}\n\nUser: ${prompt}\nAssistant:`;
 24 | 
 25 |       const response = await axios.post(
 26 |         `${this.baseUrl}/api/generate`,
 27 |         {
 28 |           model: this.model,
 29 |           prompt: fullPrompt,
 30 |           stream: false,
 31 |           options: {
 32 |             temperature: parseFloat(process.env.AI_TEMPERATURE) || 0.7,
 33 |             num_predict: parseInt(process.env.AI_MAX_TOKENS) || 2048,
 34 |           }
 35 |         },
 36 |         { timeout: this.timeout }
 37 |       );
 38 | 
 39 |       return {
 40 |         success: true,
 41 |         content: response.data.response?.trim() || '',
 42 |         provider: 'ollama',
 43 |         model: this.model,
 44 |       };
 45 |     } catch (error) {
 46 |       console.error('Ollama API Error:', error.message);
 47 |       return {
 48 |         success: false,
 49 |         error: error.message,
 50 |         provider: 'ollama',
 51 |       };
 52 |     }
 53 |   }
 54 | 
 55 |   buildSystemPrompt(context) {
 56 |     // Validate that no actual data is being sent
 57 |     const validation = validateSchemaOnlyContext(context);
 58 |     if (!validation.isValid) {
 59 |       console.warn('Ollama service detected potential data leakage:', validation.violations);
 60 |     }
 61 |     
 62 |     const { dataset, schema } = context;
 63 |     
 64 |     // Use schema-only approach
 65 |     if (dataset && schema) {
 66 |       return buildSchemaOnlyPrompt('', extractSchemaForAI(dataset, schema));
 67 |     }
 68 |     
 69 |     return `You are an intelligent data analyst assistant for InsightFlow. You help users analyze datasets and provide insights based on schema information only.
 70 | 
 71 | Your capabilities:
 72 | - Answer questions about data patterns and trends based on schema structure
 73 | - Generate SQL queries for data analysis
 74 | - Create data visualizations recommendations
 75 | - Provide business insights and recommendations
 76 | 
 77 | Guidelines:
 78 | 1. Be concise but thorough in your responses
 79 | 2. When suggesting charts, specify the chart type (bar, line, area, pie)
 80 | 3. Provide SQL queries when relevant for data analysis
 81 | 4. Focus on actionable insights based on schema structure
 82 | 5. If you don't have enough information, ask clarifying questions
 83 | 6. NEVER reference specific data values - you only have access to schema information
 84 | 
 85 | Respond in a helpful, professional manner.`;
 86 |   }
 87 | 
 88 |   async testConnection() {
 89 |     try {
 90 |       const response = await axios.get(`${this.baseUrl}/api/version`, { timeout: 5000 });
 91 |       return {
 92 |         success: true,
 93 |         version: response.data.version,
 94 |         model: this.model,
 95 |       };
 96 |     } catch (error) {
 97 |       return {
 98 |         success: false,
 99 |         error: error.message,
100 |       };
101 |     }
102 |   }
103 | }
104 | 
105 | export const ollamaService = new OllamaService();

### FILE: apps/backend/src/services/ai/ai-manager.js

Source path: `apps/backend/src/services/ai/ai-manager.js`

```js
  1 | // Master AI orchestrator with fallback chain
  2 | import { OllamaProvider } from './providers/ollama-provider.js';
  3 | import { GeminiProvider } from './providers/gemini-provider.js';
  4 | import { OpenAIProvider } from './providers/openai-provider.js';
  5 | import { AnthropicProvider } from './providers/anthropic-provider.js';
  6 | import { AIProviderError } from '../../middleware/error-handler.js';
  7 | import config from '../../config/environment.js';
  8 | 
  9 | class AIManager {
 10 |   constructor() {
 11 |     this.providers = new Map();
 12 |     this.priority = config.ai.providerPriority;
 13 |     this.fallbackEnabled = config.ai.fallbackEnabled;
 14 |     this.activeProvider = null;
 15 |     this.initialized = false;
 16 |     this.stats = {
 17 |       totalRequests: 0,
 18 |       successfulRequests: 0,
 19 |       failedRequests: 0,
 20 |       providerUsage: {}
 21 |     };
 22 |   }
 23 | 
 24 |   async initialize() {
 25 |     if (this.initialized) return;
 26 | 
 27 |     console.log('\n🎯 ===== AI MANAGER INITIALIZATION =====');
 28 |     console.log(`Provider Priority: ${this.priority.join(' → ')}`);
 29 |     console.log(`Fallback Enabled: ${this.fallbackEnabled}`);
 30 | 
 31 |     // Initialize all providers
 32 |     await this.initializeProviders();
 33 | 
 34 |     // Set active provider based on priority
 35 |     await this.selectActiveProvider();
 36 | 
 37 |     this.initialized = true;
 38 |     console.log('\n✅ AI Manager initialized\n');
 39 |   }
 40 | 
 41 |   async initializeProviders() {
 42 |     // Initialize Ollama
 43 |     if (this.priority.includes('ollama')) {
 44 |       try {
 45 |         const ollama = new OllamaProvider(config.ollama);
 46 |         await ollama.initialize();
 47 |         this.providers.set('ollama', ollama);
 48 |         console.log('✅ Ollama provider initialized');
 49 |       } catch (error) {
 50 |         console.error('❌ Failed to initialize Ollama:', error.message);
 51 |       }
 52 |     }
 53 | 
 54 |     // Initialize Gemini
 55 |     if (this.priority.includes('gemini')) {
 56 |       try {
 57 |         const gemini = new GeminiProvider(config.gemini);
 58 |         await gemini.initialize();
 59 |         this.providers.set('gemini', gemini);
 60 |         console.log('✅ Gemini provider initialized');
 61 |       } catch (error) {
 62 |         console.error('❌ Failed to initialize Gemini:', error.message);
 63 |       }
 64 |     }
 65 | 
 66 |     // Initialize OpenAI
 67 |     if (this.priority.includes('openai')) {
 68 |       try {
 69 |         const openai = new OpenAIProvider(config.openai);
 70 |         await openai.initialize();
 71 |         this.providers.set('openai', openai);
 72 |         console.log('✅ OpenAI provider initialized');
 73 |       } catch (error) {
 74 |         console.error('❌ Failed to initialize OpenAI:', error.message);
 75 |       }
 76 |     }
 77 | 
 78 |     // Initialize Anthropic
 79 |     if (this.priority.includes('anthropic')) {
 80 |       try {
 81 |         const anthropic = new AnthropicProvider(config.anthropic);
 82 |         await anthropic.initialize();
 83 |         this.providers.set('anthropic', anthropic);
 84 |         console.log('✅ Anthropic provider initialized');
 85 |       } catch (error) {
 86 |         console.error('❌ Failed to initialize Anthropic:', error.message);
 87 |       }
 88 |     }
 89 |   }
 90 | 
 91 |   async selectActiveProvider() {
 92 |     for (const providerName of this.priority) {
 93 |       const provider = this.providers.get(providerName);
 94 |       if (provider && await provider.isAvailable()) {
 95 |         this.activeProvider = providerName;
 96 |         console.log(`\n✅ Active Provider: ${this.activeProvider.toUpperCase()}`);
 97 |         return;
 98 |       }
 99 |     }
100 | 
101 |     console.warn('\n⚠️  No AI providers available! Using rule-based analysis only.');
102 |     this.activeProvider = null;
103 |   }
104 | 
105 |   async generateResponse(prompt, options = {}) {
106 |     await this.initialize();
107 |     this.stats.totalRequests++;
108 | 
109 |     if (!this.activeProvider) {
110 |       this.stats.failedRequests++;
111 |       throw new AIProviderError('No AI providers available', 'none');
112 |     }
113 | 
114 |     try {
115 |       console.log(`\n📝 Generating with ${this.activeProvider}...`);
116 |       const response = await this.executeWithProvider(this.activeProvider, 'generate', [prompt, options]);
117 | 
118 |       if (response.success) {
119 |         this.stats.successfulRequests++;
120 |         this.updateProviderStats(this.activeProvider);
121 |         return response;
122 |       }
123 | 
124 |       // Try fallback if enabled
125 |       if (this.fallbackEnabled && !options.disableFallback) {
126 |         console.warn(`⚠️  ${this.activeProvider} failed, trying fallback...`);
127 |         return await this.tryFallback('generate', [prompt, options]);
128 |       }
129 | 
130 |       this.stats.failedRequests++;
131 |       throw new AIProviderError(response.error || 'Generation failed', this.activeProvider);
132 | 
133 |     } catch (error) {
134 |       this.stats.failedRequests++;
135 |       
136 |       if (this.fallbackEnabled && !options.disableFallback && !(error instanceof AIProviderError && error.provider === 'none')) {
137 |         return await this.tryFallback('generate', [prompt, options]);
138 |       }
139 | 
140 |       throw error;
141 |     }
142 |   }
143 | 
144 |   async chat(messages, options = {}) {
145 |     await this.initialize();
146 |     this.stats.totalRequests++;
147 | 
148 |     if (!this.activeProvider) {
149 |       this.stats.failedRequests++;
150 |       throw new AIProviderError('No AI providers available', 'none');
151 |     }
152 | 
153 |     try {
154 |       console.log(`\n💬 Chatting with ${this.activeProvider}...`);
155 |       const response = await this.executeWithProvider(this.activeProvider, 'chat', [messages, options]);
156 | 
157 |       if (response.success) {
158 |         this.stats.successfulRequests++;
159 |         this.updateProviderStats(this.activeProvider);
160 |         return response;
161 |       }
162 | 
163 |       // Try fallback if enabled
164 |       if (this.fallbackEnabled && !options.disableFallback) {
165 |         console.warn(`⚠️  ${this.activeProvider} failed, trying fallback...`);
166 |         return await this.tryFallback('chat', [messages, options]);
167 |       }
168 | 
169 |       this.stats.failedRequests++;
170 |       throw new AIProviderError(response.error || 'Chat failed', this.activeProvider);
171 | 
172 |     } catch (error) {
173 |       this.stats.failedRequests++;
174 |       
175 |       if (this.fallbackEnabled && !options.disableFallback && !(error instanceof AIProviderError && error.provider === 'none')) {
176 |         return await this.tryFallback('chat', [messages, options]);
177 |       }
178 | 
179 |       throw error;
180 |     }
181 |   }
182 | 
183 |   async executeWithProvider(providerName, method, args) {
184 |     const provider = this.providers.get(providerName);
185 |     if (!provider) {
186 |       throw new AIProviderError(`Provider ${providerName} not found`, providerName);
187 |     }
188 | 
189 |     try {
190 |       return await provider[method](...args);
191 |     } catch (error) {
192 |       console.error(`❌ Error with ${providerName}:`, error.message);
193 |       throw error;
194 |     }
195 |   }
196 | 
197 |   async tryFallback(method, args) {
198 |     for (const providerName of this.priority) {
199 |       if (providerName === this.activeProvider) continue;
200 | 
201 |       const provider = this.providers.get(providerName);
202 |       if (!provider || !(await provider.isAvailable())) continue;
203 | 
204 |       try {
205 |         console.log(`🔄 Trying ${providerName}...`);
206 |         const response = await this.executeWithProvider(providerName, method, args);
207 | 
208 |         if (response.success) {
209 |           console.log(`✅ Fallback to ${providerName} successful`);
210 |           this.activeProvider = providerName;
211 |           this.stats.successfulRequests++;
212 |           this.updateProviderStats(providerName);
213 |           return {
214 |             ...response,
215 |             usedProvider: providerName,
216 |             fallback: true
217 |           };
218 |         }
219 |       } catch (error) {
220 |         console.warn(`⚠️  ${providerName} failed:`, error.message);
221 |         continue;
222 |       }
223 |     }
224 | 
225 |     this.stats.failedRequests++;
226 |     throw new AIProviderError('All providers failed', 'all');
227 |   }
228 | 
229 |   async health() {
230 |     await this.initialize();
231 | 
232 |     const health = {
233 |       initialized: this.initialized,
234 |       activeProvider: this.activeProvider,
235 |       fallbackEnabled: this.fallbackEnabled,
236 |       providers: {},
237 |       stats: this.stats
238 |     };
239 | 
240 |     // Get health status for each provider
241 |     for (const [name, provider] of this.providers) {
242 |       try {
243 |         health.providers[name] = await provider.health();
244 |       } catch (error) {
245 |         health.providers[name] = {
246 |           name,
247 |           available: false,
248 |           error: error.message,
249 |           lastChecked: new Date().toISOString()
250 |         };
251 |       }
252 |     }
253 | 
254 |     return health;
255 |   }
256 | 
257 |   async getAvailableProviders() {
258 |     await this.initialize();
259 |     const available = [];
260 | 
261 |     for (const [name, provider] of this.providers) {
262 |       if (await provider.isAvailable()) {
263 |         available.push({
264 |           name,
265 |           capabilities: provider.getCapabilities()
266 |         });
267 |       }
268 |     }
269 | 
270 |     return available;
271 |   }
272 | 
273 |   async switchProvider(providerName) {
274 |     const provider = this.providers.get(providerName);
275 |     if (!provider) {
276 |       throw new AIProviderError(`Provider ${providerName} not found`, providerName);
277 |     }
278 | 
279 |     if (!(await provider.isAvailable())) {
280 |       throw new AIProviderError(`Provider ${providerName} is not available`, providerName);
281 |     }
282 | 
283 |     this.activeProvider = providerName;
284 |     console.log(`🔄 Switched to provider: ${providerName}`);
285 |   }
286 | 
287 |   updateProviderStats(providerName) {
288 |     if (!this.stats.providerUsage[providerName]) {
289 |       this.stats.providerUsage[providerName] = 0;
290 |     }
291 |     this.stats.providerUsage[providerName]++;
292 |   }
293 | 
294 |   getStats() {
295 |     return {
296 |       ...this.stats,
297 |       successRate: this.stats.totalRequests > 0 
298 |         ? (this.stats.successfulRequests / this.stats.totalRequests * 100).toFixed(2) + '%'
299 |         : '0%',
300 |       availableProviders: this.providers.size,
301 |       activeProvider: this.activeProvider
302 |     };
303 |   }
304 | 
305 |   resetStats() {
306 |     this.stats = {
307 |       totalRequests: 0,
308 |       successfulRequests: 0,
309 |       failedRequests: 0,
310 |       providerUsage: {}
311 |     };
312 |   }
313 | }
314 | 
315 | // Create singleton instance
316 | export const aiManager = new AIManager();
317 | 
318 | // Auto-initialize on import
319 | setTimeout(() => {
320 |   aiManager.initialize().catch(console.error);
321 | }, 1000);
322 | 
323 | export default aiManager;
324 | 
```

  1 | import { callOllamaJson } from "./ollamaClient.js";

  3 | const DASHBOARD_MODEL =
  4 |   process.env.DASHBOARD_LLM_MODEL ||
  5 |   process.env.DASHBOARD_MASTER_MODEL ||
  6 |   "insightflow-master";
  7 | 
  8 | const dashboardSchema = {
  9 |   type: "object",
 10 |   additionalProperties: false,
 11 |   required: ["action", "schemaOnly", "message", "dashboardPlan"],
 12 |   properties: {
 13 |     action: {
 14 |       type: "string",
 15 |       enum: [
 16 |         "BUILD_DASHBOARD",
 17 |         "GENERATE_CHART",
 18 |         "MODIFY_CHART",
 19 |         "DELETE_CHART",
 20 |         "GENERATE_KPI",
 21 |         "FILTER",
 22 |         "CLEAR_FILTERS",
 23 |         "ANSWER",
 24 |       ],
 25 |     },
 26 |     schemaOnly: { type: "boolean" },
 27 |     message: { type: "string" },
 28 |     dashboardPlan: {
 29 |       type: "object",
 30 |       additionalProperties: false,
 31 |       required: ["kpis", "charts", "filters"],
 32 |       properties: {
 33 |         kpis: {
 34 |           type: "array",
 35 |           maxItems: 6,
 36 |           items: {
 37 |             type: "object",
 38 |             additionalProperties: false,
 39 |             required: ["title", "aggregation"],
 40 |             properties: {
 41 |               id: { type: "string" },
 42 |               title: { type: "string" },
 43 |               metric: { type: "string" },
 44 |               aggregation: {
 45 |                 type: "string",
 46 |                 enum: [
 47 |                   "count",
 48 |                   "sum",
 49 |                   "avg",
 50 |                   "min",
 51 |                   "max",
 52 |                   "median",
 53 |                   "count_unique",
 54 |                 ],
 55 |               },
 56 |               format: {
 57 |                 type: "string",
 58 |                 enum: ["number", "currency", "percent", "text"],
 59 |               },
 60 |             },
 61 |           },
 62 |         },
 63 |         charts: {
 64 |           type: "array",
 65 |           maxItems: 7,
 66 |           items: {
 67 |             type: "object",
 68 |             additionalProperties: false,
 69 |             required: ["type", "title", "xKey", "aggregation"],
 70 |             properties: {
 71 |               id: { type: "string" },
 72 |               type: {
 73 |                 type: "string",
 74 |                 enum: [
 75 |                   "bar",
 76 |                   "line",
 77 |                   "area",
 78 |                   "pie",
 79 |                   "donut",
 80 |                   "histogram",
 81 |                   "scatter",
 82 |                 ],
 83 |               },
 84 |               title: { type: "string" },
 85 |               xKey: { type: "string" },
 86 |               yKey: { type: "string" },
 87 |               aggregation: {
 88 |                 type: "string",
 89 |                 enum: [
 90 |                   "count",
 91 |                   "sum",
 92 |                   "avg",
 93 |                   "min",
 94 |                   "max",
 95 |                   "median",
 96 |                   "count_unique",
 97 |                 ],
 98 |               },
 99 |               limit: { type: "number" },
100 |             },
101 |           },
102 |         },
103 |         filters: {
104 |           type: "array",
105 |           maxItems: 6,
106 |           items: {
107 |             type: "object",
108 |             additionalProperties: false,
109 |             required: ["key", "label", "type"],
110 |             properties: {
111 |               key: { type: "string" },
112 |               label: { type: "string" },
113 |               type: {
114 |                 type: "string",
115 |                 enum: ["select", "dateRange", "numberRange"],
116 |               },
117 |             },
118 |           },
119 |         },
120 |       },
121 |     },
122 |   },
123 | };
124 | 
125 | function compactSchema(schemaProfile) {
126 |   return {
127 |     datasetName: schemaProfile.datasetName,
128 |     rowCount: schemaProfile.rowCount,
129 |     columnCount: schemaProfile.columnCount,
130 |     columns: schemaProfile.columns.map((column) => ({
131 |       name: column.name,
132 |       type: column.type,
133 |       role: column.role,
134 |       description: column.description || "",
135 |       uniqueCount: column.uniqueCount || 0,
136 |       missingPct: column.missingPct || 0,
137 |       topValues: Array.isArray(column.topValues)
138 |         ? column.topValues.slice(0, 8)
139 |         : [],
140 |       stats: column.stats || null,
141 |     })),
142 |   };
143 | }
144 | 
145 | function getColumns(schemaProfile) {
146 |   return new Set(schemaProfile.columns.map((column) => column.name));
147 | }
148 | 
149 | function fallbackDashboardPlan(schemaProfile) {
150 |   const metrics = schemaProfile.columns.filter((column) => column.role === "metric");
151 |   const dimensions = schemaProfile.columns.filter((column) => column.role === "dimension");
152 |   const dates = schemaProfile.columns.filter((column) => column.role === "date");
153 | 
154 |   const metric = metrics[0];
155 |   const metric2 = metrics[1];
156 |   const dimension = dimensions[0];
157 |   const dimension2 = dimensions[1];
158 |   const date = dates[0];
159 | 
160 |   const kpis = [
161 |     {
162 |       id: "kpi-total-records",
163 |       title: "Total Records",
164 |       aggregation: "count",
165 |       format: "number",
166 |     },
167 |   ];
168 | 
169 |   if (metric) {
170 |     kpis.push(
171 |       {
172 |         id: "kpi-average",
173 |         title: `Average ${metric.name}`,
174 |         metric: metric.name,
175 |         aggregation: "avg",
176 |         format: "number",
177 |       },
178 |       {
179 |         id: "kpi-highest",
180 |         title: `Highest ${metric.name}`,
181 |         metric: metric.name,
182 |         aggregation: "max",
183 |         format: "number",
184 |       }
185 |     );
186 |   }
187 | 
188 |   if (dimension) {
189 |     kpis.push({
190 |       id: "kpi-unique-dimension",
191 |       title: `Unique ${dimension.name}`,
192 |       metric: dimension.name,
193 |       aggregation: "count_unique",
194 |       format: "number",
195 |     });
196 |   }
197 | 
198 |   const charts = [];
199 | 
200 |   if (dimension && metric) {
201 |     charts.push({
202 |       id: "chart-main-bar",
203 |       type: "bar",
204 |       title: `Average ${metric.name} by ${dimension.name}`,
205 |       xKey: dimension.name,
206 |       yKey: metric.name,
207 |       aggregation: "avg",
208 |       limit: 10,
209 |     });
210 |   }
211 | 
212 |   if (metric) {
213 |     charts.push({
214 |       id: "chart-distribution",
215 |       type: "histogram",
216 |       title: `${metric.name} Distribution`,
217 |       xKey: metric.name,
218 |       yKey: metric.name,
219 |       aggregation: "count",
220 |       limit: 8,
221 |     });
222 |   }
223 | 
224 |   if (dimension) {
225 |     charts.push({
226 |       id: "chart-donut",
227 |       type: "donut",
228 |       title: `Records by ${dimension.name}`,
229 |       xKey: dimension.name,
230 |       aggregation: "count",
231 |       limit: 8,
232 |     });
233 |   }
234 | 
235 |   if (date && metric) {
236 |     charts.push({
237 |       id: "chart-trend",
238 |       type: "line",
239 |       title: `${metric.name} Trend`,
240 |       xKey: date.name,
241 |       yKey: metric.name,
242 |       aggregation: "sum",
243 |       limit: 30,
244 |     });
245 |   }
246 | 
247 |   if (dimension2 && metric) {
248 |     charts.push({
249 |       id: "chart-second-category",
250 |       type: "bar",
251 |       title: `Average ${metric.name} by ${dimension2.name}`,
252 |       xKey: dimension2.name,
253 |       yKey: metric.name,
254 |       aggregation: "avg",
255 |       limit: 10,
256 |     });
257 |   }
258 | 
259 |   if (metric && metric2) {
260 |     charts.push({
261 |       id: "chart-scatter",
262 |       type: "scatter",
263 |       title: `${metric.name} vs ${metric2.name}`,
264 |       xKey: metric.name,
265 |       yKey: metric2.name,
266 |       aggregation: "count",
267 |       limit: 200,
268 |     });
269 |   }
270 | 
271 |   for (const dim of dimensions) {
272 |     if (charts.length >= 7) break;
273 | 
274 |     charts.push({
275 |       id: `chart-top-${dim.name}`,
276 |       type: "bar",
277 |       title: `Top ${dim.name}`,
278 |       xKey: dim.name,
279 |       aggregation: "count",
280 |       limit: 10,
281 |     });
282 |   }
283 | 
284 |   return {
285 |     action: "BUILD_DASHBOARD",
286 |     schemaOnly: true,
287 |     message: "Dashboard generated using local fallback rules.",
288 |     dashboardPlan: {
289 |       kpis: kpis.slice(0, 6),
290 |       charts: charts.slice(0, 7),
291 |       filters: [
292 |         ...dimensions.slice(0, 4).map((column) => ({
293 |           key: column.name,
294 |           label: column.name,
295 |           type: "select",
296 |         })),
297 |         ...dates.slice(0, 1).map((column) => ({
298 |           key: column.name,
299 |           label: column.name,
300 |           type: "dateRange",
301 |         })),
302 |       ],
303 |     },
304 |   };
305 | }
306 | 
307 | function validatePlan(plan, schemaProfile) {
308 |   const columnSet = getColumns(schemaProfile);
309 | 
310 |   const safeKpis = (plan.dashboardPlan?.kpis || [])
311 |     .filter((kpi) => !kpi.metric || columnSet.has(kpi.metric))
312 |     .slice(0, 6);
313 | 
314 |   const safeCharts = (plan.dashboardPlan?.charts || [])
315 |     .filter((chart) => columnSet.has(chart.xKey))
316 |     .filter((chart) => !chart.yKey || columnSet.has(chart.yKey))
317 |     .map((chart, index) => ({
318 |       ...chart,
319 |       id: chart.id || `chart-${index + 1}`,
320 |       limit: chart.limit || 10,
321 |     }))
322 |     .slice(0, 7);
323 | 
324 |   const safeFilters = (plan.dashboardPlan?.filters || [])
325 |     .filter((filter) => columnSet.has(filter.key))
326 |     .slice(0, 6);
327 | 
328 |   const validated = {
329 |     action: plan.action || "BUILD_DASHBOARD",
330 |     schemaOnly: true,
331 |     message: plan.message || "Dashboard plan generated.",
332 |     dashboardPlan: {
333 |       kpis: safeKpis,
334 |       charts: safeCharts,
335 |       filters: safeFilters,
336 |     },
337 |   };
338 | 
339 |   if (!validated.dashboardPlan.kpis.length || !validated.dashboardPlan.charts.length) {
340 |     return fallbackDashboardPlan(schemaProfile);
341 |   }
342 | 
343 |   return validated;
344 | }
345 | 
346 | export async function planDashboardWithAI({
347 |   schemaProfile,
348 |   userQuery = "Generate a complete dashboard with KPIs, filters, and 7 useful charts.",
349 |   currentDashboard = null,
350 | }) {
351 |   try {
352 |     const result = await callOllamaJson({
353 |       model: DASHBOARD_MODEL,
354 |       schema: dashboardSchema,
355 |       messages: [
356 |         {
357 |           role: "system",
358 |           content:
359 |             "Return strict JSON only. Never return chart.data. Use schema columns only.",
360 |         },
361 |         {
362 |           role: "user",
363 |           content: JSON.stringify(
364 |             {
365 |               userQuery,
366 |               schemaOnly: true,
367 |               schemaProfile: compactSchema(schemaProfile),
368 |               currentDashboard,
369 |             },
370 |             null,
371 |             2
372 |           ),
373 |         },
374 |       ],
375 |     });
376 | 
377 |     return validatePlan(result, schemaProfile);
378 |   } catch {
379 |     return fallbackDashboardPlan(schemaProfile);
380 |   }
381 | }
382 | 
```
