### FILE: apps/backend/src/services/dashboard/dashboardAnalytics.js

Source path: `apps/backend/src/services/dashboard/dashboardAnalytics.js`

```js
  1 | import { randomUUID } from "node:crypto";
  2 | import { cleanDatasetRows, safeNumber } from "./schemaProfiler.js";
  3 | 
  4 | function isMissing(value) {
  5 |   return value === null || value === undefined || value === "";
  6 | }
  7 | 
  8 | function round(value) {
  9 |   return Number(Number(value || 0).toFixed(2));
 10 | }
 11 | 
 12 | function formatNumber(value) {
 13 |   if (typeof value !== "number" || !Number.isFinite(value)) return "0";
 14 |   return value.toLocaleString(undefined, {
 15 |     maximumFractionDigits: 2,
 16 |   });
 17 | }
 18 | 
 19 | export function aggregate(values = [], aggregation = "count") {
 20 |   const present = values.filter((value) => !isMissing(value));
 21 | 
 22 |   if (aggregation === "count") return present.length;
 23 | 
 24 |   if (aggregation === "count_unique") {
 25 |     return new Set(present.map((value) => String(value))).size;
 26 |   }
 27 | 
 28 |   const numbers = present
 29 |     .map(safeNumber)
 30 |     .filter((value) => value !== null);
 31 | 
 32 |   if (!numbers.length) return 0;
 33 | 
 34 |   if (aggregation === "sum") {
 35 |     return numbers.reduce((total, value) => total + value, 0);
 36 |   }
 37 | 
 38 |   if (aggregation === "avg") {
 39 |     return numbers.reduce((total, value) => total + value, 0) / numbers.length;
 40 |   }
 41 | 
 42 |   if (aggregation === "min") return Math.min(...numbers);
 43 |   if (aggregation === "max") return Math.max(...numbers);
 44 | 
 45 |   const sorted = [...numbers].sort((a, b) => a - b);
 46 |   const mid = Math.floor(sorted.length / 2);
 47 | 
 48 |   return sorted.length % 2
 49 |     ? sorted[mid]
 50 |     : (sorted[mid - 1] + sorted[mid]) / 2;
 51 | }
 52 | 
 53 | export function applyFilters(rows = [], filters = {}) {
 54 |   return rows.filter((row) => {
 55 |     for (const [key, value] of Object.entries(filters || {})) {
 56 |       if (!value) continue;
 57 | 
 58 |       if (typeof value === "object" && (value.start || value.end)) {
 59 |         const rowTime = Date.parse(row[key]);
 60 |         if (!Number.isFinite(rowTime)) return false;
 61 | 
 62 |         if (value.start && rowTime < Date.parse(value.start)) return false;
 63 |         if (value.end && rowTime > Date.parse(value.end)) return false;
 64 | 
 65 |         continue;
 66 |       }
 67 | 
 68 |       if (String(row[key] ?? "").toLowerCase() !== String(value).toLowerCase()) {
 69 |         return false;
 70 |       }
 71 |     }
 72 | 
 73 |     return true;
 74 |   });
 75 | }
 76 | 
 77 | export function groupByAggregate({
 78 |   rows,
 79 |   xKey,
 80 |   yKey,
 81 |   aggregation = "count",
 82 |   limit = 10,
 83 | }) {
 84 |   const groups = new Map();
 85 | 
 86 |   for (const row of rows) {
 87 |     const label = String(row[xKey] ?? "Unknown").trim() || "Unknown";
 88 | 
 89 |     if (!groups.has(label)) groups.set(label, []);
 90 |     groups.get(label).push(aggregation === "count" ? label : row[yKey]);
 91 |   }
 92 | 
 93 |   const valueKey = aggregation === "count" ? "count" : yKey;
 94 | 
 95 |   return [...groups.entries()]
 96 |     .map(([label, values]) => ({
 97 |       [xKey]: label,
 98 |       [valueKey]: round(aggregate(values, aggregation)),
 99 |     }))
100 |     .sort((a, b) => Number(b[valueKey]) - Number(a[valueKey]))
101 |     .slice(0, limit);
102 | }
103 | 
104 | export function buildHistogram({ rows, key, bins = 8 }) {
105 |   const values = rows
106 |     .map((row) => safeNumber(row[key]))
107 |     .filter((value) => value !== null);
108 | 
109 |   if (!values.length) return [];
110 | 
111 |   const min = Math.min(...values);
112 |   const max = Math.max(...values);
113 | 
114 |   if (min === max) {
115 |     return [{ range: String(min), count: values.length }];
116 |   }
117 | 
118 |   const step = (max - min) / bins;
119 |   const buckets = Array.from({ length: bins }, (_, index) => ({
120 |     start: min + index * step,
121 |     end: index === bins - 1 ? max : min + (index + 1) * step,
122 |     count: 0,
123 |   }));
124 | 
125 |   for (const value of values) {
126 |     const index = Math.min(Math.floor((value - min) / step), bins - 1);
127 |     buckets[index].count += 1;
128 |   }
129 | 
130 |   return buckets.map((bucket) => ({
131 |     range: `${Math.round(bucket.start)}-${Math.round(bucket.end)}`,
132 |     count: bucket.count,
133 |   }));
134 | }
135 | 
136 | export function buildScatter({ rows, xKey, yKey, limit = 250 }) {
137 |   return rows
138 |     .map((row) => ({
139 |       [xKey]: safeNumber(row[xKey]),
140 |       [yKey]: safeNumber(row[yKey]),
141 |     }))
142 |     .filter((row) => row[xKey] !== null && row[yKey] !== null)
143 |     .slice(0, limit);
144 | }
145 | 
146 | export function buildTimeTrend({
147 |   rows,
148 |   xKey,
149 |   yKey,
150 |   aggregation = "sum",
151 |   limit = 30,
152 | }) {
153 |   const groups = new Map();
154 | 
155 |   for (const row of rows) {
156 |     const time = Date.parse(row[xKey]);
157 |     if (!Number.isFinite(time)) continue;
158 | 
159 |     const label = new Date(time).toISOString().slice(0, 10);
160 | 
161 |     if (!groups.has(label)) groups.set(label, []);
162 |     groups.get(label).push(row[yKey]);
163 |   }
164 | 
165 |   return [...groups.entries()]
166 |     .map(([date, values]) => ({
167 |       [xKey]: date,
168 |       [yKey]: round(aggregate(values, aggregation)),
169 |     }))
170 |     .sort((a, b) => String(a[xKey]).localeCompare(String(b[xKey])))
171 |     .slice(-limit);
172 | }
173 | 
174 | export function buildKpiFromSpec(rows = [], kpiSpec = {}) {
175 |   const aggregation = kpiSpec.aggregation || "count";
176 | 
177 |   let value;
178 | 
179 |   if (aggregation === "count" && !kpiSpec.metric) {
180 |     value = rows.length;
181 |   } else {
182 |     value = aggregate(
183 |       rows.map((row) => row[kpiSpec.metric]),
184 |       aggregation
185 |     );
186 |   }
187 | 
188 |   return {
189 |     id: kpiSpec.id || randomUUID(),
190 |     title: kpiSpec.title || "KPI",
191 |     value: formatNumber(round(value)),
192 |     subtitle: aggregation,
193 |     metric: kpiSpec.metric || null,
194 |     aggregation,
195 |     format: kpiSpec.format || "number",
196 |   };
197 | }
198 | 
199 | export function buildChartFromSpec(rows = [], chartSpec = {}) {
200 |   const type = chartSpec.type || "bar";
201 |   const xKey = chartSpec.xKey;
202 |   const yKey = chartSpec.yKey;
203 |   const aggregation = chartSpec.aggregation || "count";
204 |   const limit = chartSpec.limit || 10;
205 | 
206 |   let data = [];
207 | 
208 |   if (type === "histogram") {
209 |     data = buildHistogram({
210 |       rows,
211 |       key: yKey || xKey,
212 |       bins: limit,
213 |     });
214 | 
215 |     return {
216 |       id: chartSpec.id || randomUUID(),
217 |       type,
218 |       title: chartSpec.title || `${xKey} Distribution`,
219 |       xKey: "range",
220 |       yKey: "count",
221 |       aggregation: "count",
222 |       data,
223 |     };
224 |   }
225 | 
226 |   if (type === "scatter" && xKey && yKey) {
227 |     data = buildScatter({
228 |       rows,
229 |       xKey,
230 |       yKey,
231 |       limit,
232 |     });
233 | 
234 |     return {
235 |       id: chartSpec.id || randomUUID(),
236 |       type,
237 |       title: chartSpec.title || `${xKey} vs ${yKey}`,
238 |       xKey,
239 |       yKey,
240 |       aggregation: "raw",
241 |       data,
242 |     };
243 |   }
244 | 
245 |   if ((type === "line" || type === "area") && xKey && yKey) {
246 |     data = buildTimeTrend({
247 |       rows,
248 |       xKey,
249 |       yKey,
250 |       aggregation,
251 |       limit,
252 |     });
253 | 
254 |     return {
255 |       id: chartSpec.id || randomUUID(),
256 |       type,
257 |       title: chartSpec.title || `${yKey} Trend`,
258 |       xKey,
259 |       yKey,
260 |       aggregation,
261 |       data,
262 |     };
263 |   }
264 | 
265 |   data = groupByAggregate({
266 |     rows,
267 |     xKey,
268 |     yKey: yKey || xKey,
269 |     aggregation,
270 |     limit,
271 |   });
272 | 
273 |   return {
274 |     id: chartSpec.id || randomUUID(),
275 |     type,
276 |     title: chartSpec.title || `${aggregation} by ${xKey}`,
277 |     xKey,
278 |     yKey: aggregation === "count" ? "count" : yKey,
279 |     aggregation,
280 |     data,
281 |   };
282 | }
283 | 
284 | export function buildDashboardFromPlan({
285 |   rows = [],
286 |   filters = {},
287 |   dashboardPlan = {},
288 | }) {
289 |   const cleanRows = cleanDatasetRows(rows);
290 |   const filteredRows = applyFilters(cleanRows, filters);
291 | 
292 |   const kpis = (dashboardPlan.kpis || [])
293 |     .map((spec) => buildKpiFromSpec(filteredRows, spec))
294 |     .filter(Boolean);
295 | 
296 |   const charts = (dashboardPlan.charts || [])
297 |     .map((spec) => buildChartFromSpec(filteredRows, spec))
298 |     .filter((chart) => chart && chart.data && chart.data.length);
299 | 
300 |   return {
301 |     rows: filteredRows,
302 |     kpis,
303 |     charts,
304 |     filters,
305 |   };
306 | }
307 | 
```

### FILE: apps/backend/src/services/dashboard/dashboardFixEngine.js

Source path: `apps/backend/src/services/dashboard/dashboardFixEngine.js`

```js
 1 | import { generateSchemaProfile } from "./schemaProfiler.js";
 2 | import { planDashboardWithAI } from "../ai/dashboardPlanner.js";
 3 | import { buildDashboardFromPlan } from "./dashboardAnalytics.js";
 4 | 
 5 | export async function validateAndFixDashboard({
 6 |   rows = [],
 7 |   dataDictionary = [],
 8 |   datasetName = "Uploaded Dataset",
 9 |   currentDashboard = {},
10 | }) {
11 |   const schemaProfile = generateSchemaProfile({
12 |     rows,
13 |     dataDictionary,
14 |     datasetName,
15 |   });
16 | 
17 |   const issues = [];
18 | 
19 |   if (!currentDashboard?.kpis?.length) {
20 |     issues.push({
21 |       type: "missing_kpis",
22 |       message: "Dashboard has no KPI cards.",
23 |     });
24 |   }
25 | 
26 |   if (!currentDashboard?.charts?.length) {
27 |     issues.push({
28 |       type: "missing_charts",
29 |       message: "Dashboard has no charts.",
30 |     });
31 |   }
32 | 
33 |   const hasChartDataFromAI = (currentDashboard?.charts || []).some((chart) =>
34 |     Array.isArray(chart?.data)
35 |   );
36 | 
37 |   if (hasChartDataFromAI) {
38 |     issues.push({
39 |       type: "unsafe_chart_data",
40 |       message:
41 |         "Dashboard contained chart data. It was regenerated locally from chart specs.",
42 |     });
43 |   }
44 | 
45 |   const aiPlan = await planDashboardWithAI({
46 |     schemaProfile,
47 |     userQuery:
48 |       "Validate and regenerate a correct dashboard. Use schema only. Create useful KPIs and up to 7 charts.",
49 |     currentDashboard: {
50 |       kpis: currentDashboard.kpis || [],
51 |       charts: (currentDashboard.charts || []).map((chart) => ({
52 |         id: chart.id,
53 |         type: chart.type,
54 |         title: chart.title,
55 |         xKey: chart.xKey,
56 |         yKey: chart.yKey,
57 |         aggregation: chart.aggregation,
58 |       })),
59 |       filters: currentDashboard.filters || {},
60 |     },
61 |   });
62 | 
63 |   const correctedDashboard = buildDashboardFromPlan({
64 |     rows,
65 |     filters: currentDashboard.filters || {},
66 |     dashboardPlan: aiPlan.dashboardPlan,
67 |   });
68 | 
69 |   return {
70 |     action: "FIX_DASHBOARD",
71 |     message: "Dashboard validated and regenerated from schema + local data.",
72 |     schemaOnly: false,
73 |     provider: "local-integrity-engine",
74 |     issues,
75 |     observations: [
76 |       `Rows used locally: ${correctedDashboard.rows.length}`,
77 |       `KPIs generated: ${correctedDashboard.kpis.length}`,
78 |       `Charts generated: ${correctedDashboard.charts.length}`,
79 |       "LLM did not receive raw rows.",
80 |     ],
81 |     correctedDashboard,
82 |   };
83 | }
84 | 
```

### FILE: apps/backend/src/services/dashboard/schemaProfiler.js

Source path: `apps/backend/src/services/dashboard/schemaProfiler.js`

```js
  1 | function normalizeName(value = "") {
  2 |   return String(value)
  3 |     .trim()
  4 |     .toLowerCase()
  5 |     .replace(/[^a-z0-9]+/g, "_")
  6 |     .replace(/^_+|_+$/g, "");
  7 | }
  8 | 
  9 | function isMissing(value) {
 10 |   return value === null || value === undefined || value === "";
 11 | }
 12 | 
 13 | export function safeNumber(value) {
 14 |   if (typeof value === "number" && Number.isFinite(value)) return value;
 15 | 
 16 |   const parsed = Number(
 17 |     String(value ?? "")
 18 |       .replace(/[,$₹%\s]/g, "")
 19 |       .trim()
 20 |   );
 21 | 
 22 |   return Number.isFinite(parsed) ? parsed : null;
 23 | }
 24 | 
 25 | function isDateLike(value) {
 26 |   if (value instanceof Date) return true;
 27 |   if (typeof value !== "string") return false;
 28 |   if (!/\d/.test(value)) return false;
 29 | 
 30 |   const time = Date.parse(value);
 31 |   return Number.isFinite(time);
 32 | }
 33 | 
 34 | export function cleanDatasetRows(rows = []) {
 35 |   return rows.filter((row) => {
 36 |     const keys = Object.keys(row || {}).map(normalizeName);
 37 |     const source = String(
 38 |       row?._sourceFile || row?.source || row?.fileName || ""
 39 |     ).toLowerCase();
 40 | 
 41 |     const dictionaryShape =
 42 |       keys.includes("column") &&
 43 |       keys.includes("type") &&
 44 |       keys.includes("description");
 45 | 
 46 |     return !source.includes("dictionary") && !dictionaryShape;
 47 |   });
 48 | }
 49 | 
 50 | function getDictionaryMap(dataDictionary = []) {
 51 |   const map = new Map();
 52 | 
 53 |   for (const row of dataDictionary || []) {
 54 |     const column =
 55 |       row.column ||
 56 |       row.Column ||
 57 |       row.name ||
 58 |       row.Name ||
 59 |       row.field ||
 60 |       row.Field;
 61 | 
 62 |     if (!column) continue;
 63 | 
 64 |     map.set(String(column), {
 65 |       description:
 66 |         row.description ||
 67 |         row.Description ||
 68 |         row.meaning ||
 69 |         row.Meaning ||
 70 |         "",
 71 |       type: row.type || row.Type || "",
 72 |     });
 73 |   }
 74 | 
 75 |   return map;
 76 | }
 77 | 
 78 | function inferRole(name, type, uniqueCount, rowCount) {
 79 |   const lower = name.toLowerCase();
 80 | 
 81 |   if (/id$|_id$|uuid|identifier/.test(lower)) return "id";
 82 |   if (type === "date") return "date";
 83 | 
 84 |   if (
 85 |     type === "number" &&
 86 |     !/age|score|salary|sales|amount|price|revenue|profit|income|hours|level|rating|quantity/i.test(
 87 |       name
 88 |     ) &&
 89 |     uniqueCount > rowCount * 0.7
 90 |   ) {
 91 |     return "id";
 92 |   }
 93 | 
 94 |   if (type === "number") return "metric";
 95 | 
 96 |   if (/comment|review|text|description|summary|message/i.test(name)) {
 97 |     return "text";
 98 |   }
 99 | 
100 |   return "dimension";
101 | }
102 | 
103 | export function generateSchemaProfile({
104 |   rows = [],
105 |   dataDictionary = [],
106 |   datasetName = "Uploaded Dataset",
107 | }) {
108 |   const cleanRows = cleanDatasetRows(rows);
109 |   const dictionary = getDictionaryMap(dataDictionary);
110 | 
111 |   const columnNames = [
112 |     ...new Set(cleanRows.flatMap((row) => Object.keys(row || {}))),
113 |   ].filter((key) => !key.startsWith("__"));
114 | 
115 |   const columns = columnNames.map((name) => {
116 |     const values = cleanRows.map((row) => row[name]);
117 |     const presentValues = values.filter((value) => !isMissing(value));
118 |     const sample = presentValues.slice(0, 300);
119 | 
120 |     const numericCount = sample.filter((value) => safeNumber(value) !== null).length;
121 |     const dateCount = sample.filter(isDateLike).length;
122 | 
123 |     let type = "string";
124 | 
125 |     if (sample.length && numericCount / sample.length >= 0.85) {
126 |       type = "number";
127 |     } else if (sample.length && dateCount / sample.length >= 0.85) {
128 |       type = "date";
129 |     }
130 | 
131 |     const uniqueValues = [
132 |       ...new Set(presentValues.map((value) => String(value).trim())),
133 |     ].filter(Boolean);
134 | 
135 |     const numbers = presentValues
136 |       .map(safeNumber)
137 |       .filter((value) => value !== null)
138 |       .sort((a, b) => a - b);
139 | 
140 |     const stats =
141 |       type === "number" && numbers.length
142 |         ? {
143 |             min: numbers[0],
144 |             max: numbers[numbers.length - 1],
145 |             avg:
146 |               numbers.reduce((total, value) => total + value, 0) /
147 |               numbers.length,
148 |             median:
149 |               numbers.length % 2
150 |                 ? numbers[Math.floor(numbers.length / 2)]
151 |                 : (numbers[numbers.length / 2 - 1] +
152 |                     numbers[numbers.length / 2]) /
153 |                   2,
154 |           }
155 |         : null;
156 | 
157 |     return {
158 |       name,
159 |       type,
160 |       role: inferRole(name, type, uniqueValues.length, cleanRows.length),
161 |       description: dictionary.get(name)?.description || "",
162 |       uniqueCount: uniqueValues.length,
163 |       missingPct: cleanRows.length
164 |         ? Number(
165 |             (((values.length - presentValues.length) / cleanRows.length) * 100).toFixed(2)
166 |           )
167 |         : 0,
168 |       topValues: uniqueValues.slice(0, 12),
169 |       stats,
170 |     };
171 |   });
172 | 
173 |   return {
174 |     datasetName,
175 |     rowCount: cleanRows.length,
176 |     columnCount: columns.length,
177 |     columns,
178 |   };
179 | }
180 | 
```

### FILE: apps/backend/src/services/data-merger.js

Source path: `apps/backend/src/services/data-merger.js`

```js
  1 | import { randomUUID } from "node:crypto";
  2 | 
  3 | function normalizeColumnName(name) {
  4 |   if (!name || typeof name !== 'string') return name;
  5 |   return name.toLowerCase().trim().replace(/\s+/g, '_').replace(/[^a-z0-9_]/g, '');
  6 | }
  7 | 
  8 | function preprocessRow(row, columnMapping) {
  9 |   const normalized = {};
 10 |   for (const [originalKey, value] of Object.entries(row)) {
 11 |     const newKey = columnMapping[originalKey] || normalizeColumnName(originalKey) || originalKey;
 12 |     normalized[newKey] = value;
 13 |   }
 14 |   return normalized;
 15 | }
 16 | 
 17 | export function mergeDatasets(datasets) {
 18 |   if (!datasets || datasets.length === 0) {
 19 |     return null;
 20 |   }
 21 | 
 22 |   if (datasets.length === 1) {
 23 |     return preprocessSingleDataset(datasets[0]);
 24 |   }
 25 | 
 26 |   const columnMapping = {};
 27 |   const allNormalizedColumns = new Set();
 28 |   const columnTypes = {};
 29 |   
 30 |   datasets.forEach(ds => {
 31 |     (ds.columns || []).forEach(col => {
 32 |       const normalizedName = normalizeColumnName(col.name) || col.name;
 33 |       columnMapping[col.name] = normalizedName;
 34 |       allNormalizedColumns.add(normalizedName);
 35 |       if (!columnTypes[normalizedName]) {
 36 |         columnTypes[normalizedName] = col.type;
 37 |       } else if (columnTypes[normalizedName] !== col.type) {
 38 |         columnTypes[normalizedName] = inferMergedType(columnTypes[normalizedName], col.type);
 39 |       }
 40 |     });
 41 |   });
 42 | 
 43 |   const mergedColumns = Array.from(allNormalizedColumns).map(name => ({
 44 |     name,
 45 |     type: columnTypes[name] || 'string',
 46 |     sample: []
 47 |   }));
 48 | 
 49 |   if (datasets.length > 1) {
 50 |     mergedColumns.push({
 51 |       name: '__sourceFile',
 52 |       type: 'string',
 53 |       sample: datasets.slice(0, 3).map(d => d.name || 'unknown')
 54 |     });
 55 |   }
 56 | 
 57 |   const allRows = [];
 58 |   datasets.forEach(ds => {
 59 |     const sourceName = ds.name || 'unknown';
 60 |     (ds.rows || []).forEach(row => {
 61 |       const normalizedRow = preprocessRow(row, columnMapping);
 62 |       allRows.push({ ...normalizedRow, __sourceFile: sourceName });
 63 |     });
 64 |   });
 65 | 
 66 |   let rowId = 0;
 67 |   const finalRows = allRows.map(row => {
 68 |     const newRow = { __rowId: rowId++ };
 69 |     allNormalizedColumns.forEach(col => {
 70 |       newRow[col] = row[col] ?? null;
 71 |     });
 72 |     if (row.__sourceFile) {
 73 |       newRow.__sourceFile = row.__sourceFile;
 74 |     }
 75 |     return newRow;
 76 |   });
 77 | 
 78 |   const mergedName = datasets.length > 1 
 79 |     ? `Combined (${datasets.map(d => normalizeColumnName(d.name) || 'Data').join(', ')})`
 80 |     : normalizeColumnName(datasets[0]?.name) || 'Merged Dataset';
 81 | 
 82 |   return {
 83 |     id: randomUUID(),
 84 |     name: mergedName,
 85 |     columns: mergedColumns,
 86 |     rows: finalRows,
 87 |     rowCount: finalRows.length,
 88 |     sourceType: 'merged',
 89 |     uploadedAt: new Date().toISOString(),
 90 |     sourceFiles: datasets.map(d => d.name || 'Unknown'),
 91 |     _metadata: {
 92 |       originalDatasets: datasets.length,
 93 |       totalRows: finalRows.length,
 94 |       mergedAt: new Date().toISOString()
 95 |     }
 96 |   };
 97 | }
 98 | 
 99 | function preprocessSingleDataset(dataset) {
100 |   if (!dataset) return dataset;
101 |   const columnMapping = {};
102 |   const normalizedColumns = (dataset.columns || []).map(col => {
103 |     const normalizedName = normalizeColumnName(col.name) || col.name;
104 |     columnMapping[col.name] = normalizedName;
105 |     return { ...col, name: normalizedName };
106 |   });
107 |   const normalizedRows = (dataset.rows || []).map(row => preprocessRow(row, columnMapping));
108 |   return {
109 |     ...dataset,
110 |     columns: normalizedColumns,
111 |     rows: normalizedRows,
112 |     rowCount: normalizedRows.length
113 |   };
114 | }
115 | 
116 | function inferMergedType(type1, type2) {
117 |   if (type1 === type2) return type1;
118 |   if (type1 === 'number' || type2 === 'number') return 'number';
119 |   if (type1 === 'date' || type2 === 'date') return 'date';
120 |   return 'string';
121 | }
122 | 
123 | export function detectCompatibleColumns(datasets) {
124 |   const columnMap = new Map();
125 |   
126 |   datasets.forEach(ds => {
127 |     (ds.columns || []).forEach(col => {
128 |       if (!columnMap.has(col.name)) {
129 |         columnMap.set(col.name, {
130 |           name: col.name,
131 |           type: col.type,
132 |           sources: new Set()
133 |         });
134 |       }
135 |       const entry = columnMap.get(col.name);
136 |       entry.sources.add(ds.name || 'unknown');
137 |       if (entry.type !== col.type) {
138 |         entry.type = inferMergedType(entry.type, col.type);
139 |       }
140 |     });
141 |   });
142 | 
143 |   return Array.from(columnMap.values()).map(entry => ({
144 |     name: entry.name,
145 |     type: entry.type,
146 |     appearsIn: entry.sources.size,
147 |     isCommon: entry.sources.size === datasets.length
148 |   }));
149 | }
150 | 
151 | export function buildUnifiedSchema(datasets) {
152 |   if (!datasets || datasets.length === 0) {
153 |     return null;
154 |   }
155 | 
156 |   const merged = mergeDatasets(datasets);
157 |   const compatibleColumns = detectCompatibleColumns(datasets);
158 | 
159 |   return {
160 |     mergedDataset: merged,
161 |     columnAnalysis: compatibleColumns,
162 |     summary: {
163 |       totalDatasets: datasets.length,
164 |       totalColumns: compatibleColumns.length,
165 |       commonColumns: compatibleColumns.filter(c => c.isCommon).length,
166 |       uniqueColumns: compatibleColumns.filter(c => !c.isCommon).length
167 |     },
168 |     detectedDataTypes: detectDataTypes(compatibleColumns, merged.rows)
169 |   };
170 | }
171 | 
172 | function detectDataTypes(columns, rows) {
173 |   const types = {
174 |     SALES: ['revenue', 'sales', 'profit', 'amount', 'quantity', 'units', 'orders', 'customer', 'region', 'category', 'product'],
175 |     INVENTORY: ['inventory', 'stock', 'sku', 'warehouse', 'reorder'],
176 |     MARKETING: ['campaign', 'impression', 'click', 'conversion', 'ctr', 'cost', 'lead'],
177 |     HR: ['employee', 'salary', 'department', 'hire', 'performance'],
178 |     FINANCE: ['expense', 'budget', 'income', 'balance', 'asset', 'liability']
179 |   };
180 | 
181 |   const columnNames = columns.map(c => c.name.toLowerCase());
182 |   const detected = [];
183 | 
184 |   for (const [dataType, keywords] of Object.entries(types)) {
185 |     const matches = keywords.filter(kw => columnNames.some(cn => cn.includes(kw)));
186 |     if (matches.length >= 2) {
187 |       detected.push({ type: dataType, confidence: matches.length / keywords.length, matches });
188 |     }
189 |   }
190 | 
191 |   return detected.sort((a, b) => b.confidence - a.confidence);
192 | }
193 | 
194 | export default { mergeDatasets, detectCompatibleColumns, buildUnifiedSchema };
195 | 
```

### FILE: apps/backend/src/services/data-sampling-service.js

Source path: `apps/backend/src/services/data-sampling-service.js`

```js
 1 | /**
 2 |  * Data Sampling Service
 3 |  * Efficient sampling for large datasets
 4 |  */
 5 | 
 6 | export function stratifiedSample(rows, sampleSize = 1000) {
 7 |   if (rows.length <= sampleSize) return rows;
 8 | 
 9 |   const sampled = [];
10 |   const step = Math.floor(rows.length / sampleSize);
11 | 
12 |   for (let i = 0; i < rows.length; i += step) {
13 |     if (sampled.length < sampleSize) {
14 |       sampled.push(rows[i]);
15 |     }
16 |   }
17 | 
18 |   return sampled;
19 | }
20 | 
21 | export function randomSample(rows, sampleSize = 1000) {
22 |   if (rows.length <= sampleSize) return rows;
23 | 
24 |   const sampled = [];
25 |   const indices = new Set();
26 | 
27 |   while (sampled.length < sampleSize) {
28 |     const idx = Math.floor(Math.random() * rows.length);
29 |     if (!indices.has(idx)) {
30 |       indices.add(idx);
31 |       sampled.push(rows[idx]);
32 |     }
33 |   }
34 | 
35 |   return sampled;
36 | }
37 | 
38 | export function clusteredSample(rows, columns, sampleSize = 1000) {
39 |   if (rows.length <= sampleSize) return rows;
40 | 
41 |   const groups = {};
42 |   const catCol = columns.find(c => c.type === "string");
43 | 
44 |   if (!catCol) return randomSample(rows, sampleSize);
45 | 
46 |   rows.forEach((row, idx) => {
47 |     const key = row[catCol.name];
48 |     if (!groups[key]) groups[key] = [];
49 |     groups[key].push(idx);
50 |   });
51 | 
52 |   const groupSize = Math.floor(sampleSize / Object.keys(groups).length);
53 |   const sampled = [];
54 | 
55 |   for (const groupIndices of Object.values(groups)) {
56 |     for (let i = 0; i < Math.min(groupSize, groupIndices.length); i++) {
57 |       sampled.push(rows[groupIndices[i]]);
58 |     }
59 |   }
60 | 
61 |   return sampled.slice(0, sampleSize);
62 | }
```

### FILE: apps/backend/src/services/data-visualization-service.js

Source path: `apps/backend/src/services/data-visualization-service.js`

```js
  1 | /**
  2 |  * Advanced Data Visualization Service
  3 |  * Generates sophisticated charts and visualizations
  4 |  */
  5 | 
  6 | export function generateAdvancedCharts(dataset) {
  7 |   const charts = [];
  8 | 
  9 |   const numericCols = dataset.columns.filter(c => c.type === "number");
 10 |   for (const col of numericCols) {
 11 |     charts.push(generateHistogram(dataset, col));
 12 |   }
 13 | 
 14 |   const categoricalCols = dataset.columns.filter(c => c.type === "string");
 15 |   for (const col of categoricalCols) {
 16 |     charts.push(generateCategoryChart(dataset, col));
 17 |   }
 18 | 
 19 |   if (numericCols.length > 1) {
 20 |     charts.push(generateCorrelationHeatmap(dataset, numericCols));
 21 |   }
 22 | 
 23 |   return charts.filter(Boolean);
 24 | }
 25 | 
 26 | function generateHistogram(dataset, column) {
 27 |   const values = dataset.rows
 28 |     .map(r => r[column.name])
 29 |     .filter(v => v !== null && v !== undefined && !isNaN(v))
 30 |     .map(Number);
 31 | 
 32 |   if (values.length === 0) return null;
 33 | 
 34 |   const binCount = Math.min(10, Math.ceil(Math.sqrt(values.length)));
 35 |   const min = Math.min(...values);
 36 |   const max = Math.max(...values);
 37 |   const binWidth = (max - min) / binCount;
 38 | 
 39 |   const bins = Array(binCount).fill(0);
 40 |   values.forEach(val => {
 41 |     const binIdx = Math.floor((val - min) / binWidth);
 42 |     bins[Math.min(binIdx, binCount - 1)]++;
 43 |   });
 44 | 
 45 |   const formatNumber = (num) => {
 46 |     if (num >= 1000000) return `$${(num / 1000000).toFixed(1)}M`;
 47 |     if (num >= 1000) return `$${(num / 1000).toFixed(0)}K`;
 48 |     return num.toFixed(0);
 49 |   };
 50 | 
 51 |   return {
 52 |     type: "histogram",
 53 |     title: `${column.name} Distribution`,
 54 |     column: column.name,
 55 |     data: bins.map((count, i) => {
 56 |       const rangeStart = min + i * binWidth;
 57 |       const rangeEnd = min + (i + 1) * binWidth;
 58 |       return {
 59 |         range: `${formatNumber(rangeStart)}-${formatNumber(rangeEnd)}`,
 60 |         rangeStart,
 61 |         rangeEnd,
 62 |         count,
 63 |       };
 64 |     }),
 65 |   };
 66 | }
 67 | 
 68 | function generateCategoryChart(dataset, column) {
 69 |   const freq = {};
 70 |   dataset.rows.forEach(row => {
 71 |     const val = String(row[column.name]);
 72 |     freq[val] = (freq[val] || 0) + 1;
 73 |   });
 74 | 
 75 |   const sorted = Object.entries(freq)
 76 |     .sort((a, b) => b[1] - a[1])
 77 |     .slice(0, 10);
 78 | 
 79 |   return {
 80 |     type: "bar",
 81 |     title: `Count by ${column.name}`,
 82 |     column: column.name,
 83 |     data: sorted.map(([category, count]) => ({ category, count })),
 84 |   };
 85 | }
 86 | 
 87 | function generateCorrelationHeatmap(dataset, numericCols) {
 88 |   const matrix = [];
 89 | 
 90 |   for (let i = 0; i < numericCols.length; i++) {
 91 |     const row = [];
 92 |     for (let j = 0; j < numericCols.length; j++) {
 93 |       if (i === j) {
 94 |         row.push(1);
 95 |       } else if (i > j) {
 96 |         row.push(matrix[j][i]);
 97 |       } else {
 98 |         const x = dataset.rows.map(r => Number(r[numericCols[i].name])).filter(v => !isNaN(v));
 99 |         const y = dataset.rows.map(r => Number(r[numericCols[j].name])).filter(v => !isNaN(v));
100 |         const corr = calculateCorrelation(x, y);
101 |         row.push(corr);
102 |       }
103 |     }
104 |     matrix.push(row);
105 |   }
106 | 
107 |   return {
108 |     type: "heatmap",
109 |     title: "Correlation Matrix",
110 |     columns: numericCols.map(c => c.name),
111 |     matrix,
112 |   };
113 | }
114 | 
115 | function calculateCorrelation(x, y) {
116 |   if (x.length < 2) return 0;
117 |   const n = Math.min(x.length, y.length);
118 |   const sx = x.slice(0, n);
119 |   const sy = y.slice(0, n);
120 | 
121 |   const meanX = sx.reduce((a, b) => a + b) / n;
122 |   const meanY = sy.reduce((a, b) => a + b) / n;
123 | 
124 |   let num = 0, denX = 0, denY = 0;
125 |   for (let i = 0; i < n; i++) {
126 |     const dx = sx[i] - meanX;
127 |     const dy = sy[i] - meanY;
128 |     num += dx * dy;
129 |     denX += dx * dx;
130 |     denY += dy * dy;
131 |   }
132 | 
133 |   const den = Math.sqrt(denX * denY);
134 |   return den === 0 ? 0 : num / den;
135 | }
136 | 
```

### FILE: apps/backend/src/services/dataset-role-detector.js

Source path: `apps/backend/src/services/dataset-role-detector.js`

```js
  1 | const DICTIONARY_NAMES = /dictionary|schema|metadata|data_dictionary|fields/i;
  2 | const TRAIN_NAMES = /(^|[_\-. ])train([_\-. ]|$)/i;
  3 | const TEST_NAMES = /(^|[_\-. ])test([_\-. ]|$)/i;
  4 | 
  5 | function getColumnNames(file) {
  6 |   if (Array.isArray(file.columns) && file.columns.length) {
  7 |     return file.columns.map((c) => String(c.name || c).toLowerCase());
  8 |   }
  9 | 
 10 |   const first = file.rows?.[0] || {};
 11 |   return Object.keys(first).map((key) => key.toLowerCase());
 12 | }
 13 | 
 14 | export function detectDatasetRole(file = {}) {
 15 |   const fileName = String(file.fileName || file.name || "");
 16 |   const columns = getColumnNames(file);
 17 |   const rowCount = file.rows?.length || 0;
 18 | 
 19 |   const dictionaryColumns = [
 20 |     "column",
 21 |     "column_name",
 22 |     "field",
 23 |     "field_name",
 24 |     "description",
 25 |     "type",
 26 |     "target",
 27 |     "meaning",
 28 |   ];
 29 | 
 30 |   const dictionaryHits = columns.filter((col) =>
 31 |     dictionaryColumns.some((d) => col.includes(d))
 32 |   ).length;
 33 | 
 34 |   if (DICTIONARY_NAMES.test(fileName) || dictionaryHits >= 2) {
 35 |     return {
 36 |       role: "metadata_dictionary",
 37 |       confidence: DICTIONARY_NAMES.test(fileName) ? 0.98 : 0.85,
 38 |       reason: "Looks like a schema/data dictionary file.",
 39 |     };
 40 |   }
 41 | 
 42 |   if (TRAIN_NAMES.test(fileName)) {
 43 |     return {
 44 |       role: "primary_data",
 45 |       confidence: 0.95,
 46 |       reason: "Filename indicates training/main dataset.",
 47 |     };
 48 |   }
 49 | 
 50 |   if (TEST_NAMES.test(fileName)) {
 51 |     return {
 52 |       role: "test_data",
 53 |       confidence: 0.95,
 54 |       reason: "Filename indicates test/prediction dataset.",
 55 |     };
 56 |   }
 57 | 
 58 |   if (rowCount <= 50 && dictionaryHits >= 1) {
 59 |     return {
 60 |       role: "metadata_dictionary",
 61 |       confidence: 0.75,
 62 |       reason: "Small file with metadata-like columns.",
 63 |     };
 64 |   }
 65 | 
 66 |   return {
 67 |     role: "primary_data",
 68 |     confidence: 0.65,
 69 |     reason: "Default analyzable dataset.",
 70 |   };
 71 | }
 72 | 
 73 | export function classifyUploadedDatasets(datasets = []) {
 74 |   const classified = datasets.map((dataset) => ({
 75 |     ...dataset,
 76 |     detectedRole: detectDatasetRole(dataset),
 77 |   }));
 78 | 
 79 |   const metadataFiles = classified.filter(
 80 |     (d) => d.detectedRole.role === "metadata_dictionary"
 81 |   );
 82 | 
 83 |   const testFiles = classified.filter((d) => d.detectedRole.role === "test_data");
 84 | 
 85 |   const primaryFiles = classified.filter(
 86 |     (d) => d.detectedRole.role === "primary_data"
 87 |   );
 88 | 
 89 |   const primaryDataset =
 90 |     primaryFiles.find((d) => /train/i.test(d.fileName || d.name || "")) ||
 91 |     primaryFiles[0] ||
 92 |     classified.find((d) => d.detectedRole.role !== "metadata_dictionary");
 93 | 
 94 |   return {
 95 |     classified,
 96 |     primaryDataset,
 97 |     metadataFiles,
 98 |     testFiles,
 99 |     analyzableDatasets: classified.filter(
100 |       (d) => d.detectedRole.role !== "metadata_dictionary"
101 |     ),
102 |   };
103 | }
104 | 
```

```js
 1 | export function detectDomain(columns = [], dictionary = {}) {
 2 |   const names = columns.map((c) => String(c.name || c).toLowerCase()).join(" ");
 3 |   const dictText = JSON.stringify(dictionary).toLowerCase();
 4 | 
 5 |   const text = `${names} ${dictText}`;
 6 | 
 7 |   if (/salary|experience|education|framework|language|company_size|job/.test(text)) {
 8 |     return {
 9 |       domain: "salary_hr_jobs",
10 |       confidence: 0.95,
11 |       label: "Salary / HR / Jobs",
12 |     };
13 |   }
14 | 
15 |   if (/revenue|sales|order|product|customer|profit|amount/.test(text)) {
16 |     return {
17 |       domain: "sales",
18 |       confidence: 0.9,
19 |       label: "Sales Analytics",
20 |     };
21 |   }
22 | 
23 |   if (/student|marks|score|attendance|class|course|grade/.test(text)) {
24 |     return {
25 |       domain: "education",
26 |       confidence: 0.88,
27 |       label: "Education Analytics",
28 |     };
29 |   }
30 | 
31 |   if (/transaction|balance|credit|debit|loan|bank|finance/.test(text)) {
32 |     return {
33 |       domain: "finance",
34 |       confidence: 0.82,
35 |       label: "Finance Analytics",
36 |     };
37 |   }
38 | 
39 |   return {
40 |     domain: "generic",
41 |     confidence: 0.5,
42 |     label: "Generic Dataset",
43 |   };
44 | }
45 |
```

```js
 1 | /**
 2 |  * Data Export Service
 3 |  * Export analyses and reports in multiple formats
 4 |  */
 5 | 
 6 | export function exportToJSON(analysis, filename = "analysis") {
 7 |   const json = JSON.stringify(analysis, null, 2);
 8 |   return {
 9 |     data: json,
10 |     filename: `${filename}.json`,
11 |     mimeType: "application/json",
12 |   };
13 | }
14 | 
15 | export function exportToCSV(data, filename = "export") {
16 |   if (!Array.isArray(data) || data.length === 0) {
17 |     return { data: "", filename: `${filename}.csv`, mimeType: "text/csv" };
18 |   }
19 | 
20 |   const headers = Object.keys(data[0]);
21 |   const csv = [
22 |     headers.join(","),
23 |     ...data.map(row =>
24 |       headers.map(h => JSON.stringify(row[h])).join(",")
25 |     )
26 |   ].join("\n");
27 | 
28 |   return { data: csv, filename: `${filename}.csv`, mimeType: "text/csv" };
29 | }
30 | 
31 | export function exportToMarkdown(analysis, datasetName = "Dataset") {
32 |   let md = `# Analysis Report: ${datasetName}\n\n`;
33 |   md += `Generated: ${new Date().toISOString()}\n\n`;
34 | 
35 |   if (analysis.profile) {
36 |     md += `## Dataset Profile\n`;
37 |     md += `- Rows: ${analysis.profile.summary.totalRows}\n`;
38 |     md += `- Columns: ${analysis.profile.summary.totalColumns}\n`;
39 |     md += `- Quality Score: ${analysis.profile.summary.quality}%\n\n`;
40 |   }
41 | 
42 |   if (analysis.findings) {
43 |     md += `## Key Findings\n`;
44 |     analysis.findings.forEach(finding => {
45 |       md += `- ${finding}\n`;
46 |     });
47 |     md += "\n";
48 |   }
49 | 
50 |   return { data: md, filename: `${datasetName}-report.md`, mimeType: "text/markdown" };
51 | }
```

### FILE: apps/backend/src/services/gemini-ai-service.js

Source path: `apps/backend/src/services/gemini-ai-service.js`

```js
  1 | import { GoogleGenerativeAI } from "@google/generative-ai";
  2 | import {
  3 |   GEMINI_SYSTEM_PROMPT,
  4 |   GEMINI_CONFIG,
  5 |   TIMEOUT_CONFIG,
  6 | } from "../config/gemini-config.js";
  7 | import {
  8 |   buildSchemaPacketAsync,
  9 |   formatSchemaForPrompt,
 10 |   validateColumnsExist,
 11 | } from "./schema-packet-builder.js";
 12 | 
 13 | let lastGeminiConfigState = null;
 14 | 
 15 | const readGeminiApiKey = () =>
 16 |   process.env.GEMINI_API_KEY?.trim() || process.env.GOOGLE_API_KEY?.trim() || "";
 17 | 
 18 | /**
 19 |  * Check if Gemini is configured
 20 |  */
 21 | export function isGeminiConfigured() {
 22 |   const configured = Boolean(readGeminiApiKey());
 23 | 
 24 |   if (lastGeminiConfigState !== configured) {
 25 |     console.log(`[gemini-ai] Config status: ${configured ? "CONFIGURED" : "NOT CONFIGURED"}`);
 26 |     lastGeminiConfigState = configured;
 27 |   }
 28 | 
 29 |   return configured;
 30 | }
 31 | 
 32 | /**
 33 |  * Call Gemini AI with schema packet
 34 |  */
 35 | export async function callGeminiAI(dataset, query) {
 36 |   if (!isGeminiConfigured()) {
 37 |     throw new Error("Gemini API key not configured");
 38 |   }
 39 | 
 40 |   try {
 41 |     // Build schema packet
 42 |     const schemaPacket = await buildSchemaPacketAsync(dataset);
 43 |     const schemaText = formatSchemaForPrompt(schemaPacket);
 44 | 
 45 |     // Initialize Gemini
 46 |     console.log("[gemini-ai] Initializing with model:", GEMINI_CONFIG.model);
 47 |     const genAI = new GoogleGenerativeAI(readGeminiApiKey());
 48 |     const model = genAI.getGenerativeModel({
 49 |       model: GEMINI_CONFIG.model,
 50 |       generationConfig: {
 51 |         temperature: GEMINI_CONFIG.temperature,
 52 |         topP: GEMINI_CONFIG.topP,
 53 |         topK: GEMINI_CONFIG.topK,
 54 |         maxOutputTokens: [REDACTED_SECRET],
 55 |         responseMimeType: "application/json",
 56 |       },
 57 |       systemInstruction: GEMINI_SYSTEM_PROMPT,
 58 |     });
 59 | 
 60 |     // Prepare prompt
 61 |     const userPrompt = `
 62 | SCHEMA INFORMATION:
 63 | ${schemaText}
 64 | 
 65 | USER QUERY:
 66 | "${query}"
 67 | 
 68 | Analyze this query and respond with JSON.
 69 | `;
 70 | 
 71 |     console.log("[gemini-ai] Sending request to Gemini...");
 72 |     console.log("[gemini-ai] Schema length:", schemaText.length, "chars");
 73 | 
 74 |     // Create timeout promise
 75 |     const timeoutPromise = new Promise((_, reject) => {
 76 |       setTimeout(
 77 |         () => reject(new Error("API timeout")),
 78 |         TIMEOUT_CONFIG.API_CALL_MS
 79 |       );
 80 |     });
 81 | 
 82 |     // Make API call with timeout
 83 |     console.log("[gemini-ai] Waiting for response...");
 84 |     const response = await Promise.race([
 85 |       model.generateContent(userPrompt),
 86 |       timeoutPromise,
 87 |     ]);
 88 |     console.log("[gemini-ai] Response received, parsing...");
 89 | 
 90 |     // Parse response
 91 |     const responseText = response.response.text();
 92 |     let aiResponse;
 93 |     try {
 94 |       aiResponse = JSON.parse(responseText);
 95 |     } catch (parseError) {
 96 |       console.error("[gemini-ai] JSON parse error:", parseError.message);
 97 |       console.error("[gemini-ai] Raw response:", responseText.substring(0, 500));
 98 |       throw new Error(`Invalid JSON response from Gemini: ${responseText.substring(0, 100)}`);
 99 |     }
100 | 
101 |     // Validate columns
102 |     validateColumnsExist(aiResponse.columns_used, schemaPacket);
103 | 
104 |     return {
105 |       success: true,
106 |       ...aiResponse,
107 |       usedAI: true,
108 |     };
109 |   } catch (error) {
110 |     console.warn("[gemini-ai] Error:", error.message);
111 |     return {
112 |       success: false,
113 |       error: error.message,
114 |       usedAI: false,
115 |       shouldFallback: true,
116 |     };
117 |   }
118 | }
119 | 
120 | /**
121 |  * Validate AI response structure
122 |  */
123 | export function validateAIResponse(response) {
124 |   const required = ["intent", "columns_used", "sql", "insight", "chart_type", "confidence"];
125 |   const errors = [];
126 | 
127 |   for (const field of required) {
128 |     if (!(field in response)) {
129 |       errors.push(`Missing: ${field}`);
130 |     }
131 |   }
132 | 
133 |   if (typeof response.confidence !== "number" || response.confidence < 0 || response.confidence > 1) {
134 |     errors.push("Invalid confidence");
135 |   }
136 | 
137 |   return {
138 |     valid: errors.length === 0,
139 |     errors,
140 |   };
141 | }
142 | 
143 | /**
144 |  * Sanitize SQL query for execution
145 |  */
146 | export function sanitizeSQL(sql) {
147 |   if (!sql) return null;
148 |   return sql.replace(/```sql/g, "").replace(/```/g, "").trim();
149 | }
150 | 
151 | /**
152 |  * Get confidence description
153 |  */
154 | export function getConfidenceLevel(score) {
155 |   if (score >= 0.9) return "High";
156 |   if (score >= 0.7) return "Medium";
157 |   return "Low";
158 | }
159 | 
```

### FILE: apps/backend/src/services/llama-chat-agent.js

```js
 1 | import {
 2 |   callDatasetChat,
 3 |   OLLAMA_MODELS,
 4 | } from './ollama/ollama-dual-model-service.js';
 5 | import { buildDatasetFacts } from './ollama/dataset-schema-summary.js';
 6 | 
 7 | function fallbackAnswer(query, facts) {
 8 |   const text = String(query || '').toLowerCase();
 9 | 
10 |   if (/row|record|how many/.test(text)) {
11 |     return `The dataset has ${facts.rowCount.toLocaleString()} rows and ${facts.columnCount.toLocaleString()} columns.`;
12 |   }
13 | 
14 |   if (/column|schema/.test(text)) {
15 |     return `The dataset columns are: ${facts.schema.columns
16 |       .map((column) => `${column.name} (${column.type})`)
17 |       .join(', ')}.`;
18 |   }
19 | 
20 |   const firstMetric = facts.facts.numericColumns[0];
21 | 
22 |   if (firstMetric && /average|avg|mean/.test(text)) {
23 |     return `The average ${firstMetric.name} is ${firstMetric.stats?.avg ?? 'not available'}.`;
24 |   }
25 | 
26 |   return `I analyzed the dataset schema. It has ${facts.rowCount.toLocaleString()} rows and ${facts.columnCount.toLocaleString()} columns. Ask about columns, averages, totals, categories, trends, or comparisons.`;
27 | }
28 | 
29 | export async function runLlamaDatasetChat(dataset, query) {
30 |   const facts = buildDatasetFacts(dataset);
31 | 
32 |   const safeContext = {
33 |     datasetName: facts.schema.datasetName,
34 |     rowCount: facts.schema.rowCount,
35 |     columnCount: facts.schema.columnCount,
36 |     columns: facts.schema.columns,
37 |     facts: facts.facts,
38 |     privacy: {
39 |       schemaOnly: true,
40 |       rawRowsSentToAI: false,
41 |     },
42 |   };
43 | 
44 |   const prompt = `
45 | You are InsightFlow AI Chat.
46 | 
47 | You answer questions about an uploaded dataset.
48 | 
49 | Important rules:
50 | - You receive schema and calculated statistics only.
51 | - Raw rows are not provided.
52 | - Do not invent exact values beyond provided facts.
53 | - If a value is not present in the facts, say it needs local calculation.
54 | - Keep the answer useful and concise.
55 | 
56 | User question:
57 | ${query}
58 | 
59 | Dataset safe context:
60 | ${JSON.stringify(safeContext, null, 2)}
61 | `;
62 | 
63 |   try {
64 |     const result = await callDatasetChat([
65 |       {
66 |         role: 'user',
67 |         content: prompt,
68 |       },
69 |     ]);
70 | 
71 |     return {
72 |       content: result.content,
73 |       provider: 'ollama',
74 |       model: OLLAMA_MODELS.chat,
75 |       schemaOnly: true,
76 |       insights: [],
77 |       chart: null,
78 |     };
79 |   } catch (error) {
80 |     return {
81 |       content: fallbackAnswer(query, facts),
82 |       provider: 'fallback',
83 |       model: OLLAMA_MODELS.chat,
84 |       schemaOnly: true,
85 |       aiError: error.message,
86 |       insights: [],
87 |       chart: null,
88 |     };
89 |   }
90 | }
91 | 
```

### FILE: apps/backend/src/services/llama-validation-middleware.js

Source path: `apps/backend/src/services/llama-validation-middleware.js`

```js
  1 | /**
  2 |  * Dual-Layer Validation Middleware for Llama 3.2
  3 |  * Optimized for 16GB RAM laptops
  4 |  * 
  5 |  * Layer 1: Query Parser - Converts natural language to analysis plan
  6 |  * Layer 2: Response Validator - Validates and corrects the analysis
  7 |  */
  8 | 
  9 | const OLLAMA_BASE_URL = process.env.OLLAMA_BASE_URL || "http://localhost:11434";
 10 | const OLLAMA_MODEL = process.env.OLLAMA_MODEL || "llama3.2";
 11 | const API_TIMEOUT = parseInt(process.env.AI_TIMEOUT_MS) || 60000;
 12 | 
 13 | class LlamaValidationMiddleware {
 14 |   constructor() {
 15 |     this.model = OLLAMA_MODEL;
 16 |     this.timeout = API_TIMEOUT;
 17 |     this.temperature = 0.1;
 18 |     this.cache = new Map();
 19 |   }
 20 | 
 21 |   /**
 22 |    * LAYER 1: Query Parser
 23 |    * Convert natural language to structured analysis
 24 |    */
 25 |   async parseQuery(userQuery, schema) {
 26 |     console.log('\n🔍 [LAYER 1] Parsing query with Llama 3.2...');
 27 |     console.log(`Query: "${userQuery}"`);
 28 | 
 29 |     const cacheKey = `parse_${userQuery}`;
 30 |     if (this.cache.has(cacheKey)) {
 31 |       console.log('✅ Using cached result');
 32 |       return this.cache.get(cacheKey);
 33 |     }
 34 | 
 35 |     const systemPrompt = `You are a data analytics expert. Convert user queries into structured JSON format.
 36 | 
 37 | SCHEMA AVAILABLE:
 38 | ${this.formatSchema(schema)}
 39 | 
 40 | RESPOND WITH JSON ONLY (no other text):
 41 | {
 42 |   "intent": "aggregation|comparison|trend|distribution|count|filter|unclear",
 43 |   "primary_column": "column_name",
 44 |   "secondary_column": "column_name_or_null",
 45 |   "aggregation": "sum|avg|count|min|max",
 46 |   "chart_type": "bar|line|pie|scatter|histogram|table",
 47 |   "filters": [],
 48 |   "top_n": 10,
 49 |   "confidence": 0.8,
 50 |   "reasoning": "brief explanation"
 51 | }`;
 52 | 
 53 |     const userPrompt = `Convert this query: "${userQuery}"`;
 54 | 
 55 |     try {
 56 |       const response = await this.callLlama(
 57 |         `${systemPrompt}\n\n${userPrompt}`,
 58 |         300
 59 |       );
 60 | 
 61 |       const parsed = this.extractJSON(response);
 62 |       
 63 |       const validationErrors = this.validateColumns(parsed, schema);
 64 |       if (validationErrors.length > 0) {
 65 |         console.warn('⚠️  Column validation errors:', validationErrors);
 66 |         parsed.validation_errors = validationErrors;
 67 |       }
 68 | 
 69 |       const result = {
 70 |         success: true,
 71 |         data: parsed,
 72 |         layer: 'parser',
 73 |       };
 74 | 
 75 |       this.cache.set(cacheKey, result);
 76 |       console.log('✅ Query parsed successfully');
 77 |       return result;
 78 | 
 79 |     } catch (error) {
 80 |       console.error('❌ Parse error:', error.message);
 81 |       return {
 82 |         success: false,
 83 |         error: error.message,
 84 |         fallback: this.getDefaultParsing(userQuery, schema),
 85 |       };
 86 |     }
 87 |   }
 88 | 
 89 |   /**
 90 |    * LAYER 2: Response Validator
 91 |    * Validate and correct the analysis
 92 |    */
 93 |   async validateAndCorrect(queryResult, schema, dataset) {
 94 |     console.log('\n✅ [LAYER 2] Validating analysis with Llama 3.2...');
 95 | 
 96 |     const cacheKey = `validate_${JSON.stringify(queryResult)}`;
 97 |     if (this.cache.has(cacheKey)) {
 98 |       console.log('✅ Using cached validation');
 99 |       return this.cache.get(cacheKey);
100 |     }
101 | 
102 |     const systemPrompt = `You are a data validation expert. Review and correct this query analysis.
103 | 
104 | SCHEMA:
105 | ${this.formatSchema(schema)}
106 | 
107 | VALIDATION RULES:
108 | 1. All columns must exist in schema
109 | 2. Aggregation must match column type (numeric for sum/avg)
110 | 3. Chart type must suit the data
111 | 4. Filters must be valid
112 | 
113 | RESPOND WITH JSON ONLY:
114 | {
115 |   "valid": true/false,
116 |   "corrected": { ...corrected_analysis... },
117 |   "errors": ["error1"],
118 |   "corrections": ["what_was_fixed"],
119 |   "validation_score": 0.85,
120 |   "warnings": ["warning1"]
121 | }`;
122 | 
123 |     const userPrompt = `Review this analysis:
124 | ${JSON.stringify(queryResult, null, 2)}`;
125 | 
126 |     try {
127 |       const response = await this.callLlama(
128 |         `${systemPrompt}\n\n${userPrompt}`,
129 |         350
130 |       );
131 | 
132 |       const validated = this.extractJSON(response);
133 | 
134 |       const result = {
135 |         success: true,
136 |         valid: validated.valid,
137 |         corrected: validated.corrected || queryResult,
138 |         errors: validated.errors || [],
139 |         corrections: validated.corrections || [],
140 |         score: validated.validation_score || 0.7,
141 |         warnings: validated.warnings || [],
142 |         layer: 'validator',
143 |       };
144 | 
145 |       this.cache.set(cacheKey, result);
146 | 
147 |       if (result.valid) {
148 |         console.log(`✅ Validation passed (score: ${result.score})`);
149 |       } else {
150 |         console.log(`⚠️  Validation failed - using corrections`);
151 |         console.log(`Corrections made: ${result.corrections.join(', ')}`);
152 |       }
153 | 
154 |       return result;
155 | 
156 |     } catch (error) {
157 |       console.error('❌ Validation error:', error.message);
158 |       return {
159 |         success: false,
160 |         error: error.message,
161 |         valid: null,
162 |         corrected: queryResult,
163 |         score: 0.5,
164 |       };
165 |     }
166 |   }
167 | 
168 |   /**
169 |    * MAIN PIPELINE: Process query through both layers
170 |    */
171 |   async processQuery(userQuery, dataset, schema) {
172 |     console.log('\n╔════════════════════════════════════════════╗');
173 |     console.log('║  🚀 LLAMA 3.2 VALIDATION PIPELINE START  ║');
174 |     console.log('╚════════════════════════════════════════════╝');
175 | 
176 |     const parseResult = await this.parseQuery(userQuery, schema);
177 |     
178 |     if (!parseResult.success) {
179 |       console.error('❌ Query parsing failed');
180 |       return {
181 |         success: false,
182 |         error: 'Failed to parse query',
183 |         fallback: parseResult.fallback,
184 |       };
185 |     }
186 | 
187 |     const queryData = parseResult.data;
188 | 
189 |     const validationResult = await this.validateAndCorrect(
190 |       queryData,
191 |       schema,
192 |       dataset
193 |     );
194 | 
195 |     if (!validationResult.success) {
196 |       console.warn('⚠️  Validation had issues, but continuing with corrections');
197 |     }
198 | 
199 |     const correctedAnalysis = validationResult.corrected || queryData;
200 | 
201 |     console.log('\n📊 Generating dashboard...');
202 |     const dashboard = this.generateDashboard(
203 |       correctedAnalysis,
204 |       dataset,
205 |       schema,
206 |       { parseResult, validationResult }
207 |     );
208 | 
209 |     console.log('\n╔════════════════════════════════════════════╗');
210 |     console.log('║  ✨ PIPELINE COMPLETE - DASHBOARD READY  ║');
211 |     console.log('╚════════════════════════════════════════════╝\n');
212 | 
213 |     return dashboard;
214 |   }
215 | 
216 |   /**
217 |    * Generate final dashboard
218 |    */
219 |   generateDashboard(analysis, dataset, schema, metadata) {
220 |     const { primary_column, secondary_column, aggregation, chart_type, intent } = analysis;
221 | 
222 |     if (!primary_column) {
223 |       return {
224 |         success: false,
225 |         error: 'No primary column identified',
226 |       };
227 |     }
228 | 
229 |     const chartData = this.buildChartData(
230 |       dataset.rows || [],
231 |       primary_column,
232 |       secondary_column,
233 |       aggregation || 'sum'
234 |     );
235 | 
236 |     if (chartData.length === 0) {
237 |       return {
238 |         success: false,
239 |         error: 'No data to display',
240 |       };
241 |     }
242 | 
243 |     return {
244 |       success: true,
245 |       analysis: analysis,
246 |       chart: {
247 |         type: chart_type || 'bar',
248 |         title: this.generateChartTitle(analysis),
249 |         data: chartData,
250 |         xKey: primary_column,
251 |         yKey: secondary_column || 'value',
252 |         config: {
253 |           responsive: true,
254 |           maintainAspectRatio: true,
255 |         },
256 |       },
257 |       sql: this.generateSQL(analysis),
258 |       metadata: {
259 |         model: this.model,
260 |         validation_score: metadata.validationResult?.score,
261 |         parser_confidence: analysis.confidence,
262 |         errors_corrected: metadata.validationResult?.corrections?.length || 0,
263 |         overall_confidence: Math.min(
264 |           analysis.confidence || 0.7,
265 |           metadata.validationResult?.score || 0.7
266 |         ),
267 |         warnings: metadata.validationResult?.warnings || [],
268 |       },
269 |     };
270 |   }
271 | 
272 |   /**
273 |    * Build chart data from dataset
274 |    */
275 |   buildChartData(rows, primaryCol, secondaryCol, aggregation) {
276 |     if (!rows || rows.length === 0 || !primaryCol) return [];
277 | 
278 |     const grouped = {};
279 | 
280 |     rows.forEach(row => {
281 |       const key = String(row[primaryCol] || 'Unknown');
282 |       if (!grouped[key]) {
283 |         grouped[key] = [];
284 |       }
285 |       grouped[key].push(row);
286 |     });
287 | 
288 |     return Object.entries(grouped)
289 |       .map(([key, groupRows]) => {
290 |         let value = 0;
291 | 
292 |         if (secondaryCol && aggregation !== 'count') {
293 |           const values = groupRows
294 |             .map(r => Number(r[secondaryCol]))
295 |             .filter(v => !isNaN(v));
296 | 
297 |           if (values.length > 0) {
298 |             if (aggregation === 'sum' || !aggregation) {
299 |               value = values.reduce((a, b) => a + b, 0);
300 |             } else if (aggregation === 'avg' || aggregation === 'average') {
301 |               value = values.reduce((a, b) => a + b, 0) / values.length;
302 |             } else if (aggregation === 'min') {
303 |               value = Math.min(...values);
304 |             } else if (aggregation === 'max') {
305 |               value = Math.max(...values);
306 |             } else {
307 |               value = values.length;
308 |             }
309 |           }
310 |         } else {
311 |           value = groupRows.length;
312 |         }
313 | 
314 |         return {
315 |           [primaryCol]: key,
316 |           value: Math.round(value * 100) / 100,
317 |           count: groupRows.length,
318 |         };
319 |       })
320 |       .sort((a, b) => b.value - a.value)
321 |       .slice(0, 20);
322 |   }
323 | 
324 |   /**
325 |    * Generate chart title
326 |    */
327 |   generateChartTitle(analysis) {
328 |     const { primary_column, secondary_column, aggregation, intent } = analysis;
329 |     const agg = (aggregation || 'sum').toUpperCase();
330 |     const metric = secondary_column ? ` of ${secondary_column}` : '';
331 |     return `${agg}${metric} by ${primary_column}`.trim();
332 |   }
333 | 
334 |   /**
335 |    * Generate SQL from analysis
336 |    */
337 |   generateSQL(analysis) {
338 |     const { primary_column, secondary_column, aggregation } = analysis;
339 | 
340 |     if (!primary_column) return 'SELECT * FROM dataset_rows LIMIT 100';
341 | 
342 |     let sql = 'SELECT ';
343 | 
344 |     if (aggregation === 'sum') {
345 |       sql += `${primary_column}, SUM(${secondary_column || 'value'}) as total`;
346 |     } else if (aggregation === 'avg' || aggregation === 'average') {
347 |       sql += `${primary_column}, AVG(${secondary_column || 'value'}) as average`;
348 |     } else if (aggregation === 'count') {
349 |       sql += `${primary_column}, COUNT(*) as count`;
350 |     } else if (aggregation === 'min') {
351 |       sql += `${primary_column}, MIN(${secondary_column || 'value'}) as minimum`;
352 |     } else if (aggregation === 'max') {
353 |       sql += `${primary_column}, MAX(${secondary_column || 'value'}) as maximum`;
354 |     } else {
355 |       sql += `${primary_column}, ${secondary_column || '*'}`;
356 |     }
357 | 
358 |     sql += ` FROM dataset_rows`;
359 |     sql += ` GROUP BY ${primary_column}`;
360 |     sql += ` ORDER BY ${aggregation === 'count' ? 'count' : secondary_column || 'value'} DESC`;
361 | 
362 |     return sql;
363 |   }
364 | 
365 |   /**
366 |    * Call Ollama Llama 3.2
367 |    */
368 |   async callLlama(prompt, maxTokens = 300) {
369 |     console.log(`📡 Calling Llama 3.2 (max ${maxTokens} tokens)...`);
370 | 
371 |     try {
372 |       const response = await fetch(`${OLLAMA_BASE_URL}/api/generate`, {
373 |         method: 'POST',
374 |         headers: { 'Content-Type': 'application/json' },
375 |         body: JSON.stringify({
376 |           model: this.model,
377 |           prompt: prompt,
378 |           stream: false,
379 |           temperature: this.temperature,
380 |           top_k: 40,
381 |           top_p: 0.9,
382 |           num_predict: maxTokens,
383 |           repeat_penalty: 1.1,
384 |         }),
385 |         signal: AbortSignal.timeout(this.timeout),
386 |       });
387 | 
388 |       if (!response.ok) {
389 |         throw new Error(`HTTP ${response.status}: ${response.statusText}`);
390 |       }
391 | 
392 |       const data = await response.json();
393 |       
394 |       if (!data.response) {
395 |         throw new Error('Empty response from Llama');
396 |       }
397 | 
398 |       return data.response;
399 | 
400 |     } catch (error) {
401 |       console.error('❌ Llama API error:', error.message);
402 |       throw error;
403 |     }
404 |   }
405 | 
406 |   /**
407 |    * Extract JSON from response
408 |    */
409 |   extractJSON(text) {
410 |     try {
411 |       const match = text.match(/\{[\s\S]*\}/);
412 |       if (!match) {
413 |         throw new Error('No JSON found in response');
414 |       }
415 |       return JSON.parse(match[0]);
416 |     } catch (error) {
417 |       console.error('❌ JSON extraction error:', error.message);
418 |       console.log('Response was:', text.substring(0, 200));
419 |       throw error;
420 |     }
421 |   }
422 | 
423 |   /**
424 |    * Format schema for prompt
425 |    */
426 |   formatSchema(schema) {
427 |     if (!schema) return 'No schema available';
428 |     
429 |     const cols = schema.columns || [];
430 |     const colList = cols.map(col => `- ${col.name} (${col.type})`).join('\n');
431 |     
432 |     return `Dataset: ${schema.datasetName || 'Unknown'}
433 | Rows: ${schema.rowCount || 0}
434 | Columns: ${schema.columnCount || cols.length}
435 | 
436 | Columns:
437 | ${colList || 'No columns'}
438 | 
439 | Primary Dimension: ${schema.primaryDimension?.name || schema.primaryMetric?.name || 'None'}
440 | Primary Metric: ${schema.primaryMetric?.name || 'None'}`;
441 |   }
442 | 
443 |   /**
444 |    * Validate columns exist in schema
445 |    */
446 |   validateColumns(parsed, schema) {
447 |     const errors = [];
448 |     if (!schema?.columns) return errors;
449 |     
450 |     const columnNames = new Set(
451 |       schema.columns.map(c => c.name.toLowerCase())
452 |     );
453 | 
454 |     const checkColumn = (col, type) => {
455 |       if (!col) return;
456 |       if (!columnNames.has(col.toLowerCase())) {
457 |         errors.push(`${type} column "${col}" not found in schema`);
458 |       }
459 |     };
460 | 
461 |     checkColumn(parsed.primary_column, 'Primary');
462 |     checkColumn(parsed.secondary_column, 'Secondary');
463 | 
464 |     return errors;
465 |   }
466 | 
467 |   /**
468 |    * Default parsing fallback
469 |    */
470 |   getDefaultParsing(query, schema) {
471 |     const lower = query.toLowerCase();
472 |     
473 |     let intent = 'aggregation';
474 |     if (lower.includes('trend') || lower.includes('over time')) intent = 'trend';
475 |     if (lower.includes('compare') || lower.includes('vs')) intent = 'comparison';
476 |     if (lower.includes('top') || lower.includes('best')) intent = 'ranking';
477 |     if (lower.includes('count') || lower.includes('how many')) intent = 'count';
478 | 
479 |     const firstCol = schema?.columns?.[0]?.name || 'column1';
480 |     const secondCol = schema?.columns?.[1]?.name || null;
481 | 
482 |     return {
483 |       intent,
484 |       primary_column: firstCol,
485 |       secondary_column: secondCol,
486 |       aggregation: 'sum',
487 |       chart_type: 'bar',
488 |       filters: [],
489 |       top_n: 10,
490 |       confidence: 0.3,
491 |       reasoning: 'Fallback parsing - query too complex',
492 |     };
493 |   }
494 | 
495 |   /**
496 |    * Clear cache
497 |    */
498 |   clearCache() {
499 |     this.cache.clear();
500 |     console.log('🧹 Cache cleared');
501 |   }
502 | 
503 |   /**
504 |    * Get cache stats
505 |    */
506 |   getCacheStats() {
507 |     return {
508 |       size: this.cache.size,
509 |       entries: Array.from(this.cache.keys()),
510 |     };
511 |   }
512 | }
513 | 
514 | export const llamaValidationMiddleware = new LlamaValidationMiddleware();
515 | export default LlamaValidationMiddleware;
```

```js
  1 | import { mkdirSync, existsSync } from 'node:fs';
  2 | import path from 'node:path';

  5 | import { DatabaseSync } from 'node:sqlite';

 12 | 
 13 | const sanitizeColumns = (columns = []) => {
 14 |   const seen = new Set();
 15 | 
 16 |   return columns.reduce((acc, column, index) => {
 17 |     const rawName = String(column?.name || `column_${index + 1}`).trim();
 18 |     const normalizedName = rawName || `column_${index + 1}`;
 19 |     const safeBaseName = normalizedName.toLowerCase() === 'id' ? '_source_id' : normalizedName;
 20 |     let safeName = safeBaseName;
 21 |     let suffix = 1;
 22 | 
 23 |     while (seen.has(safeName.toLowerCase())) {
 24 |       safeName = `${safeBaseName}_${suffix++}`;
 25 |     }
 26 | 
 27 |     seen.add(safeName.toLowerCase());
 28 |     acc.push({
 29 |       ...column,
 30 |       name: safeName,
 31 |       originalName: rawName,
 32 |     });
 33 |     return acc;
 34 |   }, []);
 35 | };
 36 | 
 37 | /**
 38 |  * Creates a local SQLite database from uploaded file data
 39 |  * @param {Object} dataset - Dataset with schema and data
 40 |  * @returns {Object} Database info with path and ID
 41 |  */
 42 | export const createLocalDatabase = (dataset) => {
 43 |   const datasetId = randomUUID();
 44 |   const dbPath = path.join(dataDir, `${datasetId}.sqlite`);
 45 | 
 46 |   const db = new DatabaseSync(dbPath);
 47 | 
 48 |   // Create table based on schema
 49 |   const columns = sanitizeColumns(dataset.columns || []);
 50 |   const columnDefs = columns.map(col => {
 51 |     let sqlType = 'TEXT';
 52 |     if (col.type === 'number') sqlType = 'REAL';
 53 |     else if (col.type === 'date') sqlType = 'TEXT';
 54 |     return `"${col.name}" ${sqlType}`;
 55 |   }).join(', ');
 56 | 
 57 |   db.exec(`
 58 |     CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS dataset (
 59 |       __row_id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT,
 60 |       ${columnDefs}
 61 |     );
 62 |   `);
 63 | 
 64 |   // Insert data in batches for performance
 65 |   const rows = dataset.rows || [];
 66 |   const batchSize = 1000;
 67 | 
 68 |   const insert = db.prepare(`
 69 |     INSERT INTO dataset (${columns.map(c => `"${c.name}"`).join(', ')})
 70 |     VALUES (${columns.map(() => '?').join(', ')})
 71 |   `);
 72 | 
 73 |   db.exec('BEGIN TRANSACTION');
 74 | 
 75 |   for (let i = 0; i < rows.length; i += batchSize) {
 76 |     const batch = rows.slice(i, i + batchSize);
 77 |     for (const row of batch) {
 78 |       const values = columns.map(col => {
 79 |         const val = row[col.originalName] ?? row[col.name];
 80 |         if (val === null || val === undefined) return null;
 81 |         if (col.type === 'number') return Number(val) || 0;
 82 |         return String(val);
 83 |       });
 84 |       insert.run(...values);
 85 |     }
 86 |   }
 87 | 
 88 |   db.exec('COMMIT');
 89 |   db.close();
 90 | 
 91 |   return {
 92 |     datasetId,
 93 |     dbPath,
 94 |     rowCount: rows.length,
 95 |     columnCount: columns.length,
 96 |   };
 97 | };
 98 | 
 99 | /**
100 |  * Executes a SQL query on a local database
101 |  * @param {string} datasetId - Dataset ID
102 |  * @param {string} sql - SQL query to execute
103 |  * @param {number} page - Page number (0-indexed)
104 |  * @param {number} limit - Results per page
105 |  * @returns {Object} Query results with pagination info
106 |  */
107 | export const executeLocalQuery = (datasetId, sql, page = 0, limit = 100) => {
108 |   const dbPath = path.join(dataDir, `${datasetId}.sqlite`);
109 | 
110 |   if (!existsSync(dbPath)) {
111 |     throw new Error('Local database not found');
112 |   }
113 | 
114 |   const db = new DatabaseSync(dbPath);
115 | 
116 |   try {
117 |     // Validate SQL
118 |     const upperSQL = sql.toUpperCase();
119 |     const dangerousKeywords = ['DROP', 'DELETE', 'UPDATE', 'INSERT', 'ALTER', 'CREATE', 'TRUNCATE'];
120 |     for (const keyword of dangerousKeywords) {
121 |       if (upperSQL.includes(keyword)) {
122 |         throw new Error(`Dangerous SQL keyword detected: ${keyword}`);
123 |       }
124 |     }
125 | 
126 |     // Add pagination if not present
127 |     let paginatedSQL = sql;
128 |     if (!upperSQL.includes('LIMIT')) {
129 |       paginatedSQL = `${sql} LIMIT ${limit} OFFSET ${page * limit}`;
130 |     }
131 | 
132 |     const normalizedSQL = sql.replace(/\bdataset\b/gi, 'dataset').replace(/\bdata\b/gi, 'dataset');
133 |     const finalSQL = upperSQL.includes('LIMIT')
134 |       ? normalizedSQL
135 |       : `${normalizedSQL} LIMIT ${limit} OFFSET ${page * limit}`;
136 | 
137 |     const results = db.prepare(finalSQL).all();
138 | 
139 |     // Get column names from results
140 |     const columns = results.length > 0 ? Object.keys(results[0]) : [];
141 | 
142 |     // Get total count for pagination
143 |     const countSQL = normalizedSQL
144 |       .replace(/SELECT\s+.*?\s+FROM/i, 'SELECT COUNT(*) FROM')
145 |       .split('ORDER BY')[0]
146 |       .split('LIMIT')[0];
147 |     const totalCount = db.prepare(countSQL).get()['COUNT(*)'] || results.length;
148 | 
149 |     db.close();
150 | 
151 |     return {
152 |       data: results,
153 |       columns,
154 |       pagination: {
155 |         page,
156 |         limit,
157 |         total: totalCount,
158 |         totalPages: Math.ceil(totalCount / limit),
159 |       },
160 |     };
161 |   } catch (error) {
162 |     db.close();
163 |     throw new Error(`Query execution failed: ${error.message}`);
164 |   }
165 | };
166 | 
167 | /**
168 |  * Gets data from local database with pagination
169 |  * @param {string} datasetId - Dataset ID
170 |  * @param {number} page - Page number (0-indexed)
171 |  * @param {number} limit - Results per page
172 |  * @returns {Object} Data with pagination info
173 |  */
174 | export const getLocalData = (datasetId, page = 0, limit = 100) => {
175 |   const dbPath = path.join(dataDir, `${datasetId}.sqlite`);
176 | 
177 |   if (!existsSync(dbPath)) {
178 |     throw new Error('Local database not found');
179 |   }
180 | 
181 |   const db = new DatabaseSync(dbPath);
182 | 
183 |   try {
184 |     const offset = page * limit;
185 |     const rows = db.prepare(`SELECT * FROM dataset LIMIT ${limit} OFFSET ${offset}`).all();
186 |     
187 |     const totalCount = db.prepare('SELECT COUNT(*) as count FROM dataset').get().count;
188 |     const columns = rows.length > 0 ? Object.keys(rows[0]) : [];
189 | 
190 |     db.close();
191 | 
192 |     return {
193 |       data: rows,
194 |       columns,
195 |       pagination: {
196 |         page,
197 |         limit,
198 |         total: totalCount,
199 |         totalPages: Math.ceil(totalCount / limit),
200 |       },
201 |     };
202 |   } catch (error) {
203 |     db.close();
204 |     throw new Error(`Failed to get data: ${error.message}`);
205 |   }
206 | };
207 |

215 |   if (existsSync(dbPath)) {

222 | /**
223 |  * Gets database file path for a dataset
224 |  * @param {string} datasetId - Dataset ID
225 |  * @returns {string} Database file path
226 |  */
227 | export const getDatabasePath = (datasetId) => {
228 |   return path.join(dataDir, `${datasetId}.sqlite`);
229 | };
230 |
```

```js
  1 | /**
  2 |  * ML Service Client
  3 |  * Communicates with Python AutoGluon service
  4 |  * Cost: $0 (runs locally)
  5 |  */
  6 | 
  7 | import axios from 'axios';
  8 | 
  9 | const ML_SERVICE_URL = process.env.ML_SERVICE_URL || 'http://localhost:5000';
 10 | const ML_TIMEOUT = 120000; // 2 minutes for training
 11 | 
 12 | class MLClient {
 13 |   /**
 14 |    * Check if ML service is healthy
 15 |    */
 16 |   static async health() {
 17 |     try {
 18 |       const response = await axios.get(`${ML_SERVICE_URL}/api/ml/health`, {
 19 |         timeout: 5000,
 20 |       });
 21 |       return response.data;
 22 |     } catch (error) {
 23 |       console.error('[ml-client] Health check failed:', error.message);
 24 |       return { success: false, status: 'unavailable' };
 25 |     }
 26 |   }
 27 | 
 28 |   /**
 29 |    * Train model on dataset
 30 |    */
 31 |   static async trainModel(datasetId, rows, targetColumn, problemType = 'regression') {
 32 |     try {
 33 |       console.log(`[ml-client] Training model for dataset ${datasetId}`);
 34 |       console.log(`[ml-client] Rows: ${rows.length}, Target: ${targetColumn}, Type: ${problemType}`);
 35 | 
 36 |       const response = await axios.post(
 37 |         `${ML_SERVICE_URL}/api/ml/train`,
 38 |         {
 39 |           dataset_id: datasetId,
 40 |           rows,
 41 |           target_column: targetColumn,
 42 |           problem_type: problemType,
 43 |         },
 44 |         { timeout: ML_TIMEOUT }
 45 |       );
 46 | 
 47 |       console.log('[ml-client] ✅ Model trained successfully');
 48 |       return {
 49 |         success: true,
 50 |         ...response.data,
 51 |       };
 52 |     } catch (error) {
 53 |       console.error('[ml-client] Training error:', error.message);
 54 |       return {
 55 |         success: false,
 56 |         error: error.message,
 57 |         errorType: error.response?.status || 'UNKNOWN',
 58 |       };
 59 |     }
 60 |   }
 61 | 
 62 |   /**
 63 |    * Make predictions
 64 |    */
 65 |   static async predict(datasetId, inputData) {
 66 |     try {
 67 |       console.log(`[ml-client] Making predictions for dataset ${datasetId}`);
 68 | 
 69 |       const response = await axios.post(
 70 |         `${ML_SERVICE_URL}/api/ml/predict`,
 71 |         {
 72 |           dataset_id: datasetId,
 73 |           input_data: inputData,
 74 |         },
 75 |         { timeout: 30000 }
 76 |       );
 77 | 
 78 |       console.log('[ml-client] ✅ Predictions made');
 79 |       return {
 80 |         success: true,
 81 |         ...response.data,
 82 |       };
 83 |     } catch (error) {
 84 |       console.error('[ml-client] Prediction error:', error.message);
 85 |       return {
 86 |         success: false,
 87 |         error: error.message,
 88 |       };
 89 |     }
 90 |   }
 91 | 
 92 |   /**
 93 |    * Get feature importance
 94 |    */
 95 |   static async getFeatureImportance(datasetId) {
 96 |     try {
 97 |       console.log(`[ml-client] Getting feature importance for dataset ${datasetId}`);
 98 | 
 99 |       const response = await axios.post(
100 |         `${ML_SERVICE_URL}/api/ml/feature-importance`,
101 |         { dataset_id: datasetId },
102 |         { timeout: 10000 }
103 |       );
104 | 
105 |       console.log('[ml-client] ✅ Feature importance retrieved');
106 |       return {
107 |         success: true,
108 |         ...response.data,
109 |       };
110 |     } catch (error) {
111 |       console.error('[ml-client] Feature importance error:', error.message);
112 |       return {
113 |         success: false,
114 |         error: error.message,
115 |       };
116 |     }
117 |   }
118 | 
119 |   /**
120 |    * List all models
121 |    */
122 |   static async listModels() {
123 |     try {
124 |       const response = await axios.get(`${ML_SERVICE_URL}/api/ml/models`, {
125 |         timeout: 5000,
126 |       });
127 | 
128 |       return {
129 |         success: true,

131 |       };
132 |     } catch (error) {

134 |       return {
135 |         success: false,
136 |         error: error.message,
137 |       };
138 |     }
139 |   }
140 | 
141 |   /**
142 |    * Delete model
143 |    */
144 |   static async deleteModel(datasetId) {
145 |     try {
146 |       const response = await axios.delete(
147 |         `${ML_SERVICE_URL}/api/ml/models/${datasetId}`,
148 |         { timeout: 5000 }

150 |

163 | }
```
