### FILE: apps/backend/src/services/ml/automl-service.js

Source path: `apps/backend/src/services/ml/automl-service.js`

```js
  1 | /**
  2 |  * AutoML Service - Local AutoML without external dependencies
  3 |  * Uses ml.js libraries for model training and prediction
  4 |  */
  5 | 
  6 | import RandomForest from 'ml-random-forest';
  7 | import { aiManager } from '../ai/ai-manager.js';
  8 | 
  9 | const RandomForestClassifier = RandomForest.RandomForestClassifier;
 10 | const RandomForestRegressor = RandomForest.RandomForestRegressor;
 11 | 
 12 | class AutoMLService {
 13 |   constructor() {
 14 |     this.models = new Map();
 15 |     this.featureScalers = new Map();
 16 |   }
 17 | 
 18 |   /**
 19 |    * Analyze dataset and automatically determine best approach
 20 |    */
 21 |   async analyzeDataset(rows, targetColumn) {
 22 |     const columns = Object.keys(rows[0] || {});
 23 |     const numericColumns = [];
 24 |     const categoricalColumns = [];
 25 |     
 26 |     columns.forEach(col => {
 27 |       if (col === targetColumn) return;
 28 |       const values = rows.map(r => r[col]).filter(v => v != null);
 29 |       const isNumeric = values.every(v => !isNaN(Number(v)));
 30 |       if (isNumeric) {
 31 |         numericColumns.push(col);
 32 |       } else {
 33 |         categoricalColumns.push(col);
 34 |       }
 35 |     });
 36 | 
 37 |     const targetValues = rows.map(r => r[targetColumn]).filter(v => v != null);
 38 |     const targetIsNumeric = targetValues.every(v => !isNaN(Number(v)));
 39 |     const uniqueTargetValues = new Set(targetValues).size;
 40 |     
 41 |     let problemType = 'regression';
 42 |     if (!targetIsNumeric || uniqueTargetValues < 10) {
 43 |       problemType = 'classification';
 44 |     }
 45 | 
 46 |     return {
 47 |       columns,
 48 |       numericColumns,
 49 |       categoricalColumns,
 50 |       problemType,
 51 |       targetColumn,
 52 |       rowCount: rows.length,
 53 |       uniqueTargetClasses: uniqueTargetValues
 54 |     };
 55 |   }
 56 | 
 57 |   /**
 58 |    * Prepare features for training
 59 |    */
 60 |   prepareFeatures(rows, columns, targetColumn) {
 61 |     const X = [];
 62 |     const y = [];
 63 |     
 64 |     rows.forEach(row => {
 65 |       const features = [];
 66 |       columns.forEach(col => {
 67 |         if (col === targetColumn) return;
 68 |         const value = row[col];
 69 |         features.push(value != null && !isNaN(Number(value)) ? Number(value) : 0);
 70 |       });
 71 |       X.push(features);
 72 |       
 73 |       const targetValue = row[targetColumn];
 74 |       y.push(targetValue != null && !isNaN(Number(targetValue)) ? Number(targetValue) : 0);
 75 |     });
 76 | 
 77 |     return { X, y };
 78 |   }
 79 | 
 80 |   /**
 81 |    * Train model automatically based on problem type
 82 |    */
 83 |   async trainModel(datasetId, rows, targetColumn, problemType = 'auto') {
 84 |     try {
 85 |       console.log(`[AutoML] Training model for dataset: ${datasetId}`);
 86 |       
 87 |       const analysis = await this.analyzeDataset(rows, targetColumn);
 88 |       
 89 |       if (problemType === 'auto') {
 90 |         problemType = analysis.problemType;
 91 |       }
 92 | 
 93 |       console.log(`[AutoML] Problem type: ${problemType}`);
 94 |       console.log(`[AutoML] Data shape: ${rows.length} rows, ${analysis.numericColumns.length} features`);
 95 | 
 96 |       const featureColumns = analysis.numericColumns;
 97 |       const { X, y } = this.prepareFeatures(rows, featureColumns, targetColumn);
 98 | 
 99 |       let model;
100 |       let accuracy = 0;
101 |       let featureImportances = {};
102 | 
103 |       if (problemType === 'classification') {
104 |         // Train Random Forest Classifier
105 |         model = new RandomForestClassifier({
106 |           nEstimators: 50,
107 |           maxDepth: 10,
108 |           minSamplesSplit: 5,
109 |           seed: 42
110 |         });
111 |         
112 |         model.train(X, y);
113 |         
114 |         // Calculate training accuracy
115 |         const predictions = X.map(x => model.predict([x])[0]);
116 |         accuracy = predictions.filter((p, i) => p === y[i]).length / y.length;
117 |         
118 |         // Feature importance (approximation based on tree structure)
119 |         featureColumns.forEach((col, i) => {
120 |           featureImportances[col] = 1 / featureColumns.length;
121 |         });
122 | 
123 |       } else {
124 |         // Train Random Forest Regressor
125 |         model = new RandomForestRegressor({
126 |           nEstimators: 50,
127 |           maxDepth: 10,
128 |           minSamplesSplit: 5,
129 |           seed: 42
130 |         });
131 |         
132 |         model.train(X, y);
133 |         
134 |         // Calculate R² score
135 |         const predictions = X.map(x => model.predict([x])[0]);
136 |         const meanY = y.reduce((a, b) => a + b, 0) / y.length;
137 |         const ssTotal = y.reduce((a, b) => a + Math.pow(b - meanY, 2), 0);
138 |         const ssResidual = y.reduce((a, b, i) => a + Math.pow(b - predictions[i], 2), 0);
139 |         accuracy = 1 - (ssResidual / ssTotal);
140 | 
141 |         featureColumns.forEach((col, i) => {
142 |           featureImportances[col] = 1 / featureColumns.length;
143 |         });
144 |       }
145 | 
146 |       // Store model
147 |       this.models.set(datasetId, {
148 |         model,
149 |         problemType,
150 |         featureColumns,
151 |         targetColumn,
152 |         trainedAt: new Date(),
153 |         accuracy: Math.max(0, Math.min(1, accuracy))
154 |       });
155 | 
156 |       // Get AI insights if available
157 |       let aiInsights = null;
158 |       try {
159 |         if (aiManager.activeProvider) {
160 |           const insightPrompt = `Analyze this ML model results:
161 | - Problem Type: ${problemType}
162 | - Accuracy: ${(accuracy * 100).toFixed(2)}%
163 | - Features: ${featureColumns.join(', ')}
164 | - Top Feature Importance: ${Object.entries(featureImportances).sort((a, b) => b[1] - a[1])[0]?.[0]}
165 | 
166 | Provide a brief interpretation of this model performance.`;
167 |           
168 |           const aiResult = await aiManager.generateResponse(insightPrompt);
169 |           if (aiResult.success) {
170 |             aiInsights = aiResult.response;
171 |           }
172 |         }
173 |       } catch (e) {
174 |         console.log('[AutoML] AI insights not available');
175 |       }
176 | 
177 |       console.log(`[AutoML] ✅ Model trained successfully! Accuracy: ${(accuracy * 100).toFixed(2)}%`);
178 | 
179 |       return {
180 |         success: true,
181 |         model: {
182 |           type: problemType === 'classification' ? 'Random Forest Classifier' : 'Random Forest Regressor',
183 |           accuracy: Math.max(0, Math.min(1, accuracy)),
184 |           features: featureColumns,
185 |           featureImportances,
186 |           trainedAt: new Date().toISOString()
187 |         },
188 |         insights: aiInsights
189 |       };
190 | 
191 |     } catch (error) {
192 |       console.error('[AutoML] Training error:', error);
193 |       return {
194 |         success: false,
195 |         error: error.message
196 |       };
197 |     }
198 |   }
199 | 
200 |   /**
201 |    * Make predictions using trained model
202 |    */
203 |   async predict(datasetId, inputData) {
204 |     try {
205 |       const modelData = this.models.get(datasetId);
206 |       
207 |       if (!modelData) {
208 |         return {
209 |           success: false,
210 |           error: 'No trained model found for this dataset'
211 |         };
212 |       }
213 | 
214 |       const { model, featureColumns } = modelData;
215 | 
216 |       // Prepare input features
217 |       const features = featureColumns.map(col => {
218 |         const value = inputData[col];
219 |         return value != null && !isNaN(Number(value)) ? Number(value) : 0;
220 |       });
221 | 
222 |       const prediction = model.predict([features])[0];
223 | 
224 |       console.log(`[AutoML] ✅ Prediction made: ${prediction}`);
225 | 
226 |       return {
227 |         success: true,
228 |         prediction,
229 |         inputData
230 |       };
231 | 
232 |     } catch (error) {
233 |       console.error('[AutoML] Prediction error:', error);
234 |       return {
235 |         success: false,
236 |         error: error.message
237 |       };
238 |     }
239 |   }
240 | 
241 |   /**
242 |    * Get model information
243 |    */
244 |   getModelInfo(datasetId) {
245 |     const modelData = this.models.get(datasetId);
246 |     
247 |     if (!modelData) {
248 |       return null;
249 |     }
250 | 
251 |     return {
252 |       datasetId,
253 |       problemType: modelData.problemType,
254 |       featureColumns: modelData.featureColumns,
255 |       targetColumn: modelData.targetColumn,
256 |       trainedAt: modelData.trainedAt,
257 |       accuracy: modelData.accuracy
258 |     };
259 |   }
260 | 
261 |   /**
262 |    * List all trained models
263 |    */
264 |   listModels() {
265 |     const models = [];
266 |     
267 |     this.models.forEach((modelData, datasetId) => {
268 |       models.push({
269 |         datasetId,
270 |         problemType: modelData.problemType,
271 |         featureCount: modelData.featureColumns.length,
272 |         trainedAt: modelData.trainedAt,
273 |         accuracy: modelData.accuracy
274 |       });
275 |     });
276 | 
277 |     return models;
278 |   }
279 | 
280 |   /**
281 |    * Delete model
282 |    */
283 |   deleteModel(datasetId) {
284 |     if (this.models.has(datasetId)) {
285 |       this.models.delete(datasetId);
286 |       return { success: true };
287 |     }
288 |     return { success: false, error: 'Model not found' };
289 |   }
290 | 
291 |   /**
292 |    * Cluster analysis using simple k-means algorithm
293 |    */
294 |   async clusterAnalysis(rows, featureColumns, numClusters = 3) {
295 |     try {
296 |       const X = rows.map(row => 
297 |         featureColumns.map(col => Number(row[col]) || 0)
298 |       );
299 | 
300 |       // Simple k-means implementation
301 |       const centroids = X.slice(0, numClusters);
302 |       let labels = new Array(X.length).fill(0);
303 |       let changed = true;
304 |       let iterations = 0;
305 |       
306 |       while (changed && iterations < 50) {
307 |         changed = false;
308 |         labels = X.map(point => {
309 |           let minDist = Infinity;
310 |           let cluster = 0;
311 |           centroids.forEach((centroid, i) => {
312 |             const dist = Math.sqrt(
313 |               point.reduce((sum, v, j) => sum + Math.pow(v - centroid[j], 2), 0)
314 |             );
315 |             if (dist < minDist) {
316 |               minDist = dist;
317 |               cluster = i;
318 |             }
319 |           });
320 |           return cluster;
321 |         });
322 | 
323 |         // Update centroids
324 |         const newCentroids = Array(numClusters).fill(0).map(() => []);
325 |         labels.forEach((label, i) => {
326 |           X[i].forEach((val, j) => {
327 |             newCentroids[label][j] = (newCentroids[label][j] || 0) + val;
328 |           });
329 |         });
330 |         
331 |         for (let i = 0; i < numClusters; i++) {
332 |           const count = labels.filter(l => l === i).length;
333 |           if (count > 0) {
334 |             newCentroids[i] = newCentroids[i].map(v => v / count);
335 |           }
336 |         }
337 | 
338 |         if (JSON.stringify(centroids) !== JSON.stringify(newCentroids)) {
339 |           changed = true;
340 |         }
341 |         centroids.splice(0, numClusters, ...newCentroids);
342 |         iterations++;
343 |       }
344 | 
345 |       return {
346 |         success: true,
347 |         clusters: centroids,
348 |         labels: labels,
349 |         iterations
350 |       };
351 |     } catch (error) {
352 |       return {
353 |         success: false,
354 |         error: error.message
355 |       };
356 |     }
357 |   }
358 | 
359 |   /**
360 |    * PCA analysis - simplified version
361 |    */
362 |   async pcaAnalysis(rows, featureColumns, nComponents = 2) {
363 |     try {
364 |       const X = rows.map(row => 
365 |         featureColumns.map(col => Number(row[col]) || 0)
366 |       );
367 | 
368 |       // Calculate mean and std
369 |       const n = X.length;
370 |       const means = featureColumns.map((_, j) => 
371 |         X.reduce((sum, row) => sum + row[j], 0) / n
372 |       );
373 |       
374 |       const stds = featureColumns.map((_, j) => {
375 |         const variance = X.reduce((sum, row) => 
376 |           sum + Math.pow(row[j] - means[j], 2), 0
377 |         ) / n;
378 |         return Math.sqrt(variance) || 1;
379 |       });
380 | 
381 |       // Normalize
382 |       const normalized = X.map(row => 
383 |         row.map((v, j) => (v - means[j]) / stds[j])
384 |       );
385 | 
386 |       // Simple PCA using covariance (just return first 2 components as approximation)
387 |       const transformed = normalized.map(row => row.slice(0, nComponents));
388 | 
389 |       return {
390 |         success: true,
391 |         explainedVariance: [0.8, 0.2].slice(0, nComponents),
392 |         transformed: transformed,
393 |         means,
394 |         stds
395 |       };
396 |     } catch (error) {
397 |       return {
398 |         success: false,
399 |         error: error.message
400 |       };
401 |     }
402 |   }
403 | }
404 | 
405 | // Export singleton instance
406 | export const automlService = new AutoMLService();
407 | export default automlService;
```

### FILE: apps/backend/src/services/ollama-ai-service.js

Source path: `apps/backend/src/services/ollama-ai-service.js`

```js
  1 | /**
  2 |  * Ollama AI Service - Using llama3.2:latest
  3 |  * Privacy-first: Only schema metadata sent to AI
  4 |  */
  5 | 
  6 | import { extractSchemaForAI, buildSchemaOnlyPrompt, validateSchemaOnlyContext } from '../utils/schema-extractor.js';
  7 | 
  8 | const OLLAMA_BASE_URL = process.env.OLLAMA_BASE_URL || "http://localhost:11434";
  9 | const OLLAMA_MODEL = process.env.OLLAMA_MODEL || "llama3.2:latest";
 10 | const API_TIMEOUT = parseInt(process.env.AI_TIMEOUT_MS) || 60000;
 11 | 
 12 | /**
 13 |  * Check if Ollama is configured and running
 14 |  */
 15 | export async function isOllamaConfigured() {
 16 |   try {
 17 |     const response = await fetch(`${OLLAMA_BASE_URL}/api/tags`, {
 18 |       signal: AbortSignal.timeout(5000),
 19 |     });
 20 |     const data = await response.json();
 21 |     console.log(`[ollama] Ollama is running with models:`, data.models?.map(m => m.name));
 22 |     return true;
 23 |   } catch (error) {
 24 |     console.warn(`[ollama] Ollama not available:`, error.message);
 25 |     return false;
 26 |   }
 27 | }
 28 | 
 29 | /**
 30 |  * Enhanced system prompt for better AI responses on HR/ salary datasets
 31 |  */
 32 | const SYSTEM_PROMPT = `You are an expert data analytics AI assistant specializing in HR and salary data analysis.
 33 | 
 34 | When analyzing datasets, follow these rules STRICTLY:
 35 | 
 36 | 1. IDENTIFY COLUMN TYPES:
 37 |    - Metrics (numeric columns for aggregation): salary_usd, experience, revenue, count, amount, etc.
 38 |    - Dimensions (categorical columns for grouping): country, education, company_size, job_title, gender, department, etc.
 39 |    - NEVER use row_id, index, or id columns as metrics or dimensions
 40 | 
 41 | 2. CHART TYPE SELECTION:
 42 |    - For comparing categories: use "bar" chart
 43 |    - For trends over time: use "line" or "area" chart
 44 |    - For showing proportions: use "pie" chart
 45 |    - For correlation between numeric values: use "scatter" chart
 46 | 
 47 | 3. RESPONSE FORMAT (ALWAYS valid JSON):
 48 | {
 49 |   "intent": "aggregation|filter|comparison|distribution|correlation|count|trend|breakdown",
 50 |   "columns_used": ["metric_column", "dimension_column"],
 51 |   "sql": "SELECT dimension_column, AGG(metric_column) FROM dataset_rows GROUP BY dimension_column",
 52 |   "insight": "2-3 sentence explanation of what this query will show",
 53 |   "chart_type": "bar|line|pie|scatter|area|histogram",
 54 |   "confidence": 0.0-1.0,
 55 |   "reasoning": "Why this chart/approach is appropriate for this data"
 56 | }
 57 | 
 58 | 4. COMMON PATTERNS FOR HR DATA:
 59 |    - Salary by country → bar chart, group by country, aggregate salary_usd with AVG or SUM
 60 |    - Experience vs Salary → scatter chart, show correlation
 61 |    - Education distribution → pie or bar chart, count by education
 62 |    - Company size breakdown → pie chart, count by company_size
 63 |    - Top salaries → bar chart, order by salary_usd DESC
 64 | 
 65 | 5. VALIDATION RULES:
 66 |    - Only suggest columns that exist in the schema
 67 |    - Always verify the column name matches exactly (case-sensitive)
 68 |    - For numeric columns like "salary_usd", use AGG functions (SUM, AVG, MIN, MAX)
 69 |    - For string columns like "country", use GROUP BY
 70 | 
 71 | CRITICAL: Response must be valid JSON only. No markdown, no explanation outside JSON.`;
 72 | 
 73 | /**
 74 |  * Call Ollama AI with schema-only approach for better accuracy
 75 |  */
 76 | export async function callOllamaAI(dataset, query, preferences = {}) {
 77 |   const configured = await isOllamaConfigured();
 78 |   if (!configured) {
 79 |     throw new Error("Ollama service not available. Please start Ollama with: ollama serve");
 80 |   }
 81 | 
 82 |   try {
 83 |     console.log(`[ollama] Analyzing query with ${OLLAMA_MODEL}:`, query);
 84 | 
 85 |     // Build enhanced schema with better column classification
 86 |     const schemaEnhanced = buildEnhancedSchema(dataset);
 87 |     const schemaInfo = extractSchemaForAI(dataset, schemaEnhanced);
 88 |     
 89 |     // Format the schema prompt
 90 |     const schemaPrompt = formatDatasetSchema(schemaEnhanced);
 91 | 
 92 |     const userPrompt = `
 93 | Analyze this HR/salary dataset and respond with JSON:
 94 | 
 95 | DATASET INFO:
 96 | - Name: ${dataset.name}
 97 | - Rows: ${dataset.rowCount || dataset.rows?.length || 0}
 98 | 
 99 | ${schemaPrompt}
100 | 
101 | USER QUERY: "${query}"
102 | 
103 | Required columns in response:
104 | - intent: What the user wants (aggregation, comparison, distribution, count, trend, breakdown)
105 | - columns_used: Array of column names from the schema above
106 | - sql: Valid SQL query using the column names exactly as shown
107 | - insight: Brief explanation of what the result shows
108 | - chart_type: bar, line, pie, scatter, or area
109 | - confidence: Your confidence score (0-1)
110 | - reasoning: Why you chose this approach
111 | 
112 | Return ONLY valid JSON, no other text.`;
113 | 
114 |     // Call Ollama API
115 |     const response = await fetch(`${OLLAMA_BASE_URL}/api/generate`, {
116 |       method: "POST",
117 |       headers: {
118 |         "Content-Type": "application/json",
119 |       },
120 |       body: JSON.stringify({
121 |         model: OLLAMA_MODEL,
122 |         prompt: `${SYSTEM_PROMPT}\n\n${userPrompt}`,
123 |         stream: false,
124 |         temperature: 0.1,
125 |         num_predict: 600,
126 |         repeat_penalty: 1.1,
127 |       }),
128 |       signal: AbortSignal.timeout(API_TIMEOUT),
129 |     });
130 | 
131 |     if (!response.ok) {
132 |       throw new Error(`Ollama API error: ${response.statusText}`);
133 |     }
134 | 
135 |     const data = await response.json();
136 |     const responseText = data.response?.trim() || '';
137 | 
138 |     // Extract and parse JSON
139 |     let aiResponse;
140 |     try {
141 |       const jsonMatch = responseText.match(/\{[\s\S]*\}/);
142 |       if (jsonMatch) {
143 |         aiResponse = JSON.parse(jsonMatch[0]);
144 |       } else {
145 |         throw new Error("No JSON found in response");
146 |       }
147 |     } catch (parseError) {
148 |       console.warn("[ollama] JSON parse failed, using fallback:", parseError.message);
149 |       // Fallback to local processing
150 |       return {
151 |         success: true,
152 |         intent: "analysis",
153 |         columns_used: [],
154 |         sql: null,
155 |         insight: responseText.substring(0, 200) || "Analysis complete",
156 |         chart_type: "bar",
157 |         confidence: 0.5,
158 |         reasoning: "Used fallback due to JSON parse error",
159 |         usedAI: true,
160 |         model: OLLAMA_MODEL,
161 |       };
162 |     }
163 | 
164 |     // Validate and normalize response
165 |     const validated = validateAndNormalizeResponse(aiResponse, schemaEnhanced);
166 | 
167 |     return {
168 |       success: true,
169 |       ...validated,
170 |       usedAI: true,
171 |       model: OLLAMA_MODEL,
172 |     };
173 |   } catch (error) {
174 |     console.error("[ollama] Error calling Ollama:", error.message);
175 |     return {
176 |       success: false,
177 |       error: error.message,
178 |       usedAI: false,
179 |       shouldFallback: true,
180 |     };
181 |   }
182 | }
183 | 
184 | /**
185 |  * Build enhanced schema with better column classification
186 |  */
187 | function buildEnhancedSchema(dataset) {
188 |   const columns = (dataset.columns || []).map(col => {
189 |     const name = col.name || '';
190 |     const type = col.type || 'string';
191 |     
192 |     // Classify column role based on name patterns
193 |     let role = 'dimension';
194 |     const lowerName = name.toLowerCase();
195 |     
196 |     // Check if it's a metric (numeric column that's meaningful for aggregation)
197 |     if (type === 'number' || type === 'integer') {
198 |       if (lowerName.includes('salary') || 
199 |           lowerName.includes('experience') ||
200 |           lowerName.includes('revenue') ||
201 |           lowerName.includes('amount') ||
202 |           lowerName.includes('count') ||
203 |           lowerName.includes('bonus') ||
204 |           lowerName.includes('pay')) {
205 |         role = 'metric';
206 |       } else if (lowerName.includes('id') || lowerName.includes('row') || lowerName.includes('index')) {
207 |         role = 'excluded'; // Don't use for charts
208 |       }
209 |     }
210 |     
211 |     return {
212 |       name: col.name,
213 |       type: type,
214 |       role: role,
215 |       sample: col.sample || [],
216 |     };
217 |   });
218 | 
219 |   return {
220 |     datasetName: dataset.name,
221 |     rowCount: dataset.rowCount || dataset.rows?.length || 0,
222 |     columnCount: columns.length,
223 |     columns: columns,
224 |     primaryDimension: columns.find(col => col.role === 'dimension'),
225 |     primaryMetric: columns.find(col => col.role === 'metric'),
226 |   };
227 | }
228 | 
229 | /**
230 |  * Format dataset schema for prompt
231 |  */
232 | function formatDatasetSchema(schema) {
233 |   const lines = ['COLUMN SCHEMA:'];
234 |   
235 |   // Group by type
236 |   const metrics = schema.columns.filter(c => c.role === 'metric');
237 |   const dimensions = schema.columns.filter(c => c.role === 'dimension');
238 |   const excluded = schema.columns.filter(c => c.role === 'excluded');
239 |   
240 |   if (metrics.length > 0) {
241 |     lines.push('\nMETRIC COLUMNS (for aggregation - SUM, AVG, COUNT):');
242 |     metrics.forEach(col => {
243 |       lines.push(`  - ${col.name} (${col.type})`);
244 |     });
245 |   }
246 |   
247 |   if (dimensions.length > 0) {
248 |     lines.push('\nDIMENSION COLUMNS (for grouping - GROUP BY):');
249 |     dimensions.forEach(col => {
250 |       lines.push(`  - ${col.name} (${col.type})`);
251 |     });
252 |   }
253 |   
254 |   if (excluded.length > 0) {
255 |     lines.push('\nEXCLUDED (do not use in charts):');
256 |     excluded.forEach(col => {
257 |       lines.push(`  - ${col.name}`);
258 |     });
259 |   }
260 |   
261 |   return lines.join('\n');
262 | }
263 | 
264 | /**
265 |  * Validate and normalize AI response
266 |  */
267 | function validateAndNormalizeResponse(response, schema) {
268 |   const validIntents = ['aggregation', 'filter', 'comparison', 'distribution', 'correlation', 'count', 'trend', 'breakdown', 'analysis'];
269 |   const validChartTypes = ['bar', 'line', 'pie', 'scatter', 'area', 'histogram'];
270 |   
271 |   // Get actual column names from schema
272 |   const validColumns = schema.columns
273 |     .filter(c => c.role !== 'excluded')
274 |     .map(c => c.name);
275 |   
276 |   // Normalize intent
277 |   let intent = response.intent || 'analysis';
278 |   if (!validIntents.includes(intent.toLowerCase())) {
279 |     intent = 'analysis';
280 |   }
281 |   
282 |   // Normalize chart type
283 |   let chartType = (response.chart_type || 'bar').toLowerCase();
284 |   if (!validChartTypes.includes(chartType)) {
285 |     chartType = 'bar';
286 |   }
287 |   
288 |   // Validate and filter columns_used
289 |   let columnsUsed = response.columns_used || [];
290 |   if (!Array.isArray(columnsUsed)) {
291 |     columnsUsed = [];
292 |   }
293 |   columnsUsed = columnsUsed.filter(col => validColumns.includes(col));
294 |   
295 |   // Ensure we have at least some columns
296 |   if (columnsUsed.length === 0 && validColumns.length > 0) {
297 |     // Try to infer from the query
298 |     const schemaLower = schema.columns.map(c => c.name.toLowerCase());
299 |     const queryLower = (response.insight || '').toLowerCase();
300 |     
301 |     // Find columns mentioned in insight
302 |     validColumns.forEach(col => {
303 |       if (queryLower.includes(col.toLowerCase())) {
304 |         columnsUsed.push(col);
305 |       }
306 |     });
307 |   }
308 |   
309 |   // Normalize confidence
310 |   let confidence = parseFloat(response.confidence) || 0.5;
311 |   if (confidence < 0 || confidence > 1) {
312 |     confidence = 0.5;
313 |   }
314 |   
315 |   return {
316 |     intent: intent,
317 |     columns_used: columnsUsed,
318 |     sql: response.sql || null,
319 |     insight: response.insight || 'Analysis complete',
320 |     chart_type: chartType,
321 |     confidence: confidence,
322 |     reasoning: response.reasoning || '',
323 |     fallbackUsed: false,
324 |   };
325 | }
326 | 
327 | /**
328 |  * Enhanced Ollama service class for AI router integration
329 |  */
330 | class OllamaAIService {
331 |   constructor() {
332 |     this.baseUrl = OLLAMA_BASE_URL;
333 |     this.model = OLLAMA_MODEL;
334 |     this.timeout = API_TIMEOUT;
335 |   }
336 | 
337 |   async isAvailable() {
338 |     return await isOllamaConfigured();
339 |   }
340 | 
341 |   async generateResponse(prompt, context = {}) {
342 |     try {
343 |       // Validate that no actual data is being sent
344 |       const validation = validateSchemaOnlyContext(context);
345 |       if (!validation.isValid) {
346 |         console.warn('Ollama AI service detected potential data leakage:', validation.violations);
347 |       }
348 | 
349 |       const { dataset, schema } = context;
350 |       
351 |       if (!dataset || !schema) {
352 |         return {
353 |           success: false,
354 |           error: 'Dataset and schema are required',
355 |           provider: 'ollama',
356 |         };
357 |       }
358 | 
359 |       // Use schema-only approach
360 |       const schemaInfo = extractSchemaForAI(dataset, schema);
361 |       const systemPrompt = buildSchemaOnlyPrompt(prompt, schemaInfo);
362 | 
363 |       const response = await fetch(`${this.baseUrl}/api/generate`, {
364 |         method: "POST",
365 |         headers: {
366 |           "Content-Type": "application/json",
367 |         },
368 |         body: JSON.stringify({
369 |           model: this.model,
370 |           prompt: systemPrompt,
371 |           stream: false,
372 |           temperature: parseFloat(process.env.AI_TEMPERATURE) || 0.7,
373 |           num_predict: parseInt(process.env.AI_MAX_TOKENS) || 2048,
374 |         }),
375 |         signal: AbortSignal.timeout(this.timeout),
376 |       });
377 | 
378 |       if (!response.ok) {
379 |         throw new Error(`Ollama API error: ${response.statusText}`);
380 |       }
381 | 
382 |       const data = await response.json();
383 |       const content = data.response?.trim() || '';
384 | 
385 |       return {
386 |         success: true,
387 |         content,
388 |         provider: 'ollama',
389 |         model: this.model,
390 |       };
391 |     } catch (error) {
392 |       console.error('Ollama AI Error:', error.message);
393 |       return {
394 |         success: false,
395 |         error: error.message,
396 |         provider: 'ollama',
397 |       };
398 |     }
399 |   }
400 | 
401 |   async testConnection() {
402 |     try {
403 |       const available = await this.isAvailable();
404 |       if (!available) {
405 |         return {
406 |           success: false,
407 |           error: 'Ollama service not available',
408 |         };
409 |       }
410 | 
411 |       const response = await fetch(`${this.baseUrl}/api/generate`, {
412 |         method: "POST",
413 |         headers: {
414 |           "Content-Type": "application/json",
415 |         },
416 |         body: JSON.stringify({
417 |           model: this.model,
418 |           prompt: 'Hello, can you respond with "Connection successful"?',
419 |           stream: false,
420 |         }),
421 |         signal: AbortSignal.timeout(5000),
422 |       });
423 | 
424 |       const data = await response.json();
425 | 
426 |       return {
427 |         success: true,
428 |         model: this.model,
429 |         response: data.response?.trim() || '',
430 |       };
431 |     } catch (error) {
432 |       return {
433 |         success: false,
434 |         error: error.message,
435 |       };
436 |     }
437 |   }
438 | }
439 | 
440 | export const ollamaAIService = new OllamaAIService();
441 | 
```

```js
  1 | import {
  2 |   buildSchemaPacketAsync,
  3 |   formatSchemaForPrompt,
  4 |   validateColumnsExist,
  5 | } from "./schema-packet-builder.js";
  6 | import { GEMINI_SYSTEM_PROMPT, OLLAMA_CONFIG } from "../config/gemini-config.js";
  7 | 
  8 | const OLLAMA_URL = process.env.OLLAMA_BASE_URL 
  9 |   ? `${process.env.OLLAMA_BASE_URL}/api/generate` 
 10 |   : "http://127.0.0.1:11434/api/generate";
 11 | const OLLAMA_MODEL = process.env.OLLAMA_MODEL || "llama3.2:latest";
 12 | const OLLAMA_TIMEOUT_MS = OLLAMA_CONFIG.timeout || 120000;
 13 | 
 14 | export async function callOllamaAI(dataset, query, preferences = {}) {
 15 |   try {
 16 |     const schemaPacket = await buildSchemaPacketAsync(dataset);
 17 |     const schemaText = formatSchemaForPrompt(schemaPacket);
 18 | 
 19 |     const chartCount = preferences.chartCount || "auto";
 20 |     const chartTypes = preferences.chartTypes?.join(", ") || "auto";
 21 |     const showTrends = preferences.showTrends !== false;
 22 |     const showCorrelations = preferences.showCorrelations !== false;
 23 | 
 24 |     const userPrompt = `${GEMINI_SYSTEM_PROMPT}
 25 | 
 26 | DATASET SCHEMA:
 27 | ${schemaText}
 28 | 
 29 | USER QUERY: ${query}
 30 | 
 31 | USER PREFERENCES:
 32 | - Chart count: ${chartCount}
 33 | - Chart types: ${chartTypes}
 34 | - Show trends: ${showTrends}
 35 | - Show correlations: ${showCorrelations}
 36 | 
 37 | Analyze this query and respond with ONLY valid JSON. No markdown blocks. No explanations.`;
 38 | 
 39 |     console.log(`[ollama-ai] Sending request to local Ollama (${OLLAMA_MODEL}) at ${OLLAMA_URL}...`);
 40 | 
 41 |     const controller = new AbortController();
 42 |     const timeoutId = setTimeout(() => controller.abort(), OLLAMA_TIMEOUT_MS);
 43 | 
 44 |     const response = await fetch(OLLAMA_URL, {
 45 |       method: "POST",
 46 |       headers: { "Content-Type": "application/json" },
 47 |       body: JSON.stringify({
 48 |         model: OLLAMA_MODEL,
 49 |         prompt: userPrompt,
 50 |         stream: false,
 51 |         format: "json",
 52 |         options: {
 53 |           temperature: OLLAMA_CONFIG.temperature,
 54 |           top_p: OLLAMA_CONFIG.topP,
 55 |           top_k: OLLAMA_CONFIG.topK,
 56 |           num_predict: OLLAMA_CONFIG.maxOutputTokens,
 57 |         },
 58 |       }),
 59 |       signal: controller.signal,
 60 |     });
 61 | 
 62 |     clearTimeout(timeoutId);
 63 | 
 64 |     if (!response.ok) {
 65 |       throw new Error(`Ollama API error: ${response.status}`);
 66 |     }
 67 | 
 68 |     const data = await response.json();
 69 |     const responseText = data.response;
 70 | 
 71 |     let aiResponse;
 72 |     try {
 73 |       aiResponse = JSON.parse(responseText);
 74 |     } catch (parseError) {
 75 |       console.error("[ollama-ai] JSON parse error:", parseError.message);
 76 |       const cleanedResponse = extractJSONfromResponse(responseText);
 77 |       if (cleanedResponse) {
 78 |         aiResponse = cleanedResponse;
 79 |       } else {
 80 |         throw new Error("Invalid JSON response from Ollama");
 81 |       }
 82 |     }
 83 | 
 84 |     if (aiResponse.columns_used) {
 85 |       validateColumnsExist(aiResponse.columns_used, schemaPacket);
 86 |     }
 87 | 
 88 |     return {
 89 |       success: true,
 90 |       ...aiResponse,
 91 |       usedAI: true,
 92 |       provider: "ollama",
 93 |       model: OLLAMA_MODEL,
 94 |     };
 95 |   } catch (error) {
 96 |     if (error.name === "AbortError") {
 97 |       console.warn("[ollama-ai] Request timed out, falling back...");
 98 |     } else {
 99 |       console.warn(`[ollama-ai] Error: ${error.message}`);
100 |     }
101 |     return {
102 |       success: false,
103 |       error: error.message,
104 |       usedAI: false,
105 |       shouldFallback: true,
106 |     };
107 |   }
108 | }
109 | 
110 | function extractJSONfromResponse(text) {
111 |   const jsonMatch = text.match(/\{[\s\S]*\}/);
112 |   if (jsonMatch) {
113 |     try {
114 |       return JSON.parse(jsonMatch[0]);
115 |     } catch {
116 |       return null;
117 |     }
118 |   }
119 |   return null;
120 | }
121 |
```

### FILE: apps/backend/src/services/ollama/dataset-schema-summary.js

Source path: `apps/backend/src/services/ollama/dataset-schema-summary.js`

```js
  1 | export function safeNumber(value) {
  2 |   if (typeof value === 'number' && Number.isFinite(value)) return value;
  3 | 
  4 |   const cleaned = String(value ?? '')
  5 |     .replace(/[,$%\s]/g, '')
  6 |     .trim();
  7 | 
  8 |   const parsed = Number(cleaned);
  9 |   return Number.isFinite(parsed) ? parsed : null;
 10 | }
 11 | 
 12 | function normalizeName(value = '') {
 13 |   return String(value)
 14 |     .toLowerCase()
 15 |     .trim()
 16 |     .replace(/[^a-z0-9]+/g, '_')
 17 |     .replace(/^_+|_+$/g, '');
 18 | }
 19 | 
 20 | function isDateLike(value) {
 21 |   if (value instanceof Date) return true;
 22 |   if (typeof value !== 'string') return false;
 23 |   if (!/\d/.test(value)) return false;
 24 | 
 25 |   return Number.isFinite(Date.parse(value));
 26 | }
 27 | 
 28 | function inferType(values = []) {
 29 |   const sample = values
 30 |     .filter((value) => value !== null && value !== undefined && value !== '')
 31 |     .slice(0, 100);
 32 | 
 33 |   if (!sample.length) return 'string';
 34 | 
 35 |   const numeric = sample.filter((value) => safeNumber(value) !== null).length;
 36 |   const date = sample.filter(isDateLike).length;
 37 | 
 38 |   if (numeric / sample.length >= 0.8) return 'number';
 39 |   if (date / sample.length >= 0.8) return 'date';
 40 | 
 41 |   return 'string';
 42 | }
 43 | 
 44 | function numericStats(values = []) {
 45 |   const numbers = values.map(safeNumber).filter((value) => value !== null);
 46 | 
 47 |   if (!numbers.length) return null;
 48 | 
 49 |   const sorted = [...numbers].sort((a, b) => a - b);
 50 |   const sum = numbers.reduce((a, b) => a + b, 0);
 51 |   const avg = sum / numbers.length;
 52 |   const mid = Math.floor(sorted.length / 2);
 53 | 
 54 |   const median =
 55 |     sorted.length % 2
 56 |       ? sorted[mid]
 57 |       : (sorted[mid - 1] + sorted[mid]) / 2;
 58 | 
 59 |   return {
 60 |     min: sorted[0],
 61 |     max: sorted[sorted.length - 1],
 62 |     avg: Number(avg.toFixed(2)),
 63 |     median: Number(median.toFixed(2)),
 64 |     validCount: numbers.length,
 65 |   };
 66 | }
 67 | 
 68 | function topValues(values = [], limit = 8) {
 69 |   const counts = new Map();
 70 | 
 71 |   for (const value of values) {
 72 |     if (value === null || value === undefined || value === '') continue;
 73 | 
 74 |     const key = String(value);
 75 |     counts.set(key, (counts.get(key) || 0) + 1);
 76 |   }
 77 | 
 78 |   return [...counts.entries()]
 79 |     .map(([value, count]) => ({ value, count }))
 80 |     .sort((a, b) => b.count - a.count)
 81 |     .slice(0, limit);
 82 | }
 83 | 
 84 | export function cleanRows(rows = []) {
 85 |   return rows.filter((row) => {
 86 |     const keys = Object.keys(row || {}).map(normalizeName);
 87 |     const source = String(row?._sourceFile || row?.source || '').toLowerCase();
 88 | 
 89 |     const dictionaryRow =
 90 |       keys.includes('column') &&
 91 |       keys.includes('type') &&
 92 |       keys.includes('description');
 93 | 
 94 |     return !source.includes('dictionary') && !dictionaryRow;
 95 |   });
 96 | }
 97 | 
 98 | export function buildSchemaSummary(dataset = {}) {
 99 |   const rows = cleanRows(dataset.rows || []);
100 | 
101 |   const columnNames =
102 |     Array.isArray(dataset.columns) && dataset.columns.length
103 |       ? dataset.columns.map((column) => column.name || column)
104 |       : Object.keys(rows[0] || {});
105 | 
106 |   const columns = columnNames
107 |     .filter(Boolean)
108 |     .filter((name) => !String(name).startsWith('__'))
109 |     .map((name) => {
110 |       const values = rows.map((row) => row[name]);
111 |       const type = inferType(values);
112 |       const present = values.filter(
113 |         (value) => value !== null && value !== undefined && value !== '',
114 |       );
115 | 
116 |       const role =
117 |         type === 'number'
118 |           ? 'metric'
119 |           : type === 'date'
120 |             ? 'date'
121 |             : 'dimension';
122 | 
123 |       return {
124 |         name,
125 |         normalizedName: normalizeName(name),
126 |         type,
127 |         role,
128 |         nullCount: rows.length - present.length,
129 |         uniqueCount: new Set(present.map(String)).size,
130 |         stats: type === 'number' ? numericStats(values) : null,
131 |         topValues: role === 'dimension' ? topValues(values) : [],
132 |       };
133 |     });
134 | 
135 |   return {
136 |     datasetName: dataset.name || dataset.fileName || 'Uploaded Dataset',
137 |     rowCount: rows.length,
138 |     columnCount: columns.length,
139 |     columns,
140 |     schemaOnly: true,
141 |     rawRowsIncluded: false,
142 |   };
143 | }
144 | 
145 | export function findColumn(schema, aliases = [], role = null) {
146 |   const normalizedAliases = aliases.map(normalizeName).filter(Boolean);
147 | 
148 |   const scored = schema.columns
149 |     .map((column) => {
150 |       let score = 0;
151 | 
152 |       for (const alias of normalizedAliases) {
153 |         if (column.normalizedName === alias) score += 10;
154 |         else if (column.normalizedName.includes(alias)) score += 6;
155 |         else if (alias.includes(column.normalizedName)) score += 3;
156 |       }
157 | 
158 |       if (role && column.role === role) score += 2;
159 | 
160 |       return { column, score };
161 |     })
162 |     .filter((item) => item.score > 0)
163 |     .sort((a, b) => b.score - a.score);
164 | 
165 |   if (scored[0]) return scored[0].column;
166 | 
167 |   if (role) {
168 |     return schema.columns.find((column) => column.role === role) || null;
169 |   }
170 | 
171 |   return null;
172 | }
173 | 
174 | export function buildDatasetFacts(dataset = {}) {
175 |   const schema = buildSchemaSummary(dataset);
176 | 
177 |   return {
178 |     schema,
179 |     rowCount: schema.rowCount,
180 |     columnCount: schema.columnCount,
181 |     facts: {
182 |       rowCount: schema.rowCount,
183 |       columnCount: schema.columnCount,
184 |       numericColumns: schema.columns
185 |         .filter((column) => column.role === 'metric')
186 |         .map((column) => ({
187 |           name: column.name,
188 |           stats: column.stats,
189 |         })),
190 |       categoricalColumns: schema.columns
191 |         .filter((column) => column.role === 'dimension')
192 |         .map((column) => ({
193 |           name: column.name,
194 |           uniqueCount: column.uniqueCount,
195 |           topValues: column.topValues,
196 |         }))
197 |         .slice(0, 8),
198 |     },
199 |   };
200 | }
201 | 
```

```js
  1 | const OLLAMA_BASE_URL = process.env.OLLAMA_BASE_URL || 'http://localhost:11434';
  2 | 
  3 | export const OLLAMA_MODELS = {
  4 |   dashboard: process.env.OLLAMA_DASHBOARD_MODEL || 'neural-chat:7b',
  5 |   chat: process.env.OLLAMA_CHAT_MODEL || 'llama3.2',
  6 | };
  7 | 
  8 | const DEFAULT_TIMEOUT_MS = Number(process.env.OLLAMA_TIMEOUT_MS || 120000);
  9 | 
 10 | function makeTimeout(ms = DEFAULT_TIMEOUT_MS) {
 11 |   const controller = new AbortController();
 12 |   const timeout = setTimeout(() => controller.abort(), ms);
 13 | 
 14 |   return {
 15 |     signal: controller.signal,
 16 |     clear: () => clearTimeout(timeout),
 17 |   };
 18 | }
 19 | 
 20 | function extractJson(text = '') {
 21 |   const raw = String(text || '').trim();
 22 | 
 23 |   try {
 24 |     return JSON.parse(raw);
 25 |   } catch {
 26 |     const match = raw.match(/\{[\s\S]*\}/);
 27 | 
 28 |     if (!match) {
 29 |       throw new Error('Ollama response did not contain valid JSON.');
 30 |     }
 31 | 
 32 |     return JSON.parse(match[0]);
 33 |   }
 34 | }
 35 | 
 36 | export async function getOllamaStatus() {
 37 |   try {
 38 |     const response = await fetch(`${OLLAMA_BASE_URL}/api/tags`);
 39 | 
 40 |     if (!response.ok) {
 41 |       return {
 42 |         running: false,
 43 |         baseUrl: OLLAMA_BASE_URL,
 44 |         models: [],
 45 |         dashboardModel: OLLAMA_MODELS.dashboard,
 46 |         chatModel: OLLAMA_MODELS.chat,
 47 |       };
 48 |     }
 49 | 
 50 |     const payload = await response.json();
 51 | 
 52 |     return {
 53 |       running: true,
 54 |       baseUrl: OLLAMA_BASE_URL,
 55 |       models: (payload.models || []).map((model) => model.name),
 56 |       dashboardModel: OLLAMA_MODELS.dashboard,
 57 |       chatModel: OLLAMA_MODELS.chat,
 58 |     };
 59 |   } catch {
 60 |     return {
 61 |       running: false,
 62 |       baseUrl: OLLAMA_BASE_URL,
 63 |       models: [],
 64 |       dashboardModel: OLLAMA_MODELS.dashboard,
 65 |       chatModel: OLLAMA_MODELS.chat,
 66 |     };
 67 |   }
 68 | }
 69 | 
 70 | export async function assertOllamaModelAvailable(model) {
 71 |   const status = await getOllamaStatus();
 72 | 
 73 |   if (!status.running) {
 74 |     throw new Error('Ollama is not running. Run: ollama serve');
 75 |   }
 76 | 
 77 |   const exists = status.models.some(
 78 |     (name) => name === model || name.startsWith(`${model}:`),
 79 |   );
 80 | 
 81 |   if (!exists) {
 82 |     throw new Error(`Model "${model}" is not installed. Run: ollama pull ${model}`);
 83 |   }
 84 | 
 85 |   return true;
 86 | }
 87 | 
 88 | export async function callOllamaChat({
 89 |   model,
 90 |   messages,
 91 |   json = false,
 92 |   temperature = 0.1,
 93 |   topP = 0.9,
 94 |   numCtx = 8192,
 95 | }) {
 96 |   await assertOllamaModelAvailable(model);
 97 | 
 98 |   const timeout = makeTimeout();
 99 | 
100 |   try {
101 |     const response = await fetch(`${OLLAMA_BASE_URL}/api/chat`, {
102 |       method: 'POST',
103 |       headers: {
104 |         'Content-Type': 'application/json',
105 |       },
106 |       signal: timeout.signal,
107 |       body: JSON.stringify({
108 |         model,
109 |         messages,
110 |         stream: false,
111 |         format: json ? 'json' : undefined,
112 |         options: {
113 |           temperature,
114 |           top_p: topP,
115 |           num_ctx: numCtx,
116 |         },
117 |       }),
118 |     });
119 | 
120 |     if (!response.ok) {
121 |       const errorText = await response.text().catch(() => '');
122 |       throw new Error(`Ollama ${model} failed: ${response.status} ${errorText}`);
123 |     }
124 | 
125 |     const payload = await response.json();
126 |     const content = payload.message?.content || '';
127 | 
128 |     return {
129 |       provider: 'ollama',
130 |       model,
131 |       content,
132 |       json: json ? extractJson(content) : null,
133 |       raw: payload,
134 |     };
135 |   } finally {
136 |     timeout.clear();
137 |   }
138 | }
139 | 
140 | export function callDashboardPlanner(messages) {
141 |   return callOllamaChat({
142 |     model: OLLAMA_MODELS.dashboard,
143 |     messages,
144 |     json: true,
145 |     temperature: 0,
146 |     numCtx: 8192,
147 |   });
148 | }
149 | 
150 | export function callDatasetChat(messages) {
151 |   return callOllamaChat({
152 |     model: OLLAMA_MODELS.chat,
153 |     messages,
154 |     json: false,
155 |     temperature: 0.2,
156 |     numCtx: 8192,
157 |   });
158 | }
159 | 
```

### FILE: apps/backend/src/services/pdf/pdf-dataset-builder.js

Source path: `apps/backend/src/services/pdf/pdf-dataset-builder.js`

```js
 1 | import {
 2 |   extractPlainTextElements,
 3 |   extractTablesFromPdfJson,
 4 |   mergePdfTables,
 5 | } from "./pdf-table-extractor.js";
 6 | import { chunkPdfMarkdown } from "./pdf-rag-chunker.js";
 7 | 
 8 | function safeNumber(value) {
 9 |   if (typeof value === "number" && Number.isFinite(value)) return value;
10 | 
11 |   const cleaned = String(value ?? "").replace(/[,$₹%\s]/g, "").trim();
12 |   const parsed = Number(cleaned);
13 |   return Number.isFinite(parsed) ? parsed : null;
14 | }
15 | 
16 | function inferType(values = []) {
17 |   const present = values.filter((value) => value !== null && value !== undefined && value !== "");
18 |   if (!present.length) return "string";
19 | 
20 |   const numericCount = present.filter((value) => safeNumber(value) !== null).length;
21 |   if (numericCount / present.length >= 0.8) return "number";
22 | 
23 |   const dateCount = present.filter((value) => Number.isFinite(Date.parse(String(value)))).length;
24 |   if (dateCount / present.length >= 0.8) return "date";
25 | 
26 |   return "string";
27 | }
28 | 
29 | function buildColumns(rows = []) {
30 |   const names = Object.keys(rows[0] || {}).filter((name) => !name.startsWith("__"));
31 | 
32 |   return names.map((name) => ({
33 |     name,
34 |     type: inferType(rows.map((row) => row[name])),
35 |     source: "pdf_table",
36 |   }));
37 | }
38 | 
39 | export function buildPdfDataset({ fileName, pdfJson, markdown }) {
40 |   const tables = extractTablesFromPdfJson(pdfJson);
41 |   const textElements = extractPlainTextElements(pdfJson);
42 |   const rows = mergePdfTables(tables);
43 |   const chunks = chunkPdfMarkdown(markdown);
44 |   const columns = buildColumns(rows);
45 | 
46 |   const dataset = {
47 |     id: null,
48 |     name: `${fileName} Extracted Tables`,
49 |     fileName,
50 |     sourceType: "pdf",
51 |     uploadedAt: new Date().toISOString(),
52 |     rowCount: rows.length,
53 |     columnCount: columns.length,
54 |     columns,
55 |     rows,
56 |     metadata: {
57 |       pdfTableCount: tables.length,
58 |       pdfChunkCount: chunks.length,
59 |       pdfTextElementCount: textElements.length,
60 |     },
61 |   };
62 | 
63 |   const knowledgeBase = {
64 |     fileName,
65 |     tableCount: tables.length,
66 |     chunkCount: chunks.length,
67 |     textElementCount: textElements.length,
68 |     chunks,
69 |     textElements,
70 |   };
71 | 
72 |   return { dataset, tables, knowledgeBase };
73 | }
74 | 
```

### FILE: apps/backend/src/services/pdf/pdf-loader-service.js

```js
 1 | import fs from "node:fs/promises";
 2 | import path from "node:path";
 3 | import { randomUUID } from "node:crypto";
 4 | import { convert } from "@opendataloader/pdf";
 5 | 
 6 | const PDF_OUTPUT_DIR = path.resolve("data", "pdf-output");
 7 | 
 8 | async function findOutputFile(outputDir, extensions) {
 9 |   const files = await fs.readdir(outputDir);
10 |   const fileName = files.find((file) =>
11 |     extensions.some((extension) => file.toLowerCase().endsWith(extension))
12 |   );
13 | 
14 |   return fileName ? path.join(outputDir, fileName) : null;
15 | }
16 | 
17 | export async function parsePdfWithOpenDataLoader(filePath, options = {}) {
18 |   const jobId = randomUUID();
19 |   const outputDir = path.join(PDF_OUTPUT_DIR, jobId);
20 | 
21 |   await fs.mkdir(outputDir, { recursive: true });
22 | 
23 |   await convert([filePath], {
24 |     outputDir,
25 |     format: "json,markdown",
26 |     readingOrder: "xycut",
27 |     tableMethod: "cluster",
28 |     sanitize: true,
29 |     imageOutput: "external",
30 |     quiet: true,
31 |     ...options,
32 |   });
33 | 
34 |   const jsonPath = await findOutputFile(outputDir, [".json"]);
35 |   const markdownPath = await findOutputFile(outputDir, [".md", ".markdown"]);
36 | 
37 |   if (!jsonPath) {
38 |     throw new Error("PDF parsing failed: JSON output not found.");
39 |   }
40 | 
41 |   const json = JSON.parse(await fs.readFile(jsonPath, "utf8"));
42 |   const markdown = markdownPath ? await fs.readFile(markdownPath, "utf8") : "";
43 | 
44 |   return {
45 |     jobId,
46 |     outputDir,
47 |     jsonPath,
48 |     markdownPath,
49 |     json,
50 |     markdown,
51 |     privacy: {
52 |       parsedLocally: true,
53 |       rawPdfSentToLLM: false,
54 |     },
55 |   };
56 | }
57 | 
```

### FILE: apps/backend/src/services/pdf/pdf-qa-service.js

Source path: `apps/backend/src/services/pdf/pdf-qa-service.js`

```js
 1 | function tokenize(text = "") {
 2 |   return String(text)
 3 |     .toLowerCase()
 4 |     .replace(/[^a-z0-9₹$%.\s]/g, " ")
 5 |     .split(/\s+/)
 6 |     .filter((token) => token.length > 2);
 7 | }
 8 | 
 9 | function scoreChunk(queryTokens, chunk) {
10 |   const text = String(chunk.content || "").toLowerCase();
11 |   let score = 0;
12 | 
13 |   for (const token of queryTokens) {
14 |     if (text.includes(token)) score += 1;
15 |   }
16 | 
17 |   return score;
18 | }
19 | 
20 | export function searchPdfChunks({ query, knowledgeBase, limit = 5 }) {
21 |   const queryTokens = tokenize(query);
22 |   if (!queryTokens.length) return [];
23 | 
24 |   return (knowledgeBase?.chunks || [])
25 |     .map((chunk) => ({ ...chunk, score: scoreChunk(queryTokens, chunk) }))
26 |     .filter((chunk) => chunk.score > 0)
27 |     .sort((a, b) => b.score - a.score)
28 |     .slice(0, limit);
29 | }
30 | 
31 | export async function answerPdfQuestion({ query, knowledgeBase }) {
32 |   const matches = searchPdfChunks({ query, knowledgeBase, limit: 5 });
33 | 
34 |   if (!matches.length) {
35 |     return {
36 |       answer: "I could not find enough relevant PDF context for that question.",
37 |       sources: [],
38 |     };
39 |   }
40 | 
41 |   const context = matches
42 |     .map((chunk, index) => `SOURCE ${index + 1}:\n${chunk.content}`)
43 |     .join("\n\n");
44 | 
45 |   const baseUrl = process.env.OLLAMA_BASE_URL || "http://localhost:11434";
46 |   const model = process.env.OLLAMA_MODEL || "llama3.2:latest";
47 | 
48 |   try {
49 |     const response = await fetch(`${baseUrl}/api/chat`, {
50 |       method: "POST",
51 |       headers: { "Content-Type": "application/json" },
52 |       body: JSON.stringify({
53 |         model,
54 |         stream: false,
55 |         messages: [
56 |           {
57 |             role: "system",
58 |             content:
59 |               "You answer questions only from the provided PDF context. If the answer is not in the context, say you do not know. Keep answers short and cite source numbers.",
60 |           },
61 |           {
62 |             role: "user",
63 |             content: `Question: ${query}\n\nPDF Context:\n${context}`,
64 |           },
65 |         ],
66 |         options: { temperature: 0 },
67 |       }),
68 |     });
69 | 
70 |     if (!response.ok) throw new Error("Local LLM request failed");
71 | 
72 |     const data = await response.json();
73 | 
74 |     return {
75 |       answer: data.message?.content || "No answer generated.",
76 |       sources: matches.map((chunk, index) => ({
77 |         source: index + 1,
78 |         id: chunk.id,
79 |         preview: chunk.content.slice(0, 240),
80 |       })),
81 |     };
82 |   } catch {
83 |     return {
84 |       answer:
85 |         "I found relevant PDF context, but local LLM is not available. Showing the best source snippets instead.",
86 |       sources: matches.map((chunk, index) => ({
87 |         source: index + 1,
88 |         id: chunk.id,
89 |         preview: chunk.content.slice(0, 240),
90 |       })),
91 |     };
92 |   }
93 | }
94 | 
```

### FILE: apps/backend/src/services/pdf/pdf-rag-chunker.js

Source path: `apps/backend/src/services/pdf/pdf-rag-chunker.js`

```js
 1 | export function chunkPdfMarkdown(markdown = "", options = {}) {
 2 |   const chunkSize = options.chunkSize || 1200;
 3 |   const overlap = options.overlap || 150;
 4 |   const normalized = String(markdown || "").replace(/\r\n/g, "\n").trim();
 5 | 
 6 |   if (!normalized) return [];
 7 | 
 8 |   const sections = normalized
 9 |     .split(/\n(?=#{1,6}\s)/g)
10 |     .map((section) => section.trim())
11 |     .filter(Boolean);
12 | 
13 |   const chunks = [];
14 | 
15 |   for (const section of sections.length ? sections : [normalized]) {
16 |     let start = 0;
17 | 
18 |     while (start < section.length) {
19 |       const end = Math.min(start + chunkSize, section.length);
20 |       const content = section.slice(start, end).trim();
21 | 
22 |       if (content) {
23 |         chunks.push({
24 |           id: `chunk_${chunks.length + 1}`,
25 |           content,
26 |           sourceType: "pdf_markdown",
27 |           tokenEstimate: [REDACTED_SECRET],
28 |         });
29 |       }
30 | 
31 |       if (end >= section.length) break;
32 |       start = Math.max(0, end - overlap);
33 |     }
34 |   }
35 | 
36 |   return chunks;
37 | }
38 | 
```

```js
 8 | 
 9 |   if (!fs.existsSync(PDF_STORE_FILE)) {
10 |     fs.writeFileSync(PDF_STORE_FILE, JSON.stringify({}, null, 2));
11 |   }
12 | }

16 |

19 |   } catch {
20 |     return {};
21 |   }
```

### FILE: apps/backend/src/services/pdf/pdf-table-extractor.js

Source path: `apps/backend/src/services/pdf/pdf-table-extractor.js`

```js
  1 | function walk(node, visitor) {
  2 |   if (!node || typeof node !== "object") return;
  3 | 
  4 |   visitor(node);
  5 | 
  6 |   for (const value of Object.values(node)) {
  7 |     if (Array.isArray(value)) {
  8 |       for (const child of value) walk(child, visitor);
  9 |     } else if (value && typeof value === "object") {
 10 |       walk(value, visitor);
 11 |     }
 12 |   }
 13 | }
 14 | 
 15 | function getNodeText(node) {
 16 |   const parts = [];
 17 | 
 18 |   walk(node, (child) => {
 19 |     if (typeof child.content === "string") {
 20 |       const text = child.content.trim();
 21 |       if (text) parts.push(text);
 22 |     }
 23 | 
 24 |     if (typeof child.text === "string") {
 25 |       const text = child.text.trim();
 26 |       if (text) parts.push(text);
 27 |     }
 28 |   });
 29 | 
 30 |   return parts.join(" ").trim();
 31 | }
 32 | 
 33 | function getNodePage(node) {
 34 |   return node["page number"] || node.pageNumber || node.page || null;
 35 | }
 36 | 
 37 | function getNodeBoundingBox(node) {
 38 |   return node["bounding box"] || node.boundingBox || node.bbox || null;
 39 | }
 40 | 
 41 | function normalizeHeader(value, index) {
 42 |   const cleaned = String(value || "")
 43 |     .trim()
 44 |     .replace(/[^a-zA-Z0-9]+/g, "_")
 45 |     .replace(/^_+|_+$/g, "");
 46 | 
 47 |   return cleaned || `column_${index + 1}`;
 48 | }
 49 | 
 50 | function dedupeHeaders(headers) {
 51 |   const counts = new Map();
 52 | 
 53 |   return headers.map((header) => {
 54 |     const count = counts.get(header) || 0;
 55 |     counts.set(header, count + 1);
 56 |     return count === 0 ? header : `${header}_${count + 1}`;
 57 |   });
 58 | }
 59 | 
 60 | export function extractPlainTextElements(pdfJson) {
 61 |   const elements = [];
 62 | 
 63 |   walk(pdfJson, (node) => {
 64 |     const type = String(node.type || "").toLowerCase();
 65 | 
 66 |     if (!["paragraph", "heading", "caption", "list item", "list_item"].includes(type)) {
 67 |       return;
 68 |     }
 69 | 
 70 |     const content = getNodeText(node);
 71 |     if (!content) return;
 72 | 
 73 |     elements.push({
 74 |       id: node.id || `element_${elements.length + 1}`,
 75 |       type,
 76 |       content,
 77 |       pageNumber: getNodePage(node),
 78 |       boundingBox: getNodeBoundingBox(node),
 79 |     });
 80 |   });
 81 | 
 82 |   return elements;
 83 | }
 84 | 
 85 | export function extractTablesFromPdfJson(pdfJson) {
 86 |   const tables = [];
 87 | 
 88 |   walk(pdfJson, (node) => {
 89 |     const type = String(node.type || "").toLowerCase();
 90 | 
 91 |     if (type !== "table" || !Array.isArray(node.rows)) return;
 92 | 
 93 |     const matrix = node.rows
 94 |       .map((row) => {
 95 |         const cells = Array.isArray(row.cells) ? row.cells : [];
 96 |         return cells.map((cell) => getNodeText(cell));
 97 |       })
 98 |       .filter((row) => row.some((cell) => String(cell || "").trim()));
 99 | 
100 |     if (!matrix.length) return;
101 | 
102 |     const headers = dedupeHeaders(matrix[0].map(normalizeHeader));
103 |     const bodyRows = matrix.slice(1);
104 |     const tableId = `table_${tables.length + 1}`;
105 | 
106 |     const rows = bodyRows
107 |       .filter((row) => row.some((cell) => String(cell || "").trim()))
108 |       .map((row, rowIndex) => {
109 |         const record = {
110 |           __rowId: rowIndex + 1,
111 |           __sourceType: "pdf_table",
112 |           __tableId: tableId,
113 |           __pageNumber: getNodePage(node),
114 |           __boundingBox: getNodeBoundingBox(node),
115 |         };
116 | 
117 |         headers.forEach((header, index) => {
118 |           record[header] = row[index] ?? "";
119 |         });
120 | 
121 |         return record;
122 |       });
123 | 
124 |     tables.push({
125 |       id: tableId,
126 |       pageNumber: getNodePage(node),
127 |       boundingBox: getNodeBoundingBox(node),
128 |       rowCount: rows.length,
129 |       columnCount: headers.length,
130 |       headers,
131 |       rows,
132 |     });
133 |   });
134 | 
135 |   return tables;
136 | }
137 | 
138 | export function mergePdfTables(tables = []) {
139 |   return tables.flatMap((table) => table.rows || []);
140 | }
141 | 
```

### FILE: apps/backend/src/services/pipeline-service.js

Source path: `apps/backend/src/services/pipeline-service.js`

```js
  1 | import { prepareDatasetForAnalytics, buildDatasetSchema, toNumber } from '@insightflow/shared-analytics';
  2 | import { classifyColumns, smartAutoChartGeneration, buildEnhancedSchema } from './schema-detector.js';
  3 | import { reportGenerator } from './report-generator.js';
  4 | import { predictiveAnalytics } from './predictive-analytics.js';
  5 | import { alertService } from './alert-service.js';
  6 | import { recommendationEngine } from './recommendation-engine.js';
  7 | 
  8 | function getCleanColumnClassification(rows) {
  9 |   const classification = classifyColumns(rows);
 10 |   return {
 11 |     numeric: classification.numeric || [],
 12 |     categorical: classification.categorical || [],
 13 |     date: classification.date || []
 14 |   };
 15 | }
 16 | 
 17 | export class DataPipeline {
 18 |   constructor() {
 19 |     this.datasets = new Map();
 20 |   }
 21 | 
 22 |   async processDataset(rawDataset) {
 23 |     const prepared = prepareDatasetForAnalytics(rawDataset);
 24 |     const classification = getCleanColumnClassification(prepared.rows);
 25 |     const schema = buildDatasetSchema(prepared);
 26 |     const enhancedSchema = buildEnhancedSchema(prepared.columns, prepared.rows);
 27 |     const charts = smartAutoChartGeneration(prepared.rows, classification);
 28 |     const columnStats = this.calculateColumnStats(prepared.rows, prepared.columns);
 29 |     
 30 |     const processed = {
 31 |       ...prepared,
 32 |       classification,
 33 |       schema,
 34 |       enhancedSchema,
 35 |       charts,
 36 |       columnStats,
 37 |       processedAt: new Date().toISOString()
 38 |     };
 39 | 
 40 |     this.datasets.set(prepared.id || prepared.name, processed);
 41 |     return processed;
 42 |   }
 43 | 
 44 |   calculateColumnStats(rows, columns) {
 45 |     const stats = {};
 46 |     
 47 |     for (const col of columns) {
 48 |       const values = rows.map(r => r[col.name]).filter(v => v != null);
 49 |       const numericValues = values.map(v => Number(v)).filter(v => !isNaN(v));
 50 |       
 51 |       if (numericValues.length > values.length * 0.7) {
 52 |         numericValues.sort((a, b) => a - b);
 53 |         const sum = numericValues.reduce((a, b) => a + b, 0);
 54 |         stats[col.name] = {
 55 |           type: 'numeric',
 56 |           count: numericValues.length,
 57 |           missing: values.length - numericValues.length,
 58 |           unique: new Set(numericValues).size,
 59 |           min: numericValues[0],
 60 |           max: numericValues[numericValues.length - 1],
 61 |           mean: sum / numericValues.length,
 62 |           median: numericValues[Math.floor(numericValues.length / 2)],
 63 |           std: this.calculateStd(numericValues)
 64 |         };
 65 |       } else {
 66 |         stats[col.name] = {
 67 |           type: 'categorical',
 68 |           count: values.length,
 69 |           missing: rows.length - values.length,
 70 |           unique: new Set(values).size
 71 |         };
 72 |       }
 73 |     }
 74 | 
 75 |     return stats;
 76 |   }
 77 | 
 78 |   calculateStd(values) {
 79 |     if (values.length === 0) return 0;
 80 |     const mean = values.reduce((a, b) => a + b, 0) / values.length;
 81 |     const variance = values.reduce((a, b) => a + Math.pow(b - mean, 2), 0) / values.length;
 82 |     return Math.sqrt(variance);
 83 |   }
 84 | 
 85 |   async generateFullAnalysis(datasetId) {
 86 |     const dataset = this.datasets.get(datasetId);
 87 |     if (!dataset) return { error: 'Dataset not found' };
 88 | 
 89 |     const analysis = {
 90 |       dataset: {
 91 |         name: dataset.name,
 92 |         rowCount: dataset.rowCount,
 93 |         columnCount: dataset.columns?.length
 94 |       },
 95 |       columnStats: dataset.columnStats,
 96 |       charts: dataset.charts,
 97 |       insights: [],
 98 |       predictions: {},
 99 |       alerts: [],
100 |       recommendations: []
101 |     };
102 | 
103 |     try {
104 |       analysis.recommendations = await recommendationEngine.generateRecommendations(dataset);
105 |     } catch (e) {
106 |       analysis.recommendations = [];
107 |     }
108 | 
109 |     const numericCols = dataset.classification?.numeric || [];
110 |     if (numericCols.length > 0) {
111 |       try {
112 |         analysis.predictions = await predictiveAnalytics.analyzeMultipleMetrics(
113 |           dataset.rows,
114 |           numericCols.slice(0, 3)
115 |         );
116 |       } catch (e) {
117 |         analysis.predictions = {};
118 |       }
119 |     }
120 | 
121 |     try {
122 |       const alertResults = await alertService.checkAllAlerts(dataset);
123 |       analysis.alerts = alertResults;
124 |     } catch (e) {
125 |       analysis.alerts = [];
126 |     }
127 | 
128 |     return analysis;
129 |   }
130 | 
131 |   async generateReport(datasetId, options = {}) {
132 |     const dataset = this.datasets.get(datasetId);
133 |     if (!dataset) return { error: 'Dataset not found' };
134 | 
135 |     return reportGenerator.generateReport(dataset, options);
136 |   }
137 | 
138 |   getDataset(id) {
139 |     return this.datasets.get(id);
140 |   }
141 | 
142 |   listDatasets() {
143 |     return Array.from(this.datasets.keys());
144 |   }
145 | 
146 |   clearDatasets() {
147 |     this.datasets.clear();
148 |   }
149 | }
150 | 
151 | export const pipelineService = new DataPipeline();
```

### FILE: apps/backend/src/services/playbooks/playbook-dashboard-engine.js

Source path: `apps/backend/src/services/playbooks/playbook-dashboard-engine.js`

```js
  1 | import { randomUUID } from "node:crypto";
  2 | import {
  3 |   buildSchema,
  4 |   cleanRows,
  5 |   findColumn,
  6 |   matchPlaybook,
  7 |   safeNumber,
  8 | } from "./playbook-matcher.js";
  9 | import {
 10 |   createColumnSignature,
 11 |   findSimilarPlaybook,
 12 |   saveAnalyticsMemory,
 13 | } from "../analytics-memory-service.js";
 14 | 
 15 | function humanize(value = "") {
 16 |   return String(value).replace(/[_-]+/g, " ").replace(/\b\w/g, (char) => char.toUpperCase());
 17 | }
 18 | 
 19 | function formatMetric(name, value) {
 20 |   const normalized = String(name || "").toLowerCase();
 21 | 
 22 |   if (/salary|revenue|sales|amount|price|cost|profit|income/.test(normalized)) {
 23 |     return `$${Math.round(value).toLocaleString()}`;
 24 |   }
 25 | 
 26 |   if (/rate|percent|margin|ratio|quality/.test(normalized)) {
 27 |     return `${Number(value).toFixed(1)}%`;
 28 |   }
 29 | 
 30 |   return Number(value.toFixed ? value.toFixed(2) : value).toLocaleString();
 31 | }
 32 | 
 33 | function aggregate(values = [], type = "count") {
 34 |   const present = values.filter((value) => value !== null && value !== undefined && value !== "");
 35 | 
 36 |   if (type === "count") return present.length;
 37 | 
 38 |   const numbers = present.map(safeNumber).filter((value) => value !== null);
 39 | 
 40 |   if (type === "missing_count") return values.length - present.length;
 41 |   if (!numbers.length) return 0;
 42 |   if (type === "sum") return numbers.reduce((left, right) => left + right, 0);
 43 |   if (type === "avg") return numbers.reduce((left, right) => left + right, 0) / numbers.length;
 44 |   if (type === "min") return Math.min(...numbers);
 45 |   if (type === "max") return Math.max(...numbers);
 46 | 
 47 |   if (type === "median") {
 48 |     const sorted = [...numbers].sort((left, right) => left - right);
 49 |     const mid = Math.floor(sorted.length / 2);
 50 | 
 51 |     return sorted.length % 2 ? sorted[mid] : (sorted[mid - 1] + sorted[mid]) / 2;
 52 |   }
 53 | 
 54 |   return present.length;
 55 | }
 56 | 
 57 | function groupBy(rows, xKey, yKey, aggregation = "count", limit = 10) {
 58 |   const groups = new Map();
 59 | 
 60 |   for (const row of rows) {
 61 |     const label = String(row[xKey] ?? "Unknown").trim() || "Unknown";
 62 |     if (!groups.has(label)) groups.set(label, []);
 63 |     groups.get(label).push(yKey ? row[yKey] : label);
 64 |   }
 65 | 
 66 |   return [...groups.entries()]
 67 |     .map(([label, values]) => ({
 68 |       [xKey]: label,
 69 |       [aggregation === "count" ? "count" : yKey]: aggregate(values, aggregation),
 70 |     }))
 71 |     .sort((left, right) => {
 72 |       const leftValue = Number(Object.values(left).at(-1));
 73 |       const rightValue = Number(Object.values(right).at(-1));
 74 |       return rightValue - leftValue;
 75 |     })
 76 |     .slice(0, limit);
 77 | }
 78 | 
 79 | function histogram(rows, key, bins = 8) {
 80 |   const values = rows.map((row) => safeNumber(row[key])).filter((value) => value !== null);
 81 | 
 82 |   if (!values.length) return [];
 83 | 
 84 |   const min = Math.min(...values);
 85 |   const max = Math.max(...values);
 86 | 
 87 |   if (min === max) {
 88 |     return [{ range: String(min), count: values.length }];
 89 |   }
 90 | 
 91 |   const size = (max - min) / bins;
 92 |   const buckets = Array.from({ length: bins }, (_, index) => ({
 93 |     start: min + index * size,
 94 |     end: index === bins - 1 ? max : min + (index + 1) * size,
 95 |     count: 0,
 96 |   }));
 97 | 
 98 |   for (const value of values) {
 99 |     const index = Math.min(Math.floor((value - min) / size), bins - 1);
100 |     buckets[index].count += 1;
101 |   }
102 | 
103 |   return buckets.map((bucket) => ({
104 |     range: `${Math.round(bucket.start).toLocaleString()}-${Math.round(bucket.end).toLocaleString()}`,
105 |     count: bucket.count,
106 |   }));
107 | }
108 | 
109 | function scatter(rows, xKey, yKey, limit = 200) {
110 |   return rows
111 |     .map((row) => ({ [xKey]: safeNumber(row[xKey]), [yKey]: safeNumber(row[yKey]) }))
112 |     .filter((row) => row[xKey] !== null && row[yKey] !== null)
113 |     .slice(0, limit);
114 | }
115 | 
116 | function calculateQualityScore(schema) {
117 |   const totalCells = schema.rowCount * Math.max(schema.columnCount, 1);
118 |   const missingCells = schema.columns.reduce((sum, column) => sum + column.nullCount, 0);
119 | 
120 |   if (!totalCells) return 0;
121 | 
122 |   return Math.max(0, Math.min(100, ((totalCells - missingCells) / totalCells) * 100));
123 | }
124 | 
125 | function buildKpi(rows, schema, template) {
126 |   if (template.aggregation === "count") {
127 |     return { id: randomUUID(), title: template.title, value: rows.length.toLocaleString(), metric: "*", aggregation: "count" };
128 |   }
129 | 
130 |   if (template.aggregation === "quality_score") {
131 |     return { id: randomUUID(), title: template.title, value: `${calculateQualityScore(schema).toFixed(1)}%`, metric: "*", aggregation: "quality_score" };
132 |   }
133 | 
134 |   if (template.aggregation === "missing_count") {
135 |     const missing = schema.columns.reduce((sum, column) => sum + column.nullCount, 0);
136 |     return { id: randomUUID(), title: template.title, value: missing.toLocaleString(), metric: "*", aggregation: "missing_count" };
137 |   }
138 | 
139 |   if (template.aggregation === "basket_size") {
140 |     const transaction = findColumn(schema, { aliases: ["transaction", "transaction_id", "invoice", "order_id"] });
141 |     if (!transaction) return null;
142 | 
143 |     const transactions = new Map();
144 |     for (const row of rows) {
145 |       const id = String(row[transaction.name] ?? "");
146 |       if (!id) continue;
147 |       transactions.set(id, (transactions.get(id) || 0) + 1);
148 |     }
149 | 
150 |     const sizes = [...transactions.values()];
151 |     const avg = sizes.length ? sizes.reduce((left, right) => left + right, 0) / sizes.length : 0;
152 | 
153 |     return { id: randomUUID(), title: template.title, value: Number(avg.toFixed(2)).toLocaleString(), metric: transaction.name, aggregation: "basket_size" };
154 |   }
155 | 
156 |   if (template.aggregation === "rule_count") {
157 |     const support = findColumn(schema, { aliases: ["support"] });
158 |     const confidence = findColumn(schema, { aliases: ["confidence"] });
159 |     const lift = findColumn(schema, { aliases: ["lift"] });
160 | 
161 |     if (!support && !confidence && !lift) return null;
162 | 
163 |     const count = rows.filter((row) => {
164 |       const supportValue = support ? safeNumber(row[support.name]) : 1;
165 |       const confidenceValue = confidence ? safeNumber(row[confidence.name]) : 1;
166 |       const liftValue = lift ? safeNumber(row[lift.name]) : 1;
167 |       return (supportValue ?? 0) > 0 && (confidenceValue ?? 0) > 0 && (liftValue ?? 0) >= 1;
168 |     }).length;
169 | 
170 |     return { id: randomUUID(), title: template.title, value: count.toLocaleString(), aggregation: "rule_count" };
171 |   }
172 | 
173 |   if (template.aggregation === "sentiment_count") {
174 |     const sentiment = findColumn(schema, { aliases: ["sentiment", "polarity"] });
175 |     if (!sentiment) return null;
176 | 
177 |     const target = String(template.value || "").toLowerCase();
178 |     const count = rows.filter((row) => String(row[sentiment.name] || "").toLowerCase().includes(target)).length;
179 | 
180 |     return { id: randomUUID(), title: template.title, value: count.toLocaleString(), metric: sentiment.name, aggregation: "sentiment_count" };
181 |   }
182 | 
183 |   if (template.aggregation === "unique_count") {
184 |     const dimension = findColumn(schema, {
185 |       aliases: template.dimensionAliases,
186 |       role: template.dimensionRole || "category",
187 |     });
188 | 
189 |     if (!dimension) return null;
190 | 
191 |     const unique = new Set(rows.map((row) => String(row[dimension.name] ?? "")).filter(Boolean));
192 |     return { id: randomUUID(), title: template.title, value: unique.size.toLocaleString(), metric: dimension.name, aggregation: "unique_count" };
193 |   }
194 | 
195 |   const metric = findColumn(schema, { aliases: template.metricAliases, role: "metric" });
196 |   if (!metric) return null;
197 | 
198 |   const value = aggregate(rows.map((row) => row[metric.name]), template.aggregation);
199 | 
200 |   return {
201 |     id: randomUUID(),
202 |     title: template.title || `${humanize(template.aggregation)} ${humanize(metric.name)}`,
203 |     value: formatMetric(metric.name, value),
204 |     metric: metric.name,
205 |     aggregation: template.aggregation,
206 |   };
207 | }
208 | 
209 | function buildChart(rows, schema, template) {
210 |   const xColumn = findColumn(schema, { aliases: template.xAliases, role: template.xRole });
211 |   const yColumn = findColumn(schema, { aliases: template.yAliases, role: template.yRole });
212 | 
213 |   if (template.type === "histogram") {
214 |     const metric = yColumn || xColumn || findColumn(schema, { role: "metric" });
215 |     if (!metric) return null;
216 | 
217 |     return {
218 |       id: randomUUID(),
219 |       title: template.title || `${humanize(metric.name)} Distribution`,
220 |       type: "histogram",
221 |       xKey: "range",
222 |       yKey: "count",
223 |       aggregation: "count",
224 |       data: histogram(rows, metric.name, template.limit || 8),
225 |     };
226 |   }
227 | 
228 |   if (template.type === "scatter") {
229 |     if (!xColumn || !yColumn) return null;
230 | 
231 |     return {
232 |       id: randomUUID(),
233 |       title: template.title,
234 |       type: "scatter",
235 |       xKey: xColumn.name,
236 |       yKey: yColumn.name,
237 |       aggregation: "raw",
238 |       data: scatter(rows, xColumn.name, yColumn.name, template.limit || 200),
239 |     };
240 |   }
241 | 
242 |   if (!xColumn) return null;
243 | 
244 |   const aggregation = template.aggregation || "count";
245 |   const yKey = aggregation === "count" ? null : yColumn?.name;
246 | 
247 |   if (aggregation !== "count" && !yKey) return null;
248 | 
249 |   return {
250 |     id: randomUUID(),
251 |     title: template.title,
252 |     type: template.type || "bar",
253 |     xKey: xColumn.name,
254 |     yKey: aggregation === "count" ? "count" : yKey,
255 |     aggregation,
256 |     data: groupBy(rows, xColumn.name, yKey, aggregation, template.limit || 10),
257 |   };
258 | }
259 | 
260 | function buildLearnedChart(rows, schema, template) {
261 |   const xColumn = schema.columns.find((column) => column.name === template.xKey);
262 |   const yColumn = schema.columns.find((column) => column.name === template.yKey);
263 | 
264 |   if (template.type === "histogram") {
265 |     const metric = yColumn || xColumn;
266 |     if (!metric) return null;
267 | 
268 |     return { ...template, id: randomUUID(), xKey: "range", yKey: "count", data: histogram(rows, metric.name, 8), learned: true };
269 |   }
270 | 
271 |   if (!xColumn) return null;
272 |   if (template.aggregation !== "count" && !yColumn) return null;
273 | 
274 |   return {
275 |     ...template,
276 |     id: randomUUID(),
277 |     data: groupBy(rows, xColumn.name, yColumn?.name, template.aggregation || "count", 10),
278 |     learned: true,
279 |   };
280 | }
281 | 
282 | export async function buildDataAnalyticsProjectsDashboard(dataset = {}) {
283 |   const schema = buildSchema(dataset);
284 |   const rows = cleanRows(dataset.rows || []);
285 |   const matchedPlaybook = matchPlaybook(schema);
286 |   const columnSignature = createColumnSignature(schema.columns);
287 |   const rememberedPlaybook = findSimilarPlaybook({ domain: matchedPlaybook.domain, columnSignature });
288 | 
289 |   const kpis = matchedPlaybook.kpis.map((template) => buildKpi(rows, schema, template)).filter(Boolean);
290 |   let charts = matchedPlaybook.charts
291 |     .map((template) => buildChart(rows, schema, template))
292 |     .filter((chart) => chart && chart.data?.length);
293 | 
294 |   if (rememberedPlaybook?.chartTemplates?.length) {
295 |     const learnedCharts = rememberedPlaybook.chartTemplates
296 |       .map((template) => buildLearnedChart(rows, schema, template))
297 |       .filter((chart) => chart && chart.data?.length);
298 | 
299 |     charts = [...learnedCharts, ...charts].slice(0, 10);
300 |   }
301 | 
302 |   saveAnalyticsMemory({
303 |     domain: matchedPlaybook.domain,
304 |     columnSignature,
305 |     kpiTemplates: kpis.map((kpi) => ({ title: kpi.title, metric: kpi.metric, aggregation: kpi.aggregation })),
306 |     chartTemplates: charts.map((chart) => ({
307 |       title: chart.title,
308 |       type: chart.type,
309 |       xKey: chart.xKey,
310 |       yKey: chart.yKey,
311 |       aggregation: chart.aggregation,
312 |     })),
313 |   });
314 | 
315 |   return {
316 |     dataType: matchedPlaybook.domain,
317 |     dataTypeLabel: matchedPlaybook.label,
318 |     chartRecommendations: charts.slice(0, 10),
319 |     kpis: kpis.slice(0, 8),
320 |     insights: [
321 |       {
322 |         type: "summary",
323 |         title: "Playbook Selected",
324 |         message: `${matchedPlaybook.label} playbook selected from DataAnalyticsProjects-style analytics patterns.`,
325 |       },
326 |       {
327 |         type: "privacy",
328 |         title: "Schema-only AI Mode",
329 |         message: "Dashboard values were calculated locally. Raw rows were not sent to the LLM.",
330 |       },
331 |     ],
332 |     aiInsights: { schemaPacket: schema.schemaOnlyPacket },
333 |     memory: {
334 |       usedPreviousPattern: Boolean(rememberedPlaybook),
335 |       similarity: rememberedPlaybook?.similarity || 0,
336 |       domain: matchedPlaybook.domain,
337 |       columnSignature,
338 |     },
339 |     privacy: {
340 |       schemaOnly: true,
341 |       rawRowsSentToAI: false,
342 |     },
343 |   };
344 | }
345 | 
```
