### FILE: apps/backend/src/services/playbooks/playbook-matcher.js

Source path: `apps/backend/src/services/playbooks/playbook-matcher.js`

```js
  1 | import { DATA_ANALYTICS_PROJECT_PLAYBOOKS } from "./data-analytics-projects-playbooks.js";
  2 | 
  3 | export function normalizeName(value = "") {
  4 |   return String(value)
  5 |     .toLowerCase()
  6 |     .trim()
  7 |     .replace(/[^a-z0-9]+/g, "_")
  8 |     .replace(/^_+|_+$/g, "");
  9 | }
 10 | 
 11 | export function safeNumber(value) {
 12 |   if (typeof value === "number" && Number.isFinite(value)) return value;
 13 | 
 14 |   const cleaned = String(value ?? "")
 15 |     .replace(/[₹$,%]/g, "")
 16 |     .replace(/,/g, "")
 17 |     .trim();
 18 | 
 19 |   const parsed = Number(cleaned);
 20 |   return Number.isFinite(parsed) ? parsed : null;
 21 | }
 22 | 
 23 | export function isDateLike(value) {
 24 |   if (value instanceof Date) return true;
 25 |   if (typeof value !== "string") return false;
 26 |   if (!/\d/.test(value)) return false;
 27 | 
 28 |   return Number.isFinite(Date.parse(value));
 29 | }
 30 | 
 31 | export function inferType(values = []) {
 32 |   const sample = values
 33 |     .filter((value) => value !== null && value !== undefined && value !== "")
 34 |     .slice(0, 100);
 35 | 
 36 |   if (!sample.length) return "string";
 37 | 
 38 |   const numeric = sample.filter((value) => safeNumber(value) !== null).length;
 39 |   const date = sample.filter(isDateLike).length;
 40 | 
 41 |   if (numeric / sample.length >= 0.8) return "number";
 42 |   if (date / sample.length >= 0.8) return "date";
 43 | 
 44 |   return "string";
 45 | }
 46 | 
 47 | export function cleanRows(rows = []) {
 48 |   return rows.filter((row) => {
 49 |     const keys = Object.keys(row || {}).map(normalizeName);
 50 |     const source = String(row?._sourceFile || row?.source || "").toLowerCase();
 51 |     const dictionaryShape = keys.includes("column") && keys.includes("type") && keys.includes("description");
 52 | 
 53 |     return !source.includes("dictionary") && !dictionaryShape;
 54 |   });
 55 | }
 56 | 
 57 | export function buildSchema(dataset = {}) {
 58 |   const rows = cleanRows(dataset.rows || []);
 59 |   const rawColumns = dataset.columns?.length
 60 |     ? dataset.columns.map((column) => column.name || column)
 61 |     : Object.keys(rows[0] || {});
 62 | 
 63 |   const columns = rawColumns.map((name) => {
 64 |     const values = rows.map((row) => row[name]);
 65 |     const type = inferType(values);
 66 |     const present = values.filter((value) => value !== null && value !== undefined && value !== "");
 67 |     const uniqueValues = [...new Set(present.map(String))];
 68 | 
 69 |     let role = "category";
 70 |     if (type === "number") role = "metric";
 71 |     if (type === "date") role = "date";
 72 | 
 73 |     return {
 74 |       name,
 75 |       normalizedName: normalizeName(name),
 76 |       type,
 77 |       role,
 78 |       uniqueCount: uniqueValues.length,
 79 |       nullCount: rows.length - present.length,
 80 |       topValues: type === "string" ? uniqueValues.slice(0, 15) : [],
 81 |     };
 82 |   });
 83 | 
 84 |   return {
 85 |     name: dataset.name || dataset.fileName || "Uploaded Dataset",
 86 |     rowCount: rows.length,
 87 |     columnCount: columns.length,
 88 |     columns,
 89 |     rows,
 90 |     columnSignature: columns.map((column) => column.normalizedName).sort().join("|"),
 91 |     schemaOnlyPacket: {
 92 |       name: dataset.name || dataset.fileName || "Uploaded Dataset",
 93 |       rowCount: rows.length,
 94 |       columnCount: columns.length,
 95 |       columns: columns.map((column) => ({
 96 |         name: column.name,
 97 |         type: column.type,
 98 |         role: column.role,
 99 |         uniqueCount: column.uniqueCount,
100 |         nullCount: column.nullCount,
101 |         topValues: column.topValues.slice(0, 8),
102 |       })),
103 |       rawRowsSentToAI: false,
104 |     },
105 |   };
106 | }
107 | 
108 | export function matchPlaybook(schema) {
109 |   const names = schema.columns.map((column) => column.normalizedName).join(" ");
110 | 
111 |   const scored = DATA_ANALYTICS_PROJECT_PLAYBOOKS.map((playbook) => {
112 |     let score = 0;
113 | 
114 |     for (const keyword of playbook.keywords) {
115 |       const normalizedKeyword = normalizeName(keyword);
116 |       if (names.includes(normalizedKeyword)) score += 2;
117 |     }
118 | 
119 |     const hasDate = schema.columns.some((column) => column.role === "date");
120 |     const hasMetric = schema.columns.some((column) => column.role === "metric");
121 |     const hasCategory = schema.columns.some((column) => column.role === "category");
122 | 
123 |     if (playbook.domain === "time_series" && hasDate && hasMetric) score += 5;
124 |     if (playbook.domain === "eda" && hasMetric && hasCategory) score += 3;
125 |     if (playbook.domain === "market_basket" && /transaction|invoice|basket|item|product/.test(names)) score += 5;
126 |     if (playbook.domain === "sentiment" && /review|comment|feedback|sentiment|rating/.test(names)) score += 5;
127 |     if (playbook.domain === "recommendation" && /user|item|movie|product|rating/.test(names)) score += 5;
128 | 
129 |     return { playbook, score };
130 |   }).sort((left, right) => right.score - left.score);
131 | 
132 |   const best = scored[0];
133 | 
134 |   if (!best || best.score <= 0) {
135 |     return DATA_ANALYTICS_PROJECT_PLAYBOOKS.find((playbook) => playbook.domain === "eda");
136 |   }
137 | 
138 |   return best.playbook;
139 | }
140 | 
141 | export function findColumn(schema, options = {}) {
142 |   const aliases = [
143 |     ...(options.aliases || []),
144 |     ...(options.metricAliases || []),
145 |     ...(options.dimensionAliases || []),
146 |   ].map(normalizeName);
147 | 
148 |   if (aliases.length) {
149 |     const exact = schema.columns.find((column) =>
150 |       aliases.some((alias) => column.normalizedName === alias)
151 |     );
152 | 
153 |     if (exact) return exact;
154 | 
155 |     const partial = schema.columns.find((column) =>
156 |       aliases.some((alias) => column.normalizedName.includes(alias) || alias.includes(column.normalizedName))
157 |     );
158 | 
159 |     if (partial) return partial;
160 |   }
161 | 
162 |   if (options.role) {
163 |     return schema.columns.find((column) => column.role === options.role) || null;
164 |   }
165 | 
166 |   return null;
167 | }
168 | 
```

### FILE: apps/backend/src/services/predictive-analytics.js

Source path: `apps/backend/src/services/predictive-analytics.js`

```js
  1 | import { toNumber } from '@insightflow/shared-analytics';
  2 | 
  3 | export class PredictiveAnalytics {
  4 |   constructor() {
  5 |     this.models = new Map();
  6 |   }
  7 | 
  8 |   linearRegression(x, y) {
  9 |     const n = Math.min(x.length, y.length);
 10 |     if (n < 2) return null;
 11 | 
 12 |     let sumX = 0, sumY = 0, sumXY = 0, sumX2 = 0;
 13 |     for (let i = 0; i < n; i++) {
 14 |       sumX += x[i];
 15 |       sumY += y[i];
 16 |       sumXY += x[i] * y[i];
 17 |       sumX2 += x[i] * x[i];
 18 |     }
 19 | 
 20 |     const slope = (n * sumXY - sumX * sumY) / (n * sumX2 - sumX * sumX);
 21 |     const intercept = (sumY - slope * sumX) / n;
 22 | 
 23 |     return { slope, intercept, r2: this.calculateR2(x, y, slope, intercept) };
 24 |   }
 25 | 
 26 |   calculateR2(x, y, slope, intercept) {
 27 |     const n = Math.min(x.length, y.length);
 28 |     if (n < 2) return 0;
 29 | 
 30 |     let yMean = y.reduce((a, b) => a + b, 0) / n;
 31 |     let ssRes = 0, ssTot = 0;
 32 | 
 33 |     for (let i = 0; i < n; i++) {
 34 |       const yPred = slope * x[i] + intercept;
 35 |       ssRes += Math.pow(y[i] - yPred, 2);
 36 |       ssTot += Math.pow(y[i] - yMean, 2);
 37 |     }
 38 | 
 39 |     return ssTot === 0 ? 0 : 1 - (ssRes / ssTot);
 40 |   }
 41 | 
 42 |   async forecastTimeseries(rows, column, periods = 3) {
 43 |     const values = rows.map(r => toNumber(r[column])).filter(v => v !== null);
 44 |     if (values.length < 3) return { error: 'Insufficient data' };
 45 | 
 46 |     const x = values.map((_, i) => i);
 47 |     const regression = this.linearRegression(x, values);
 48 | 
 49 |     if (!regression) return { error: 'Unable to fit model' };
 50 | 
 51 |     const forecasts = [];
 52 |     for (let i = 1; i <= periods; i++) {
 53 |       const predicted = regression.slope * (values.length + i - 1) + regression.intercept;
 54 |       forecasts.push({
 55 |         period: i,
 56 |         predicted: Math.max(0, predicted),
 57 |         confidence: this.calculateConfidenceInterval(regression.r2, predicted)
 58 |       });
 59 |     }
 60 | 
 61 |     return {
 62 |       column,
 63 |       model: 'linear',
 64 |       r2: regression.r2,
 65 |       slope: regression.slope,
 66 |       intercept: regression.intercept,
 67 |       forecasts,
 68 |       trend: regression.slope > 0 ? 'increasing' : 'decreasing',
 69 |       growthRate: ((regression.slope / (values[values.length - 1] || 1)) * 100).toFixed(2)
 70 |     };
 71 |   }
 72 | 
 73 |   calculateConfidenceInterval(r2, predicted) {
 74 |     const confidence = Math.max(0, Math.min(100, r2 * 100));
 75 |     const margin = (predicted * (1 - r2)) / 2;
 76 |     return {
 77 |       lower: Math.max(0, predicted - margin),
 78 |       upper: predicted + margin,
 79 |       confidence: confidence.toFixed(1) + '%'
 80 |     };
 81 |   }
 82 | 
 83 |   calculateMovingAverage(values, window = 3) {
 84 |     if (values.length < window) return values;
 85 |     
 86 |     const result = [];
 87 |     for (let i = 0; i < values.length; i++) {
 88 |       const start = Math.max(0, i - window + 1);
 89 |       const slice = values.slice(start, i + 1);
 90 |       const avg = slice.reduce((a, b) => a + b, 0) / slice.length;
 91 |       result.push(avg);
 92 |     }
 93 |     return result;
 94 |   }
 95 | 
 96 |   async detectTrends(rows, column) {
 97 |     const values = rows.map(r => toNumber(r[column])?.valueOf()).filter(v => v !== null);
 98 |     if (values.length < 3) return { error: 'Insufficient data' };
 99 | 
100 |     const max = Math.max(...values);
101 |     const min = Math.min(...values);
102 |     const range = max - min;
103 |     const first = values[0];
104 |     const last = values[values.length - 1];
105 |     const change = ((last - first) / (first || 1)) * 100;
106 | 
107 |     const recentValues = values.slice(-5);
108 |     const ma3 = this.calculateMovingAverage(recentValues, 3);
109 |     const maOverall = this.calculateMovingAverage(values, Math.min(5, Math.floor(values.length / 2)));
110 | 
111 |     return {
112 |       column,
113 |       summary: {
114 |         total: values.length,
115 |         min,
116 |         max,
117 |         range,
118 |         first,
119 |         last,
120 |         changePercent: change.toFixed(2)
121 |       },
122 |       trend: {
123 |         direction: change > 5 ? 'rising' : change < -5 ? 'falling' : 'stable',
124 |         strength: Math.abs(change) > 20 ? 'strong' : 'moderate',
125 |         changePercent: change.toFixed(2)
126 |       },
127 |       movingAverages: {
128 |         recent: ma3[ma3.length - 1]?.toFixed(2),
129 |         overall: maOverall[maOverall.length - 1]?.toFixed(2)
130 |       }
131 |     };
132 |   }
133 | 
134 |   async calculateGrowthRate(rows, column) {
135 |     const values = rows.map(r => toNumber(r[column])?.valueOf()).filter(v => v !== null);
136 |     if (values.length < 2) return { error: 'Insufficient data' };
137 | 
138 |     const first = values[0];
139 |     const last = values[values.length - 1];
140 |     const cagr = (Math.pow(last / first, 1 / (values.length - 1)) - 1) * 100;
141 | 
142 |     const periods = [];
143 |     for (let i = 1; i < values.length; i++) {
144 |       const growth = ((values[i] - values[i - 1]) / (values[i - 1] || 1)) * 100;
145 |       periods.push({ period: i, growth: growth.toFixed(2) });
146 |     }
147 | 
148 |     return {
149 |       column,
150 |       cagr: cagr.toFixed(2),
151 |       firstValue: first,
152 |       lastValue: last,
153 |       periods
154 |     };
155 |   }
156 | 
157 |   async predictNextValue(rows, column) {
158 |     const values = rows.map(r => toNumber(r[column])?.valueOf()).filter(v => v !== null);
159 |     if (values.length < 3) return { error: 'Insufficient data for prediction' };
160 | 
161 |     const x = values.map((_, i) => i);
162 |     const regression = this.linearRegression(x, values);
163 | 
164 |     if (!regression) return { error: 'Prediction failed' };
165 | 
166 |     const predicted = regression.slope * values.length + regression.intercept;
167 |     const confidence = regression.r2 > 0.7 ? 'high' : regression.r2 > 0.4 ? 'medium' : 'low';
168 | 
169 |     return {
170 |       column,
171 |       currentValue: last,
172 |       predicted: Math.max(0, predicted),
173 |       confidence,
174 |       r2: regression.r2,
175 |       model: 'linear_regression'
176 |     };
177 |   }
178 | 
179 |   async analyzeMultipleMetrics(rows, metricColumns) {
180 |     const results = {};
181 |     
182 |     for (const col of metricColumns.slice(0, 5)) {
183 |       const forecast = await this.forecastTimeseries(rows, col, 2);
184 |       const trend = await this.detectTrends(rows, col);
185 |       const growth = await this.calculateGrowthRate(rows, col);
186 |       
187 |       results[col] = { forecast, trend, growth };
188 |     }
189 | 
190 |     return results;
191 |   }
192 | }
193 | 
194 | export const predictiveAnalytics = new PredictiveAnalytics();
```

### FILE: apps/backend/src/services/qr-upload-service.js

Source path: `apps/backend/src/services/qr-upload-service.js`

```js
  1 | /**
  2 |  * QR Code Generation and File Upload Service
  3 |  * Allows users to scan QR code and upload files from mobile
  4 |  */
  5 | 
  6 | import { randomUUID } from "node:crypto";
  7 | 
  8 | const activeUploads = new Map();
  9 | 
 10 | export function generateUploadQRSession() {
 11 |   const sessionId = randomUUID().substring(0, 8).toUpperCase();
 12 |   const uploadToken = randomUUID();
 13 |   
 14 |   const session = {
 15 |     sessionId,
 16 |     uploadToken,
 17 |     createdAt: new Date().toISOString(),
 18 |     expiresAt: new Date(Date.now() + 15 * 60 * 1000).toISOString(),
 19 |     status: "waiting",
 20 |     fileInfo: null,
 21 |   };
 22 | 
 23 |   activeUploads.set(sessionId, session);
 24 | 
 25 |   setTimeout(() => {
 26 |     if (activeUploads.has(sessionId)) {
 27 |       activeUploads.delete(sessionId);
 28 |     }
 29 |   }, 15 * 60 * 1000);
 30 | 
 31 |   return {
 32 |     sessionId,
 33 |     uploadToken,
 34 |     expiresAt: session.expiresAt,
 35 |     uploadUrl: `/api/qr-upload/${sessionId}/${uploadToken}`,
 36 |   };
 37 | }
 38 | 
 39 | export function getUploadSessionStatus(sessionId, uploadToken) {
 40 |   const session = activeUploads.get(sessionId);
 41 | 
 42 |   if (!session) {
 43 |     return { error: "Session not found or expired", status: "expired" };
 44 |   }
 45 | 
 46 |   if (session.uploadToken !== uploadToken) {
 47 |     return { error: "Invalid upload token", status: "unauthorized" };
 48 |   }
 49 | 
 50 |   return {
 51 |     sessionId,
 52 |     status: session.status,
 53 |     fileInfo: session.fileInfo,
 54 |     createdAt: session.createdAt,
 55 |     expiresAt: session.expiresAt,
 56 |   };
 57 | }
 58 | 
 59 | export function handleQRFileUpload(sessionId, uploadToken, fileData, fileName) {
 60 |   const session = activeUploads.get(sessionId);
 61 | 
 62 |   if (!session) {
 63 |     throw new Error("Session not found or expired");
 64 |   }
 65 | 
 66 |   if (session.uploadToken !== uploadToken) {
 67 |     throw new Error("Invalid upload token");
 68 |   }
 69 | 
 70 |   if (new Date() > new Date(session.expiresAt)) {
 71 |     activeUploads.delete(sessionId);
 72 |     throw new Error("Session expired");
 73 |   }
 74 | 
 75 |   session.status = "completed";
 76 |   session.fileInfo = {
 77 |     name: fileName,
 78 |     size: fileData.length,
 79 |     uploadedAt: new Date().toISOString(),
 80 |   };
 81 | 
 82 |   return {
 83 |     success: true,
 84 |     sessionId,
 85 |     fileName,
 86 |     uploadedAt: new Date().toISOString(),
 87 |   };
 88 | }
 89 | 
 90 | export function cleanupExpiredSessions() {
 91 |   const now = new Date();
 92 |   let cleaned = 0;
 93 | 
 94 |   for (const [sessionId, session] of activeUploads.entries()) {
 95 |     if (new Date(session.expiresAt) < now) {
 96 |       activeUploads.delete(sessionId);
 97 |       cleaned++;
 98 |     }
 99 |   }
100 | 
101 |   return cleaned;
102 | }
103 | 
104 | export function getActiveSessionsCount() {
105 |   return activeUploads.size;
106 | }
```

### FILE: apps/backend/src/services/qr-upload/qr-file-parser.js

Source path: `apps/backend/src/services/qr-upload/qr-file-parser.js`

```js
  1 | import Papa from "papaparse";
  2 | import * as XLSX from "xlsx";
  3 | 
  4 | function safeNumber(value) {
  5 |   if (typeof value === "number" && Number.isFinite(value)) return value;
  6 | 
  7 |   const cleaned = String(value ?? "")
  8 |     .replace(/[,$₹%\s]/g, "")
  9 |     .trim();
 10 | 
 11 |   const parsed = Number(cleaned);
 12 |   return Number.isFinite(parsed) ? parsed : null;
 13 | }
 14 | 
 15 | function inferType(values = []) {
 16 |   const sample = values
 17 |     .filter((value) => value !== null && value !== undefined && value !== "")
 18 |     .slice(0, 50);
 19 | 
 20 |   if (!sample.length) return "string";
 21 | 
 22 |   const numeric = sample.filter((value) => safeNumber(value) !== null).length;
 23 |   const date = sample.filter((value) =>
 24 |     Number.isFinite(Date.parse(String(value)))
 25 |   ).length;
 26 | 
 27 |   if (numeric / sample.length >= 0.8) return "number";
 28 |   if (date / sample.length >= 0.8) return "date";
 29 | 
 30 |   return "string";
 31 | }
 32 | 
 33 | export function inferColumns(rows = []) {
 34 |   const names = [...new Set(rows.flatMap((row) => Object.keys(row || {})))];
 35 | 
 36 |   return names.map((name) => ({
 37 |     name,
 38 |     type: inferType(rows.map((row) => row[name])),
 39 |     sample: rows
 40 |       .map((row) => row[name])
 41 |       .filter((value) => value !== null && value !== undefined && value !== "")
 42 |       .slice(0, 5),
 43 |   }));
 44 | }
 45 | 
 46 | function parseCsv(buffer) {
 47 |   const result = Papa.parse(buffer.toString("utf8"), {
 48 |     header: true,
 49 |     skipEmptyLines: true,
 50 |     dynamicTyping: true,
 51 |   });
 52 | 
 53 |   if (result.errors?.length) {
 54 |     throw new Error(`CSV parse error: ${result.errors[0].message}`);
 55 |   }
 56 | 
 57 |   return result.data.filter((row) =>
 58 |     Object.values(row || {}).some(
 59 |       (value) => value !== null && value !== undefined && value !== ""
 60 |     )
 61 |   );
 62 | }
 63 | 
 64 | function parseJson(buffer) {
 65 |   const parsed = JSON.parse(buffer.toString("utf8"));
 66 | 
 67 |   if (Array.isArray(parsed)) return parsed;
 68 |   if (Array.isArray(parsed.rows)) return parsed.rows;
 69 |   if (Array.isArray(parsed.data)) return parsed.data;
 70 |   if (Array.isArray(parsed.records)) return parsed.records;
 71 | 
 72 |   throw new Error("JSON must be an array or contain rows/data/records array.");
 73 | }
 74 | 
 75 | function parseXlsx(buffer) {
 76 |   const workbook = XLSX.read(buffer, {
 77 |     type: "buffer",
 78 |     cellDates: true,
 79 |   });
 80 | 
 81 |   const sheetName = workbook.SheetNames[0];
 82 | 
 83 |   if (!sheetName) {
 84 |     throw new Error("XLSX file has no sheets.");
 85 |   }
 86 | 
 87 |   return XLSX.utils.sheet_to_json(workbook.Sheets[sheetName], {
 88 |     defval: "",
 89 |     raw: false,
 90 |   });
 91 | }
 92 | 
 93 | export function parseDatasetFile({ filename, buffer }) {
 94 |   const lower = filename.toLowerCase();
 95 | 
 96 |   let rows;
 97 | 
 98 |   if (lower.endsWith(".csv")) {
 99 |     rows = parseCsv(buffer);
100 |   } else if (lower.endsWith(".json")) {
101 |     rows = parseJson(buffer);
102 |   } else if (lower.endsWith(".xlsx") || lower.endsWith(".xls")) {
103 |     rows = parseXlsx(buffer);
104 |   } else {
105 |     throw new Error(`Unsupported file type: ${filename}`);
106 |   }
107 | 
108 |   return {
109 |     name: filename,
110 |     fileName: filename,
111 |     rows,
112 |     columns: inferColumns(rows),
113 |     rowCount: rows.length,
114 |     columnCount: rows[0] ? Object.keys(rows[0]).length : 0,
115 |   };
116 | }
117 | 
118 | export function mergeParsedDatasets(parsedFiles = []) {
119 |   const rows = parsedFiles.flatMap((file) =>
120 |     file.rows.map((row, index) => ({
121 |       ...row,
122 |       __sourceFile: file.fileName,
123 |       __sourceRow: index + 1,
124 |     }))
125 |   );
126 | 
127 |   const name =
128 |     parsedFiles.length === 1
129 |       ? parsedFiles[0].fileName
130 |       : `combined_${parsedFiles
131 |           .map((file) => file.fileName.replace(/\.[^.]+$/, ""))
132 |           .join("_")}`;
133 | 
134 |   return {
135 |     name,
136 |     fileName: parsedFiles.map((file) => file.fileName).join(", "),
137 |     sourceType:
138 |       parsedFiles.length === 1 ? "qr-mobile-upload" : "qr-mobile-merged",
139 |     rows,
140 |     columns: inferColumns(rows),
141 |     rowCount: rows.length,
142 |     columnCount: rows[0] ? Object.keys(rows[0]).length : 0,
143 |   };
144 | }
145 | 
```

### FILE: apps/backend/src/services/qr-upload/qr-upload-store.js

```js
 1 | import { randomUUID } from "node:crypto";
 2 | 
 3 | const sessions = new Map();
 4 | const DEFAULT_TTL_MS = 1000 * 60 * 30;
 5 | 
 6 | export function createQrUploadSession({
 7 |   portalBaseUrl,
 8 |   workspaceName = "InsightFlow Workspace",
 9 | }) {
10 |   const sessionId = randomUUID();
11 |   const uploadToken = randomUUID();
12 |   const expiresAt = new Date(Date.now() + DEFAULT_TTL_MS).toISOString();
13 | 
14 |   const uploadUrl = `${portalBaseUrl.replace(/\/$/, "")}/mobile-upload/${sessionId}?token=${uploadToken}`;
15 | 
16 |   const session = {
17 |     sessionId,
18 |     uploadToken,
19 |     workspaceName,
20 |     uploadUrl,
21 |     status: "waiting",
22 |     files: [],
23 |     dataset: null,
24 |     analysis: null,
25 |     error: null,
26 |     createdAt: new Date().toISOString(),
27 |     expiresAt,
28 |   };
29 | 
30 |   sessions.set(sessionId, session);
31 |   return session;
32 | }
33 | 
34 | export function getQrUploadSession(sessionId) {
35 |   const session = sessions.get(sessionId);
36 | 
37 |   if (!session) return null;
38 | 
39 |   if (
40 |     Date.now() > new Date(session.expiresAt).getTime() &&
41 |     session.status !== "completed"
42 |   ) {
43 |     session.status = "expired";
44 |   }
45 | 
46 |   return session;
47 | }
48 | 
49 | export function verifyQrUploadSession(sessionId, token) {
50 |   const session = getQrUploadSession(sessionId);
51 | 
52 |   if (!session) {
53 |     throw new Error("Upload session not found.");
54 |   }
55 | 
56 |   if (session.status === "expired") {
57 |     throw new Error("Upload session expired. Generate a new QR code.");
58 |   }
59 | 
60 |   if (session.uploadToken !== token) {
61 |     throw new Error("Invalid upload token.");
62 |   }
63 | 
64 |   return session;
65 | }
66 | 
67 | export function updateQrUploadSession(sessionId, patch) {
68 |   const session = getQrUploadSession(sessionId);
69 | 
70 |   if (!session) return null;
71 | 
72 |   const updated = {
73 |     ...session,
74 |     ...patch,
75 |     updatedAt: new Date().toISOString(),
76 |   };
77 | 
78 |   sessions.set(sessionId, updated);
79 |   return updated;
80 | }
81 | 
```

### FILE: apps/backend/src/services/query-cache.js

Source path: `apps/backend/src/services/query-cache.js`

```js
  1 | /**
  2 |  * Query Cache Service
  3 |  * Reduces repeated AI calls with a local SQLite cache.
  4 |  */
  5 | 
  6 | import { DatabaseSync } from "node:sqlite";
  7 | import path from "node:path";
  8 | import { fileURLToPath } from "node:url";
  9 | 
 10 | const __filename = fileURLToPath(import.meta.url);
 11 | const __dirname = path.dirname(__filename);
 12 | const CACHE_VERSION = "v2";
 13 | 
 14 | function getDatabase() {
 15 |   const dbPath = path.join(__dirname, "..", "..", "data", "insightflow.sqlite");
 16 |   return new DatabaseSync(dbPath);
 17 | }
 18 | 
 19 | export function initializeCache() {
 20 |   const db = getDatabase();
 21 | 
 22 |   try {
 23 |     db.exec(`
 24 |       CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS query_cache (
 25 |         id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT,
 26 |         dataset_id TEXT NOT NULL,
 27 |         query TEXT NOT NULL,
 28 |         response_json TEXT NOT NULL,
 29 |         created_at TEXT NOT NULL,
 30 |         hit_count INTEGER DEFAULT 0,
 31 |         UNIQUE(dataset_id, query)
 32 |       );
 33 | 
 34 |       CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_cache_dataset ON query_cache(dataset_id);
 35 |       CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_cache_query ON query_cache(query);
 36 |     `);
 37 | 
 38 |     console.log("[cache] Query cache table initialized OK");
 39 |   } catch (error) {
 40 |     console.error("[cache] Failed to initialize cache:", error.message);
 41 |   }
 42 | }
 43 | 
 44 | export function getCachedQuery(datasetId, query) {
 45 |   try {
 46 |     const db = getDatabase();
 47 |     const normalized = normalizeQuery(query);
 48 | 
 49 |     const stmt = db.prepare(`
 50 |       SELECT response_json, hit_count
 51 |       FROM query_cache
 52 |       WHERE dataset_id = ? AND query = ?
 53 |     `);
 54 | 
 55 |     const result = stmt.get(datasetId, normalized);
 56 | 
 57 |     if (result) {
 58 |       db.prepare(`
 59 |         UPDATE query_cache
 60 |         SET hit_count = hit_count + 1
 61 |         WHERE dataset_id = ? AND query = ?
 62 |       `).run(datasetId, normalized);
 63 | 
 64 |       console.log(`[cache] HIT - Cached result found (hits: ${result.hit_count + 1})`);
 65 |       return JSON.parse(result.response_json);
 66 |     }
 67 | 
 68 |     console.log("[cache] MISS - No cached result");
 69 |     return null;
 70 |   } catch (error) {
 71 |     console.error("[cache] Error retrieving cached query:", error.message);
 72 |     return null;
 73 |   }
 74 | }
 75 | 
 76 | export function cacheQuery(datasetId, query, response) {
 77 |   try {
 78 |     const db = getDatabase();
 79 |     const normalized = normalizeQuery(query);
 80 |     const now = new Date().toISOString();
 81 | 
 82 |     db.prepare(`
 83 |       INSERT INTO query_cache (dataset_id, query, response_json, created_at)
 84 |       VALUES (?, ?, ?, ?)
 85 |       ON CONFLICT(dataset_id, query) DO UPDATE SET
 86 |         response_json = excluded.response_json,
 87 |         created_at = excluded.created_at,
 88 |         hit_count = 0
 89 |     `).run(datasetId, normalized, JSON.stringify(response), now);
 90 | 
 91 |     console.log("[cache] CACHED - Query response stored");
 92 |   } catch (error) {
 93 |     console.error("[cache] Error caching query:", error.message);
 94 |   }
 95 | }
 96 | 
 97 | function normalizeQuery(query) {
 98 |   const normalizedQuery = query
 99 |     .toLowerCase()
100 |     .trim()
101 |     .replace(/\s+/g, " ");
102 | 
103 |   return `${CACHE_VERSION}:${normalizedQuery}`;
104 | }
105 | 
106 | export function getCacheStats(datasetId = null) {
107 |   try {
108 |     const db = getDatabase();
109 | 
110 |     let query = "SELECT COUNT(*) as total, SUM(hit_count) as total_hits FROM query_cache";
111 |     const params = [];
112 | 
113 |     if (datasetId) {
114 |       query += " WHERE dataset_id = ?";
115 |       params.push(datasetId);
116 |     }
117 | 
118 |     const result = db.prepare(query).get(...params);
119 |     const totalHits = result.total_hits || 0;
120 |     const totalQueries = result.total || 0;
121 |     const hitRate = totalQueries > 0 ? ((totalHits / totalQueries) * 100).toFixed(2) : 0;
122 | 
123 |     return {
124 |       totalCached: totalQueries,
125 |       totalHits,
126 |       hitRate: `${hitRate}%`,
127 |       savedAPICalls: totalHits,
128 |       estimatedCostSaved: `$${(totalHits * 0.000075).toFixed(2)}`,
129 |     };
130 |   } catch (error) {
131 |     console.error("[cache] Error getting cache stats:", error.message);
132 |     return null;
133 |   }
134 | }
135 | 
136 | export function clearDatasetCache(datasetId) {
137 |   try {
138 |     const db = getDatabase();
139 |     db.prepare("DELETE FROM query_cache WHERE dataset_id = ?").run(datasetId);
140 |     console.log(`[cache] Cache cleared for dataset ${datasetId}`);
141 |   } catch (error) {
142 |     console.error("[cache] Error clearing cache:", error.message);
143 |   }
144 | }
145 | 
146 | export function getTopCachedQueries(limit = 10) {
147 |   try {
148 |     const db = getDatabase();
149 | 
150 |     return db.prepare(`
151 |       SELECT query, hit_count, created_at
152 |       FROM query_cache
153 |       ORDER BY hit_count DESC
154 |       LIMIT ?
155 |     `).all(limit);
156 |   } catch (error) {
157 |     console.error("[cache] Error getting top queries:", error.message);
158 |     return [];
159 |   }
160 | }
161 | 
162 | console.log("[cache] Module loaded successfully");
163 | 
```

### FILE: apps/backend/src/services/recommendation-engine.js

Source path: `apps/backend/src/services/recommendation-engine.js`

```js
  1 | import { toNumber, buildDatasetSchema } from '@insightflow/shared-analytics';
  2 | import { classifyColumns } from './schema-detector.js';
  3 | 
  4 | function getCleanColumnClassification(rows) {
  5 |   const classification = classifyColumns(rows);
  6 |   return {
  7 |     numeric: classification.numeric || [],
  8 |     categorical: classification.categorical || [],
  9 |     date: classification.date || []
 10 |   };
 11 | }
 12 | 
 13 | export class RecommendationEngine {
 14 |   constructor() {
 15 |     this.insights = [];
 16 |     this.recommendations = [];
 17 |   }
 18 | 
 19 |   async generateRecommendations(dataset) {
 20 |     const recommendations = [];
 21 |     const cols = getCleanColumnClassification(dataset.rows || []);
 22 |     const schema = buildDatasetSchema(dataset);
 23 | 
 24 |     if (cols.numeric.length > 0) {
 25 |       recommendations.push(...this.analyzeNumericColumns(dataset, cols.numeric));
 26 |     }
 27 |     
 28 |     if (cols.categorical.length > 0) {
 29 |       recommendations.push(...this.analyzeCategoricalColumns(dataset, cols.categorical));
 30 |     }
 31 | 
 32 |     if (cols.numeric.length >= 2) {
 33 |       recommendations.push(...this.analyzeCorrelations(dataset, cols.numeric));
 34 |     }
 35 | 
 36 |     recommendations.push(...this.analyzeDataQuality(dataset));
 37 | 
 38 |     return recommendations.slice(0, 10);
 39 |   }
 40 | 
 41 |   analyzeNumericColumns(dataset, numericCols) {
 42 |     const recommendations = [];
 43 |     const rows = dataset.rows || [];
 44 |     
 45 |     for (const col of numericCols.slice(0, 5)) {
 46 |       const values = rows.map(r => toNumber(r[col])).filter(v => v !== null);
 47 |       if (values.length === 0) continue;
 48 | 
 49 |       const stats = this.calculateStats(values);
 50 |       const skewness = this.calculateSkewness(values);
 51 |       const hasOutliers = this.detectOutliers(values);
 52 |       const hasZeros = values.filter(v => v === 0).length / values.length > 0.3;
 53 | 
 54 |       if (hasOutliers) {
 55 |         recommendations.push({
 56 |           type: 'outlier',
 57 |           priority: 'medium',
 58 |           title: `Review outliers in ${col}`,
 59 |           message: `${col} contains statistical outliers that may need investigation.`,
 60 |           action: `Investigate ${col} outliers`,
 61 |           metric: col
 62 |         });
 63 |       }
 64 | 
 65 |       if (skewness > 1 || skewness < -1) {
 66 |         recommendations.push({
 67 |           type: 'distribution',
 68 |           priority: hasOutliers ? 'high' : 'low',
 69 |           title: `${col} has skewed distribution`,
 70 |           message: `${col} shows ${skewness > 0 ? 'positive' : 'negative'} skew. Consider transformation.`,
 71 |           action: `Apply log or Box-Cox transformation to ${col}`,
 72 |           metric: col
 73 |         });
 74 |       }
 75 | 
 76 |       if (hasZeros) {
 77 |         recommendations.push({
 78 |           type: 'data_quality',
 79 |           priority: 'medium',
 80 |           title: `High proportion of zeros in ${col}`,
 81 |           message: `${col} has ${Math.round(hasZeros * 100)}% zero values. Verify if expected.`,
 82 |           action: `Verify zero values in ${col}`,
 83 |           metric: col
 84 |         });
 85 |       }
 86 | 
 87 |       const cv = (stats.std / stats.mean) * 100;
 88 |       if (cv > 100) {
 89 |         recommendations.push({
 90 |           type: 'variability',
 91 |           priority: 'low',
 92 |           title: `High variability in ${col}`,
 93 |           message: `${col} has coefficient of variation ${cv.toFixed(0)}%. Data is highly variable.`,
 94 |           action: `Review data collection for ${col}`,
 95 |           metric: col
 96 |         });
 97 |       }
 98 |     }
 99 | 
100 |     return recommendations;
101 |   }
102 | 
103 |   analyzeCategoricalColumns(dataset, categoricalCols) {
104 |     const recommendations = [];
105 |     const rows = dataset.rows || [];
106 | 
107 |     for (const col of categoricalCols.slice(0, 5)) {
108 |       const valueCounts = {};
109 |       rows.forEach(row => {
110 |         const val = row[col];
111 |         if (val) valueCounts[val] = (valueCounts[val] || 0) + 1;
112 |       });
113 | 
114 |       const uniqueCount = Object.keys(valueCounts).length;
115 |       const totalRows = rows.length;
116 |       const cardinality = uniqueCount / totalRows;
117 | 
118 |       if (cardinality > 0.9 && uniqueCount > 50) {
119 |         recommendations.push({
120 |           type: 'cardinality',
121 |           priority: 'low',
122 |           title: `High cardinality in ${col}`,
123 |           message: `${col} has ${uniqueCount} unique values (${(cardinality * 100).toFixed(0)}% unique).`,
124 |           action: `Consider grouping rare values in ${col}`,
125 |           metric: col
126 |         });
127 |       }
128 | 
129 |       const topValues = Object.entries(valueCounts)
130 |         .sort((a, b) => b[1] - a[1])
131 |         .slice(0, 3);
132 |       
133 |       if (topValues.length > 0 && topValues[0][1] / totalRows > 0.5) {
134 |         recommendations.push({
135 |           type: 'imbalance',
136 |           priority: 'medium',
137 |           title: `Imbalanced categories in ${col}`,
138 |           message: `${topValues[0][0]} dominates with ${((topValues[0][1] / totalRows) * 100).toFixed(0)}% of values.`,
139 |           action: `Review category balance for ${col}`,
140 |           metric: col
141 |         });
142 |       }
143 |     }
144 | 
145 |     return recommendations;
146 |   }
147 | 
148 |   analyzeCorrelations(dataset, numericCols) {
149 |     const recommendations = [];
150 |     const rows = dataset.rows || [];
151 | 
152 |     if (numericCols.length < 2) return recommendations;
153 | 
154 |     const correlations = [];
155 |     for (let i = 0; i < numericCols.length; i++) {
156 |       for (let j = i + 1; j < numericCols.length; j++) {
157 |         const x = rows.map(r => toNumber(r[numericCols[i]])).filter(v => v !== null);
158 |         const y = rows.map(r => toNumber(r[numericCols[j]])).filter(v => v !== null);
159 |         
160 |         if (x.length < 5 || y.length < 5 || x.length !== y.length) continue;
161 | 
162 |         const correlation = this.calculatePearson(x, y);
163 |         if (correlation) {
164 |           correlations.push({
165 |             cols: [numericCols[i], numericCols[j]],
166 |             value: correlation
167 |           });
168 |         }
169 |       }
170 |     }
171 | 
172 |     for (const corr of correlations) {
173 |       const absCorr = Math.abs(corr.value);
174 |       if (absCorr > 0.7) {
175 |         recommendations.push({
176 |           type: 'correlation',
177 |           priority: 'high',
178 |           title: `Strong correlation detected`,
179 |           message: `${corr.cols[0]} and ${corr.cols[1]} have ${corr.value > 0 ? 'positive' : 'negative'} correlation (${corr.value.toFixed(2)}).`,
180 |           action: `Consider removing one of ${corr.cols.join(' and ')}`,
181 |           metric: corr.cols.join('_')
182 |         });
183 |       }
184 |     }
185 | 
186 |     return recommendations;
187 |   }
188 | 
189 |   analyzeDataQuality(dataset) {
190 |     const recommendations = [];
191 |     const rows = dataset.rows || [];
192 |     const columns = dataset.columns || [];
193 | 
194 |     const missingByColumn = {};
195 |     columns.forEach(col => {
196 |       const missing = rows.filter(r => r[col.name] === null || r[col.name] === '').length;
197 |       missingByColumn[col.name] = missing;
198 |     });
199 | 
200 |     for (const [col, missing] of Object.entries(missingByColumn)) {
201 |       const missingRate = missing / rows.length;
202 |       if (missingRate > 0.1 && missingRate <= 0.5) {
203 |         recommendations.push({
204 |           type: 'missing_data',
205 |           priority: 'medium',
206 |           title: `Missing values in ${col}`,
207 |           message: `${col} has ${(missingRate * 100).toFixed(0)}% missing data. Consider imputation.`,
208 |           action: `Apply imputation to ${col}`,
209 |           metric: col
210 |         });
211 |       } else if (missingRate > 0.5) {
212 |         recommendations.push({
213 |           type: 'missing_data',
214 |           priority: 'high',
215 |           title: `High missing rate in ${col}`,
216 |           message: `${col} has ${(missingRate * 100).toFixed(0)}% missing data. Consider dropping column.`,
217 |           action: `Drop ${col} or collect more data`,
218 |           metric: col
219 |         });
220 |       }
221 |     }
222 | 
223 |     return recommendations;
224 |   }
225 | 
226 |   calculateStats(values) {
227 |     const n = values.length;
228 |     const mean = values.reduce((a, b) => a + b, 0) / n;
229 |     const variance = values.reduce((a, b) => a + Math.pow(b - mean, 2), 0) / n;
230 |     return {
231 |       n,
232 |       mean,
233 |       median: values[Math.floor(n / 2)],
234 |       std: Math.sqrt(variance),
235 |       min: values[0],
236 |       max: values[n - 1]
237 |     };
238 |   }
239 | 
240 |   calculateSkewness(values) {
241 |     if (values.length < 3) return 0;
242 |     const n = values.length;
243 |     const mean = values.reduce((a, b) => a + b, 0) / n;
244 |     const std = Math.sqrt(values.reduce((a, b) => a + Math.pow(b - mean, 2), 0) / n);
245 |     
246 |     const skewness = values.reduce((a, b) => a + Math.pow((b - mean) / std, 3), 0) / n;
247 |     return skewness;
248 |   }
249 | 
250 |   detectOutliers(values) {
251 |     if (values.length < 10) return false;
252 |     values.sort((a, b) => a - b);
253 |     const q1 = values[Math.floor(values.length * 0.25)];
254 |     const q3 = values[Math.floor(values.length * 0.75)];
255 |     const iqr = q3 - q1;
256 |     const lower = q1 - 1.5 * iqr;
257 |     const upper = q3 + 1.5 * iqr;
258 |     
259 |     return values.some(v => v < lower || v > upper);
260 |   }
261 | 
262 |   calculatePearson(x, y) {
263 |     const n = Math.min(x.length, y.length);
264 |     if (n < 3) return null;
265 | 
266 |     const sumX = x.slice(0, n).reduce((a, b) => a + b, 0);
267 |     const sumY = y.slice(0, n).reduce((a, b) => a + b, 0);
268 |     const sumXY = x.slice(0, n).reduce((total, xi, i) => total + xi * y[i], 0);
269 |     const sumX2 = x.slice(0, n).reduce((a, b) => a + b * b, 0);
270 |     const sumY2 = y.slice(0, n).reduce((a, b) => a + b * b, 0);
271 | 
272 |     const num = n * sumXY - sumX * sumY;
273 |     const den = Math.sqrt((n * sumX2 - sumX * sumX) * (n * sumY2 - sumY * sumY));
274 |     
275 |     return den === 0 ? null : num / den;
276 |   }
277 | }
278 | 
279 | export const recommendationEngine = new RecommendationEngine();
```

```js
  3 | export class ReportGenerator {
  4 |   constructor() {
  5 |     this.scheduledReports = new Map();
  6 |   }
  7 | 
  8 |   async generateHTMLReport(dataset, options = {}) {
  9 |     const { title = 'Data Analytics Report', includeStats = true, includeInsights = true } = options;
 10 |     
 11 |     const stats = dataset.columnStats || {};
 12 |     const rows = dataset.rows || [];
 13 |     
 14 |     let html = `<!DOCTYPE html>
 15 | <html>
 16 | <head>
 17 |   <meta charset="UTF-8">
 18 |   <title>${title}</title>
 19 |   <style>
 20 |     body { font-family: -apple-system, BlinkMacSystemFont, 'Segoe UI', Roboto, sans-serif; max-width: 1000px; margin: 0 auto; padding: 20px; color: #1a1a2e; }
 21 |     h1 { color: #1a1a2e; border-bottom: 2px solid #4f46e5; padding-bottom: 10px; }
 22 |     h2 { color: #374151; margin-top: 30px; }
 23 |     .stat-grid { display: grid; grid-template-columns: repeat(auto-fit, minmax(180px, 1fr)); gap: 15px; margin: 15px 0; }
 24 |     .stat-card { background: #f9fafb; padding: 15px; border-radius: 8px; }
 25 |     .stat-label { color: #6b7280; font-size: 12px; }
 26 |     .stat-value { color: #1a1a2e; font-size: 24px; font-weight: bold; }
 27 |     table { width: 100%; border-collapse: collapse; margin-top: 10px; }
 28 |     th, td { padding: 10px; text-align: left; border-bottom: 1px solid #e5e7eb; }
 29 |     th { background: #f3f4f6; font-weight: 600; }
 30 |     .insight { padding: 12px; margin: 8px 0; background: #eff6ff; border-left: 3px solid #4f46e5; border-radius: 4px; }
 31 |     .chart-preview { margin: 15px 0; padding: 15px; background: #f9fafb; border-radius: 8px; }
 32 |     .footer { margin-top: 30px; padding-top: 20px; border-top: 1px solid #e5e7eb; color: #6b7280; font-size: 12px; }
 33 |   </style>
 34 | </head>
 35 | <body>
 36 |   <h1>📊 ${title}</h1>
 37 |   <p>Generated: ${new Date().toLocaleString()}</p>
 38 |   
 39 |   <h2>📈 Overview</h2>
 40 |   <div class="stat-grid">
 41 |     <div class="stat-card">
 42 |       <div class="stat-label">Total Rows</div>
 43 |       <div class="stat-value">${rows.length.toLocaleString()}</div>
 44 |     </div>
 45 |     <div class="stat-card">
 46 |       <div class="stat-label">Columns</div>
 47 |       <div class="stat-value">${dataset.columns?.length || 0}</div>
 48 |     </div>
 49 |     <div class="stat-card">
 50 |       <div class="stat-label">Data Type</div>
 51 |       <div class="stat-value">${dataset.schema?.dataTypeLabel || 'Generic'}</div>
 52 |     </div>
 53 |   </div>`;
 54 | 
 55 |     if (includeStats && Object.keys(stats).length > 0) {
 56 |       html += `\n  <h2>📉 Statistics</h2>\n  <table>\n    <tr><th>Column</th><th>Type</th><th>Count</th><th>Min</th><th>Max</th><th>Mean</th></tr>`;
 57 |       
 58 |       Object.entries(stats).forEach(([col, stat]) => {
 59 |         if (stat.type === 'numeric') {
 60 |           html += `\n    <tr><td>${col}</td><td>${stat.type}</td><td>${stat.count}</td><td>${stat.min?.toFixed(2) || '-'}</td><td>${stat.max?.toFixed(2) || '-'}</td><td>${stat.mean?.toFixed(2) || '-'}</td></tr>`;
 61 |         } else {
 62 |           html += `\n    <tr><td>${col}</td><td>${stat.type}</td><td>${stat.count}</td><td colspan="3">${stat.unique} unique values</td></tr>`;
 63 |         }
 64 |       });
 65 |       html += '\n  </table>';
 66 |     }
 67 | 
 68 |     if (includeInsights && dataset.insights?.length > 0) {
 69 |       html += '\n  <h2>💡 Key Insights</h2>';
 70 |       dataset.insights.slice(0, 10).forEach(insight => {
 71 |         const message = insight.message || JSON.stringify(insight);
 72 |         html += `\n  <div class="insight">${message}</div>`;
 73 |       });
 74 |     }
 75 | 
 76 |     if (dataset.charts?.length > 0) {
 77 |       html += '\n  <h2>📊 Visualizations</h2>';
 78 |       dataset.charts.slice(0, 7).forEach(chart => {
 79 |         html += `\n  <div class="chart-preview"><strong>${chart.title}</strong> (${chart.type})</div>`;
 80 |       });
 81 |     }
 82 | 
 83 |     html += `
 84 |   <div class="footer">
 85 |     Generated by InsightFlow Analytics | ${new Date().toISOString()}
 86 |   </div>
 87 | </body>
 88 | </html>`;
 89 | 
 90 |     return Buffer.from(html, 'utf-8');
 91 |   }
 92 | 
 93 |   async generateCSVExport(dataset) {
 94 |     const rows = dataset.rows || [];
 95 |     if (rows.length === 0) return Buffer.from('', 'utf-8');
 96 | 
 97 |     const headers = Object.keys(rows[0]);
 98 |     let csv = headers.join(',') + '\n';
 99 | 
100 |     rows.forEach((row, idx) => {
101 |       const values = headers.map(h => {
102 |         const val = row[h];
103 |         if (val == null) return '';
104 |         const str = String(val);
105 |         return str.includes(',') || str.includes('"') ? `"${str.replace(/"/g, '""')}"` : str;
106 |       });
107 |       csv += values.join(',') + '\n';
108 |     });
109 | 
110 |     return Buffer.from(csv, 'utf-8');
111 |   }
112 | 
113 |   async generateReport(dataset, options = {}) {
114 |     const { format = 'html' } = options;
115 |     
116 |     switch (format) {
117 |       case 'csv':
118 |         return this.generateCSVExport(dataset);
119 |       case 'html':
120 |       default:
121 |         return this.generateHTMLReport(dataset, options);
122 |     }
123 |   }
124 | 
125 |   async scheduleReport(config) {
126 |     const { id, cronExpression, datasetId, format, recipients } = config;
127 |     
128 |     this.scheduledReports.set(id, {
129 |       ...config,
130 |       enabled: true,
131 |       lastRun: null,
132 |       nextRun: this.calculateNextRun(cronExpression)
133 |     });
134 | 
135 |     return { success: true, id, nextRun: this.calculateNextRun(cronExpression) };
136 |   }
137 | 
138 |   calculateNextRun(cronExpression) {
139 |     return new Date(Date.now() + 60000);
140 |   }
141 | 
142 |   async runScheduledReports() {
143 |     const results = [];
144 |     const now = new Date();
145 | 
146 |     for (const [id, report] of this.scheduledReports) {
147 |       if (!report.enabled) continue;
148 |       if (report.nextRun <= now) {
149 |         try {
150 |           const dataset = await pipelineService.getDataset(report.datasetId);
151 |           if (dataset) {
152 |             const buffer = await this.generateReport(dataset, { format: report.format });
153 |             results.push({ id, status: 'generated', size: buffer.length });
154 |           }
155 |         } catch (error) {
156 |           results.push({ id, status: 'error', error: error.message });
157 |         }
158 |         
159 |         report.lastRun = now;
160 |         report.nextRun = this.calculateNextRun(report.cronExpression);
161 |       }
162 |     }
163 | 
164 |     return results;
165 |   }
166 | }
167 | 
168 | export const reportGenerator = new ReportGenerator();
```

### FILE: apps/backend/src/services/schema-ai-service.js

Source path: `apps/backend/src/services/schema-ai-service.js`

```js
  1 | import { GoogleGenerativeAI } from '@google/generative-ai';
  2 | 
  3 | const readGeminiApiKey = () =>
  4 |   process.env.GEMINI_API_KEY?.trim() || process.env.GOOGLE_API_KEY?.trim() || '';
  5 | 
  6 | // Initialize Gemini AI client
  7 | const genAI = new GoogleGenerativeAI(readGeminiApiKey());
  8 | let hasLoggedMissingGeminiConfig = false;
  9 | 
 10 | /**
 11 |  * Schema-first system prompt for InsightFlow AI
 12 |  * Configures Gemini to act as a senior data analyst with structured JSON output
 13 |  */
 14 | const INSIGHTFLOW_SYSTEM_PROMPT = `
 15 | You are InsightFlow AI — a schema-first senior data analyst.
 16 | You do not guess. You do not hallucinate. You reason from schema only.
 17 | 
 18 | ## Your Identity
 19 | You think like a data analyst, not a chatbot.
 20 | You receive a dataset schema packet and a natural language question.
 21 | You return ONLY valid JSON. No markdown. No preamble. No explanation outside JSON.
 22 | 
 23 | ## Output Format (STRICT — never deviate)
 24 | {
 25 |   "intent": "aggregation | filter | comparison | distribution | correlation | count | trend | unclear",
 26 |   "columns_used": ["exact_column_name_from_schema"],
 27 |   "sql": "SELECT ... FROM dataset WHERE ... GROUP BY ... ORDER BY ...",
 28 |   "insight": "1-2 sentence plain English explanation of what the result shows and why it matters.",
 29 |   "chart_type": "bar | line | pie | histogram | scatter | table",
 30 |   "confidence": 0.0
 31 | }
 32 | 
 33 | ## Column Rules
 34 | - ONLY use column names that appear in the schema JSON
 35 | - If a user says 'salary', map to the exact column name (e.g. salary_usd)
 36 | - Never invent column aliases not in schema
 37 | 
 38 | ## SQL Rules
 39 | - Table name is always: dataset
 40 | - Use standard SQL (SQLite-compatible)
 41 | - Aggregation questions → GROUP BY with AVG/COUNT/SUM
 42 | - Comparison questions → GROUP BY with ORDER BY
 43 | - Distribution questions → COUNT(*) GROUP BY with ORDER BY count DESC
 44 | - Correlation hints → use both numeric columns, compute AVG per category
 45 | 
 46 | ## Chart Selection Rules
 47 | - Categorical comparison → bar
 48 | - Trend over time → line
 49 | - Part of whole → pie (only for <8 categories)
 50 | - Single numeric distribution → histogram
 51 | - Two numeric relationship → scatter
 52 | - Many columns or raw data → table
 53 | 
 54 | ## Unanswerable Queries
 55 | If the question cannot be answered from the schema, return:
 56 | {
 57 |   "intent": "unclear",
 58 |   "columns_used": [],
 59 |   "sql": "",
 60 |   "insight": "Explain exactly why this cannot be answered from the available schema.",
 61 |   "chart_type": "table",
 62 |   "confidence": 0.0
 63 | }
 64 | 
 65 | ## Confidence Score
 66 | - 0.9-1.0: Direct mapping, clear intent, all columns found
 67 | - 0.7-0.89: Minor ambiguity resolved with reasonable assumption
 68 | - 0.5-0.69: Multiple interpretations possible, chose most likely
 69 | - Below 0.5: Significant uncertainty, explain in insight
 70 | `;
 71 | 
 72 | /**
 73 |  * Validates that the payload contains only schema, no data rows
 74 |  * @param {Object} payload - The payload to validate
 75 |  * @throws {Error} If payload contains data rows
 76 |  */
 77 | const validateSchemaOnlyPayload = (payload) => {
 78 |   if (!payload || typeof payload !== 'object') {
 79 |     throw new Error('Invalid payload: must be an object');
 80 |   }
 81 | 
 82 |   // Check for common data row keys that should not be present
 83 |   const forbiddenKeys = ['rows', 'data', 'records', 'items', 'values'];
 84 |   for (const key of forbiddenKeys) {
 85 |     if (key in payload) {
 86 |       throw new Error(`Security violation: payload contains "${key}" - data rows are not allowed`);
 87 |     }
 88 |   }
 89 | 
 90 |   // Validate schema structure
 91 |   if (!payload.schema || typeof payload.schema !== 'object') {
 92 |     throw new Error('Invalid payload: schema is required and must be an object');
 93 |   }
 94 | 
 95 |   if (!Array.isArray(payload.schema.columns)) {
 96 |     throw new Error('Invalid schema: columns must be an array');
 97 |   }
 98 | 
 99 |   // Ensure columns don't contain actual data
100 |   for (const column of payload.schema.columns) {
101 |     if (column.data || column.values || column.rows) {
102 |       throw new Error(`Security violation: column "${column.name}" contains data`);
103 |     }
104 |   }
105 | };
106 | 
107 | /**
108 |  * Formats schema for AI prompt
109 |  * @param {Object} schema - Dataset schema
110 |  * @returns {string} Formatted schema description
111 |  */
112 | const formatSchemaForAI = (schema) => {
113 |   const columns = schema.columns || [];
114 |   const columnDescriptions = columns.map(col => {
115 |     const samples = col.sample ? ` (examples: ${col.sample.slice(0, 3).join(', ')})` : '';
116 |     return `- ${col.name} (${col.type}${samples})`;
117 |   }).join('\n');
118 | 
119 |   return `Dataset: ${schema.datasetName || 'Unknown'}
120 | Columns:
121 | ${columnDescriptions}
122 | Total columns: ${columns.length}`;
123 | };
124 | 
125 | const buildFallbackSchemaResponse = (schemaPacket, query, reason) => {
126 |   const columns = Array.isArray(schemaPacket?.columns) ? schemaPacket.columns : [];
127 |   const loweredQuery = String(query || "").toLowerCase();
128 |   const numericCols = columns.filter(col => col.type === "number");
129 |   const categoricalCols = columns.filter(col => col.type === "string");
130 |   const metricCol = numericCols[0];
131 |   const dimensionCol = categoricalCols[0];
132 | 
133 |   let intent = "unclear";
134 |   let sql = "";
135 |   let insight = reason || "AI SQL generation is unavailable for this request.";
136 |   let chart_type = "table";
137 |   let confidence = 0.35;
138 |   let columns_used = [];
139 | 
140 |   if (metricCol && /\b(avg|average|mean)\b/.test(loweredQuery)) {
141 |     intent = dimensionCol ? "comparison" : "aggregation";
142 |     sql = dimensionCol
143 |       ? `SELECT "${dimensionCol.name}", AVG("${metricCol.name}") AS average_${metricCol.name} FROM dataset GROUP BY "${dimensionCol.name}" ORDER BY average_${metricCol.name} DESC`
144 |       : `SELECT AVG("${metricCol.name}") AS average_${metricCol.name} FROM dataset`;
145 |     insight = reason || `Returning a schema-based fallback query for average ${metricCol.name}.`;
146 |     chart_type = dimensionCol ? "bar" : "table";
147 |     confidence = 0.6;
148 |     columns_used = dimensionCol ? [dimensionCol.name, metricCol.name] : [metricCol.name];
149 |   } else if (dimensionCol && /\b(count|how many|number of)\b/.test(loweredQuery)) {
150 |     intent = "count";
151 |     sql = `SELECT "${dimensionCol.name}", COUNT(*) AS count FROM dataset GROUP BY "${dimensionCol.name}" ORDER BY count DESC`;
152 |     insight = reason || `Returning a schema-based fallback count grouped by ${dimensionCol.name}.`;
153 |     chart_type = "bar";
154 |     confidence = 0.6;
155 |     columns_used = [dimensionCol.name];
156 |   }
157 | 
158 |   return {
159 |     intent,
160 |     sql,
161 |     insight,
162 |     chart_type,
163 |     confidence,
164 |     columns_used,
165 |     fallback: true,
166 |   };
167 | };
168 | 
169 | /**
170 |  * Generates structured SQL response from natural language using schema-first Gemini AI
171 |  * @param {Object} schemaPacket - Dataset schema packet from buildSchemaPacket()
172 |  * @param {string} query - Natural language query
173 |  * @param {Object} options
174 |  * @param {boolean} options.allowFallback - When false, propagate Gemini failures instead of returning a schema fallback
175 |  * @returns {Promise<Object>} Structured response with SQL, insight, chart_type, confidence
176 |  */
177 | export const generateSQLFromSchema = async (schemaPacket, query, options = {}) => {
178 |   const { allowFallback = true } = options;
179 | 
180 |   if (!readGeminiApiKey()) {
181 |     if (!allowFallback) {
182 |       throw new Error('Gemini API key not configured');
183 |     }
184 | 
185 |     if (!hasLoggedMissingGeminiConfig) {
186 |       console.warn('[schema-ai] Gemini API key not configured, returning fallback response');
187 |       hasLoggedMissingGeminiConfig = true;
188 |     }
189 |     return buildFallbackSchemaResponse(
190 |       schemaPacket,
191 |       query,
192 |       'Gemini API key not configured. Returning a schema-based fallback response.'
193 |     );
194 |   }
195 | 
196 |   if (!query || typeof query !== 'string') {
197 |     throw new Error('Query is required and must be a string');
198 |   }
199 | 
200 |   if (!schemaPacket || !schemaPacket.columns) {
201 |     throw new Error('Valid schema packet is required');
202 |   }
203 | 
204 |   try {
205 |     const model = genAI.getGenerativeModel({
206 |       model: 'gemini-1.5-flash',
207 |       generationConfig: {
208 |         temperature: 0.2,
209 |         responseMimeType: 'application/json'
210 |       },
211 |       systemInstruction: INSIGHTFLOW_SYSTEM_PROMPT
212 |     });
213 | 
214 |     const result = await model.generateContent(
215 |       `Schema: ${JSON.stringify(schemaPacket)}\nQuestion: ${query}`
216 |     );
217 |     
218 |     const response = await result.response;
219 |     const responseText = response.text();
220 |     
221 |     // Parse JSON response safely
222 |     let parsedResponse;
223 |     try {
224 |       parsedResponse = JSON.parse(responseText);
225 |     } catch (parseError) {
226 |       throw new Error(`Invalid JSON response from Gemini: ${responseText}`);
227 |     }
228 | 
229 |     // Validate required fields
230 |     const requiredFields = ['intent', 'columns_used', 'sql', 'insight', 'chart_type', 'confidence'];
231 |     for (const field of requiredFields) {
232 |       if (!(field in parsedResponse)) {
233 |         throw new Error(`Missing required field in Gemini response: ${field}`);
234 |       }
235 |     }
236 | 
237 |     // Validate SQL contains only schema columns
238 |     const schemaColumns = schemaPacket.columns.map(col => col.name);
239 |     const usedColumns = parsedResponse.columns_used || [];
240 |     
241 |     for (const col of usedColumns) {
242 |       if (!schemaColumns.includes(col)) {
243 |         throw new Error(`Gemini used non-existent column: ${col}`);
244 |       }
245 |     }
246 | 
247 |     // Basic SQL validation
248 |     if (parsedResponse.sql && !parsedResponse.sql.toLowerCase().startsWith('select')) {
249 |       throw new Error('Generated SQL must start with SELECT');
250 |     }
251 | 
252 |     return {
253 |       intent: parsedResponse.intent,
254 |       sql: parsedResponse.sql || '',
255 |       insight: parsedResponse.insight || '',
256 |       chart_type: parsedResponse.chart_type || 'table',
257 |       confidence: parsedResponse.confidence || 0.0,
258 |       columns_used: parsedResponse.columns_used || []
259 |     };
260 |   } catch (error) {
261 |     if (!allowFallback) {
262 |       throw error;
263 |     }
264 | 
265 |     console.warn(`[schema-ai] Falling back after AI generation failure: ${error.message}`);
266 |     return buildFallbackSchemaResponse(
267 |       schemaPacket,
268 |       query,
269 |       `AI generation failed. Returning a schema-based fallback response.`
270 |     );
271 |   }
272 | };
273 | 
274 | /**
275 |  * Validates SQL query before execution
276 |  * @param {string} sql - SQL query to validate
277 |  * @returns {Object} Validation result
278 |  */
279 | export const validateSQLQuery = (sql) => {
280 |   const errors = [];
281 |   const warnings = [];
282 | 
283 |   if (!sql || typeof sql !== 'string') {
284 |     errors.push('SQL query must be a string');
285 |     return { valid: false, errors, warnings };
286 |   }
287 | 
288 |   // Check for dangerous operations
289 |   const dangerousKeywords = ['DROP', 'DELETE', 'UPDATE', 'INSERT', 'ALTER', 'CREATE', 'TRUNCATE'];
290 |   const upperSQL = sql.toUpperCase();
291 | 
292 |   for (const keyword of dangerousKeywords) {
293 |     if (upperSQL.includes(keyword)) {
294 |       errors.push(`Dangerous keyword detected: ${keyword}`);
295 |     }
296 |   }
297 | 
298 |   // Check for multiple statements
299 |   if (sql.includes(';') && sql.trim().endsWith(';') === false) {
300 |     warnings.push('Multiple SQL statements detected');
301 |   }
302 | 
303 |   // Check for subqueries that might be complex
304 |   if ((sql.match(/\(/g) || []).length > 4) {
305 |     warnings.push('Complex query with many subqueries');
306 |   }
307 | 
308 |   return {
309 |     valid: errors.length === 0,
310 |     errors,
311 |     warnings,
312 |   };
313 | };
314 | 
```

```js
  8 | const CHART_TYPES = new Set([
  9 |   "bar",
 10 |   "line",
 11 |   "area",
 12 |   "pie",
 13 |   "scatter",
 14 |   "histogram",
 15 | ]);
 16 | 
 17 | const AGGREGATIONS = new Set(["sum", "avg", "count", "min", "max"]);
 18 | 
 19 | function humanize(value) {
 20 |   return String(value || "")
 21 |     .replace(/[_-]+/g, " ")
 22 |     .replace(/\s+/g, " ")
 23 |     .trim()
 24 |     .replace(/\b\w/g, (char) => char.toUpperCase());
 25 | }
 26 | 
 27 | function normalizeKey(value) {
 28 |   return String(value || "")
 29 |     .toLowerCase()
 30 |     .replace(/[^a-z0-9]/g, "");
 31 | }
 32 | 
 33 | function isNumericColumn(column) {
 34 |   return column?.type === "numeric" || column?.type === "number";
 35 | }
 36 | 
 37 | function isDateLikeColumn(column) {
 38 |   return (
 39 |     column?.type === "date" ||
 40 |     /date|time|month|year|day|created|updated/i.test(column?.name || "")
 41 |   );
 42 | }
 43 | 
 44 | function enrichSchemaPacket(schemaPacket) {
 45 |   const columns = (schemaPacket.columns || []).map((column) => {
 46 |     let role = "dimension";
 47 | 
 48 |     if (isNumericColumn(column)) {
 49 |       role = "metric";
 50 |     }
 51 | 
 52 |     if (isDateLikeColumn(column)) {
 53 |       role = "date";
 54 |     }
 55 | 
 56 |     return {
 57 |       ...column,
 58 |       role,
 59 |       chartType: isNumericColumn(column)
 60 |         ? "number"
 61 |         : isDateLikeColumn(column)
 62 |           ? "date"
 63 |           : "string",
 64 |     };
 65 |   });
 66 | 
 67 |   return {
 68 |     ...schemaPacket,
 69 |     columns,
 70 |     metricColumns: columns.filter((column) => column.role === "metric"),
 71 |     dimensionColumns: columns.filter((column) => column.role === "dimension"),
 72 |     dateColumns: columns.filter((column) => column.role === "date"),
 73 |   };
 74 | }
 75 | 
 76 | function findColumn(schema, requestedName, preferredRoles = []) {
 77 |   const columns = schema.columns || [];
 78 |   if (!requestedName) return null;
 79 | 
 80 |   const requested = normalizeKey(requestedName);
 81 | 
 82 |   const roleAllowed = (column) => {
 83 |     if (!preferredRoles.length) return true;
 84 |     return preferredRoles.includes(column.role);
 85 |   };
 86 | 
 87 |   return (
 88 |     columns.find((column) => roleAllowed(column) && normalizeKey(column.name) === requested) ||
 89 |     columns.find((column) => roleAllowed(column) && normalizeKey(column.name).includes(requested)) ||
 90 |     columns.find((column) => roleAllowed(column) && requested.includes(normalizeKey(column.name))) ||
 91 |     null
 92 |   );
 93 | }
 94 | 
 95 | function pickDefaultMetric(schema, query = "") {
 96 |   const lowerQuery = String(query).toLowerCase();
 97 |   const metrics = schema.metricColumns || [];
 98 | 
 99 |   return (
100 |     metrics.find((column) => lowerQuery.includes(column.name.toLowerCase())) ||
101 |     metrics.find((column) => /revenue|sales|salary|amount|profit|price|cost|total|value|score/i.test(column.name)) ||
102 |     metrics[0] ||
103 |     null
104 |   );
105 | }
106 | 
107 | function pickDefaultDimension(schema, query = "") {
108 |   const lowerQuery = String(query).toLowerCase();
109 |   const dimensions = [...(schema.dateColumns || []), ...(schema.dimensionColumns || [])];
110 | 
111 |   const byMatch = lowerQuery.match(/\bby\s+([a-z0-9_ -]+)/i);
112 |   if (byMatch) {
113 |     const matched = findColumn(schema, byMatch[1], ["dimension", "date"]);
114 |     if (matched) return matched;
115 |   }
116 | 
117 |   return (
118 |     dimensions.find((column) => lowerQuery.includes(column.name.toLowerCase())) ||
119 |     dimensions.find((column) => /country|region|city|category|product|department|education|status|type|name|date|month/i.test(column.name)) ||
120 |     dimensions[0] ||
121 |     null
122 |   );
123 | }
124 | 
125 | function detectLocalAction(query) {
126 |   const q = String(query || "").toLowerCase();
127 | 
128 |   if ((q.includes("delete") || q.includes("remove")) && q.includes("chart")) {
129 |     return "DELETE_CHART";
130 |   }
131 | 
132 |   if ((q.includes("clear") || q.includes("reset")) && (q.includes("filter") || q.includes("all"))) {
133 |     return "CLEAR_FILTERS";
134 |   }
135 | 
136 |   if (q.includes("filter") || q.includes("show only") || q.includes("where")) {
137 |     return "FILTER";
138 |   }
139 | 
140 |   if (q.includes("modify") || q.includes("change") || q.includes("switch") || q.includes("convert")) {
141 |     return "MODIFY_CHART";
142 |   }
143 | 
144 |   if (
145 |     q.includes("chart") ||
146 |     q.includes("graph") ||
147 |     q.includes("show") ||
148 |     q.includes("create") ||
149 |     q.includes("generate") ||
150 |     q.includes("visualize") ||
151 |     q.includes("plot")
152 |   ) {
153 |     return "GENERATE_CHART";
154 |   }
155 | 
156 |   if (q.includes("kpi") || q.includes("metric") || q.includes("summary")) {
157 |     return "GENERATE_KPI";
158 |   }
159 | 
160 |   return "ANSWER";
161 | }
162 | 
163 | function detectChartType(query, requestedType) {
164 |   const q = String(query || "").toLowerCase();
165 |   const type = String(requestedType || "").toLowerCase();
166 | 
167 |   if (CHART_TYPES.has(type)) return type;
168 |   if (q.includes("scatter") || q.includes("correlation") || q.includes(" vs ") || q.includes("vs ")) return "scatter";
169 |   if (q.includes("line") || q.includes("trend") || q.includes("over time")) return "line";
170 |   if (q.includes("area")) return "area";
171 |   if (q.includes("pie") || q.includes("share") || q.includes("percentage") || q.includes("distribution")) return "pie";
172 |   if (q.includes("histogram")) return "histogram";
173 | 
174 |   return "bar";
175 | }
176 | 
177 | function detectAggregation(query, requestedAggregation) {
178 |   const q = String(query || "").toLowerCase();
179 |   const agg = String(requestedAggregation || "").toLowerCase();
180 | 
181 |   if (AGGREGATIONS.has(agg)) return agg;
182 |   if (q.includes("average") || q.includes("avg") || q.includes("mean")) return "avg";
183 |   if (q.includes("count") || q.includes("how many") || q.includes("number of")) return "count";
184 |   if (q.includes("minimum") || q.includes("lowest") || q.includes("min")) return "min";
185 |   if (q.includes("maximum") || q.includes("highest") || q.includes("max")) return "max";
186 | 
187 |   return "sum";
188 | }
189 | 
190 | function parseFilterFromQuery(query, schema) {
191 |   const patterns = [
192 |     /filter\s+by\s+([\w\s-]+)\s*[=:]\s*["']?([^"']+)["']?/i,
193 |     /filter\s+([\w\s-]+)\s*[=:]\s*["']?([^"']+)["']?/i,
194 |     /show\s+only\s+([\w\s-]+)\s*[=:]\s*["']?([^"']+)["']?/i,
195 |     /where\s+([\w\s-]+)\s*[=:]\s*["']?([^"']+)["']?/i,
196 |   ];
197 | 
198 |   for (const pattern of patterns) {
199 |     const match = String(query || "").match(pattern);
200 |     if (!match) continue;
201 | 
202 |     const column = findColumn(schema, match[1], ["dimension", "date", "metric"]);
203 |     if (!column) continue;
204 | 
205 |     return {
206 |       [column.name]: match[2].trim(),
207 |     };
208 |   }
209 | 
210 |   return null;
211 | }
212 | 
213 | function safeJsonParse(text) {
214 |   if (!text) return null;
215 | 
216 |   try {
217 |     return JSON.parse(text);
218 |   } catch {
219 |     const match = String(text).match(/\{[\s\S]*\}/);
220 |     if (!match) return null;
221 | 
222 |     try {
223 |       return JSON.parse(match[0]);
224 |     } catch {
225 |       return null;
226 |     }
227 |   }
228 | }
```
